Topic: many significant Effendal artifacts were lost during the Purges, where can I search to find these cultural artifacts?
column5: As most of the Effendal homesteads were either abandoned or destroyed during the Purges, it is likely that many of their cultural relics were left behind if they fled and were able to lockdown their homes before departing. For those that weren't so lucky (the Etilvor who collapsed a mountain on top of them, the Durhen Megil whose home was invaded by Amalgamation soldiers), those artifacts may have been stolen or scavenged by the humans in the region. Futher investigation into these lost artifacts would need to begin with having Effendal of the appropriate House do additional research to even discover what there once was.

Topic: unicorns - how does one find a unicorn? horns of black unicorns used in The Moonless Vessels ritual, and celestials have unicorn horns. can we create a holy artifact (a blessed apple) to feed a unicorn?
column5: Since the celestials first came to Adelrune from the Golden Realms, there have been legends and rumors of unicorns - the result of a celestial being merging with and inhabiting the body of a horse when it comes to Adelrune. They're extremely rare, and impossible to gauge when and where they will appear. Black unicorns are almost impossibly rare.

Topic: onorie
column5: Onorie's symbol is often seen as a callalily. She is the child of Aer and Elena. She is also the goddess of sculpture. She is known to have been killed by the forbidden gods, who are often associated with demon cities. She was worshipped by the Trahazi

Topic: riel
column5: Riel was once known as the living city. The daughter of Cinorast and Ur. It was believed she was the soul of the city and West Riel the capitol of the Celestine Empire was named in her honor while East Riel was renamed Cyrus by the Amalgamation conquest. East Riel was the historic home of the Trahazi people. Was known to be the goddess of sculpture.

Topic: how the college of Cardinals pick the new voice of chorus
column5: It was only after the Echoing of Voices that the College of Cardinals began to choose the new Voice of Chorus - it was always someone with great magic, that which can only exist beyond the power of mortal men and must be granted by divinity itself. Chorus as The Maker, personally blessing the one who would become his next Voice, and it was only through much prayer, searching the country, and seeking the guidance from Chorus that the new Voice would be discovered and raised as such. It was rare that they would promote from within, almost never was it a Cardinal or high ranking Church member, but someone blessed by Chorus directly.

Topic: if Adelrune soul energy is inevitable all flowering toward the death realms and leaving Adelrune, what will happen to Adelrune, on a long enough timeline, when all the soul energy is gone? no life? souls from elsewhere? other?
column5: There never seems to be a lack of souls on Adelrune as species continue to procreate (especially with the rapid birth cycles of humanity). On a long enough timeline, assuming the cycle of ages does not progress to the Age of the Great Death which brings the destruction of all things, the world will always be reset under the guidance of the Clocktower and the world will begin anew with more life.

Topic: metaphysical origins of the tears of life that are on the persons of newly returned. includes moral and metaphysical breakdown, and previous attempts to create alternatives
column5: Items that newly Returned arrive in town with are the items that were on their person upon their first death, thus the Tears of Life are stable and crafted potions identical to the stable potions that can be crafted in Port Frey, with all of the same moral quandaries. However, it's unlikely that most people in the world would know what components are used to make Tears of Life, except for Grandmaster Alchemists who have made that potion before. It's not common knowledge that everyone would talk about in the streets, and as Child's Tears are a black market item in the world economy, it's unlikely any alchemist would go around proclaiming what they are doing.

Topic: underwood insight: connections between the parliament of owls and Nadine traitor houses
column5: The Parliament of Owls is a traitorous group that is dedicated to preserving all knowledge even if it goes against the whims of the Empress. Though in Ad Decimum they are more revered though secretive in their nature.

Topic: underworld insight: Lord montgomery
column5: Montgomery has ties with a number of underworld organizations that are known for information dealing and brokering in rare artifacts. The Dancing Spiders and VARIOUS COLLECTORS. He is known in the smuggler runs and corridors in Port Frey but is rarely known to stick with one group, too interfaced with multiple groups at once. It's clear he is looking for something specific, but it is not known what.

Topic: investigate scandals: Lord montgomery (the scandal in Cestral)
column5: When the Vicaul king, Geir married the Cestral princess Méabh, Lord Montgomery was drunk and tripped and knocked over a brazier that caught the princess's dress on fire. In his efforts to put out the fire he grabbed a bowl of wine from the altar and accidentally drenched her. Flustered, he tried to dry her off with his great kilt and not only entangled himself, but summarily flashed the entire wedding party.

Topic: scouting: location of Trina anhavia
column5: They found her body under the docks of the northern warf. In a cesspit.

Topic: Celestine/Dacian wedding customs
column5: Typically, marriage in Dace is rarely a love match and is almost always a business contract. From the day that children are born or while the parents are pregnant, their parents are in the market for marriage lawyers/marriage negotiators. The lawyer is assigned for the child's life till marriage. Lawyer needs to get along with kid and MOST IMPORTANTLY not die. Lawyers oversee playdates, makes introductions, keeps interests of the family as priority. The engagement process is all litigation happening in a third party and are locked into a room until they come to terms. For smaller weddings, it's a few hours or a day or two for formality's sake. For bigger more notable marriages it could take up to a month. People gather in the streets to holler at the window and cheer on during litigation. Other traditions include: the groom to be takes fiance and kidnaps the "bride" and their friends for the bachelorette party. Wedding dress is high priority. NOT WHITE. Goal is to set the fashion trend for the year. Finding your dressmaker is almost as important as marriage lawyer. It is important for the wedding to be the biggest shindig around (ie-snatch up all the vendors so no one else can book them for the same day). Common gifts are fancy pens for signing marriage contracts and frames to display the marriage contract in the home.

Topic: what is the origin of the sword of the meek?
column5: The origins of the Sword of the Meek are shrouded in mystery, but as you look back in history, you find it's mentioned in even the oldest of fairytales, across dozens of cultures, none of which seem to have a common source or origin of the sword.

Topic: how do we make/replicate glass damage?
column5: you don't really have the applicable lores to research this question about creating weapons, but glass damage is not something known to Adelrune to be craftable, so it would likely be something learned from elsewhere

Topic: how is the iron Chalice involved? note: short one assistant
column5: from the submission of the research, it looks like you were short one assistant (only 5 were listed for 3 research actions), so please be careful of that in the future when doing joint research. additionally, you don't really have an applicable lore related to the Iron Chalice, but what you can find is that the Sword of the Meek long pre-dates the Iron Chalice, and it's likely they don't have direct involvement with it as an order. Mordecai specifically seems to have some amount of influence with it, however, which is unrelated to the Iron Chalice.

Topic: the parents of nicodemus
column5: In his quest to conquer the Clocktower, it was revealed that King of the Darlings, Nicodemus, was participating as he sought the power of godhood in order to punish his parents, Styx and Morcin. Seeking his revenge against them for the part they played in his dark creation and subsequent imprisonment when they had discovered the horror they had birthed.

Topic: origin of cursed mask of mor'drake and similar objects
column5: These are cursed items that have origins in the demon cities of Breach. Further research would require lores relevant to demons.

Topic: we are looking for all of the details we can find about ctirad (oblivion)'s death
column5: From the talk and notes you can find around the religious communities of Port Frey, Ctirad's death in the spring of 10r has been strongly associated with the so-called "God Killing Force" that Priestess Itzel's later writings identify more specifically as the overabundance and spread of entropy in the universe

Topic: what stories and information can be found with regards to artifacts that relate to the old ways god Bear?
column5: Requires: Native Lore: Vein and Lore: Old Ways

Topic: history of cinnorast, who and what race is he (human? dragon?), current and past events
column5: also called "The Hammer", "the Burning Touch", "The God Killer". Cinnorast was once a godly smith of great renown who loved craft and metal. When the other gods came to him for weapons of war to wage violence against other gods, he refused to take part in their violence and refused to craft weapons. Frustrated with his refusal, others crippled his hands so he could not work, and separated his soul from his body, then shoved his soul into a hammer which could be used to forge weapons capable of killing gods. While separated from his body, the body can do nothing, while the Hammer can be wielded to cause great destruction as a weapon of war himself - and has given a front row seat to the violence and death of gods. It is known that Cinnorast was used to severely wound Haelo, and kill Brynioch. Cinnorast is son of Nero and Cri, the brother of Eon and Daece, and the father to Kesa and Riel. In the Celestine Tradition he has only ever been depicted or known as a hammer, and his history is talked about as if he once had the form of a man.

Topic: INFL: styx
column5: Styx is sometimes referred to by other names such as "The Blood Fae", "The Lady of Whispers", and "The Lady of Pain". Imagery and visions associated with her are often presented a fae-esque female with red eyes and long nails, though she is generally believed to be a Greater Demon. On the continent of Southern Breach, the largest body of fresh water is called Lake Styx. The water is magically influenced by the Lady of Pain, and it causes agonizing pain to those who consume it or consume crops that have grown from it.

Topic: INFL: brynioch
column5: Brynioch is known as one of the dead/lost gods, and was formerly the patron god of Antioc and its soldiers. In imagery, Brynioch is presented as a human-looking man clad in golden armor. In the Celestine tradition, he's the child of Aer and Eliena, and the brother to Aisthes and Onorie, though he was never worshipped by the Celestine Church. He is known to have been killed by "Cinnorast" the hammer, wielded by (name has been smudged out and seemingly purposefully redacted from any source you can find on the topic).

Topic: Influence: Investigate History. Any and all information about Phoenix
column5: Phoenix holds a unique position among the gods - known as a high-god, one of the progenitors to whom no known or theorirized parentage exists. Often they are classified among the Titans, yet they are wholy unique. Sometimes called Chaos Phoenix, there is some discussion that they were created out of Chaos, spun by the threads of Destiny to give order to the seasons, but there is no substantial evidence to this in any regard. They are called the "Guardian of Seasons" the being who guides the year through it's revolution, or "the Fire Bird", but other names attributed to them paint a darker picture - "The Beautiful Death" and "She who is cursed to Return". Whether they are depicted in the form of a phoenix itself, or a painfully beautiful woman with spiked jewelry and pointed ears, there is one who calls upon her in their greatest time of need - those who martyr themselves. It is rumored that those who call upon her in their moment of martyrdom will find salvation in her ashes, to be reborn anew. This information is found in a general book about journal about dragons, not merely gods, and scribbled in the margins in an unknown handwriting is a single word - "Ackeroniell"

Topic: Influence: Investigate History. Any and all information about Kesa
column5: It is Riel once had a sister known by this name. The child of Cinorast and Eur. Her fate is unknown and she is not currently worshipped by any faiths.

Topic: Influence: Investigate History. any infor about the children of the Titans
column5: Haelo, the First Born, son of Tempest and Cri. Aer, the younger brother, child of Tempest and Cri. Esparei, child of Tempest and Eur. Daece and Eon, twins, younger brother is Cinnorast, children of Nero and Cri. Mentor, child of Nero and Eur.

Topic: Influence: Background Research and Investigate History. The Iron Chalice.
column5: The iron chalice as with all chalice orders, was founded out of a previous knightly order that existed. In this case the brotherhood of Leucraven. They were based out of Antioc up until the era of the crusades when the mists fell across tear. Their speciality was in hunting fae but they were adapt at hunting greater demons and handling dangerous artifacts. The vault of the iron chalice is legendary in its defenses and is well hidden behind multiple layers of magic including warding and dream.

Topic: Influence x4 - battleship.
column5: info about Olya from Rex, letter from Gwendolyn of the Iron Chalice (leads to the anchor sidecar where the players have to use the Iron Chalice rune to control Olya to save her and keep her off the field of the clocktower)

Topic: Is there a copy of or any mention of the blood ritual that Leucraven and Circe Delfestrae subjected the Brotherhood to at the creation of the group in Ambroisus' journal?
column5: No

Topic: Could many faiths cast the curse of frailty on Leucraven simultaneously to cut off Leucraven's connection to Eden?
column5: Theoretically, yes. Though you may run into unattended side-effects given that Leucraven is the chosen one of a very powerful god.

Topic: are there similarities between the seal of Quetxolo and the coin of the damned?
column5: The similarities between these two objects start and end that they have been connected to divine entities and that they have been touched by blood. Beyond that all similarities are circumstantial.

Topic: theories on how the clocktower connect to the world tree?
column5: When the world tree was damaged the clocktower was built to serve the same function as the world tree, to keep the ages turning.

Topic: information on the guardians of the world tree - who they worship. how they protect it. what they value that we could provide
column5: The protectors of the World tree are a lost order of paladins dedicated to the forgotten gods that gave their lives to save the world tree when it was on the brink of destruction. As for what they value, they are in need of nothing since their goal is to care for the tree.

Topic: relighting the flame of the white stag from Great Altar game
column5: The blessings given during the building of the great altar were only temporary gifts given to those chosen to carry the flames of their god. This flame was also used to reconnect the great altar to the gods after the great silence. It is not something that can be turned back on. It is something the gods must do and is only a physical reminder of what gift the candle bore before.

Topic: Wolf - recent events have been of significance? historical figures of significance? specific/unique worship practices?
column5: Wolf is always connected to Thete throughout history.In recent history when Thete was imprisoned by Deroan, Wolf was responsible for prayers. Wolf was at Thete's side the moment the imprisonment ended. Historically Wolf was known to look after Fox's followers as Fox was like a little brother to Wolf. It is rare for a follower of Wolf to worship alone they are almost always found in packs. GET BACK TO THIS

Topic: sketchy/underworld ties of Siton
column5: The spider has always been a symbol associated with underworld elements and backroom deals, along with luring unsuspecting people into their webs. From groups such as the Dancing Spiders who have a world-wide network of informants, spies, and blackmarket occult items, to the Widowers of Dace, the references are wide spread and varied, though unclear as to whether or not they are connected to Siton as a god, or just more the symbol of the spider and imagery that is derived from her oldest worship practices. Like the symbol of the owl is respected for intelligence and knowledge across the world even in non-Old Ways cultures, the spider is much the same in it's broad symbolism.

Topic: the nature of Vojin's departure, why, what he was trying to accomplish, where the fuck is he?
column5: Note: This is information best gathered through influence spends related to investigation. Historically he departed the Nadine empire in the age of Tane. And only reappeared for the crusades but has otherwise not been seen or heard from since.

Topic: how to extract Cinnorast from the hammer?
column5: Note: the last time a god was removed from an item it was inside of it took 50 research actions and the god died.

Topic: where or how could we find more dragon bones?
column5: This is better spent as an influence action because it is related to the current state of things. Typically finding dragon bones has to do with finding a dead dragon or killing a living one to get its bones.

Topic: what are the steps we need to take to ensure that the followers of Chorus (past/present/future) will have a death realm where they can find peace and rest?
column5: Without a death realm or a death god who has enough power to claim the souls of the faithful to Chorus, all unmarked souls will go to Eden's realm. To establish a death realm, theoretically, since it has never been done you would need a death god with enough power to challenge Eden and carve out a space of their own in the death realms where the faithful they have claimed can go. Given that this was the outcome for Diana's *seventh* wish, you suspect it would take quite a bit of power from a not insignificant god to survive this fight.

Topic: cure/prevention of swamp crotch, has alchemy and lore: disease, nadine
column5: Swamp crotch is an infection that’s caused by a fungus. It's a form of ringworm that likes to live in warm, moist places on your body. Early symptoms of swamp crotch are itching and burning in the groin region, that progresses to a red, scaly, circular rash with raised edges and inflammation. Treatment is typically a salve made by an alchemist of any rank, and comprised of: sedative, rejuvenation, purification.

Topic: Snake - worship practices, historical figures/events
column5: NOTE: any written information regarding Snake continues to count him in the pantheon as these sources are often written by civilized academics from the outside. However, within Old Ways cultures, Snake was long-ago cast out from the faith due to his utter betrayal of the pantheon. In watching the rising of new gods and faiths, he learned from the pettiness and infighting of the Celestine gods that by killing another god he can become more powerful. And thus, he killed Fox. Still, among Old Ways cultures, cults of Snake can be found among smaller tribes and clans, often considered heretical or darker-focused groups like the Snake Tribe of the Coatl on Tear or the Banner of the Red Snake of the Gael, and common worship practices circle often around the gifts that Snake provided to humanity. Known as The Cunning Liar, using one's words to twist the truth in a way to benefit the worshipper is the lesson he taught humanity in the beginning, but his betrayal gave Humanity the lesson of selfish ambition, to always seek power for oneself. As with all Old Ways gods, using their gifts is always the most effective form of worship. In 7ar, with the waking of the Old Ways gods, Snake was called back to the Pantheon by the Returned, though it is unclear if he has returned with the forgiveness of the gods for his past actions.

Topic: Investigate History: Owl
column5: The Owl is deeply tied and connected with nearly every culture on Adelrune as a symbol of wisdom and intelligence. The form the owl takes is localized by region, but the symbolism of the owl representing wisdom and information gathering organizations is as present in the Celestine Empire as it is for the Saek, as it is for the Vicaul. There are rumors of a destroyed information network called the Parliament of Owls in the Nadine Empire, as well as the snow white Owl. god of the Old Ways who is said to scratch at the eyes of his followers with sharp talons, blinding them until they see the guidance he is giving them. Traditionally, the owls represent wisdom in that they are able to take in a lot of information and forget that which is irrelevent, only keeping what is important.

Topic: Investigate History: "the Lady of Darkness"
column5: as you begin this investigation, you quickly get the sense that you are being watched/followed. the only real lead you find links her to the founder of Ad Decimum, Belisarius, as someone he greatly fears. before your research can go any further, an assination attempt is made on your life. it doesn't succeed, they stabbed you near the healer's guild where they knew you would be found, but you suspect it was a purposeful choice - like this was a warning, rather than a serious attempt.

Topic: Investigate History: "emperor of the gods" and his court
column5: as you reach out to scholars to inquire about the "emperor of the gods", you seemingly find few sources that are willing to speak with you about such things. your search leads you to a quieter part of town to speak with a particular source of information who is willing to speak with you, but as you approach his home, a crowd of peasants push past you murmuring "Praise Chorus!" amongst themselves. reaching his home, you find the source stabbed to death with dozens of wounds, and unable to be resusitated.

Topic: Political Insight: Antioc leadership (Delfestrae)
column5: Before the Mists fell, there was a small diplomatic envoy of Delfestrae (approximately 5) who traveled to Antioc with the aim to come to some sort of formalized peace agreement in the aftermath of Towerfall. These five, led by Taegan Armelian (a known ally of Perillinen Siili Sininen), were then stranded in Antioc when the Mists rolled in. During their time in Antioc, they have found themselves in favored positions, with Taegan acting as Viceroy Paxima's right hand, and being given the rank of Legatus. Being allied with Sininen, this envoy was chosen for their pragmatism and finding the best outcome for Delfestrae. From the outside, however, it's hard to tell what their current goals and progress may be, if they truly have joined the Nadine, or if they are merely surviving the Mists in the best way they know how.

Topic: Eavesdrop: Baron Paxima
column5: We will have logi refund you this cost, as getting servants into Antioc is not possible under the current conditions of the city

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Nadine/Effendal children
column5: From the rumors you heard from those who have visited Antioc since the Mists, there are a handful of young children in the city. There are very few, and only a couple seemed to have had elongated ears on quick glance. However, it is unclear if they were born between unions of the visiting Delfestrae envoy and Nadine, or if there are some within the Nadine ranks who had Effendal blood.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Tabitha Terminatus
column5: Rumor has it, since the departure of Princes Aeris and the Blood Lords from Tear, Tabitha seems to have relaxed significantly in the aftermath of the destruction of Antioc. This coincides with her recent absences from Court. Rumor has it, she has been receiving an exceptionally large amount of correspondence from the Nadine Empire since the lifting of the Mists. Rumor has it, Tabitha's ship was seen leaving port with a number of effendal on board, only to return a few short months later. Rumor has it, her ship appears to be being prepared for an upcoming long journey. Rumor has it, Tabitha's most recent lover has gone missing. Rumor has it, Tabitha cleared out Baron Paxima's home and office in Port Frey as soon as news reached her of the detruction of Antioc. Rumor has it, Tabitha may have taken a new lover, as a mysterious figure has been seen exiting and entering her home at odd hours of the night.

Topic: What or who in the Dreaming could nullify Leucraven or similar entities and how can we appeal to them?
column5: Whose-who questions like this are generally more approachable with influence spends or in-character roleplay, as written records about the Dreaming are very rare, and unreliable at best. Additionally, there is much debate about whether or not the Dreaming can interact with beings of Death or blessed by Death, such as Leucraven who is a favored Death Knight of Eden.

Topic: Can we find a ritual to trap an entity in another plane that has been done before (ie Leucraven)?
column5: The Books of Verody tend to have these types of rituals in them. Of particular note, in the early days of the Returned there is notation that the Returned trapped Valdir in the Mists with a stake (currently held by Black Council/Moon Tribe) and a necklace that had belonged to his dead wife

Topic: cultural celestine wedding traditions & specific events or special requirements
column5: Most Celestine weddings typically happen in the Spring, though that is not a hard and fast rule. In paricular for Nobles, they will happen at one of the greater temples rather than small town temples or someones' home. This is a way for the couples to go honor the gods and pay their respects to all of the gods. Blue Celestine Roses are the most frequently used flowers and are grown in greenhouses throughout the Empire specifically for spring blooms and used for weddings. Throughout the last Age, tradition has it that rather than a bachelor party, the groom must defeat any challengers in a duel. Note: These are rarely true challengers for her hand, typically it is members of the spouse's family or his friends who will make a challenge for the showmanship for it. However, even when mainly done for show, they will still try to win and will not throw the fight on their own honor. It is believed if you win these duels, the gods will bless the marriage. Note: Either member of the couple can fight the duel, it is not limited to the groom, many brides also take on the duel.

Topic: Ancient Hazori Sickle - can the effect be replicated? what region was it from? how old is this relic?
column5: The Ancient Hazori sickle is from the Hazori lands near Riel. As there is no real way to carbon date an exact year, you can certainly tell this is from the Age of Tane, from sometime after the founding of Riel and before the fall of Riel. The effect can potentially be researched through blacksmithing but the exact effect would be difficult to replicate without Rielian blacksmiths.

Topic: who was Saoirse Tackett? (161ch) - did she do anything significant in her time? what historical events were happening at this time?
column5: Saoirse Tackett is one of the most renown members of House Tackett in recent centuries. She had one younger sister, Dahlia Tackett (Clove's mother), and left her home after her own husband and children died to a sickness just a few short years after her marriage. In the ensuing years, she was a good politician who ran royal messages, and served as a courier in the outlying villages and Houses, and assisted in evacuating during Vicaul raids. In 155ch, during the Bloody Landing, she could not save her brother-in-law during the evacuation, but was able to rescue the child he had been trying to protect - the young heir to House Tairgrid. It was not her goal in life to reclaim the good standing of House Tackett, but that day her bravery gained them the approval of House Tairgrid if they should continue to seek the path to remove the sable mark on the family. She was later executed during the War of Betrayal, the order given by the corrupt king who sought to silence her for knowing too much as the royal courier.

Topic: further research on Dacian & Celestine wedding traditions - particularly day-of traditions
column5: Celestine weddings are heavily religious. For instance: the bride's bouquet gets distributed and broken up in offering to the different altars of the pantheon that she wants to recieve blessings from. Upon engagement (for nobility) a sculpture is comissioned to represent the union ("I will love you/commit to you until the stone is worn to sand"). For peasantry, they might carve their initials into a stone kept in their home, or upon a large boulder or fountain in the town square that is intended for this same purpose.  The sculptures remains in their home till they separate or die. The morning of the wedding, the sculpture will go with them to each of the shrines as they are blessed. The statue recieves the blessing and will remain in their home as a vessel of the blessings.

Topic: Eleina - any and all information about her
column5: Eleina is the Painter also called the Volcano (for no particularly known reason). She is the child of Phoenix and Nero and is heavily associated with and popular in any of the Celestine offshoot nations (Dace and Eirie, and formerly Kotu and Theddespari). The most northern city in the Celestine Empire is named for her, near the Disputed Lands at Westwall. For all of this, however, she is one of the weaker gods of the pantheons, and as such is believed to be particularly at risk of being consumed by the entropy of the universe. Worship for her was once more abundant as the old churches were filled with paintings and frescos, but in more recent decades, sculpture and stained glass have grown to be more prominently displayed.  One particularly renown painter out of Dace, Bellisimo, is known to be a true devotee to the goddess and has sworn his life to painting and creating beauty in her name.

Topic: Siton - historical events, specific/unique worship practices. historical figures of significance
column5: Siton, called "The Spider" or the "Wicked Spider" is known among the Old Ways to be the god who taught humanity to fear - who gave them their fight or flight response, and who teaches them the toughest lessons through prophecy by showing them their own deepest fears and pressing them to overcome them. Siton is often called upon in times of trial and tribulation, when the Old Ways followers are in need of courage to press on through the darkness and fear they are experiencing, and often associated with the winter when darkness reigns over the lands in the north - especially for the Gael and Vicaul. In more recent years, during the Northern War, it was the Clan of the Night Spider who brought destruction to the Temple of Destiny, while rumor has it that at the same time, Siton used an avatar of herself to attack Destiny through the conduit of a Returned who was dedicated to the Celestine goddess. While Siton's avatar was ultimately repelled from the attack on the goddess, she was able to cause a deep and lasting wound on the other goddess before disengaging from the conflict. The Temple of Destiny was not so lucky - it was truly sacked, it's holy texts damaged, destroyed, or looted, and it's entire legion and handmaiden taken down in the conflict.

Topic: Veyn - historical events, specific/unique worship practices, historical figures of significance (CESTRAL PERSPECTIVE)
column5: Like Belaiesh, Veyn holds a position of reverence and fear among the Cestral who follow the Old Ways. Over the centuries, Cestral has fallen victim to many floods that are traditionally believed to be caused by Veyn, but as the Fresh Water he provides is necessary for life and farming, he is also worshipped more heavily for his favor than a destructive god like Balaiesh. Seasonal flooding among the lowlands is not uncommon in Cestral, so villages and towns have different traditions to gain his favor, but some of the more common ones are scrificing the first fishing haul of the year, and having Druids bless the low-laying cranberry fields before they release the dams they have built to control the flooding of the cranberry bogs for harvest. With clever irrigation techniques as well, the Cestral farmers honor the god of strategists to use his gift to their best advantage.

Topic: information on great wyrms - origins, last known appearances on Adelrune, powers, connections to planar cosmology, etc
column5: Zegimmon - "The Sea Beast" - Eater of dreams - last known wearabouts: the Dreaming Kcelizex - "Lord of Slime" - Giant Slimy Scaled Worm - rumored to be in the Breach Xaganorix - "The Bloated Tyrant" - Bloated Scaled Worm with Wings, Eater of Fae Shselecs [ Antitaetesh ] - "The Spirit Wyrm" - Spectral Scaled Worm Glowing White, eater of souls - Bound to Eden's realm Rtkre - "The Festering One" / "The Eater of Worlds" - A Scaled Worm Covered in festering sours, eater of worlds - rumored to be imprisoned

Topic: can herbs with spiritual properties be used in the creation of Breath of Life, particularly as a replacement for Celestial blood
column5: No. Any form of resurrection requires energy of pure life given for resurrection. In certain cases (like Breath of Life), Celestial blood can be substituted as Celestials themselves contain a potent energy capable of fulfilling this requirement due to their pure nature and intrinsic ability to resurrect the recently deceased.

Topic: looking for all information I can find on Loremaster Gaetan, his book collections, and the books he retrieved from sistinas fortress
column5: Loremaster Gaetan was born in 131ch, and is known to have worked out of Alexander in Cestral in the latter half of the Age of Chorus under the guidance of a Celestine scholar, Walworth, frequently running errands for him in his early years. In academic circles he is reknowned for rescuing banned books and such objects that were censored by the Church of Chorus, however he continually denied such actions when questioned. After making a name for himself as a troublemaker and nusiance in the Amalgamation, Celestine Empire, and just about every city on the Expanse, his later life was spent at Alexander and assisting the Crown in Cestral as an advisor. Note: Any books he stole from various nations are likely to still be held at Alexander, with their origins denied/not recorded. However, such books are likely held under the protection of the Loremasters Guild in Special Collections, and their current status is currently unknown given the events of the War of Radiance. At the exceptional age of 77 years old, he died in the Battle of Alexander in 8ar, at Ser Aislynn's side as they held a collapsing wall long enough the last survivors to find safety from the approaching demon horde.

Topic: INFLUENCE: INVESTIGATE HISTORY: history of the brotherhood in relation to creating the coins of the damned

Another rumor is that the high priest is dabling in dark magics. An additional rumor is that Pious Justineous has abandoned the church and gone to found a new one. Another rumor is that Inquisitors are coming to Port Frey to purge blasphemous members of the church. Another rumor says that Chorus is not dead and that this is only a test of the faithful and if enough nonhumans are purged in his name, he will return.

Topic: INFLUENCE: UNDERWORLD INSIGHT: local church of Chorus
column5: While the local Church of Chorus itself does not have known connections in the underworld, there have been questions and concerns related to individuals within the Church. Beginning with its Chroister who is known to have been a companion of Lord Bellus and many Dreamers, to some of its members having connections to Churchwood Brotherhood, as well as other various mercenary and gang connections on a lower level. It doesn't seem like they are acting on behalf of the church, but to have prominent members and leaders acting in underworld circles on their own basis raises general suspicion. The dark magic tome called Ig is known to be found frequently within the church, to the distrust of much of the local populace, and a number of prominent Dream Mages have been heard to speak of how the Church itself was used for illicit dream magic activities in relation to the Red Knightmare.

Topic: INFLUENCE: STATE OF THE COURT - infrastructure of Port Frey to determine defences and status
column5: Port Frey has earthen work walls that were hastily and quickly constructed over the swamp land the town rests on during the War of Radiance. Over time, these walls have been compacted down so they are not as high nor effective as they once were. Some areas have taken additional damages in recent wars such as the invasion of T'Tocs' forces and the Nadine Siege of Port Frey. Measures have been taken on multiple occassions to shore up the worst of these areas. NOTE: Belltower?

Topic: INFLUENCE: UNDERWORLD INSIGHT: the Broken Brick
column5: The Broken Brick is an underground group of anarchists formed from members of the lower ranks of Thorn's Military. They are known for causing disruptions in day to day dealings and operations, and occasionally making a bigger point at ceremonial and military functions. They are unique to Castle Thorn as they are acting out against the various military houses and basic structure of Thorn. There are rumors that they are connected to the Nameless Faith but as most information of this group is shrouded in mystery it is impossibly to verify the validity of this situation. The best protection Thorn has against the actions of the Broken Brick is that they are lower ranked soldiers and thus don't have the influence or resources to cause a bigger situation that would undermine the integrity of Castle Thorn. But the superiors have no doubt that if a higher ranking arficer were to join their cause that the disruptions would be more dificult to deal with.

Rumor has it they were funded by the same highly ranked foreign spy that worked incognito among the Thorns during the War of Radiance and was never caught. Topic: INFLUENCE: MILITARY INSIGHT: knights of the blood chalice, locations and leadership
column5: Officially, the Knights of the Blood Chalice were destroyed and disbanded by the White Chalice during the purification. However, a well-guarded cultural secret within Mandala is that the order is still active and quietly monitoring blood mages, eliminating those who get too out of hand with their practices. Beyond the borders of Mandala, there are no active cells, only the ocassional knight or small squad moving out to do their work under the guise of a diplomatic mission for Mandala.

Topic: INFLUENCE: UNDERWORLD INSIGHT: who is killing merchants?
column5: This spend generally requires an inquiry into a single person or faction, but there has been enough talk circulating as of late to give confirmation that it wasn't a single person or faction behind the excessive disappearances and killings of International Merchant's Guild members - more that it seemed to be many different Returned acting independently of one another as the merchants ventured into the dangerous Returned District of Port Frey. As such, the more reputable Merchant's Guild is pulling their roots from Port Frey and moving to the interior towns with their base on Tear operating out of Solace, and ships departing from Shearwater to the north of Port Frey. This has left a hole in the economy of Port Frey for wildly fluctuating prices as the underworld factions vie for domination of the market and with the ongoing issue of the Mists, an entire market collapse doesn't look to be far off.

However, it is believed that there are a number of people who were involved in these killings, and not just one person.

Topic: INFLUENCE: UNDERWORLD INSIGHT: Loremaster Gaetan
column5: As an old man of exceptional age, Loremaster Gaetan Goode served faithfully at his post at Alexander, and has no known direct ties to any particular underworld organizations. However, in his younger years, he had been charged with smuggling, heresy, and theft in every kingdom of the Amalgamation and was known to work with various criminal elements across the Expanse to accomplish his goals until a severe injury from an assassination attempt took him out of the field in 176ch.

Topic: INFLUENCE: INVESTIGATE HISTORY: difference between Cestral Old ways practices and others Old ways
column5: While Old Ways worshippers follow the same gods across all of Adelrune, the early Druids of Cestral feared the wrath of the gods and the plague of natural disasters that were not uncommon to their new land. The great storms of Balaiesh, the terrible floods of Veyn, and the poor growing seasons of Ren and Hawk told of the displeasure the gods had towards the people of the newly settled island. Tales told of the favor of Thte and Wolf were given to the Vicaul, sent to punish the early Cestral for abandoning their home for this new land, their faith rocky and uncertain even then as the Hazori had already begun to shift from the Old Ways. They were not seen as Betrayers by the other Old Ways cultures *yet* but only because the displeasure of the gods was tangible in the early years of Cestral. The druids there returned to the practices of the Old Ways to appease the gods, their druidic enclaves forgetting about the burgeoning faith the Hazori that had begun to develop, and from the very beginning there was a split in the faith of the island. The druids became more reclusive, tending to the lands and their forests, appeasing the gods with sacrifices and worship far from the prying eyes of their burgeoning city. Living an almost separate existance from the rest of the people upon their island. Over the centuries this separation grew more pronounced - with the common Cestral focused on gratitude for the tangible gifts of the gods (a good harvest, clever irrigation systems, or clear sunny skies), appreciative for what the gods provide to them, but offering little in return. It's rumored that it is the druids who live the more reclusive lives that pay the cost for the rest of their island having the idyllic life that modern Cestral are accustomed to. What that cost is, is unclear.

Topic: INLFUENCE: INVESTIGATE HISTORY: Old Ways Practices that are ingrained in Cestral culture
column5: Throughout the year, there are many common Old Ways traditions that are part of normal Cestral society, to the point that many seem to have forgotten that they were Old Ways traditions at all, and thus still continue on in harmony even among the followers of the Church of Chorus or the rare Celestine faithful. The Jam Festival, for example, is often decorated with antlers, and in more religious communities there are often prayers and offerings given to Stag as thanks for providing enough for the winter even after the Vicaul raids. The Jam Festival itself and the common decorating motifs are still attended by non-Old Ways followers as to them it is not religious in nature, but part of Cestral life. The spring planting crosses over frequently with rituals for Ren (a blessing of good growth - below ground crops planted under a new moon), Veyn (for nuturing rains - above ground crops planted under a full moon with high tides), and Stag (for a bountiful harvest), and Wolf for choosing which days to plant based on the moon phases. Fishermen will either toss back their first catch of the year in honor of Veyn, or burn the catch as an offering for Stag, as is tradition. Alexander has owl statues and sculptures throughout the university and library, often gifted by foreign visitors and royals studying from abroad - as the symbolism of owls being wise and knowledgeable permeates much of the world, even nations such as the Celestine Empire and Amalgamation. Most of their scary stories told to children are about spiders invading their islands, both a reference to the yearly Vicaul raids, but also the lessons of Siton on how to overcome their fears. While the Druidic Order of Cestral has deeper more secretive practices aligning with most of these more common traditions, the simple point of it is that whether religiously-minded or not, Old Ways traditions permeate all aspects of Cestral life.

Topic: INVESTIGATE HISTORY: what are the earliest stories we can find about the Trahazi god Gidu?
column5: The earliest stories of Gidu date from the decades following the Exile, in a time when few caravans had priests trained in the faith of the Hazori (and fewer still had ever seen a holy text), when the Trahazi Zodiac was only just coming into being.   One of the most commonly told stories of Gidu tells of the kindly grandfather who had great expectations for his children. Neruk, Inya, and Nytchka grew and became successful in each of their own ways, but always on the outskirts dwelt Dechko, the odd chicken out of the siblings. Now, Gidu wasn't called Gidu at such a time, which will be important later, so often in this particular story he is called Grandfather, and Dechko is called Uncle.  Now, Uncle did not like being the odd one out, he did not like that the others walked proudly alongside Grandfather and he was overlooked. When the younger children sought comfort, they went to Grandfather to help, for what good was the hug of an odd Uncle? But Uncle was determined that he would be seen and the others would want to spend time with him, but no one knew Uncle's name and he had no reputation of strength and support, nor the steadfast ambition possessed by Grandfather. So when he left home to go out into the world, Uncle chose to use Grandfather's name to his advantages, adopting the name Dechko. He leaned into Grandfather Dechko's reputation for reliably chasing away monsters and claimed the name for himself, spinning wild stories of his fantastical adventures saving children and fighting off nightmares. In time, stories about Dechko's great acts began to find their way back home, and Grandfather grew unhappy to know that Uncle was using his name. Even as he demanded Uncle give up the name Dechko, the followers who had met Dechko and heard his stories told others of him and spread Uncle Dechko's name to the corners of Adelrune and among all the caravans, and it became Uncle Dechko who the children came to for comfort in the dark and the greatest stories rather than Grandfather . Without a name of his own now, Grandfather became more stern and demanding of his family, more watchful over their actions as distrust in their reliability grew within his heart, and so he remains grumpy even now, for his name and all of those who loved him once were stolen from him by his own child.

Topic: why does a true name etched into a sword hurt true? can it be replicated for other creatures?
column5: A true name is possessed by a creature that is not from the plane of Adelrune. That name is what allows it to be here as its tether. Using a sword that has true name, doesn't just strike the body on adelrune, it strikes the true being. If the true name was put on a shield it would give resistance to being harmed by that being. True names can be used to summon creatures or to bind or effect them in strong rituals as well. But true names cannot be spoken and they are closely and deeply guarded secrets. Additionally, while you may know what a true name looks like, unless it is on ritualistic paper it cannot be reused.

column5: King Halvar - Northern Vicaul - Dislikes King Tore, considers him weak. Hates the Scylvol Clan for taking his daughters, Aud and Drifa, away from him. Topic: INFLUENCE: POLITICAL INSIGHT - Church of Chorus
column5: With the loss of the upper leadership of the Church, small, disorganized groups who have someone strong enough to lead are lashing out against anyone they can, and are leaning into old prejudices and teachings. What remains of the Church of Chorus on the Expanse seems to be consolidating in the ruins of Cyrus, gathering around a New Voice.

Topic: INFLUENCE: INVESTIGATE HISTORY - Church of Chorus
column5: The Church of Chorus has been the most powerful and wealthy religion in the world through the Age of Chorus. They were involved in and led every way on the Eastern Expanse throughout the entirety of the age, and as a result of the Crown Wars took control of the Amalgamation as a theocracy, placing the Church above the Kings, and reserving the right of approval and coronation of those they found deserving of the crown. The Assassination of the Voice in 200ch and lack of new Voice marked the beginning of the collapse of the Church, and a decade later it is in but ruins.

Topic: INFLUENCE: INVESTIGATE HISTORY - Inquisition
column5: For a nation as rife with internal struggle as the Amalgamation, the Inquisitors are always on the fields of battle to lead the armies of the Amalgamation. During the Crusades and Purification, Inquisitors were at the forefront of the charge, as well as later wars with the Nadine and the War of Giants with the Vein. Outside of battle, Inquisitors have served several key roles throughout history: first is to spread the mandates of the Church, second is to seek out heresy and threats to the nation and destroy it, and third is to maintain order through fear.

Topic: INFLUENCE: POLITICAL INSIGHT - Inquisition
column5: The majority of the Inquisition who were still on the Expanse after the destruction of the Church were looking for a fight, someone to lash out against as they sought meaning after the death of Chorus and destruction of the Church and their now-godless nation, breaking their spirit and zeal. Those who were charismatic enough to lead drew small forces to them and leaned into old prejudices as they attempted to continue in the ways they had known. In more recent months, with Pious Justinius on a spiritual retreat with the other Piouses and unreachable from the Expanse, these Inquisitors have begun to rally with their smaller forces in Cyrus - joining with the New Voice.

Topic: INFLUENCE: MILITARY INSIGHT - Inquisition
column5: Church Forces: The Disciples of the Ardent Choir 4200 religious fanatics Inquisitor Claudia Hospitalus 130 Church Guard, 9 Inquisitors, 1800 Soldiers Inquisitor Letholdus 1000 Church Guard, 27 Inquisitors, 1800 Soldiers Inquisitor Jarrett 81 Church Guard, 12 Inquisitors "The Voice": 500 Church soldiers, 1,000 religious fanatics, 2,000 poorly trained peasants, The Exiled Central Army 10,600 Poorly trained and equiped Soldiers who were formerly dominated and forced into Bel'e'athru's service, have joined with The Voice who promised them absolution through service.

Topic: rumored or known members of the Choirs of Chorus?
column5: As a highly elite, specialty team appointed personally by the last Voice of Chorus and serving his orders directly, the Seventh Choir of Chorus is as follows:  The Seventh Choir of Chorus/The Last Choir Sir Eckhart Cynweard de Andovere - from a long list of famous Inquisitors, his son and grandson would carry on his legacy in Voi Sir Lorenz Benzinger - fought in the War of Giants as a young knight, served as Church influence in the Kingdom of Vein-Vercrux during the merging of the nations Sir Zelig Voight - assigned to the Eastern Army, heavily dealt with the Ko’aat threat Dame Gisel Fieber - oversaw a special detachment responsible for censorship and removal of heretical texts Sir Egon Tilgner - a commander of the Northern Army

Topic: identity of Baba
column5: Baba is the loving grandmother, the center and root of the family. It should come as no surpise then that Baba's older name was Ren. As Ren is known as the root of creation in the Old Ways practices, the creation of humanity, it is only logical that they would remain in a grandmotherly role as the Zodiac developed. While they may not be "head of the family", they are the elder of the family and deeply respected as such. Protective magic is especially associated with Ren and Baba both, and this practice has carried on as well as the making of new things is still seen in her traditions in knotwork and shawls.

Topic: identity of Inya
column5: Inya is the quieter of the family, the peacekeeper, and so it's not unrealistic or unexpected that Inya's personality has developed from the ancient goddess of Kesa - "the Silent One" / "The Still One" / "Lady Order". Kesa being the power behind the tarot also lends to the personality of Inya being the encouraging and supportive aunt, known to help guide her family on the paths ahead of them, or caution them against taking certain roads without foresight. As Inya has been imprisoned for so long, even before the time of the Hazori, her original religious practices are not known, so it is unclear how they differ from the more ancient worship practices of Kesa.

Topic: identity of kuyeh
column5: It's commonly known among the Trahazi that Vesh is the god of Betrayal. In the earlier days of the Hazori, Vesh was reveared more out of fear than true appreciative worship. This can still be seen in phrases and practices like "Vesh always follows, so never stay in place for too long." Over the years, however, many of the stories that featured his lesser betrayals or the malicious intents towards the Trahazi developed into forgetful pranks that he had "forgot" he set up. And so the two personalities developed in their own separate ways: Vesh was kept as the malicious betrayer, and Kuyeh became the loveable prankster. Unlike the other zodiac gods, both of these personalities have persisted under separate names.

Topic: Trahazi wedding traditions, have they changed, different caravans
column5: Wedding traditions greatly by caravan, region, and by even who is getting married as weddings are not legally binding in any way. Some more effendal-dominate caravans follow Effendal practices where the marriages only last 10 years, while in some caravans the marriages are shorter or longer respectively. Most frequently, when a marriage is proposed, there is a tradition of building something together to be certain the marriage will be strong enough to solve problems together - whether that is building a vardo, knitting a scarf, or writing a play together - whatever is built is meant to be something designed to be a gift to their caravan and community, representing their commitment to building a life together. Giving gifts to the marriage is extremely uncommon as the relationship should build/collect their possessions together and will often share what other families have until they are able to get their own, though for some caravans with influence from cultures where such things are common (like Cestral) gift giving to a union may be practiced.

Topic: Research: House Ra'e'len arrowhead - when/who/why it was in that stash
column5: While the ceremonial arrowheads do not have names on them, they do often have distinguishing marks, beads, or gems added to them to tell them apart. It takes quite some time for Palauttaa to come back to you with a name and information about this particular arrowhead as the records of Ra'e'len were old and worn and took time to go through.    This particular ceremonial arrowhead belonged to the great Giehta, the First of Leneth, who was called "The Unwavering Hand of Vengeance" in his life, and after his death was given the title of "The Last Standing Stone" by the survivors of Svivore. He had a particular knack for tearing through Ko'aat, and had a reputation as the stone wall that could take down horses and weilders of magic with nary a glance in his life. In his last battle against the Snake Ko'aat in 191t, he stood firm, taking out dozens of them even as they overran him and his tribe. Their dark pacts and the magic they wielded could be withstood, but not forever. And unlike the Ra'e'len Homestead and Theddespari, they did not have a fortress of walls to provide them a more defensive footing. Bolstered by his strength, his soldiers wove themselves through trees and stone, moving quickly across the battlefield. They were tactical and strategic in their movements against the chaos of the Ko'aat, taking out as many as they could, but it was a losing battle as the tide of Ko'aat never slowed. In the end, with Leneth nearly wiped out completely and Svivore's numbers dwindling, he ordered the Svivore to retreat. With the last of his men, he charged into the heart of the Ko'aat ranks, straight for the Snake Queen herself. There were few who remained behind to see the fight, but it was long and drawn out fight between the two leaders, with the Snake Queen's strikes inflicting poison into Giehta's blood with every glancing touch of her daggers. The last of Leneth fell around him, but still he fought on, determined to take her down with him. In the end, he was not able to overcome the poisons that slowed his responses and weighed down his limbs. For the few Svivore still hidden amongst the high branches of the trees, they watched their last bastion of Leneth finally fall under the hand of the Snake Queen who was painted in his blood as she snatched away the ceremonial arrowhead that hung from his neck and rode on, adorning her horse's mane with yet another trophy of a leader slain by her hand.   When the Svivore eventually returned to the battlefield to collect their fallen, the single surviving child of Leneth, a strange little boy who had always shown an affinity for stonework over the blade, pressed the body of Giehta into a great standing stone, entombing him there as a reminder of what was lost that day.

Topic: what happens when the spirit eats a soul? how did it get that title? why is it locked in Eden's realm?
column5: Antitaetesh, as the spirit wyrm is currently known, gains power and nourishment from eating souls. Recently eaten souls can be barfed up as soldiers of war, in twisted and torutured forms, but once fully digested the souls are dissolved entirely. Formerly the Spirit Wyrm gained power as the god of possession, but that power was stripped from them, and in doing so they were weakened enough that it allowed Leucraven to lock them up with a binding ritual, providing Eden's realm with a pure force of nature as a guard dog. Any souls that wander too close to it or are sent to it from Eden are consumed.

Topic: does NF back the Shrike's power? how does she answer prayers if not a god? unless she has a god on retainer? is the shrike one of the nameless faith gods?
column5: There is no evidence or way to discover the metaphysical truth of gods and what powers religions through research. The faithful of the Blood Cauldron display the same types of gifts and powers as any other religion or cult following, so the Shrike seemingly has some way to have that happen whether or not she's a true god. It is known that she is capable of accepting and using the power of Red Candles.

Topic: what are all recently seen ancient religious artifacts that can possibly be found in the historical record? or heard about through rumor?

Publicly known religious artifacts that are known to have been seen in recent years include the Mask of Haelo, the Gauntlet of Haelo, the Sword of Haelo, and Cinnorast, the god hammer.

Topic: did any fae make cestral their domain? could the fae have made the land abundant with life?
column5: Fae are not native to Cestral, and do not make homes for themselves on Adelrune as they are not native to this plane. They are merely visitors here. There is no evidence that the abundance and life that the land of Cestral displays has anything to do with fae, or is fae in origin.
