column5: Teaching Dream Magic is now a full law, no longer a proclamation, and as with any other exemptions, one can appeal directly to the Unity Parliament to plead their case for the necessity of the exemption

Topic: if Eden (or any god with power over souls) no longer existed, what would happen to the souls that are not god claimed
column5: As happened with Chorus' death realm when it collapsed, the unmarked souls will be absorbed into other death realms, mostly to the next most powerful death god.

Topic: do dragons & wyrms reincarnate ONLY into other dragons or wyrms, respectively?
column5: There is no way to know or discover this information, and no evidence that a reincarnated anything remembers it's prior life.

Topic: the existance of any colish national alchemical recipes or concoctions that are special to or originated in Cole. also the exitence of any famous colish alchemists
column5: Alchemy is especially important for sailing cultures like Cole because chanelling and other methods of healing are difficult to do at sea on long voyages. This is especially true for healing draughts and also for treatments of disease or illness such as motion sickness potions for those new to the ocean or casual travelers unaccustomed to being on a ship for a long period of time. While at sea, many alchemists are known to dabble in brew-making and concocting various types of moonshine from whatever food may be on board and starting to go bad.

Topic: is there an alchemical basis to the nature of greater beings. we theorize, based on lazarus' ritual in the Guardian's journal, where Laz turned himself into a demon, that there is a connection between humours, alchemical elements, and greater beings
column5: There are many methods to move from being a "lesser" being to a "heroic" being (like Returned), but there is little evidence or known method to become a "greater" being except through extraordinary circumstances like the Clocktower. In the case of Lazarus and his transformation in the Guardian's Journal, he was already a heroic being, and did not become a true demon. He mutated and transformed his body by infusing it with demonic corruption to become something else, not something "greater".

Topic: any recent information, included the last known, recorded location of the book of Kus, the book of Verody for alchemy
column5: The last known location for the Book of Kus was in the Breach city of Haen, when the city was being sacked. It is rumored to have been smuggled out of the city in the chaos, but there is no evidence as to the truth of this rumor.

Topic: what is the history of unicorns? is there an expert on unicorns in adelrune that we can write to or talk to? how does one go about finding a unicorn
column5: Since the celestials first came to Adelrune from the Golden Realms, there have been legends and rumors of unicorns - the result of a celestial being merging with and inhabiting the body of a horse when it comes to Adelrune. They're extremely rare, and impossible to gauge when and where they will appear.

Topic: Political Insight: Whitehall (motivations, leanings, diplomatic relations)
column5: Whitehall has a fantastic reputation within the Amalgamation, it's managed well, and Lord Darius Lelaroy is celebrated as a hero in Voi. The current concerns and unspoken worries both within the city and with its allied kingdoms is the question of if the city will be able to remain standing without the support of Voi or the Southern Army. If the Trahazi or others resettle in the region, it will surely stand, but with all of the Voi noble families having sought refuge in the other kingdoms, there is much uncertainty for Whitehall's future.

Topic: Follow the Money: interest rates from banks
column5: Loans in the world of Adelrune are highly unregulated and anyone with money can lend it to anyone else on their own terms, based on whatever agreed repayment both parties come to. The more reputable sources for major loans are the various banks - like the Bank of Paloma, or Bank of Benlowe - or the various Merchant Guilds, where loans are more often taken out as part of a trade agreement, or by crafters looking to start out somewhere new, at which point they're typically paying back the Guild directly with work rather than paying back in coin, or the Guild itself owns their workshop and home.

Topic: Investigate History: who were the first knights of the black chalice? are they still alive? (other than Valdir/Vojin)
column5: Valdir was not a Founding Knight of the Black Chalice, he and Celia joined the order during the Crusades. Beyond Ser Vojin, the following knights were founding members: Sir Thorin Angistine, Sir Cronus Decimus, Sir Thayer Atius, Sir Dominick Jay, and Sir Theodore Terminatus. As for their current status, that is not something covered by research spends, but by influence.

Topic: Political Insight: how Vein differs from the other former Amalg nations
column5: Following the War of Giants (165ch-175ch) in which Pious Cassius Hospitalus, Lord Inquisitor of the Amalgamation is defeated by Vein Ludgar in a single combat duel to the death, the demoralized Kingdom of Vercrux sue for peace. The Vercrux promise their son in an arranged marriage to wed Vein Ludgar's daughter when both children come of age. This ensures a lasting peace and the joining of their nations. While new to the Amalgamation table, the Vein were truly a bolstering force for the kingdom. They are not a Betrayer nation, and have kept strong ties with the Old Ways and Gael, and while some Veinish converted to the Church of Chorus and became extremely fanatical Inquisitors, there is no national religion within the Kingdom in the aftermath of the collapse of the Church of Chorus. The Vein are the pioneers of mining and there is great wealth to be found in their mountains, which was used throughout their history as they were the forefront leaders in the development of war, weapons, castle developement, and defensive and siege tactics, which has only strengthened the former Kingdom of Vercrux. The Vein are renowned for keeping their oaths, moreso than even knights, but among their politicians, merchants, and every day folk as well, and this habit and belief has spread strongly throughout the combined kingdom over the last two generations. In more recent history, a rift was caused between the Vicaul and the Vein, and as such, the Veinish King Rolf shared their fire barge technology with the Cestral, which assists in keeping the Vicaul raids to their shorelines minimal and in expected locations where they can prepare defenses.

Topic: Distract the Mark + Healthy Paranoia: following investigate rumors
Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: local rumors about powerful blood mages being potted in the Nadine Empire
column5: There are no more blood mages than usual in or around Siegeholt, but blood magic is not uncommon within the Empire, especially when connected the Blood Cauldron who specializes in blood rites.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: rumors about the current location of Whitehall blood knife
column5: Rumor has it, the blood knife was sent immediately from Port Frey to Siegeholt in the Nadine Empire, and has not left since. It is unclear who within the city has the knife - possibilities include the Necromancer's Tower, the mages of the Blood Cauldron, the Empress' personal mage researchers, or even Lord Wraith Harlock, the Head of the Arcane.

Topic: Investigate History: Hawk
column5: Hawk is the Messenger of the Sun, his most frequently celebrated "holidays" are on the equinoxes and solstices. Particularly in Cestral, where local druids and archons will gather on Hawk's Island, the eastern most known land on Adelrune, to see the first sunrise of the season. The practices and traditions held there are secretive and not shared beyond the island itself. It is often debated if the bird on the Celestine flag is a hawk or other bird of prey but old druids of the Gael claim that it is Hawk and the Celestines, even as Betrayers, kept his image for his connections to the sun, which also carries connotations and connections to the Golden Realms, and celestial beings.

Topic: Investigate History: Thte
column5: Thte, "the Warrior," is often referred to as the first man. Raised to godhood by receiving the gifts of each of the Old Ways gods except Lion (who it is said has never given his gift to mankind), Thte was blessed with these gifts to fight back against Balaeiesh. With Wolf at his side, there are tales all throughout history of Thte fighting against Balaiesh, pushing back against the destruction to protect humanity. These tales of heroism on behalf of humanity, fighting against the evils of the world and powerhungry gods are plentiful. An old tale, only murmured from the mouths of the druids of long ago (in this case, Caeli Oakenthorn herself), tells the tale of Thte going up against the World Eater to rip him from an ancient tower. In more recent years, he was devestatingly wounded and imprisoned by Deroan, fed upon for years, before the faithful of the weakened Old Ways practices rallied to his side and led by Wolf. The freeing of Thte, and subsequent imprionment of Deroan, caused the newly awakened Warrior to call his faithful to war, and War did rage in the name of Thte in 4AR in the Northern War as the Gael united together to stand against the Celestine Empire and Ad Decimum, and the Landing in which the Vicaul led an invasion of Cestral. The Vein were already at war with the other Kingdoms of the Amalgamation in the War of Wine, the Coatl rallied against the kingdom of De'Fey, and even the Drir (centuries removed from the days their ancestors once showed some level of deference to the Old Ways) grew restless when they heard the call and engaged Mandala in the Bloodmoon War while Mandala's allies were otherwise occupied with wars of their own.

Topic: Investigate History: Mentor
column5: Mentor is commonly called "The Dragon", "The Father of Dragons" and "The Purifier" by those who follow him. The Celestine god of War, he's the child of Nero and Eur, father of Avirtia, and is most commonly depicted as a knight in white armor, or a white dragon. There is a common belief that Mentor is a name he was forced to adapt and take on, as his original name was stolen from him by another god, who took his name and his earliest followers.

Topic: Investigate History: Aisthes
column5: Called "She that is pleasure", Aisthes is commonly believed to be part of the Eirie Faith, and a grandchild of the Greater Celestine pantheon. She's said to be the child of Aer and Eliena, sister to the dead gods Brynioch and Onorie - but she has always been the weakest of the family, and sometimes has been considered to be more of a patron than a true god, especially among her most devoted followers "The Ladies of Aisthes" where it is rumored she may have lived amongst.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: info on any assignments Mio was involved in at the time of his death, esp if involved with broken brick
column5: - Rumor has it, - Rumor has it, - Rumor has it, - Rumor has it, - Rumor has it,

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: connections between the "Lady of Darkness" and her cauldron of blood, and the Shrike
column5: There is no way to confirm or refute rumors of gods seen in visions and the metaphysics of a faith. The best you can get is a confirmation that the faith of the Blood Cauldron does indeed worship the Shrike as a goddess.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: any info re: death of CT's previous Little Bird
column5: Rumor has it the last Little Bird died during the War of Radiance. Rumor has it the last Little Bird was a monster hidden away in a tower who could read minds and was more of a tool of war, trapped and controlled by Castle Thorn for their far-reaching abilities to get into the minds of their enemies. Rumor has it the last Little Bird was killed from within, being thrown from the tower and swallowed by the remnants of the Attack Ivy at the base of the cliffs. Rumor has it the last Little Bird was addicted to demon's blood and was working against the Castle and setting the soldiers up for failure in the Breach and abroad by feeding them bad intel. Rumor has it the last Little Bird was shockingly incompetent and was demoted quickly.

Topic: Underworld Insight: investigate loan shark activity
column5: Loans in the world of Adelrune are highly unregulated and anyone with money can lend it to anyone else on their own terms, based on whatever agreed repayment both parties come to. As such, it's a dangerous game to take a loan from a less reputable source like gangs, who are known for extremely high interest rates, unreasonable repayment expectation, exploitation, and blackmail. To some extent, it's expected that every gang with any amount of money is likely to put out at least some loans in order to benefit from them, but it takes a particularly shrewed mind to build a wide profit magrin on them without taking major losses.

Topic: what makes a celestial fallen? are there any physical symptoms to a celestial falling? what happens to a celestial's soul when they fall?
column5: A celestial falls when they have lost all hope in humanity. While there are no real physical traits to falling, behaviorally the celestial will take their virtue to an extreme. So a celestial of warmth would build a volcano, a celestial of purity will create a huge lake to cleanse the earth, a celestial of mercy will kill everything to put it out of its misery, etc. A fallen celestial or one suffering from the early stages of falling, can be saved by their mortal being destroyed so their soul can return to the Golden Realm for renewal of purpose and given a fresh start. Since celestials are immortal beings, this ultimately doesn't cause them lasting harm as long as they are returned to the Golden Realm rather than being imprisoned within a soul gem. A final note, when a celestial is fallen, other celestials will hunt them to send them back to be reborn.

Topic: Due to our astari marks, do we feel any specific attunement to the artifacts (Ancient Astari Bracelet and Ancient Astari Star Gem)
column5: No, no particular attunement is felt or experienced.

Topic: dp we feel any impacts on specific magics, divination or otherwise?
column5: Marks like Astari marks and god marks are typically less *active*, so they wouldn't affect magics or other skills, but serve has a sort of pre-requisite for using or accessing magical or divine items connected to them. This can be anything from a blessed sword that does more damage for one bearing the correct mark, to a magical door that only one with the right mark can pass through or open.

Topic: are there any records of similar artifacts or info in the Effendal luibrary that could tell us what purpose these artifacts had for the the Astari?
column5: The ancient Astari bracelet is just an archeological relic of the past. It is non-magical and it's only real importance is that it was from the Astari. The Star Gem is a unique component that would be part of a bigger piece, likely jewelry, rather than a metaphysical artifact itself.

Topic: how are those marked by the moon different than those marked by the stars?
column5: The general distinction between the two has more to do with the character's outlook on life and worldview based on which bodies in the sky they are drawn to, while in practice they are merely two different marks that provide access to different things.

Topic: how can we spiritually connect o the astari given our marks and artifacts?
column5: To connect deeper to the Astari, it's important to learn more about them and what they have left behind - perhaps finding another dig site related to the Astari, learning the forgotten history, or finding Astari artifacts to attune to. It's likely that some of this information would be found in the deepest, most protected libraries and vaults of the Effendal houses. It is also suspected the Obelisks might hold some sort of secrets to the Astari as well.

Topic: what is the possible route the book of Kus took out of Haen during the War of Radiance?
column5: There is no true evidence whether or not Kus left the city, or if it was in the hands of friend or foe in the days after the attack on the city of Haen during the Age of Chorus. If it was taken from the city, it would have immediately disappeared somewhere into Southern Breach and there is no guessing which route it might have taken without knowing who took it.

Topic: stories & legends of panther
column5: One of the more popular stories about Panther becomes from the era in which the Celestine pantheon was emerging and the first Betrayers were turning against the old gods in favor of the new. Among the first to emerge from this era was Haelo - the first child - who came out trying to prove himself against the Old Gods. One day, while out leading a hunting trip against the old gods with his brother and some of the other early Celestine gods, Panther stalked them in return, keeping to the shadows - unassuming and unseen due to her size and coloring - and when the opportunity struck that they were least prepared to defend themselves, she attacked from the shadows. This alerted Bear to the situation as she was the one being hunted, and she too joined in the attack. Together Bear and Panther caused Haelo a grievous wound to his face that never truly healed. This is why Panther is called the Hidden

Topic: the history of the iron chalice and last known location
column5: The Iron Chalice was founded by many members of Leucraven's Brotherhood, though not all became Liege Knights of the Iron Chalice, such as Inigma. Later soldiers in Antioc joined the Iron Chalice as well, including Lord Faramond Telesca and his son Leon Telesca, and the ultimate fate of the Iron Chalice was to be destroyed in Castle Thorn during the Purification. The last known location of the *actual* Iron Chalice cup was in Port Frey for the War of Radiance.

Topic: in reference to the summoning of twilight in the book of ig: who or what is sumrah? what is the obelisk of eternity? has there ever been an example of a fae's aspect being transferred to another being?
column5: The Sumrah were an ancient tribal civilization that practiced Rituals. They were so adept at them, they commented that normal Summoning was what their children would do. Before the advent of books and paper, the ancient Sumrah would tattoo their "Pages" with all the information needed for a Ritual. In regards to a fae's aspect being transferred to another being, yes it has been done before, though not noted as part of the Summoning of Twilight - it was done recently, in the history of the Returned, as noted vaguely in the red book about fae, written by Caeli Oakenthorn in the Guild of Academics, when talking about the Returned Vicaul-Yeong Boneheart, the fae parading as a demon-Inexo, and the ultimate fate of both.

Topic: how is baleaish revered/worshipped? what are common rites/practices associatedwith this god? who/what are recent events associated with this god?
column5: Baleaish is not worshipped, he is revered out of fear. He is appeased at best and at worst, Old Ways followers perform rituals to banish him entirely from Adelrune. It rarely holds him long, but it does give them some respite for a short period. Most practices to appease him or banish him involve human sacrifice to appease him, or supernatural sacrifice to banish him (See: The Great Banishing in the Book of Ig). Since many of the stories and practices of Old Ways center around Thte and the other gods banding together against Balaiesh and his corrupting ways, Old Ways followers who devote themselves to Baleaish are often distrusted and considered heretical. This is especially noticeable with the Esturmann clan of the Vicaul who follow the Mist Goddess and use her connection with Baleaish to bring harm to other Vicaul clans.

Topic: why is kselix called lord of slime? what are they doing in the breach?
column5: Kcelizex is called the Lord of Slime because he's a giant, slimey scaled wyrm. Throughout the Breach, the underground tunnels are coated in his particular distinctive slime coating, which is used by the locals as a unique alchemical component, but gathering it is especially dangerous. In addition to the locals harvesting it, the slime is sought after and eaten by Breach grubs and sand worms, helping them grow to massives sizes and causing them other unique mutations (such as an extremely durable carapace that can mean a grub cannot be harmed externally and it must be attacked from within).

Topic: white stag - history? rituals? practices? holidays? ties to other gods?
column5: Stag is known as the Bringer of Plenty and is worshipped in all ways that follow that purview, specifically for a good harvest he is recognized and honored as well as any other situation that provides - this includes a fruitful hunt, and a successful raiding season for the Vicaul. The Druiduc order in Cestral provide blood sacrifice for Stag during the planting season in the spring, and their fall jam festival often honors Stag for what they have left after the summer Vicaul raids. For the Vein, Vicaul and Gael it is much more common to have animal sacrifices burned in Stag's honor. Early in the hunting season or more drastically when food is running thin in the winter there are numerous traditions where a portion of the food that is left is offered to Stag in hopes that what is left is enough to last the winter (and that by sharing a portion with Stag that he will stretch what remains). It is believed that those who did not offer enough during their hunts or farming earlier in the year will not have their pleas heard and answered by Stag. Occassionally in the most desparate of times, these sacrifices have also included human sacrifice, when a whole village is at risk of starvation. The major holiday for Stag is often the fall harvest and the start and ending of hunting/raiding seasons.

Topic: kesa - history of the goddess of order and how to properly worship her
column5: Like Haelo, Kesa has never been worshipped by the Celestine Church. She is the sister of Riel, and the child of Cinnorast and Eur. Like Riel, which the Hazori named their capital city after, she is likely tied to the Hazori faith before it evolved into the Trahazi Zodiac after the Purification.

Topic: what is the origin of a god?
column5: Gods have many different origins - some were born of other gods, some were created with an infusion of divine power (such as from the Clocktower), and others always were, like Chaos and Destiny. In some instances it's difficult to tell the difference between a supremely powerful entity like a full fae, or a greater demon or celestial, or dragon and a true god, but while such entities of sufficient power can *contain* and even use divine power to some lesser extent, there is something metaphysically unique, indifinable and intangible that differentiates a GOD from these beings of immense power. While it is theorized this is something to do with a god's so-called "domain", there is no true evidence one way or another, as even powerful entities that are not considered true gods are credited as having control of various "domains".

Topic: State of the Court: Hastings
column5: - War - the Nadine are testing the newly established Eastern Expanse Alliance, to see how Hastings and Vein responds to their continuous threat against Whitehall. Hastings in particular is uncertain in their response - mustering the army and moving them to defend Whitehall takes time and is expensive for Hastings (and even moreso for Vein), while it's relatively cheap and local for the Nadine Empire. The Hastings court is heavily debating if this is just an act of attrition, slowly wearing down their ability to respond by keeping them constantly on their toes, or if it's a true threat, and what will happen to the Alliance and Whitehall itself if they respond incorrectly. - Stability - there is much concern about the lack of a line of succession should something actually happen to Arielle, and her refusal to cede power in any form to a husband keeps the uncertainty a hot topic in court. - Gauntlet of Haelo - following the Battle of the Clocktower, the court of Hastings is extremely impressed with the Returned for upholding their promise in returning the relic home to the Kingdom of Hastings, leaving the nation with a generally positive feeling toward the Returned at the moment.

Topic: Political Insight: Hastings
column5: - While the most logical marriage for Arielle is a marriage alliance with Rolf or Darius, she regularly butts heads with both of them and ultimately will not marry either because she refuses to cede power to a husband, which would be expected should she marry a ruler of a nation - the ruling body of the Kingdom of Hastings is anti-religion following the execution of the royal family by the Church of Chorus, Arielle's rebellion in response, and severe punishment on all of the upper noble families as well. Among Hastings nobles, they are not just anti-Church of Chorus, but anti-religion as a whole. The peasantry have little feeling on the matter one way or another.

Topic: State of the Court: Unity (citizens)
column5: The peasantry in Port Frey are currently is quite divided. There are many who are glad the Returned are being held accountable for their actions, but they are still hurt and struggling from the attack by the Bloddy Egde and the anarachists that have been causing property damage and vandalism in recent times. There is a lot of positive feedback about the building of the walls and belltowers, both providing defenses to the non-returned districts and alerting the citizenry of incoming dangers, but there is still grumbling about if enough is being done to punish the dream mages that continues to plague the town with the fallout of the consequences of their actions. It seems like for every step forward in rebuilding relations with the Returned, there are two steps taken back. Additionally, much of the local talk is about the *reactions* of the laws to the actions of the Returned, but cannot seem to preempt actions taken that have dire consequences for the populace.

Topic: State of the Court: Citadel (Kaelin)
column5: The Citadel is on metaphorical (and in some instances literal) fire following the release of all the entrapped creatures on Tear. The Citadel is overrun by demons, celestials, and various elemental forces. While many of the celestials and demons are fighting each other using the Citadel as ground zero, many of the others who are more angry (specifically the fallen Celestials who were trapped in soul gems) are actively hunting members of House Kaelin who either entrapped them or used their powers as summoners. Many of the Kaelin elderly and children have fled to the north seeking safety with the Myros, or the Rakuten and Svivore.

Topic: Investigate Prominant Groups: Port Frey
column5: influence follow-up: Investigate Prominant Groups: Port Frey  - The Hand, nobody knows who they are but you got to hand it to them, they got their fingers in all the pies.  - With the collapse of the Jackals' Children's Tears empire, the Salamanders have been finding their way into all sorts of trouble and business. It seems between the Salamanders and the Skull Children, the children of Port Frey are enterprising and willing to get their hands dirty.  - The International Merchant's Guild, which is responsible for the administration of the port itself (and paying taxes to Unity), has had issues with the Returned that heavily damaged relations locally, but thanks to the help of the Harbormaster, the government was able to limit the embargo to just the Returned so the Port operations continue as normal. There is reason to believe that the former Harbor Master Caliban and the current town Treasurer Orsino Paloma are making strong headway in repairing these relations.  - Another prominent group is Unity! Explanation? Enough said.  - The Concerned Citizens Brigade which is a coalition of citizens who are unhappy with everything, but aren't necessarily looking to fix it. They're just angry. - - Citizens for a Better Port Frey - is a loosely organized group of peasantry who came to Port Frey as refugees from the wars on the Expanse. They have banded together and are doing the best they can to improve their parts of town and their lots in life. This group is responsible for the Yule Market that happens annually and a number of small festivals throughout the year to bring together the citizens in a positive way.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Necromancer's Tower (are they frustrated with the Blood Mages using their space)
column5: No

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Wraith Harlock using which school of magic? Is he using blood? necro? other?
column5: Wraith Harlock is a ritual necromancer

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Blood Cauldron if they are frustrated that THEY are not doing the blood magic for the dagger
column5: No, because they have the dagger

Topic: Military Insight: Whitehall
column5: Whitehall is being used as a rallying point for anyone from the region who are willing to fight - peasants are being trained and any surviving soldiers from the Southern Army and Voi army have mostly moved to Whitehall. Currently Whitehall's forces are holding strong at 19,000 soldiers, 550 spies, 110 assassins, 4000 guards, and 200 White Chalice knights

Topic: Political Insight: Drir Unity Seat
column5: After the last Unity Representative was eaten by the Drir Returned, there was a hotly contested conflict which of the tribes would serve as the new Unity Seat. The Amimaerun sent a replacement, which the Mortichyte on the island quickly beheaded and tossed his head into Port Frey for the Returned to deal with. The Mortichyte put forth one of their own who is inexperienced and young, and the Peleset put forth a Mask containing the spirit of an ancient Drir who is currently in the Abyss. Through much debate and competition, involving the hunting and eating of hearts, the Mask came out victorious. The Head was determined his proxy assistant and then the living Mortichyte as tertiary should the other two be unavailable. As such, it is difficult to describe the political leanings of the Mask. However, the Peleset are known for being the slightly-more peaceful and diplomatic of the Drir tribes and will often act as the peacekeepers between the Amimaerun and the Mortichyte, so one can only hope that continues. As of recent Unity meetings, the Mask seems to be fairly neutral and uninterested in international politics if they are not related to the Drir. It is, however, aligned (as most Drir are) with the fallen celestial Teppidus, known to be the defender of the Drir.

Topic: Political Insight: Mandalan Unity Seat
column5: While High Priestess Miriam is still around Port Frey, she has been difficult to pin down in the post- Mist era of Port Frey and so Mandala has not had a stable Unity seat for some time. It is often filled in for with various lower ranking politicians and handled more by group consensus than a single one making any decisions. In general, without an established connection to their homeland, the Mandalan representatives seem to be a little aimless unless there is something clearly specific they should be involved in or have an opinion on like the Clocktower or the golden egg that recently came through town. As long as their actions seem to align in service to their country or the dragons, they will likely go along with it. All of the recent situations with extra fae being brought into the world and given the opportunity to make big actions has the Mandalans on edge, and rumor has it that such things have woken even the ancient primogenitor dragons.

Topic: Political Inisght: Darius
column5: At the moment, Darius Lelaroy's entire political focus is on the strengthening and survivability of Whitehall. He orchestrated the Eastern Expanse Alliance because it benefits Whitehall significantly more than the other kingdoms of the former Amalgamation. The invitation for the repatriation of the Trahazi and Effendal to the region also serve the same purpose

Topic: Investigate History: Concerti
column5: Founded in 65t and captured by the Nadine Empire in 68t, in the "Battle of the Coasts", Concerti is incredibly important to the economy and trade industry of the Nadine Empire by being a major crossroads for travel and merchants. It's known dominantly for it's plethora of gravemills where the undead and bodies that are no longer useful to the Empire are ground up to be used as fertilizer, which is also one of their major exports, especially with nearby Cestral. As a center of trade, it had a particularly nice downtown shopping district with rare clothes, and unqiue high end items imported from around the world.

Topic: Investigate History: Ash Storm
column5: Etyr, the Ash Storm, was dominantly active in an era of pre-history before books and records were kept, so most knowledge of him is from old stories that weren't written down until generations later. As such, it's unclear when he actually went to sleep as he was presumed dead in the tales. What is known is that he was native to the Nadine region, a territory he was known to have shared with his mate, the "Fire Bird", and was the unequivicoble leader of the red dragons on Adelrune. For decades he raged against the Nadine Empire as they encroached on his region, and there are stories of great forest fires and the destruction he wraught against the territory, which earned him the title of "the Ash Storm". Imperial dragon hunters used dragon bone weapons which were particularly effective against dragons, and so the common belief (until his recent reemergence) had been that he had met his demise one day when he had been cornered by Imperial dragon hunters and his mate joined the fray, which ultimately ended in a blinding supernova of flame and destruction leaving nothing but ash and debris in it's wake. The fate of his presumed-dead mate is currently unknown, given his own reappearance.

Topic: Beastiary Analysis: Demonhawks
column5: NOTE: REFUND SEE LORE DOCUMENT

Topic: Investigate Fame: Ash Storm
column5: He burned it all down. All of it. Yes, even that forest. And that one. And that one again.

Topic: Investigate Rivals: Ash Storm
column5: It is well-known that Etyr hates blue dragons. Particularly he has an old, deep set rivalry in Noren, "The Wizard", a blue dragon known to have had territory in what is now the Vicaul lands. Once before after a great battle, Etyr finally won over the blue dragon and while his hatred and focused anger have abated, there is still a deep rivalry between the pair.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Ash Storm
column5: - Rumor has it when provoked, Etyr never calms down or lets it go - Rumor has it, Etyr hates stained glass - Rumor has it, Etyr prefers skirmish tactics rather than direct conflict against the Nadine forces - Rumor has it, old wounds that are still healing can be seen on the dragon even at a distance - Rumor has it, that those who are strong enough to survive a blast by his fire breath go into a frenzy afterwards, taking out the few who survived.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Concerti
column5: - Rumor has it, trolls have moved in. - Rumor has it, Dourhawk Manor is still considered one of the most valuable properties in the Nadine Empire. - Rumor has it, the former property owners in Concerti are gathering assassins on retainer. - Rumor has it the mists are thinning over the Nadine Empire. - Rumor has it, the Ninth legion is losing, and they are down to a fraction of a single division left in the fight.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Darius Lelaroy
column5: - Rumor has it, Lord Darius is fine. - Rumor has it, Lord Darius has never been better. - Rumor has it, Lord Darius is in perfect health. - Rumor has it, Lord Darius has just returned from a relaxing vacation on the coast with a golden tan.
