column5: CAMERON

Topic: Contact Scholars: Lore Ravens
column5: REFUND

Topic: Contact Scholars: Wyrms
column5: PICNIC

Topic: Military Insight: celestial forces at the clocktower
column5: the Clocktower fight had a mix of Celestial factions present, while there were Harbingers of the Crescent Sun, there were also a significant number of members of the Choir of the Effervescent Song with their utility units who specialize in communication, speed, and abundance.

Topic: Contact Scholars: knights of the blood chalice
column5: DAN

Topic: Investigate Rumors: the mask of prophecy - location? whole? who has it?
column5: Rumor has it House Paloma has the mask. Rumor has it House Montaige has the mask. Rumor has it no seriously, House Paloma has the mask. Rumor has it, no I swear for real House MONTAIGE has the mask. Rumor has it the destruction of the mask caused a divine curse upon House Paloma that is coming to pass.

Topic: Military Insight: the Gael Forces on Tear
column5: There are three known tribes of Gael on Tear, who historically do not get along with each other but came together under the Banner of the Black Wolf during the War of Radiance. Since the end of the War, the Banners have made arrangements to garrison in Effendal lands on Tear and have strong alliances with the Citadel Effendal and Radolond, but keep to the southern part of the island and out of Svivore and Rakuten territories. The Banner of the Black Wolf under the leadership of Klev Vol'ghos has 9,200 Gael Warriors, the Banner of the One-Winged Owl under the leadership of Erontu has 9000 Gael Warriors (though the first of these have departed already for the ruined Gael lands), and the Banner of the Blood Crow has 11,100 Elite Warriors, and 5000 Blood Mages under the guidance of Scadox.

Topic: Investigate History: Golden Tomes
column5: The Golden Tomes were an artefact of the Lore Masters' Guild once held in the posession of every chapter house across Adelrune. With the bitter rivalry and opposing viewpoints of the Storytellers, many of these chapter houses were razed and its Golden Tome destroyed over the century until only one Golden Tome remains in Alexander, the last known public library of the Lore Masters' Guild.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: what has become of the Bloody Edge without Leucraven? are they looking for work?
column5: - Rumor has it, the Bloody Edge is disbanding. - Rumor has it the Bloody Edge are gearing up for another attack on Port Frey when the Returned are least expecting it, so they can recover Leucraven's body. - Rumor has it the former Bloody Edge members are reforming their old mercenary companies. - Rumor has it, the former Bloody Edge members just want to go back to Edge and their homes, and take a much-deserved vacation. - Rumor has it, they have no interest in continuing this fight.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: murder of Trina Anhavia
column5: Rumor has it Trina Anhavia was a shapeshifter demon working for T'tocs and when she was discovered she was killed for it. Rumor has it Trina Anhavia discovered something she shouldn't have and was never heard from again. Rumor has it the last place Trina Anhavia was seen was talking to a group of dark-clad people near the walls during the siege of T'tocs. Rumor has it Trina Anhavia got to close to the shapeshifter spy within Unity and was captured, tortured for information, and then disposed of. Rumor has it Trina Anhavia was sacrificed to a dark god.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: who is the (new) voice, and how did they become this?
column5: - Rumor has it, the Voice of Chorus has brought godliness to a godless land. - Rumor has it, this person claiming to be the new Voice has the title because they have a following who believe and there is no one who can dispute it. - Rumor has it, the new Voice is from a peasant from the Kingdom of De'Fey who fled when the Coatl attacked. - Rumor has it, the Church needed hope, and in their darkest moment, the light of Chorus returned to the people in the form of this new Voice, who has rallied the people together and defended them with miracles. - Rumor has it, the new Voice has been authenticated because they have performed the miracles of Chorus, and the Inquisition has confirmed that they are not a demon, celestial, or fae.

Topic: Whitehall bonus info beyond Domni's (military)
column5: In addition to a solid military force (15000+ soldiers), Whitehall also has an extremely strong network of spies collecting information and spreading misinformation. Particularly they have 550 spies and 110 assassins

Topic: Where is the native habitat of the Golden Moth and how can we safely acquire them?
column5: An Excerpt from a research paper entitled: Golden Eggs: Population Ecology of the Endangered Golden Moth.The Golden Moth is considered to be nearly extinct on Adelrune due to a drastic loss of habitat and overharvesting of their golden eggs, which are both a valuable alchemical component and a delicacy that was highly favored by King Arresto Dacean during his reign. The only known population of Golden Moths that survives in the current Age is at the Moonmoss-Vercrux Academy of Alchemy, where the eggs are carefully evaluated to keep the population at a healthy level of biodiversity while providing the necessary alchemical reagents for further study of their unique properties.

Topic: What creatures make yellow pearls & where can we find them?
column5: Yellow pearls are the among the rarest pearls on Adelrune, due to the fact that no one has yet identified their true geographical source. Produced by the Gold-Lipped Oyster, yellow pearls are approximately 1 inch in diameter and are considered a nearly priceless treasure. For the rare merchant or trader who comes into possession of one, they are often extremely tight-lipped about their source of such an item.

Topic: Is it possible to repair Ficher's Basin? If it is repaired, will Filcher be remade?
column5: The basin in question is one that is used by fae to forget memories, this one in particular belonged to Filcher, but others exist in the world. It does not have the ability to remake a fae as that power does not truly exist in the world, but if you are able to repair it and design a sufficient ritual, some of the memories may potentially be recoverable/viewable.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: Ferdinand Cynwerd being followed/assassinated
column5: While there was someone who thought Ferdinand was once being followed suspiciously and alerted him to the potential threat, any rumor of him continuing to be followed or potential assassinations seems to be conjecture and paranoia circulating within the Church of Chorus followers.

Topic: Investigate History: Lisra
column5: Lisra, the Celestine goddess of music, is also called "the tornado" and is often connected to wind patterns, especially the whistling through the trees or the howl of the wind whipping through narrow city streets. She is the child of Pheonix and Tempest. The northernmost city of the Celestine Empire is named after Lisra, and is located within the hotly disputed lands that were declared a military-free zone between the Celestine and Gael at the end of the Northern War. It remains to be seen if peace will hold in the region. During the War of Radiance with the necessity of song being used at the Battle of Port Frey to combat Bel'e'athru's control over the Celestial Star, it was fortuitous and considered a good omen that it was the Legion of Lisra who arrived in Port Frey on behalf of the Celestine Empire.

Topic: INVESTIGATE HISTORY: Celestine incursion on Tear
column5: By order of Captain Masefield, on this the first day of the March moon, the Second Year of Adelrune, on behalf of General Tolon, under her Majesty, Queen Elainne Ceien Celestine’s rule, the 16th Royal Scout Detachment is hereby ordered to withdraw along a northern route and make camp just north of Two Rivers. The 16th will use this position as a staging camp, and will continue to send delegations to the Effendal house of Kaelin for deliberation. Until negotiations have concluded, and an agreement reached upon. Celestine forces are hereby ordered to cease all aggressive behavior and report to their superiors for further orders. The newly discovered populace now known as ‘the Returned’ is given full liberties and may leave at their discretion. Officers are also authorized to equip ‘the Returned’ with whatever food and provisions they deem necessary to enable said people to survive the proposed journey southward. Blankets, tents, and other necessities may also be provided at the discretion of a ranking officer within the boundaries of reason and temperance. In her majesty’s name, ~Herald Flavious Justinson, Royal Herald, 16th Scout Detachment

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Kiesh Hahn
column5: - Rumor has it, Kiesh often can be found wandering through town. He has been mingling with people from all walks of life and across nations- more openly these past few gatherings.  - Rumor has it, Kiesh is openly not a very devote member of the Church of Chorus yet is often seen traveling to and from the Church, even when nobody else is around. During the services he does attend he has been seen fast asleep. - Rumor has it Kiesh is working to take down the Church of Chorus and is secretly leading an effort to hunt down and execute Inquisitors and any remaining Church leadership on behalf of Princess Arielle Hastings - Rumor has it, Kiesh is a known sorcerer but also is known to associate with members of the Black Council. - Kiesh often carries a sword but has been seen blocking a sword with his bare hand. - Rumor has it, Kiesh was an integral part of the success of the Hastings Rebllion due to his connections to various nefarious organizations within the former Amalgamation - Rumor has it, Kiesh is one of the last remaining descendents of The Way and has restarted the notorious Thieves' Guild - Kiesh seems to revel in the ruination of alcohol and may of his fellow returned avoid him if they are particularly deep in the bottle.

Topic: State of the Court: Public Works Projects (Port Frey)
column5: Due to the sudden and unexpected disappearance of Iron, Port Frey and it's general infrastructure has taken excessive damages over the summer. Many structures were significantly weakened because of sudden switch to silver nails. As such, the office of the Unity Quartermaster has put out a call for smiths to make manganese nails to assist in repairs. They are in need of fifty units of manganese to be shaped into nails by smiths of any kind (blacksmith, armorsmith, weaponsmith, locksmith), and as many engineers as possible to assist in the repairs around town.  (Note: if the manganese tags are collected, a mod is planned for as many engineers as can be gathered to occur Saturday night at approximately 1am (meet Crystal at the staff trailer in NPC camp)

Topic: Political Insight: Golden Wolf
column5: Arbane Talthon, known as The Golden Wolf has made himself known amongst the effendal Returned the past few years. He has been a loud voice for Effendal relations with the Returned. His recent campaign to collect the Lost Sparrows has been considered a strong success amongst the Effendal Council and many are curious to see if he can continue to add to the ranks of the Citadel by reconnecting the Returned with their lost culture. He is highly placed in the Kaelin hierarchy and has been trusted in both political and military matters. His most recent exploit leading the joint Effendal and Returned attack on T’tocs forces was extremely successful and daring. Not only was it a political success coordinating Delfestrae, Kaelin and Eltilvor forces but a diplomatic one as well, convincing the Council to allow the Returned to use secret magical means to reach the battlefield undetected. The military success of the mission itself is of course self evident. Overall, Talthon is considered an honorable Ritari and a prime example of his people

Topic: Political Inisght: Sir Seriyu
column5: Sir Seriyu is a veteran of the War of Radiance and the former bodyguard for the Mandalan Unity Representative (High Priestess Myriam de Ariete) and has filled in for her politically on a number of occassions. He's a man of few words, preferring to listen and observe, and takes his job extremely seriously. He leads by example, and above all desires peace. Fiercely loyal to Mandala, he is generally indifferent towards the Effendal, but has a strong disdain for the Drir, which also bleeds over into his feelings towards all of the wild nations and would prefer that none of them have Seats on Unity. Curiously, he does not wear emblems of his knightly order or bring attention to it unnecessarily, merely acknowledging his knighthood title when introducing himself. In his offtime, he prefers the solitude of fishing to most other pursuits

Topic: Political Insight: Ser Wenrys Bizenwerth
column5: While Wenrys isn't vying for the War Seat themself, as they were recommended by Dame Nightshade without their knowledge, ability-wise Wenrys is even-headed and resourceful, and understands the need to use all skills at ones' disposal. They certainly fall under the axiom of "those who do not seek power are most qualified to hold it." A lot of their work within Ad Decimum was top secret research, though in recent years they've spent a lot of time being a field researcher, and they've earned a lot of respect from many nations and Orders through the assistance they've provided to Unity in Port Frey, as they previously they were never overly visible in what they did. As a Crown Knight, and through their previous top secret work, they are closely tied to and aligned with Belisarius and his research into divinity. While not directly involved in the experimentation on the Gael during the Northern War, his known stance was "hopefully something good will come of it".

Topic: Political Insight: Ser Verrus Barkus
column5: Politically speaking, Varrus Barkus came to Tear as part of the contingent group from the Kingdom of Vercrux before the Returned changed history, but he has no knowledge of that difference in the world, so he has always believed himself to be of the Kingdom of Vein. His loyalty is first and foremos to Kingdom over Church, and generally only attended Church of Chorus serves as he was required to under the theocratic rule of the Amalgamation. As such, his first focus is always Veinish people. Barkus is extremely lawful, and while serving in Solace lands, he believes in justice and the protection of society by Unity Law (often he is asked to represent Unity for trials and execution), and since the founding of Unity, he has always filled the Amalgamation's Unity Parliament Seat as their Representative since Darius was abroad and unable to. He is well-trained in the art of war, serving as the standing commander of Veinish troops on Tear, he went to Breach in the Second Crusade, and stood as King Rolf Vein-Vercrux's right hand man during the Battle of Port Frey in the War of Radiance. As a soldier back home, he was part of the elite forces, fighting the Vicaul specifically (though he doesn't actively dislike them, he was following orders), and he's very comfortable in a fight. During the War of the Wine, he struggled with the regicide/patricide committed against of King Galen Vercrux, but remained neutral in the fallout as he needed to see that Rolf was focused on keeping the Vein strong. As the Kingdom of Vein holds a unique crossover position in the world (in both timelines) he is supportive of the Wild Nations, but still admires the moral justice of Whitehall in doing "the right thing" by inviting the Effendal and Trahazi back to their former lands (though he thinks it's quite risky). Verrus feels at home in the woods and enjoys hunting.

Topic: Political Insight: Erontu
column5: Erontu was not interested in filling the position of Unity Seat for the Gael, but only took up the position because as the leader of one of the three Gael tribes on Tear he refused to let the Banner of the Black Wolf continue to dominate peace negotiations and treaties on behalf of his tribe following the War of Radiance. As such, he hates his job and leaves most of the day-to-day work on Caeli Oakenthorn's desk. He's extremely flattering in his speech, including the insults he throws, which regularly throws people off who don't expect such words from a Gael. In the majority of his Unity voting, his focus is first and foremost the Gael, and then with the Effendal, who he considers his allies. Beyond that, if Caeli doesn't persuade him on a particular path he will always vote with the majority, regardless of what measure is being discussed. Outside of his duties, his favored hobbies include watching feats of strength and the grafting of trees. Though his tribe and the other Gael on Tear are settled comfortably in Effendal lands, he desires to return his people to the Gael forest. He seems to desire peace and has held a tenuous peace with the other Gael tribes on Tear, and in recent months he has been seen taking trips to look out at the ocean for long periods of time.

Topic: Political Insight: Sir Augustus Vespillo
column5: From what you can gather, Sir Augustus Vespillo has a long storied history serving the Nadine Empire Loyally. Diplomatically, he leads the Knights Sanguine on Tear as their acting Knight Captain. This position was granted to him after faithful service to the Empress in her very own First Legion. In an act of unquestionable dedication, he relinquished his position and pins as Praetor when asked to command the Knights Sanguine's expedition to Tear. His forces landed upon Tear during the Siege of Port Frey in August 10AR. They did not actively participate in the siege, so their goals upon the island, and his reasons for being appointed still remain unclear. It is clear however; he will act in whatever capacity his Empire asks of him.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Salamanders and Knives are disappearing?
column5: There does not seem to be any more Salamanders or Knives disappearing than usual. The stray disappearance to getting caught by the guard, getting killed by Returned, or getting into a gang turf war are all fairly normal occurances.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: - Rumor has it, anywhere Belisarius goes, dark shadows follow. - Rumor has it, one of the Neri family luxury ships has been in Port Frey Harbor since last gathering, likely where Scatho has been staying. Rumor has it he has been spending money and seeking out every connection he can, like a madman to find any hint of what happened to his brother. Most recently, pulling in several political favors, inviting several wealthier nobles of Port Frey to dine with him. - Rumor has it, someone has been purchasing dark magic scrolls locally, and the description matches Scatho's bodyguard from the Fairwinds Festival. - Rumor has it, the symbol IX has been carved in trees and stones around town, or scribbled on the sides of buildings and doors. - Rumor has it, the Nameless gather near the docks in the early hours of the night to make bloody sacrifices to their dark gods.

Topic: Investigate History: Laylines and Rifts, and connections between the two
column5: There is certainly a connection between rifts and leylines - specifically in that any ritual that can create rifts would require the power source of being on at LEAST one leyline, but quite often such rituals would be performed at convergence points of multiple leylines. This is particularly found in how the cities of power (most capital cities of nations) are on leyline convergence points, such as where Castle Thorn now, the Breach/straight between the continents was the site of a White Chalice ritual at the Battle of the Breach at the end of the Crusades.

Topic: Investigate History: Demenor
column5: Demenor is called the "Demon Lord of Nightmares" and depictions of him present him as an elf with wings of shadow and smoke. Some of the oldest stories about him reference that he was the original one who stole Cinnorast the Hammer, and he is rumored to have even stole the name "Demenor" once upon a time. In the first Dark Future, the Returned believed he manipulated them to kill the fae Inexo, who was believed to be a demon until the moment of her death, as such killing her three years in their future meant killing her in the present time and at all times throughout history.

Topic: Investigate History: Minos (Aspect of Eden)
column5: Minos (also called "Fate") is the Aspect of Time, one of Eden's three aspects of death - the others being Malady, the Aspect of Plague, and Murder. When in a physical form, Minos takes the shape of an ethereal white spider and seems to contain powers of time, as it has been observed turning anything it touches to dust - as if the object or being has lived out the rest of its days in an instant. During the days of Theddespari, Briar worked with the Theddespari mages to capture and trap Minos in the Black Sands Hourglass, stopping time for the Mages to finish their studies. Minos was finally freed by the Returned when they broke the Hourglass in their earliest days.

Topic: Investigate History: Falcon
column5: Falcon was called The Hunter, and when seen in visions was often noted to be a black falcon. The god of the hunt, Falcon gave humanity the gift to overcome obstacles, whether small or even those bigger than they are. True to form, Falcon is known to be one of the most solitary of all the Old Ways gods, only coming together with the others during a time of great need or turmoil, and often went out on adventures, inspiring many of the great beast hunters of the Vicaul to hunt and take down the superfauna of Eur and come home victorius. It is theorized that his solitary nature is ultimately left him vulnerable to be killed in March of 10AR, taken by the spreading entropy of the universe.

Topic: Investigate History: Lisra
column5: Lisra, the Celestine goddess of music, is also called "the tornado" and is often connected to wind patterns, especially the whistling through the trees or the howl of the wind whipping through narrow city streets. She is the child of Pheonix and Tempest. The northernmost city of the Celestine Empire is named after Lisra, and is located within the hotly disputed lands that were declared a military-free zone between the Celestine and Gael at the end of the Northern War. It remains to be seen if peace will hold in the region. During the War of Radiance with the necessity of song being used at the Battle of Port Frey to combat Bel'e'athru's control over the Celestial Star, it was fortuitous and considered a good omen that it was the Legion of Lisra who arrived in Port Frey on behalf of the Celestine Empire.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Antioc's destruction (other than dream)
column5: Investigate Rumors can confirm that there was actually a dream mage manipulating and misrepresenting the situation at Antioc during the Mists.

Topic: Military Insight: Coatl in the Kingdom of De'Fey
column5: Going into the Mists without an orb for protection is highly dangerous for your scouts collecting the information for you from the region, and with the variable nature of the Mists it is impossible to settle on exact numbers especially across such a broad expanse as the former lands of De'Fey. Without the chokepoint of fortifications that used to make up the Eastern Wall, there is nothing stopping or slowing down the Coatl horde across the plains. From a military standpoint, it is clear that just claiming part of the land is untenable for any length of time without a very large defensive wall such as the Eastern Wall to create chokepoints, dozens to hundreds of towers to raise alarm at a distance, and an army of tens of thousands to support it. The Coatl army is quick to move with their mounted units and well-trained in keeping out or killing those who try to move onto their plains.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Dancing Spiders (Widow's ex-lover who is a Returned)
column5: Maliya's ex-lover, David, is called the Brown Recluse. He Returned in the summer of 2018, telling those who aligned with him that Maliya killed him to take over the Dancing Spiders' web on Tear.

Topic: What are important historical events that Bastion was involved with/intervened with?
column5: There are numerous storybooks about celestials coming to Adelrune in times of great need, but it's unclear if they are from the Golden Realms or Bastion. The most prominant, currently relevent group that is from Bastion is the Harbingers of the Crimson Sun.

Topic: Historical accounts of transcended celestials?
column5: Due to trancending happening when a soul dies, it's nearly impossible to verify those who have actually transcended, though lots of people claim that their loved ones have, especially from the Celestine Empire. One of the most famous humans who were expected to have transcended, if not for a moment of horor, was Anziel. During the Age of Tane, Anziel was beloved by celestials for being so good and righteous, she emboided that which celestials value most. She was meant to marry King Rouchard Syltane I "the Golden King" of Castle Syltane, and for the wedding, dozens of celestials had been invited to be in attendance. Unbenknownst to anyone else, C'thon Darkweaver had managed to entrap Anziel and bound her to an amulet to manipulate and control her. During the wedding, with all of the most influential celestials, royals, and nobles in attendance, C'thon used her to open a portal straight to the Abyss. From this portal rushed out demons in disuises to look like Celestials, and the wedding became a blood bath, with dozens upon dozens dying. King Rouchard and his nation felt utterly betrayed by the Celestials who, in their eyes, were no better than demons, and from this day, eventually his son Davidas, would form the Gold Chalice because the harm they caused was personal. So much hurt and life altering pain occured that day that it forever changed the relationship between humans and celestials - and yet, the celestials never put the fault on humanity. The Celestials understood that the people of Castle Syltane and eventually the Gold Chalice were GOOD people who were tricked and betrayed, and so they didn't properly fight back except to defend themselves, causing as little harm to the humans as they could because they refused to take the lives of good people. Others often named to have transcended are those like Thomas and the Wanderer.

Topic: stories of large trees throwing boulders and saplings dancing with children - any local locations? connected to Greensage trees?
column5: Yes, there are various places across Tear where giant boulders exist in odd areas that such large rocks shouldn't be or geographically don't make sense. They are especially prevelant in areas near lumberyards. These locations do not seem to coincide with the Greensage trees.

Topic: strange rocks in Shenanigans/Magpie's pockets? (3 rocks)
column5: leave at check-in

Topic: can we build on how Ambroius tehtered part of the soul to an item, to understand how Ayan boned himself to his tethers? ultimately, we want to learn how to create 4 dream tethers like Ayan/Shakes to tie someone to their dream permanently.
column5: Making a powerful dream construct with multiple tethers generally requires an EXTREMELY powerful dream creature with the knowledge of how to do so - in the case of Shakes and Euridice, Demenor created them; the remnants of the Red Dreams were shaped by a powerful dream creature at the Fall of Theddespari.

Topic: legends of bear
column5: After being imprisoned by his parents for the horrific creature they had created, Nicodemus sought vengeance for his time spent locked away. This vengeance was not sought only against his own parents, but against all godly parents who showed protection and favor for their children by hunting their children down. Bear, the Heart of the Hearth, is a fierce defender of her followers and "children", and so when the Lord of the Axe appeared in the forests to take down her faithful, she would not leave them to suffer at his hands. Bear attacked Nicodemus with a force and vengeance he could only dream of having, refusing to see a single hair harmed on the heads of her children, and tore his divinity from him. It was thrown away where he could not retrieve it, for in her eyes, such dark divinity was not needed to be carried by any true god. Severly wounded and lacking in his divinity, Nicodemus retreated to the corners of the world to create a new breed of dark children in his own image.

Topic: mandalan torc from war's end
column5: This particular torc comes from a time long ago, and with its detailing it's clear that it is one that comes with much history. It is said to be a gift from Suo, the Dragon of Devotion, to their one true love - a human warrior. It was a token of gratitude and deep love not to be forgotten when Suo's lover, Isa Fedeleu, the founder of the lost House of Fedeleu, departed from Mandala for he had grown tired of war and sought peace elsewhere. The stories say that he begged Suo to accompany him, but Suo could not, and so he wore the torc, for the rest of his days as a reminder of their devotion to one another. This is a powerful Pure of Heart token. If the wearer is a follower of the Mandalan Faith with the Pure of Heart skill, and declares their devotion to Suo in a religious rite, it provides double the benefits of Pure of Heart (two uses of willpower) per rest. Note: It may have additional benefits if Suo returns the wearer's devotion. Please destroy the tag you were originally given, you may pick up your new tag from the bulletin board during check-in.

Topic: 1. Obsidian Star - was the object damage? 2. is it related to a dragon, vault, or temple? 3. is the object magical or holding magical energy? 4. what is it's purpose as related to the astari? 5. does it feel like a key? 6. has it been effected by being housed inside Ira as a fae construct? 7. what/where is its origin? 8. is it inherently dangerous?
column5: 1. The Obsidian Star is probably coated and nasty with Ira crust, but is likely undamaged otherwise. 2. It is impossible to discern what the Obsidian Star is directly related to. 3. The Obidian Star has magic entangled with it, but it, itself, is not intrinsically magical. 4. The Obsidian Star is a key 5. It feels as the physical prop feels. 6. It is coated and nasty with Ira crust, but does not seem to have a lasting effect from its former housing. 7. The Obsidian Star was created by the Astari. 8. It is impossible to discern if the Obsidian Star itself is inherently dangerous.

Topic: can we, via spell or ritual, manifest a willing & known dream entity in the waking? the idea is not to be able to do it with any random dream NPC, but more like a major dream NPC that can be brought here as an ambassador or an ally in a fight. kinda like manifest tether in reverse.
column5: Yes, it is likely possible since rituals exist to force-summon extraplanar creatures (demons, celestials, and fae) onto Adelrune using their true names. Alternatively, you could potentially pitch it as a dream walking type spell to talk to Dream entities (similar to Spirit Walk), but that would need to be pitched through Rules. Otherwise, to approach this as a ritual, it would need to be handled as a mod-specific situation at staff discretion (Story staff has been informed of your interest in this), rather than being pushed or forced forward through player research.

Topic: what magics might be connected or ? ? of creationist magic? are there any that are stronger or more closely tied? because dragons wield creationist magic, is draconic magic related/descended from it?
column5: Most magics were discovered/adapted by humans trying to mimic what dragons can innately do by being creatures of magic. This magic was spread significantly in the Ages of Eur and Tane by the demons, to support humanities strength against the dragons. (for the shorthand, draconic/creationist magic are the same thing)

Topic: are there texts that describe "creation" magic? are there living beings who have knowledge of it? creationist magic creatured adelrune and was wielded by dragons, but has been lost in history
column5: Any texts related to "creation" magic are extremely ancient, created before written history and only exist as archeological record. The most common "appearances" of this magic are as glyphs that the dragons carved into stones with their claws, which were capable of reshaping reality and the world itself. The symbols and words of power draw on the native power of the world for change, and so they can speak/breathe change into the world on a whim. Living beings that know this magic are the dragons and any particular ancient or extraplanar beings (especially the fae) are passingly familiar with it as they snuch into the world long ago to see the dragons use it.

Topic: what are the origins and properites of the moss? potential uses?
column5: The moss of itself is a living, sentient being. Or rather many living sentient beings that function as a hive mind within each pod. It's origin seems to be native to Tear as it is not found anywhere else in Adelrune, but it does not seem to be Fae or other planar in origins. It is clearly seen to choose a new host through infecting fresh wounds with spores, and as the being goes about its life, the moss spores will slowly disperse through the body and take over its host in a parasitic fashion by devouring the flesh of the being till there is nothing left but bone as a vessel to carry the moss. It is not limited to humanoid bodies but humanoid vessels are larger and tend to live longer while infected so they are easier to take over as the newborn mosspod has time to grow, develop, and learn to move and use the body.

Topic: darklings: origins and society?
column5: write up from Lizette about Darkling activity in Port Frey, Darklings are the children of a half fae/half-demon abomination called Nicodemus who was present at the gathering. They arrived in Port Frey offering gifts to the Returned who took them like fools and did not question why they were being given so freely. When the creatures invited the Returned to attend a party later that evening, the Returned gave in to the temptation with no restraint and disregarded any concerns or cautions voiced by myself, Ser Aislynn of the White Chalice, or Sir Sifrid Payne, the fae defender. The Returned, in fact, almost tripped over themselves in their exhuberance to attend this party and rushed off to get ready for it. Following the madness, we three waited on the street outside, expecting trouble. We were not disappointed, for after less than twenty minutes, screams were heard from inside as the Returned in attendance were being devoured by Nicodemus himself. We rushed in and saved as many as were possible to, though Nicodemus did escape with a final hostage, only to be chased down by Sir Sifrid before the abomination fled into the night. No words of thanks were provided for the those who saved many lives that night, and the Returned continue to prove themselves as petty, ungrateful children.

Topic: what makes a Returned soul different from a normal soul? can ferrymen be asked to bring a soul from the planes of the dead? can a Returned be created?
column5: Returned Souls are stronger than other souls and due to the Ritual that was done to call back the heroes of old to be the Returned, they are more capable of crossing over from the realms of death to life. In Rituals found in the Book of Ig, souls can be moved from one death realm to another, but at this time there is no known evidence of a Ferryman bringing a specific soul out of the death realm that isn't attached to a strong Returned soul.

Topic: Teppidus Scouting Operation
column5: Getting near the Drir Isles through the Mists proves to be exceptionally difficult and Tabitha's warship runs aground much sooner than initially expected. Using a smugglers skiff, the scouting party is able to get a bit closer, but quickly discover that the Drir seem to be coralled in a more tight-knit manner than normal, and the Guardians of Kin'Mae (Drir knights) are on high alert near the volcano, making it next to impossible to get closer for more detailed information by anyone who isn't Drir (or disguised as such). There does not appear to be any demon activity in the area or non-Drir involvement, as the Nadine siege weaponry that was once on the islands stands abandoned with no Nadine in sight. With effort, the scouts are able to retreat back to the large ship and get it ungrounded without drawing the attention of the Drir who are focused more intently on what is going on within their society than what is going on off in the distance of their waters during these times of Mist.

Topic: What is the purpose of the marks of the moon/stars? (ie a conduit, a key to artifact, or what?)
column5: Marks like Astari marks and god marks are inactive marks left upon the soul, so they wouldn't affect magics or other skills, but serve has a sort of pre-requisite for using or accessing magical or divine items connected to them. This can be anything from a blessed sword that does more damage for one bearing the correct mark, to a magical door that only one with the right mark can pass through or open.

Topic: where could we find more astari ruins and artifacts?
column5: As the Astari existed before recorded history, there are no sources to be found in the libraries that note any sites beyound those that were already explored. Most artifacts and archeological dig sites are ultimately found through a benefactor sending out teams of archeologists to search in a particular region for ancient ruins, as Kenrin was the benefactor funding the last expeditions. Speaking with an archeologist or archeology department of a reputable learning institution (or perhaps disreputable treasure seekers, though that may bring other troubles), would be helpful to further your exploration, as would just hiring a team to go out in search of ruins in the regions you know the Astari to have been settled in.

Topic: what connection, if any, do the marks of the astari have to the obelisk & others like it? shared purpose or possibility to commune or access as a key?
column5: The Marks of the Stars and Moon do not have a specific connection to the obelisk beyond a common origin - the obelisks were built by the dragons and modified by the fae, while the Astari were made by the fae to act as ambassadors to the dragons, thus both were used as methods of communication between realms. You would find at this point in your research that there is little more to be found on the subject in libraries or books, as such things happened long before recorded history and have not been heavily studied in ensuing years.

Topic: what can I discover about the "eyeless knight" in regards to the astari?
column5: The "Eyeless Night" is less connected to the Astari, but through your knowledge of dragons, you would know that it is a common motif throughout history, typically representative of the wyrms that exist to eat realms rather than a dragon.

Topic: is there a spiritual sigificance for the Mandalans who settled in War's End, to have chosen that place to settle?
column5: There was no particular spiritual significance to the area in which explorers settled at War's End, only that it was to the west, beyond the Expanse and (they believed) the reach of war. It was known to have been green dragon territory but it was well after the dragons had gone into hiding across the world. War with the area's earlier inhabitants quickly proved their hopes to be fruitless, and the settlement was abandoned after only 3 years.

Topic: can the fae blood of a specific fae be used ot find the true name of a fae? can fae blood be used to make a better formadehyde? can this grant a returned powers of a high fae? what causes the brains to melt, what does one see?
column5: Fae blood is not a method to finding the true name of a fae, nor are any of it's physical component parts. Appropriate surgeries can be researched to replace a mortal's organs with viable fae body parts or blood, but this is often the realm of the Stitcher's Guild and will not give the recipient high fae powers, but something more akin to a fae-blooded or fae-touched. While technically you've asked far too many questions for one research action, I will give you this last unreleated question as well - What one sees when licking the Aspect of a True Fae is unknown, for it causes the brains of the one who licked it to melt and die. An Aspect is a physical manifestation of time and reality, and as such, it has been theorized that they have seen too much that the feeble mortal mind cannot comprehend, and thus it's only defense mechanism is to melt to ruin.

Topic: looking for historical records of leylines on Tear and where they are located. how they change over time?
column5: In your research, you are able to find the following map, and some additional notes regarding the locations of leylines. While there are different scholarly approaches and beliefs in regards to leylines, they are general quite stable and predictable, only shifting even a few inches due to a tremendous release of energy within the earth - whether by a large earthquake or an exceptionally powerful ritual. In fact the only location that has been documented to see such a drastic change in its leylines is what is now the Breach or the Strait of Roses, following the White Chalice ritual during the Last Battle of the Crusades. More typically, leylines are observed to naturally fluctuate in their strength and power at any given time.

Topic: what are the qualities of aa leyline? such as affinity, flow, direction, endpoints?
column5: The flow of leylines are quite identifiable at a quick glance, as they are most often defined by exceptional geographical features, such as mountains and rivers. While some scholars often claim that leylines have a particular affinity (a common local legend shared by the demon Mieletassa is that the city of Solace lays upon the intersection of the leylines of death and nightmare), there is actually no evidence to make this point. Furthermore, on the topic of Solace, the city is a known intersection of three identified leylines and thus suggests that the confluence of death and nightmare is likely rumors to perpetuate the legend of the Returned as even more spooky than merely being beings of exceptional power brought back from the dead to save the world. In general, there are not identifiable terminus to leylines, except at confluence points, though that is not to say they do not have endpoints, only that such features have not been observed and are beyond mapping at this time, due to ocean depths, extreme geographical features, and the Mists themselves.

Topic: myths of veyn
column5: The myths and legends of Veyn paint the many sides of the god - punishing, blessing, and caring - There is first a Vicaul tale of a young girl, the blessed and favored of Ren. Trained as a druid and loved by all of the gods as she gave honor to each of them in turn, even Veyn whom she loved dearly. But when unfaithful, when she doubted, Veyn stole her life away as a punishment for her failure to trust in the gods. There is a Cestral tale from the time of the Second Landing, of a low-lying Cestral village a few hours inland from the sea. As the Vicaul pressed inland, looking for a place to settle and call their own, the Cestral had warning from the coastal villages and made to evacuate the village with all they could carry and make for higher ground. At the top of the hill, they met a druid who had forseen their plight and came to pray with them. Hours later, as the Vicaul entered the village to raid it and ultimately settle, with the sun setting behind the hill so they could not be seen from the lowland, they prayed for Veyin's blessing and protections, and that the Vicaul would not move any closer to the capital. As night drew near, the rain came in a vast flood, soaking them through and overwhelming the channels and dams the Cestral had built to irrigate their farming fields. So swift was the flood of fresh water falling from the sky that the Vicaul had no time to get out to find higher ground as they had slept in stolen beds, and the invaders drowned, leaving not a single one alive to return to their home. To this day, the village of Enidfen is still beneath water, and it's said that only when Veyn is displeased with the Cestral do the waters recede to reveal the old stonework of the village, reminding the Cestral people that Veyn protected them on that day. It's been said that Veyn and Panther share a bond unlike many of the other gods, and it is often this tale shared to show that connection. In the days following Fox's death, after Panther had taken a swipe at and wounded Snake for his transgression and betrayal, Panther sat with Veyn at the side of the pool to strategize. There must be some way to find Fox and restore his life - it wasn't right for a god to die, could they even die? Panther was convinced they could not - that Fox was held somewhere beyond, a place that a god could not normall reach, but as The Hidden and with Veyn's gifts of strategy, perhaps they would have the ability to find and recover their fallen companion. Together, they traversed the spirit realms, Veyn leading the way as he followed the trail of Fox's essence, and Panther at his side, the river winding through the realms of death and spirits. As they neared the end of their journey, as they reached the end of all things, the darkness beyond where no light could pierce, they found themselves face to face with the great Spirit Wyrm who blocked their way. Panther and the Wyrm fought endlessly, her determination to make things right driving her onward through her exhaustion, but still the great wyrm fought back against her, expelling half-digested and corrupted spirits to aid in the fight against her. Finally, when it seemed like the fight was too much for her, and the Spirit Wyrm came down upon her with its thousands of teeth, wounding her critically, Veyn knew something must be done. There was little he could do in a fight in this realm, but he had made a plan through the days this fight had gone on, building a protective barrier so that the great wyrm would not be able to reach Fox and devour him - not yet, at least. With Fox's essence protected, and Panther wounded so deeply, Veyn made his final move and the river swelled to a great depth, forcing back the spirit worm and enveloping Panther in its healing waters. Drawing her back downstream with a gentle current to protect her too, so she would survive and together they could restrategize and try again in due time. Back in the safety of Crow's realm, Panther rested in Veyn's shallow healing waters, deep in a healing slumber.

Topic: first and cursed
column5: Tales of the Firstkin are as old, if not older than, the Vicaul as a people. Back when they were all Gael, before the splitting began, those who most devout to the Old Ways gods, those who were Marked and blessed but beyond that they lived and breathed their devotion to their god - they became known as the Firstkin. Those who were living avatars of their gods on Adelrune, who carried with them a true blessing of connection to their god (similar to a warlock's pact) that allowed them to transform into the animals of their gods and act as the living embodiment of their god in the physical realm. Only the First can be of the Firstkin because they were solely blessed with such power and ability. It is now their duty to hunt down the Cursed. The Cursedkin, or just the Cursed, are those who sought to obtain or be given the powers of the Firstkin through other means than the blessings of the gods. The Cursed will be transformed into their animal forms by outside triggers - time, season, etc - and unlike the Firstkin, they have no control of their animal form either for the transformation or while in that form. They become mindless, killing machines for the duration they are in the form, and there is little they can do to control it. Becoming Cursed, while many have sought it, its not an easy process, and most often results in death.

Topic: how to turn someone into a demon?
column5: There are many known methods to becoming a demon-blooded. The most common method is through surgery, as is practiced by the Stitcher's Guild. Less well-known, but not particularly uncommon, is through a ritual performed by a Greater Demon such as Prince Bellus or Davidas. Still, there are other even less common methods, such as those discovered by the Returned called Heka, or through methods known to the Effendal Houses like Delfestrae or Etilvor.

Topic: what are the earliest teachings of Chorus? where did these teachings originate? when and how did they change?
column5: The earliest teachings of the Church of Chorus came during the Crusades, as the Church was growing and expanding, and focused around the strength found as humanity came together for a common purpose against a greater threat. The earliest texts were a collection of notes and letters, journals and meditations, written by Crusaders - by knights and war leaders. In the following decades, these earliest teachings and texts were put together as holy texts by the College of Cardinals as doctrine became canonized under a central leadership.

Topic: raven - how are they worshipped, what are common rites/practices, recent events?
column5: As one of the most powerful and popular gods of the Old Ways faith, Raven is worshipped with the burning of sacrifices, specifically entrails and eyeballs. And like his brother, the offerings of shiny things. As all of these offerings require some level of tool to obtain or create them, it is a double use of showing Raven that his followers are using the gifts he gave them to obtain these things on his behalf. Rites of Prosperity are often associated with Raven. In the springtime, there are childhood holidays in each of the Old Ways cultures where children play tricks on each other in honor of Raven's love of trickery, fun, and laughter.   In your search of the library, you'll find a leather-bound journal deep in the recesses of the shelves, brimming with letters, ripped out articles from the Assassin with notes and underlines, notes on various topics of research, and the writer's thoughts on various topics and events. Raven is mentioned in one of these entries:  May 7ar. I am starting to hear the Gods once more. Tonight as I walked home to my rooms in the guilds, I could hear the ravens and the crows overhead, arguing about something, debating. Cawing at one another as their voices have sounded to me since my Returning, but then for a moment it seemed to change. I heard in their caws words spoken in the common tongue. “Call for the gods,” they said. “Caeli,” the crows repeated. “Crow Caeli,” calling my attention to their words. “The time is coming,” the ravens replied, “Be prepared, for you are needed. We will waken them all once more. The time is close.”  And then just like that, their voices returned to the standard caw of corvids in the trees, fighting over stolen food. Had I imagined it all? Did the gods speak out once more? Is is just my hopeful mind, my earnest desire to hear their voices again? I will be faithful. I will prepare. The time of War is coming. The need for Unity is coming. We need the gods to stand with us if we are to survive this. I can only hope that the words I have heard tonight are true – that Raven and Crow will stand with us, that they will be with me and see me through what is coming. And when it is done, that Crow will welcome me beneath his wings, for I know my time is growing short now. War is coming.

Topic: given stradha's input that music enhances magic, and that musical magic is often connected to ritual circles and changing vibrations, can we harness the magical vibrations of the song to reveal what is hidden in the circular area rather than put us to sleep?
column5: While you would know that yes, music enhances magic, any study down that path doesn't seem to match to what you know about the area that is obscurred to divination. The region and effect of the magic seem to have been a combination of a few different things - from Esdeline's knowledge and upbringing, she would remember having seen Odessa Atherton's sails bearing Avirtia's symbol - a goddess of the Celestine Faith, which also has Lisra, the goddess of song - suggesting a holy ground of some sort, which aligns with the more obscure stories of Odessa Atherton being a follower of the Celestine faith, as well as the fact it is blocked against divination and scrying. As Ember looks at maps and through obscure texts, studying the rhythms of the song and hearing it play within her mind, for the first time she falls asleep to the song. It's a peaceful sleep, a quiet moment amongst books, rocked to a gentle sleep upon the Mare of the Mists as it takes her back to a time she doesn't remember - whether a dream or a lost memory, it is hard to say, but it is particularly vivid and uninfluenced by dream magic. She does not necessarily feel as if she is herself, but she is comfortable and lounged in a large, plush bed, wine in hand, and soft laughter ringing out in the room. It's not so fine a room to be a Captain's quarters, but easily a first mate upon a well-to-do ship. A woman lays sideways across the bed, her head pillowed on Ember's stomach, and Ember's hand stroking gently through her hair as the woman trails off the end of her story into more laughter. The room smells of well-worn wood of an old ship, with deeper notes of amber and warm spices. Between the blankets and wine, it provides a comfortable warmth on a cool night, keeping the inside of the cabin cozy. It's only when the woman laying with Ember turns her head to look at her with a wide smile that the woman is finally recognizable - Tatiana Grey, in her prime, not the ghostly form that had been bound to the cave on the south of Tear. She seems utterly besotted in that moment, but the peaceful moment is not to last - as a warning bell is rung in the night and the shout of men can be heard from the deck calling out that a ship is approaching under the cover of night. The room fades slowly as Tatiana stands, shushing the question on Ember's lips - "Lock the door behind me and douse the lights. Do not let anyone in until I come back, do you understand?" The last thing Ember feels as the room disappears is the gentle press of a kiss on her forehead before she wakes, once again at the library table on the Mare of the Mists.

Topic: what is the connection between zegimmon and the dreaming? did he come from there or is he locked in there? did the other wyrms come from the dreaming?
column5: Zegimmon is the wyrm destined to destroy the realm of the Dreaming by devouring the realm in the Age of the Great Death, when the universe is unmade. While the wyrms did not come from the Dreaming, their origin existed long, long before recorded history. By all accounts, they have always been there, since the dawning of the Universe.

Topic: where would one look for Bastion artifacts?
column5: Artifacts of Bastion are most commonly found in or near Bastion. Generally any artifacts found any distance from Bastion are going to be in the possession of Greater Celestials. Additionally, in your study of celestial artifacts, you will find notes in the Guild of Academics discussing the pieces of Beleathru's regalia, and the need of the Returned to collect the pieces before Star/Beleathru could collect them during the War of Radiance - specifically, the multiple pieces of Beleathru's throne, which was an artifact known to amplify and spread the influence of Beleathru's control across the world from anywhere the throne was set up.

Topic: looking to the early history of the Church of Chorus - earliest mentions, when Chorus as a faith first emerged if it predates the founding of the Church?
column5: The Church of Chorus came together as a faith in the years leading into the Crusades, and through the three decades of the Crusades it grew in prominance and influence along with the rest of the newly-established Amalgamation, bringing together humanity against the common threat of the demons.

Topic: what are the realms that have a border with Adelrune? and what are the names of the those realms and the names of the barriest between them and Adelrune?
column5: While there is much scholarly debate on the topic of planes and other realms, as any so-called evidence is nearly impossible to verify, there is a general accepted theory in the modern age in which the various planes/realms do not directly touch against one another as neighbors. That which is closest to touching - the Dreaming - does not physically touch Adelrune, but metaphysically touches it in the sense of it touches the mind of Adelrune's inhabitants (see: The Realm That Touches Us All). There are a number of commonly known "crossover" points across the world in which one can traverse to another realm, but that does not mean it is a physical gateway to a neighboring plane - these crossover points include but are not limited to: the Mists, Hedge Gates, mirrors, rifts, and portals. Additionally, there is no actual term for these various crossover points, though colloquially "the Veil" is used to represent all separation between planes/realms and Adelrune. Finally, in your research you were able to discover the following map of planes, though it is not by any means exhaustive (for example the spirit plane is not represented).

Topic: beastiary analysis: burrow's grub
column5: It’s an animate fae construct that is actually a collection of tiny microbial fungus hyphae, which give it its “fluffy” appearance Name Fungal hy-fae,   -magical fae construct  -baby/young stage of multistage lifecycle  -white fur appearance is actually a form of fungus known as hyphae  -limited animalistic intelligence  -weaknesses: it’s as defenseless as a literal grub  -eats by spreading its hyphae over any organic matter and slowly breaks it down into nutrients while in close proximity

Topic: Underworld Insight: newly emptied soul gems that have gone up for sale
column5: In the aftermath, there was certainly a rise in the market of empty soul gems, but nothing overly telling except the cost of soul gems had fallen to a general market value of 2 silver. Like many of the Returned summoners were able to retain a hold on their tethered spirits, other summoners with filled gems were able to react quickly to do the same.

Topic: Investigate History: Ceilieen
column5: Celieen is commonly accepted to be a child of Lisra and Nero, and sister of Anika. While not one of the main gods of the Celestine Faith or Eirie Faith, she is nonetheless quietly worshipped in both by those who are called to her - she is revered as the patron goddess of visions, drug use, and insanity. After being blinded at the hands of another god, whose identity seems to have been stricken from the stories, she gained notoriety as "the Blind Oracle" which is her most-known and most-often used epithet, and almost exclusively goes by this title to her followers. It is often said that diviners who have lost their physical sight are particularly blessed by her, for they are no longer limited by their physical form to see into the beyond. She is commonly associated with variations of divination, including runes and astrology, though there are other gods also associated with these subjects as well.

Topic: underworld insight: happy gentlemen
column5: the Happy Gentlemen are a very small gang of former Dacian nobles, who are renowned duelists. Happy Gentlemen is domanently a gentlemen's club, where members dare and challenge one another to do more elaborate and extravagant cons and heists.

Topic: spread through song: "druid kaelan's curse. destroy the scroll or destroy the soul"
investigate history: lion
column5: Lion, called the True Pride, is known to be a high-god of the Old Ways faith and the head of the pantheon. Where the other animal gods were raised to godhood for the gifts they gave to humanity and Thte, Lion is the only one who has never given his gift to mankind, for he gives humanity something to continue to strive for - leadership and true nobility. It is well known he was the first of the Old Ways gods to go to sleep as he was unimpressed with what humanity had to show. In July of 7AR, at the hands of the Returned, Lion was awoken with the other sleeping Old Ways gods. Even then he did not give his gift to man as the Old Gods all blessed the Returned for their awakening and the construction of the Great Altar - for Lion's Roar was heard through the voice of his candlebearer, the only one he deemed worthy - the full celestial Aislynn. (If you do further research on this topic, there is an extensive account of it in Caeli's journal)

Topic: investigate history: gods and powerful beings of devotion
column5: Devotion is an interesting term in relation to gods and powerful beings, as being devout and having true devotion to another are important attributes to many such beings. Suo, of the Mandalan Faith, is worshipped as the Dragon of Devotion, but what are dragons devoted to beyond their horde? Within the Church of Chorus, they have a holy text called The One Devotion. Entire cities, citystates, and even nations were founded in devotion to various gods who inspired such devotion. Within the lines of The Serpent's Song, devotion is spun, devotion so strong that it binds even in death. A Tale of Two Soldiers, another ancient text in which the true devotion of the hearts of men are exposed before a being in black with golden wings. The Choirs of Chorus, whose devotion to their god supercedes death. Devotion is found all throughout religion and faith - inspiring devotion, being given devotion. But what can be noted is this - it is something older.

Topic: malady
column5: Malady is well-known to the Returned from their earliest days. At that point, the Aspect of Plague was still contained within the Black Orb, an artifact used to control and contain the Aspect from spreading plague openly and wildly. The Orb came in and out of the possession of the Returned in the early days, used in a number of big rituals, and as a containment unit for souls that were contributed to it much in the way a soul lantern is used to power big rituals. It's final action with Malady still contained within it was when the demon Hope Lightbringer used all the souls held within the Orbin in a ritual to give the celestial Aidhreal the strength of a god, which she then used to march on Cyrus against Star/Beleathru and devour a strong being that was held beneath the city, before Star could. At that time, Malady was freed (though not consumed by the ritual as the other souls had been) and was free to travel and spread their diseases. Like Eden's other Aspects of Death, Malady is not a force to be reasoned with or befriended as an ally, it is a pure embodiment of disease and it exists to spread disease and death in benefit of Eden. And also like the other Aspects, they were all trapped within their respective prisons by the fae Briar and the mages of Theddespari. With knowledge that the Dagger of Murder had been removed from the Oubliette in the last few years, during the time of the Returned, it stands to reason that all of Eden's aspects walk freely once more beyond their containment.

Topic: underworld insight: four corners
column5: Either because Four Corners is a new guild, or they are exceptionally clean, but there's no news or dealings involving Four Corners within the underworld circles of Tear. However some of the members of Four Corners do have intersecting paths that stray into underworld affairs, in some form or another.   Lady Lilliana, simply by being a Dacian and due to the cultural norms within Dacian society simply has no qualms working with unsavory individuals, and has such connections. Doroga, a self proclaimed big-game hunter, has a fair share of information brokers that provide him with updates on potential creatures worth hunting, whether wild or domestic. Alethea always seems to have someone keeping an eye on her from the shadows, it's unknown from an outside perspective if this individual is keeping a lookout for Alethea, stalking them, or some other twisted reason. If you ask anyone within the underworld about Zixx, they do not seem to know who this individual is or offer any useful information

Topic: follow the money: four corners
column5: Four Corners was founded on a ten gold investment from Ziix used to start the company. Of that coin, seven of it was used to purchase a hothouse during an auction hosted by the International Merchants Guild, and the other three gold was used to purchase a printing press at that same auction. Some of the core members have frivolous spending habits, with both Alethea and Zixx having an eye for finer things. Alethea is interested in fashion, and Zixx is known to eat and drink only the highest quality food and wine. Both Lady Lilliana and Alethea come from prominent economic based families, and are born into merchant connections outside of Tear. Lilliana is of from House Vallaro, a Dacian House with a firm grip on the fashion and jewelry industry in Dace. Alethea's family specializes in agricultural trade, and grows produce in the Celestine Empire on their farm, Hortensia Orchards. Four Corners has no outstanding debts and pays it's employees a comfortable and reliable wage. Though at the time the guild has only hired individuals with a more standard skillset and not Master or Grandmasters. The company tends to celebrate after each gathering is sales have been successful, and are known to be generous in that time

Topic: investigate rumors: the king's blade
column5: - rumor has it, King's Blades were made by the Swordmaker clan for the Kings of the newly-minted Amalgamation during the Crusades - rumor has it they haven't been seen or produced in over 100 years - rumor has it the Swordmaker clan has fully died out, as Dans Swordmaker and his only living relative - a sister - were executed for his Unforgivable Crimes against the Nadine Empire and Amalgamation - rumor has it Dans Swordmaker's sister is hiding out in the court of Princess Arielle Hastings - rumor has it, Souldrinker (Valdir's sword) is a King's Blade - rumor has it, a kings blade requires a summoner to entrap the soul of a powerful mage (typically a sorcerer) within the weapon itselfp

Topic: investigate rumors: who has the dagger?

column5: there seems to be at least some truth that a group gathers near the docks at night, and often less leave after their gathering than who arrived earlier in the evening. however there is no way to confirm if they are nameless or not without approaching

Topic: investigate fame: the rise of the church of chorus
column5: The Church of Chorus rose to prominance as the faith of the Crusades, built upon the strength and unity of humanity under one banner, and grew alongside the amassing strength and position of the newly-minted Amalgamation. It remained in prominence in the following decades through the Purification as it once more attempted to bring humanity together against a common enemy. Following the Crown Wars, the Church of Chorus grew into it's final form, taking ultimate leadership over the kingdoms of the Amalgamation so even kings had to answer to them. Through the Age of Chorus, the Church of Chorus was the largest and wealthiest religion in the world, boasting the largest army. None dared challenge them until the very end of the age.

Topic: investigate history: legendary treasure in Edge (Sparrow spirit called magpie)
column5: Communing with the sparrow spirit called Magpie, you are able to gather a story of a legendary treasure hidden near Edge, tucked deep into an ocean crevice along the rocky nothern shore of Tear. The little bird would tell you about all of the strange things that happen there, that water seems to spill out from this crevice into the sea even though there is no source for the water, keeping the water levels high even at the lowest of tides and making it nearly impossible to find your way into - unless you climb in from up above, or you have a special ship that will pull itself into the crevice, regardless of winds or tides pushing it away. That these large ships seemingly disappear into the space under a full moon, only to reappear a month or two later when the moon is once again full, often lighter in men, but occassionally weighted down with great treasures.

Topic: underworld insight: the effendal group that poisoned Harvester
column5: there does not seem to be any connection to the human underworld (at least not without a specific name or organization to look into)

Topic: investigate rumors: the effendal group that poisoned harvester
column5: - rumor has it, the Effendal did it - rumor has it, it wasn't an Effendal - rumor has it, secret societies can be found even in dark places - rumor has it, the brotherhood left behind one last gift in Port Frey before they disappeared - instead of blood glyphs against demons, they were blood glyphs against fae - rumor has it, the Delfestrae have horrific poisons hidden in the depths of the Citadel - rumor has it, the Nameless can have anyone in the world poisoned at any moment

Topic: investigate history: oldest stories of Myzka from the Trahazi perspective
column5: It is said that Myzka can find beauty in anything - in bird song, in sunlight dappled through the trees, in the word of law, within stone, or within the heart of even the most terrible of people.  Though Myzka has always been flighty, when faced with a challenge she will always find stillness and concentration to solve it in any creative manner. And so one afternoon, Cousin Kuyeh rolled the ugliest large rock Myzka had ever seen down a hill towards the caravan's encampment - they claimed it wasn't meant to hit one of the wagons, it was just a game - but it was only Baki's quick response that saved a wagon from being splintered under the giant thing. Due to remain in this encampment for a few weeks, everyone gathered around and frowned at the giant thing blocking the middle of their camp and debated what to do with it. Some tried to move it but it could not be budged. Others suggested to use it to drape the large clothes and blankets to dry. But Myzka sat there and thoughtfully studied it for a long while, before announcing that trapped within it's great confines was a new friend, and she would bring them out to set them free. The others did not understand, but waved off Myzka's whim as a flight of fancy and left her to her own devices. Over the following weeks, with a chisel in hand, Myzka spent countless hours toiling under the hot sun, chip by chip seeking out that friend she claimed was trapped within the stone. Finally, in the final days before the caravan was to depart she revealed the physical form of eternal beauty that had been trapped, until finally he was freed entirely from the stone prison and he stood with her and smiled in the sunlight, feeling its warmth upon his face for the first time in many years. When asked how she knew he was within the stone, she smiled enigmatically and simply said that beauty can always be found if one is only willing to look for it.

Topic: background research: dancing spiders
column5: The Dancing Spiders, while primarily operating in the realms of commerce, and underworld information brokering, have quite a surprising connection to the realms of academics, particularly in their well-known interest and study of the occult and unique items of supernatural or extra-planar origins. While it is typically difficult to gain access to such things, once one is tangled within the web of the Dancing Spiders, access to and knowledge of such rare and unique artifacts, parts, and items are reasonably obtainable. The Dancing Spiders seem to have connections in nearly every nation, including Cestral (particularly Alexander, though their contact remains unknown) and Ad Decimum.

Topic: eavesdrop: Prince Ahearn
column5: Servants talk, and those of Cestral are perhaps more nosy than most. Especially regarding the royal family, as they are favored by the peasantry. Following cabinet meetings, which Prince Ahearn attends with his father, the talk is often about how the Prince is getting into weekly fights with Declan Stone and occassionally his father, when the king agrees with Declan Stone. As he's the only child at home since his sisters went off and got married, his mother has begun to pressure him to marry for love and has been focusing all her attention on him, which is slowly driving the Prince up the wall. His study is littered with sketches and diagrams of things he calls machines, and an imported clock from Ad Deciumum has been dismantled on a table with many drawers in which he keeps all the little pieces and finds different ways to attach and twist them around one another to build small contraptions. Whenever a Merchant's Guild ship arrives from the mainland, he is always eager and hopeful for mail, though what he is waiting for never seems to come, ultimately leaving him disappointed and doubly so when there is no additional news of the Mists lifting from the Eastern Expanse. In recent months, Prince Ahearn has taken to solitary rides away from the castle, without escort of guard or servant. During one such ride he came in from the storm, famously drenched from the rain with his shirt nearly sheer and clinging to his fit form, leaving the servants and locals in quite a tizzy over the state of their Prince.

Topic: state of the court: Nadine
column5: conversation in the Nadine Court is currently focused around a number of subjects, but all of them keep coming back to the main topic: Is the Empress losing her touch with a string of recent defeats and heavy losses? - with the dragon in Concerti and the near loss of a full-legion, a Nadine legion has never been lost in it's entirety before. does that spell the end of Nadine military might in the world? - can the dragon be an ally? is making it an ally conceding defeat and giving up territory to the beast rather than killing it outright? - heated debate about dream magic and if it is a tool that the Nadine should adopt both in defense so the same thing that happened to Antioc doesn't happen anywhere else in the Nadine Empire, but also argued vehemently against due to the potential for internal damage it can case within the Empire - the opportunity to take down Whitehall (and Lord Darius) once and for all - the slow response of the so-called Eastern Alliance supporting Whitehall (it takes time for Hastings and Vein to send troops that far south), and the expectation to press the offensive once they've worn down the Alliance's willingness to keep sending troops in a game of attrition - if the Empress is not victorius over Darius or Whitehall, she has most certainly lost her ability to lead
