Topic: Bestiary Analysis: Firebird in the Druid Grove - “red and orange bird who caught fire intermittently”
Investigate History: Noren
column5: Noren, "The Wizard", was a great blue dragon known to once have had territory and made his home in what is now the Vicaul lands. He has a deep-rooted hatred for red dragons, which was made all the worse when (after a long-time rivalry and many violent altercations with the Ash Storm), Etyr came out victorious over Noren in a devastating conflict that left Noren severely lacking in power and strength. Though they were a very even match for centuries, the loss against Etyr was debilitating for Noren, who swore he would get his vengeance against the red dragon. It is unclear when he went to sleep, and he has not been sighted since the dragons began to wake.

Topic: Investigate History: Etyr
column5: Etyr, the Ash Storm, was dominantly active in an era of pre-history before books and records were kept, so most knowledge of him is from old stories that weren't written down until generations later. As such, it's unclear when he actually went to sleep as he was presumed dead in the tales. What is known is that he was native to the Nadine region, a territory he was known to have shared with his mate, the "Fire Bird", and was the unequivicoble leader of the red dragons on Adelrune. For decades he raged against the Nadine Empire as they encroached on his region, and there are stories of great forest fires and the destruction he wraught against the territory, which earned him the title of "the Ash Storm". Imperial dragon hunters used dragon bone weapons which were particularly effective against dragons, and so the common belief (until his recent reemergence) had been that he had met his demise one day when he had been cornered by Imperial dragon hunters and his mate joined the fray, which ultimately ended in a blinding supernova of flame and destruction leaving nothing but ash and debris in it's wake. The fate of his presumed-dead mate is currently unknown, given his own reappearance.

Topic: Investigate History: Olgero
column5: Olgero is a giant scaled sea turtle, with suprisingly little written about him in the books likely due to his independent and reclusive nature in keeping to the deepest seas. Once regarded by some sailors to be the god of oceans and trade, worship of the Turtle is almost non-existant in the modern day. He is generally regarded as a benevolent dragon, and ocassionally a story will crop up from ships who found themselves too close to the Mists, or who were stranded at sea waiting for a strong wind to help them continue on their way, that were pushed along by a great turtle back towards safety and civilization. None of these stories tell of the great turtle speaking to anyone aboard the ships, and most who hear of the tales disregard them as fairy stories or attribute them to the many beasts of the sea, rather than a sentient dragon.

Topic: Investigate History: The Loremasters
column5: Loremasters are an organization founded late in the age of Tane, early age of Chorus, who serve as advisers to royalty in every civilized nation. They favor books and records and desire to use knowledge for the betterment of society and the world by not repeating the mistakes of the past. This organization is generally viewed as benevelent and well meaning but in some circles has the reputation of having the ear of the ruler. Their counterpart and major nemesis is another organization known as the Order of Broken Pages.

Topic: Underworld Insight: White Oak Brewery – Hastings
column5: This brewery has trade agreements with numerous other small brewers as well, creating an unofficial brewers guild within the former Amalgamation.  Brewers and trade partners associated with the White Oak are expected to maintain high levels of quality while also being competatively priced.  While some of their product does make its way to less-savory taverns and trade organizations, there does not appear to be any underworld dealings directly tied to the White Oak Brewery, which is run by peasant merchants and craftsmen just doing their best to make a good life for themselves and their associates.

Topic: Background Research: Central School of Highest Learning in Mandala
column5: The greatest school of highest learning in Mandala’s capital is known as the Academy of Equanimical Advancement and is a state-sponsored school which accepts the brightest candidates of each house. The academy has many coursework paths ranging from political, scientific, artistic, and everything in between. Students are expected to excel in all areas to maintain their tuition, as the motto of the school rings: “Balance Breeds Sublimity.” This academy notably does not teach the magical arts. Admission to the academy is extremely exclusive and it is expected that those who graduate from the academy will move on to bring great merit to Mandala.

Topic: Background Research: Greatest School of Magical Learning in Mandala
column5: There is no single greatest school of magical learning in Mandala. Or, to be more specific, there are five. Each of the five Great Families maintain schools of magical learning separately, and each claims to be the best at what it does. These schools are typically private and open only to the family itself, as well as any lesser scion families taken under their wing. As one might expect, each of these schools specialize in their respective families’ disciplines of choice. While each school pays lip service to the idea of working together for the betterment of Mandala, it is something of an open secret that the schools are quite competitive against one-another - especially those which teach overlapping disciplines. The names of the five schools are as follows: The Celsus family maintains the Earth’s Grasp Sanctury, the smallest of the five schools and focuses equally on the magical and martial. The Pliny family maintains Blossom Hall, a school that simultaneously acts as something of a hospital. The De Ariete family maintains the Library of Whispered Scrolls, of which very little is known. The Rubra Vena family maintains Frostburn Arena, which additionally acts as an entertainment venue where duels are common. The Benlowe family maintains the Dowser’s Hand, which commonly offers divination services to the public.

Topic: Political Insight: Seriyu de Ariete
column5: Political Inisght: Sir Seriyu  Sir Seriyu is a veteran of the War of Radiance and the former bodyguard for the Mandalan Unity Representative (High Priestess Myriam de Ariete) and has filled in for her politically on a number of occassions. He's a man of few words, preferring to listen and observe, and takes his job extremely seriously. He leads by example, and above all desires peace.  Fiercely loyal to Mandala, he is generally indifferent towards the Effendal, but has a strong disdain for the Drir, which also bleeds over into his feelings towards all of the wild nations and would prefer that none of them have Seats on Unity. Curiously, he does not wear emblems of his knightly order or bring attention to it unnecessarily, merely acknowledging his knighthood title when introducing himself. In his offtime, he prefers the solitude of fishing to most other pursuits

Topic: Investigate Rumors & Healthy Paranoia: the symbol IX has been carved into buildings and trees
column5: As you walk through town, you can certainly find the symbol IX carved into trees and written on the sides of buildings

Topic: Investigate Rumors & Healthy Paranoia: Nameless & Trahazi
column5: As Nameless and the Forbidden faiths are often conflated to be one and the same, though they are quite different, the Trahazi Zodiac being full of demons hiding behind other names is often lumped in together. Without deeper research, there is nothing to prove or disprove overlap between the Nameless and the Trahazi.

Topic: Investigate Rumors & Healthy Paranoia: Nameless & Knights of Mysteries
column5: Last fall, during the time of the governor election, there were rumors circulating that Sir Hieronymous Galrath was a devout follower of the Nameless and was witnessed taking part in dark rites and disappearing with Archivist the Nameless ferryman. There seems to be some truth to these rumors.

Topic: Investigate History: Dietra
column5: Dietra is called "The Temptress", and when depicted or spoken of, she is described as a beautiful, spoiled young woman with the deepest blue eyes, who can charm and convince most beings to her desires. She is the considered to be the child of Esparei and Nero, and sister to Eirie and Ryed, and as such is recognized by both the Celestine Faith and Eirie Faith.

Topic: Investigate History: The First Voice of Chorus
column5: The First Voice of Chorus came into power in 163ch, during a time of great turmoil in the Amalgamation - with the Second War of Crowns reaching it's height, the nation stood on the brink of utter ruin and collapse. The War of Feathers was being fought on the Hastings and Voi borders between the Celestine Empire and Nadine Empire, and the Coatl were taking advantage of the distraction of the kingdoms fighting amongst themselves to hit the villages of De'Fey and Voi, causing even more chaos and division of attention. War was brewing in the north between the Vercrux and their neighboring Vein, made worse by the in-fighting of the kingdoms. Seeing the nation on the knife's edge of collapse and the threat of an even more horrific war between the kingdoms, the College of Cardinals came together to make a decision for the best of their people and the Amalgamation as a whole - they declared the Echoing of Voices and the appointment of the First Voice, who demanded peace (however tentative) between the kingdoms. The names of the Voices are not commonly recorded, but the heroic actions of the First Voice saving the nation and bringing them together once more against their common enemies (the War of Giants began only a couple short years later) are well-known to history for reminding the people and particularly the nobility and royals of what the Amalgamation was founded on - their unification and promise to stand against the darkness. Under their leadership, power was consolidated and focused, and the hard press for the destruction of corrupting forces (including magics that caused additional chaos and trouble in the kingdoms) began.

Topic: Investigate History: Aspect of Murder
column5: Eden is said to have inflicted many curses upon humanity with her considerable power, including age, disease, hunger, jealousy, and *murder*. Like Eden's other Aspects of Death, Murder is not a force to be reasoned with or befriended as an ally, it is a pure embodiment of murder and destruction in benefit of Eden to bring more souls to her realm. And also like the other Aspects, they were all trapped within their respective prisons by the fae Briar and the mages of Theddespari. In the first days of the Returned, the Dagger of Murder was seen in the Oubliette, but in more recent years, it was noticeably missing, as if it had been removed from the Oubliette during the time of the Returned. Assuming the Dagger had been on Tear, it stands to reason that Harvester's freeing of all things imprisioned against their will would have freed Murder, meaning all of Eden's aspects walk freely once more beyond their containment. At the time of it's location within the Oubliette, it was known that the dagger had never been used to harm or commit a murder since the aspect was contained within the blade, as such it's powers and abilities are unknown to anyone.

Topic: Investigate History: Crow
column5: Crow is called the "Soul Messenger" or "Messenger of the Dead", and is one of the known death gods who stands opposed to Eden, but due to her immeasurable strength, he is not always to collect all the souls that ought to come to his realm. With his twin, Raven, they are said to guide the most devout of the Old Ways in creative methods to accomplish what they believe the world needs. As a death god, Crow stands in a unique position of being both living within his wilderness in the spirit realm, while also "dead" - the stories say he was killed by the Hunter with an arrow through his eye. His season is in the fall, and Crow's Day is a common holiday celebrated as the nights grow longer and the living write letters to their loved ones who have passed on, wrapping the letters in shining objects to tempt the crows to carry them across to the realms of death when the veil is thinnest between Adelrune and the realms of death.

Topic: Investigate History: Caught Lightning
column5: For the use in rituals, caught lightning is one of the easier components to put together – a sorcerer must cast 10 mana worth of lighting into a spell crystal

Topic: Investigate History: “The White Serpent”/”skin of a white serpent”
column5: Though lighter colored snakes are not uncommon on Adelrune, to find one of pure white is exceptionally rare as they typically have spotting and coloring to allow them to camoflage themselves. While any breed of snake could plausibly produce a stray white version, they are most likely to appear deep in the swamps of Ackeroniell's Forest within the lawless and wild forests of the Nadine Empire. It is far safer to obtain the skin of a white serpent from traders of esoteric and exotic ritual and spell components than to seek one out yourself.

Topic: Investigate History: previous siege tactics used against Whitehall
column5: a number of tactics have been attempted over the years, but rarely have the sieges been long-lasting or ultimately successful. most often, sieges of Whitehall were used strategically to pin down the soldiers of Whitehall within their walls, while allowing the rest of the Nadine army to move deeper into the Amalgamation. Siege engines are a generally uncommon approach due to the distance they would need to be moved through the Nadine mountains. Sending in diseased corpses is most effective, and while attempts at sapping have been made, little progress or success came from them and the technique was mostly abandoned.

Topic: Investigate Fame: Domni Atius in the Nadine Empire
column5: Domni Atius is the rising star of the Nadine Empire. Gaining an impeccable reputation as a master negotiator after getting everything she could for the Empire at the end of the Towerfall conflict with the Returned.  It’s said she found Baron Paxima Corventine to be a Traitor and had him and the traitorous legions executed by the army she led. On her own initiative, she turned the dragon’s allegiance to stand with the Empress and brought down Whitehall, a task that has not been achievable in 400 years of attempts. She is spoken of as a mythological hero in the Empire, though some have doubts that she’s done all of this on her own. At this point the people are split between wondering what she could possibly do next, or have doubts that someone with standing in a minor house could have achieved all this in such a short amount of time without taking credit from others.

Topic: State of the Court: Whitehall
column5: - Darius made a public appearance to name his successor at Whitehall's Court to right a historical wrong, and to do so named Sir Bereli, Knight of the White Chalice, a Trahazi as his successor - He looked unwell when making the announcement, but of clear mind - The announcement causes an uproar/division within his own ranks, and even his most loyal followers are beginning to turn against him - Former knights of Voi who had moved to Whitehall after the fall of their kingdom are abandoning Whitehall in favor of moving north to Hastings and Vein, following Darius' announcement - a small settlement of Trahazi is being built outside of the walls of Whitehall - anarchy and vandalism is happening within Whitehall, adding to the confusion and distrust

Topic: Spread Through Song: Hearth & Home Festival promoting Trahazi autumn celebrations
column5: 1 - Trailsong (I believe I titled the action 'Trahazi travelling to Whitehall') A travelling/drinking song about the various adventures, trials, and tribulations that a caravan encounters along the way towards a final destination, in this case Whitehall. A lot of improvisation on the nature of the stories in verses - tall tales about freak weather, magnificent creatures, and far too much drinking - with different bards free to craft their own. All of them are flavoured towards being excited to finally reach Whitehall, and might note the toils of the travel eg. being a little haggard, snappy, or even having lost some of the typical Trahazi colorufulness from vardos/clothing ('I rode a hundred miles in a red and gold shirt - now it's just brown and caked in dirt!'). Sung & shared with non-Trahazi as well, meant to be a very inviting opportunity to join together in a round at a bar or campfire.  2 - Hearth and home A sweet, almost lullaby style ballad about celebrating the winter season. Touches on Yule, but also on some common ways Trahazi festivities. Specific things that would be mentioned are building festival pyres scented with spices or fragrant wood, building wreaths, crowns, & circlets out of leaves/vines/flowers and blessing new friends with them, and inviting the local communities to enjoy and share their hearth and homes.

Topic: Rtkre – are the worlds consumed different planes? How did it come to be in the oubliette and why?
column5: As the prophecies say, when the material plane of Adelrune itself meets its end, it will be consumed by Rtkre, called the world-eater, and destined to consume the world when we enter the age of the Great Death when entropy overcomes all. Rtkre is not contained within the Oubliette and has never been.

Topic: create a bronze alloy using copper and silver to replace iron?
column5: This is research crafting and should be submitted to the Rules committee in the way that a new spell or crafting recipe would be submitted, rather than an open-ended research submission to the story team

Topic: search libraries and collections for old fencing/fighting manuals and books on self-defense
column5: this is not something that would require a research action, as it's already covered in the rulebook with the skill “Military Drill”. Please let me know if you would like to change your research question to a different topic related to the lores: Celestine Empire, Dace, or Law

Topic: how can a ritual nullify/modify a curse
column5: Ambrosius' journal is only a research object rather than an IC document, so there is no way to collect the information about “what the book says” about any particular topic. A curse is merely a high-level enchantment or just a ritual itself, so it can be countered or affected by other rituals to redirect it or adjust the outcome. Typically this requires the counter ritual to happen before the initial ritual takes full effect

Topic: how do blood curses affect someone's soul?
column5: Ambrosius' journal is only a research object rather than an IC document, so there is no way to collect the information about “what the book says” about any particular topic or rituals included in the text. Blood curses generally are focused on the physical body and living will of the person, rather than directly affecting one's soul. If it is a ritual that affects the soul, it is more likely to be a combination ritual involving summoning magic.

Topic: Panther – how are they worshipped/revered? Common rites? Recent events/historical figures?
column5: Called "The Hidden," for those who do not know her so well, Panther is able to disappear in the shadows of the forests, where she is unassuming to those who might come for her. For those who do know her, Panther's spots and true self can be seen for those who seek to know her. The most fierce, independent women of the Old Ways find themselves drawn to Panther, relying on their intuition and ability to be comfortable in their own skin. Panther is known for teaching confidence in the ability of one self, and self-reliance as they generally carry a solitary nature and are difficult to get close to, but more importantly she teaches patience, and knowing when to strike at the right moment to maximize one's power and striking force. Clever and strategic, it's no wonder she has a closer connection to Veyn than any of the other gods, and often serves as companion with her love of the water. Panther was known to be asleep for centuries, resting in Veyn's healing waters after an altercation with the Spirit Wyrm, and only recently was reawoken by the Returned in 7AR. Rites typically associated with Panther are Life and War. While there are not holidays specifically for Panther, as she was asleep for so long and went with less worship during that time, children often invoke her name while playing hide-and-seek games.

Topic: research: who were the most notable/key figures in the formation of the crusades? Which of these were most important of the formation of the church of chorus
Destiny & Chaos: how are these gods worshipped & origin legends
column5: It is said that in the beginning there was only Destiny and Chaos, twins and siblings in the vast and infinite void of space. Desiring more, Destiny wove the strands of the world out of Chaos and created the Titans as she created the world – the land, the water, the air, and the fire. In seeing that Destiny's world was too perfect, Chaos imparted their own children upon the world and gave birth to the seasons, and the Phoenix to protect them. While Chaos is not directly worshipped by the Celestine faith (much like the Titans), they are still revered as a key figure in their origin story of the world. Destiny, however, is still one of the dominantly worshipped gods of the Celestines and even has a holy text named in her honor – the Book of Destiny – and one of the greatest temples – the Temple of Destiny, which was sacked by the Gael during the Northern War. True followers of Destiny understand that all actions must be thought out at length through meditation until their Destiny is revealed, when actions must be delivered swiftly with devotion. All actions of prophecy and the gods relation to it (such as Ceileen, the blind oracle) find their origins in Destiny and her weaving of the world and order out of chaos.

Topic: what gods are rumored to have been killed by Arezen “the giant”/”the cruel”? Description and why is he called the cruel?
column5: Arezen has been described as a giant the size of a mountain who takes pleasure in causing harm to others. The names of the gods he has killed have long been lost to time, but an epithet to one - “the forest troll” is mentioned in an ancient document, killed without remorse by Arezen while wielding Cinnorast.

Topic: are there any hints or evidence of a Theddespari tower in the golden realms?
column5: While this is not truly something that can be found in a book within a library, no, there is no evidence of a Theddespari tower in the Golden Realms.

Topic: what is the situation regarding the church of chorus in Cestral?
column5: for current situations and prominent figures, that information is best gathered through various influence actions. If you would like to re-word your question towards a focus of history that can be found in a book, I would be happy to answer it

Topic: research: myths about the brothers Raven & Crow
Coatl myths about unicorns? Demonic unicorns?
column5: Since the celestials first came to Adelrune from the Golden Realms, there have been legends and rumors of unicorns - the result of a celestial being merging with and inhabiting the body of a horse when it comes to Adelrune. They're extremely rare, and impossible to gauge when and where they will appear. In the Coatl legends of the Seven Steeds that could traverse the planes, it is believed that the Celestial steed was likely a winged-unicorn.

Topic: investigate history: the storytellers
column5: There is no written history about the Storytellers because they prefer oral tradition and the freedom of creativity over being bound by corrupt leaders and the laws of kings. In some records, they are called the Order of Broken Pages who stand in opposition to the Loremaster's Guild who they believe to be corrupt and power hungry and who with their manipulation of kings, control the people of the world (they are fully in support of freewill and allowing people to make their own mistakes rather than trying to control the masses). Often in times of major revolt, there are reports of people wearing patchwork clothing supporting the people and the overthrowing of tyrany.

Topic: Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: Nadine will kill Trahazi in Whitehall
column5: If they get in the way, it is possible. Ultimately it will depend on the actions of the Trahazi.

Topic: Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: Whitehall will attack Nadine
column5: Whitehall doesn't have the numbers or ability to launch an offensive attack against the Nadine. From it's inception, Whitehall was built as a defensive fortress.

Topic: Military Insight: Whitehall
column5: 9,000 soldiers, 350 spies, 60 assassins, 2,000 Voi Guards, 100 White Chalice Knights (does not include the soldiers who arrived with Dame Lizette Flavius-Vercrux in the fall)

Topic: Military Insight: Nadine forces attacking Whitehall
column5: The Nadine continue to launch small skirmishes and attacks, but are dominantly still harrassing Whitehall rather than ammassing a force on their border. With so many fleeing Whitehall following the appointment of Sir Bireli as Prince, the Nadine have fallen back to allow those forces to leave and dissert their posts (any organized attack at the moment would be counter-productive in unifying and rallying those departing soldiers back to the cause).

Topic: State of the Court: Non-Returned of Port Frey
column5: In general, the town is struggling all around. The nobles in particular are apprehensive without the protections guaranteed under the Rules of Society. Soldiers seem to be generally fine and expect to have work any day now. Merchants on the whole are not doing great, though the Bank of the Dead has stabilized issues in the merchant's district somewhat. Peasants not involved in gangs or underworld organizations are taking the brunt of the harm and are being heavily extorted with protection rackets run by the gangs in every district except the merchant district. Peasants involved in the gangs, however, are doing great and loving the lawlessness and ability to run wild that they've had for the the last six months.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Whispering Ghosts
column5: the Whispering Ghosts are one of the weaker gangs, who took a big hit after the Battle of Edge. They've never recovered from the situation when the Whispering Ghost mercenary crew lost their charter after they sided with the Blood Rose & Father Lysander during the War of Radiance. They made multiple attempts to regain their status, but continued to fail in those attempts. Now, too small of a gang to be highwaymen or a physical challenge, they're focused on being information brokers who will run instead of fight. They act as informants and spies, and are leveraging what power they do have now that there is no longer a guard in town.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Trina Anhavia got too close to Unity spy shapeshifter
column5: It took some digging and heavy investigation to question all of the townsfolk who live in the part of town that Trina Anhavia was last seen, but this rumor is proven definitively false. An older woman who doesn't sleep well at night was reluctant to admit she had been sitting at her window in the dark the night Trina Anhavia was killed, and she'd seen the woman watching a group of people in dark robes performing some sort of religious ritual. The group had apparently known she was there, having followed one of their members, and as their ceremony came to a conclusion, they grabbed Trina and sacrificed her there with no regard for those who might see.

Topic: Beastiary Analysis: Zegimmon
column5: Zegimmon is unique among wyrms as a nightmare entity, as it can take any form. Most often what is seen when one glimpses Zegimmon is truly the destruction wrought by the beast. It is known in stories as the beast that consumes dreams and excretes nightmares. Zegimmon is one of the few creatures in the world who can wholly and completely destroy something because it can destroy the very memory of a being by consuming it.

Topic: Investigate History: Beleathru's Regalia
column5: In searching for information about Bel'e'athru's regalia, an old, well-worn journal is discovered in the Guild of Academics. It is over-stuffed with journal entries, notes, letters, and various other pages sewn into it's pages so they are not lost.   Two pages of the journal are applicable to what you seek: First, an entry that was seemingly updated a number of times as new information came to light. Each successive sentence of update in different colored ink or scribbled in a quicker or slower hand as if written at different times. This page also has a number of pages and articles torn from the Assassin and attached along the edge of the page to keep them with this particular entry.  *Tick tock.*  *The Wheel has spoken again. A constant reminder of what is coming. What we must be doing. The Sleeping Demon is looking for his regalia. He cannot be permitted to find it before we do... if he does, there will be no war to fight... the world will be lost before the war even begins.*  *His Crown – ~~Location unknown.~~ Visions have been had. Confirmed location: a vault in Dace. Confirmed: the crown is in Star's possession – Dace has sided with the demons, the Dark Future's warning of Dace's possession by the demons came to pass. Curse Sychariel and the Dacians who did not warn their people that this would happen. We've known this was coming for _three years_.*  *His Scepter – Confirmed Location: Locked deep in the pits of the Iron Chalice vault beneath Antioc. C'thon Darkweaver sieges the city in order to infiltrate the vaults on behalf of his master. Syltane has refused Aislynn's request for access. Blessed are the clever little foxes, for it is now in safe hands and out of reach of those who continue to seek it. It will remain in the most unexpected of hands and unexpected of places so none will find it before the time comes. With the ritual provided by Antares, it will be used in the battle to hold back that which will try to escape when we open the Oubliette to seal away the Sleeping Demon – I trust Victoria will do well in her role to perform this ritual.*  *His Throne – Confirmed: Broken into three pieces and scattered upon his presumed death in the age of Zyte. Confirmed: Star has collected one piece from the mermaid city near the Coatl lands. Confirmed: One piece in our possession, no reliable information on where or how we recovered it, but it is safe in our vault. Third piece still unaccounted for. Post-war report: the piece we possess poses no direct threat, but will remain in the Vault in the Guild of Academics. Third piece never recovered or accounted for.*

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Bellus is searching for Beleathru's throne
column5: truth. as publicly stated by Ritari Rom in open Court in September, he (and possibly other Effendal) have given at least one piece of the throne to Prince Bellus

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines/Healthy Paranoia: Bellus
column5: While he typically spends most of his time in in Redemption, he's been based out of Port Frey in recent months. Bellus' true vice is unknown, but it well-known that he favors visiting houses of ill-repute on his rare ventures beyond his ship while he's in Port Frey. Gambling halls, bars, and pleasure houses alongside succubi and incubi are all places he's visited, but never in the same form that he shows around the Returned.

Topic: Investigate Enemies: Bellus
column5: Bellus' enemies include the Harbingers of the Crescent Sun, C'thon Darkweaver and his forces, Arasimiel and his forces, and the Paladins of Radiance.

Topic: Investigate History: eternal conflict between demons & celestials
column5: The eternal conflict is one of ideology, wherein while many have their theories as to the final outcome and resolution, few things are certain to them. The first, is that when one side wins, their realms will merge. This can either precede or not occur until the endtime, which is coming and will occur. In the endtime, all the realms will merge, and all demons and celestials know that it is hugely important to win for that point. While it is commonly thought that nothing comes after, the hope that something does, is often strengthened by the idea that the winner of the eternal conflict will somehow modify or affect whatever is to come next. And while all demons and celestials know they will never get to see this, the sheer hope of their wills and ideal concepts of existence moving forward is more than enough to motivate. It is typically of the highest priority of demons and celestials to kill any fallen or penitent members of their kind, so as to prevent them from actually putting a point on the scoreboard of the enemy team.

Topic: Investigate History: demonic hierarchy (princes, kings, etc)
column5: Ultimately, the "demonic hierarchy" falls to the simplest phrase: Might Makes Right. However, some demons just naturally have more power than others and are more attuned to/in-tune with the Great Conflict. As such, these demons are typically the ones who can use the Regalia and would have reason to (unlike demon gods who draw their power from their divinity rather than the Abyss). There is no set "hierarchy" of monarch-prince-etc, and it is wholly unrelated to Greater Demons/Demon Lords of Breach cities. Titles and honorifics are self-claimed denoting their importance, such as (the aspiring) Abyssal Monarch Mehs, (former) Prince Bellus, (former) Abyssal Monarch Bel'e'athru.

Topic: Political Insight: Erontu
column5: Erontu was not interested in filling the position of Unity Seat for the Gael, but only took up the position because as the leader of one of the three Gael tribes on Tear he refused to let the Banner of the Black Wolf continue to dominate peace negotiations and treaties on behalf of his tribe following the War of Radiance. As such, he hates his job and leaves most of the day-to-day work on Caeli Oakenthorn's desk. He's extremely flattering in his speech, including the insults he throws, which regularly throws people off who don't expect such words from a Gael. In the majority of his Unity voting, his focus is first and foremost the Gael, and then with the Effendal, who he considers his allies. Beyond that, if Caeli doesn't persuade him on a particular path he will always vote with the majority, regardless of what measure is being discussed. Outside of his duties, his favored hobbies include watching feats of strength and the grafting of trees. Though his tribe and the other Gael on Tear are settled comfortably in Effendal lands, he desires to return his people to the Gael forest. He seems to desire peace and has held a tenuous peace with the other Gael tribes on Tear, and in recent months he has been seen taking trips to look out at the ocean for long periods of time.

Topic: Investigate History: stories of White Stag tied to "rebirth", renewal, change
column5: Stories of Old Ways are not typically written down, as the Old Ways is a oral tradition.

Topic: Eavesdrop: are Erontu's actions related to the sea, Mist Faith, or Mist-Maddened?
column5: Erontu does not employ servants, so this action yields no information.

Topic: what goes into making another clinic?
column5: Each clinic costs: 25 economic influence for the wooden structure (50 for stone), 1 foreman to run the clinic, 10 workers to do the menial tasks/cleaning, 25 healers to look after patients.  currently there are clinics in: The Returned District/Town Center, and the Slums. the other districts that could use clinics are: the Docks, the Merchant District, Embassy District, the Walls

Topic: Political Insight: Caeli Oakenthorn
column5: One of the original founders and creators of the Unity Parliament, Caeli's personal ties connect her to both the Gael and the Vicaul, and so she plays proxy to both seats, handling the paperwork and communications for the representatives who prefer not to have those responsibilities. Her allegiances, while expected to be for the Vicaul and Gael first, have been dominantly for the best benefit of Solace as a whole. Whether that meant aligning with the Effendal and helping them to cure the Blood Rage, warning the Cestral to prepare for the incoming Vicaul invasion (and eventually negotiating the marriage alliances between the Vicaul and Cestral), standing with the White Chalice during the War of Radiance, attending both coronations and funerals for notable Celestine and Antioc leadership, and penning the treaty to end the Northern War, Caeli has always called for peace and cooperation between the nations. One of the few stances she holds a hard line on is association with demons, and she has never wavered from calling out both Mist Faith and Trahazi for trying to disguise their worship of demons by calling them by different names.

Topic: State of the Court: Unity
column5: For every person trying to do their job, there is someone afflicted by the fae, and regardless of affliction, there are outside spies and saboteurs halting any paperwork from proceeding, which has brought the continued review of Unity policies and daily functions to a grounding halt.

Topic: State of the Court: Nadine
column5: - relations between the Nadine and the dragon Etyr are much better than they have been, and goverment officials have been making the journey to Concerti to meet with him, and not all of them have gotten burnt to a crisp - sabateurs (Amalgamation scouts) were caught attempting to steal the Darius dagger - many of the scouts were killed within the cathedral, but the dagger is still intact and the Blood Cauldron has apprehended one of the saboteurs, a Churchwood Brotherhood member, bringing up talk and gossip of a conspiracy from within the court of Hastings

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Caeli Oakenthorn is obstructing Trahazi's position in Unity and/or the Rules of Society
column5: There is nothing suggesting this is the case. Caeli has no ability to influence the Rules of Society, and the Trahazi are not seeking to join the Rules of Society. Additionally, the Trahazi have never petitioned to join Unity so there has not been anything to obstruct.

Topic: Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: Nameless Faith & Dacians in Port Frey
column5: REFUND

Topic: Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: Archivist the Nameless Ferryman
column5: Most tales of ferrymen are considered more legend than rumor but a new ferryman has been seen around town in the last year. Dressed in red with odd runes on his face, this ferryman seems to have an intense stare. While it is not confirmed they say his realm is a library and he hungers for stories. He will linger around town after returning people on the off chance he may hear one. Still those that say they have seen him report that maybe he is a bit too eager, like a predator facing down its prey.

Topic: Investigate History: artifacts of Panther
column5: There are no known artifacts related to Panther.

Topic: Political Insight: realtionship of House Vallaro and House Ariadne
Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: is Kiesh killing clergy on Princess Arielle's orders?
column5: there is no evidence that Kiesh has been involved in any nefarious assassinations of Church clergy, though he has been having an excessive number of secret meetings as of late

Topic: Investigate History: golden incense (where to find the two trees mentioned in the cleansing ritual)
Investigate History: the demon god of disease killed by Lazarous
column5: Hexameran, the Living Disease, is the demon god of disease. The son of Ameran, he devourered Aien, gaining the name Flesh Eater for such a horrific act perpetrated against the Celestial knight. Known for the spreading of diseases and feeding on the destruction it brought to the world, he met his match in Lazarus who finally killed the demon, sending it back to the Abyss.

Topic: Investigate History: ghost ship near War's End
Eavesdrop: Sir Bireli
column5: Sir Bireli is new to the life of being a Prince and having servants, so his are all very new to their positions as well, though his squire hsa been with him for some time. Much of what they chatter about is that he seems as if he is trying to carry the weight of the world on his shoulders. He had known there would be backlash from the announcement of his appointment to Prince, he was a Trahazi-born who grew up in Whitehall after all - he was accustomed to the hate and scorn that had been directed at him since he was a child. He had not been prepared for just HOW bad it was, leaving him second guessing everything including his appointment, because he has already failed his people when over a quarter of the populace of Whitehall walked out practically overnight.

Topic: Investigate Political Stance: Sir Bireli's feeling about the Trahazi resettling Whitehall
column5: Sir Bireli is very pro-Trahazi resettling the lands of Whitehall. He understands the history and nuance of the conflict and has faced the prejudice of his history since he was a child. He genuinely wants to do right by both of his people.

Topic: Investigate Scandal: Sir Bireli
column5: There are no skeletons in Bireli's closet, the only scandal attributed to his name is the current conflict of his appointment to be Prince of Whitehall, while not having the seniority, connections, command experience, or tactical aptitude that the soldiers of Whitehall expect from their future ruler.

Topic: Investigate Criminal History & Healthy Paranoia: Sir Bireli
column5: Sir Bireli seems to be a good person with no criminal history. The worst that can be said for the guy other than his inexperience, is some youthful rebellion during his days as a squire - understandably given his situation, he had a lot of teenage rage, but it was tempered through being able to redirect the negative energy into excess physical training and commiting to his duties as a squire and eventually a knight. He is exceptional in combat and very skilled, which he often attributes to the extra training he had as a teenager.

Topic: Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: author of the Assassin article "heir announced"
column5: It is impossible to discover the authors of articles in the Assassin, even in rumors.

Topic: Investigate History: Myre
column5: As you are doing this spend before we are willing or ready to release more information about Myre, there is little to no information available, as Myre was an ancient, lost settlement. 138t - Myre Tower Rebels in "The War of Poisons" 152t - Myre Tower wins its independance.  War of poisons ends. 155t - The Citystate of Myre is created (Formerly an Imperial Outpost.) 180t -The Citystate of Myre is destroyed by the Nadine Empire in "the Battle of Broken Wills."

Topic: Investigate History: Esparei
column5: Esparei, the "Beautiful Bird" or "The Mother of Birds," is the child of Tempest and Eur. She's called the goddess of Beauty and Birds, and in art is typically depicted as a beautiful, winged elf. She's considered to be the mother of Eirie, Dietra, and Ryed, and is one of the few Celestine gods who does not have a Celestine city or fortress named after her. Which is not to say there is no city named for her, as Theddespari was dedicated to her and her beauty.

Topic: Investigate History: Verody
column5: REFUND

Topic: Investigate History: Bear
column5: Bear is called the Heart of the Hearth. Little is known or recorded about this Old Ways god as she was one of the earliest gods to go to sleep in a prehistoric age, but she was awakened by the Returned in the summer of 7ar. She is known to be a fierce protector, and the stories tell that she left lasting wounds on both Haelo and Nicodemus for the harm done to those closest to her.

Topic: Investigate History: Noren
column5: Noren, called the Wizard, was formerly the God of Spells. He was known to take on the humanoid form of a blue-eyed elven wizard in ages long past. His hatred of red dragons led to an ages-long conflict in which he ultimately lost his divinity to the red dragon Etyr. Since their final fight, which left both greviously wounded, Noren retreated to his territory in the Vicaul lands and laid down to sleep, taking on the shape of a great mountain in his rest. Prior to the Mists falling across Adelrune, there were no reports of the mountain waking nor Noren appearing, but one can suspect that when he does, he will seek revenge against Etyr.

Topic: research: Lion
Can the waters of the River Styx be used to create the black sands for the hourglass? what are common uses for this (rituals/spells)? can it be used to weaken fae?
column5: (for clarity, the River in Eden's realm is not called the River Styx as that is a real-world reference to Greek mythology) The waters of the river in Eden's realm of death does not contribute to the potency of the black sands. While there is black sand on the beaches, it is not THE Black Sands, though it is a component in making them, which then must be ritually charged (often in the Sacred Urn) to become THE Black Sands and be useful in rituals and for other purposes. Many of the known uses for the Black Sands are the various rituals in the Book of Nox and Book of Ig, including the ritual to Disenchant the Returned.

Topic: can shimmers go back to Bastion? do they have celestial blood?
column5: As with any being, Shimmers cannot return to Bastion once they leave, except through an open door. Shimmers are minor beings, much like the celestial-equivalent of imps, and as such do not have true *celestial blood* as a proper celestial being would. They are unable to procreate on Adelrune, as the act requires a great quantity of power from the Golden Realms.

Topic: looking into a ritual to convene or contact the Council of Elders? (use the tarot card, use mist orb, zodiac rite)
column5: any of the three ideas you suggested are potentially viable approaches, they would require actual experimentation to do so. in the case of the Mist Orb, you would need to have an idea of where the caravans are in the Mists and send scouts out with the orbs to reach each individual caravan

Topic: Rown and his relationship with Dawn and Dusk
column5: Rown is a Dawn fae, and the god of animals. Comparative to the other fae, he is generally considered stronger because he has the support of divine magic and his divinity, much like Shenanigans did as the God of Luck. However, he rarely exercises this strength as he does not spend time among other fae or in Paradox as the Malcontent do, in fact he is rarely seen with any thinking creatures, much preferring the isolated forests and time spent with animals

Topic: rewoven tapestry - what might happen if we cast this ritual on Theddespari? how might we determine if there is a rift?
column5: There was once a well-known rift in the Witherwood, ripped open by the Witch of the Witherwood to recover her mentor Chimerubim. Seeing the horror she wrought, she was expelled from Effendal society and had her name removed from history, so she is only known as the Witch of the Witherwood, while Chimerubim took on a fate worse than death to atone for her actions - he placed himself within the rift to hold it shut to the best of his ability. After two hundred years, with his soul torn to shreds and tatters, and with nothing left to hold it shut, Returned began to take over in the gateway to hold it shut. Many Returned sought methods to seal the rift, each more horrific than the last, but finally the Re-Woven Tapestry was discovered with the recovery of the Book of Ig. The Returned enacted this ritual and successfully sealed the rift in February 10ar. Following this sealing, there is no rift near Theddespari, and thus it would have no effect if completed.

Topic: prisons for aspects of Eden - how were they made, if they were released by Harvester, can they be rejailed in the same prisons? cam we communicate with the aspects while they're imprisoned?
column5: The initial containers for the Aspects of Eden were created by Briar and the Theddespari mages, thus there is no information to be found in how they were created. The Aspects are not sentient beings that can be communicate with, they are forces of nature and death. If one was to attempt to recapture an Aspect, they would need to experiment with both the container and the Aspect present at the time, which is a particularly dangerous concept when faced with the Aspects of Time, Pestilence, and Murder.

Topic: aspects of eden: how to communicate without dying? are they intelligent and capable of conversation?
column5: While the Aspects are sentient and capable of understanding when spoken to, they themselves do not communicate in return. They are sheer forces of will and nature, placed on Adelrune to serve Eden in the way she created them to - collecting souls by spreading their aspects upon humanity.

Topic: lights over Theddespari - what magic is it? divine, fae, dragon, etc? artifacts/myths?
column5: The lights over Theddespari are not designatable by any particular type of magic or being. The lights are the culmination and reaction of dozens of creatures of different abilities being released all at once, fighting one another and against the Theddespari mages who were working to contain, kill, or expell each of the beings in turn.

Topic: warding or other school of magic to imbue intelligible speach for tweet tweet
column5: while there are some spells that might be helpful (such as Comprehend Mortal Languages in Divination), in general spells are less effective since she speaks the common tongue. to make Tweet Tweet more intelligible, the most straight-forward approach would be something like trading a fae boon, or gaining the favor of a fae (or potentially a curse), however this will be a permanent effect and not something that would be able to turn on and off when they want to.

Topic: myths of white stag
why are some full fae more powerful than others?
column5: Fae are generally on a similar power level to other fae, to keep within the balance, however the amount of power they *display* can vary greatly on their personality differences, and outside forces. For example, the fae Olya openly displayed her power to a point that the Iron Chalice took great measures to contain her power, and imprisoned her, leaving her exceptionally weakened when she was eventually freed. Other fae, such as Rown and Shenanigans had significantly more power than the average fae because they have the backing of divinity as gods. The Malcontent appear to have more power because they are a consolidated group who has stolen divine power (though not divinity itself), whereas the Dawn are much more scattered and their attentions spread thin as they are the caretakers for the order of time and the balance of the world.

Topic: why is it called "the mist of myre", how is it related to the fae? is there dream involved?
column5: The Mists of Myre is the name the Assassin and general humanity has given to the black mists that surround the area that is Castle Myre. The Mists were caused by the Malcontent when a wish was granted to raise Myre from its ruins. Being fae in origin, it is highly unlikely that dream magic be involved in any form, as fae do not dream.

Topic: Mists of Mandala and the Mists of Myre relationship? how does the mist orb effect the dark mists? what type of ritual can create dark mist?
column5: The dark Mists that covered the former area of Ad Decimum and the region of Mandala, as well as up into the Gael lands, are in fact now being called the Mists of Myre by the Assassin and is the generally accepted term to differentiate it from the normal Mists that blanket the rest of the world. The orb seemingly has no effect to cut through the dark mists, as that news was sent back from Mandala with the ship that returned to Port Frey.

Topic: are the Mists of Myre related to the dragonkin mist from Friday night? does it affect the spirit/soul, or mind?
column5: Yes, the Mists of Myre seem related to the Mists that abducted the dragonkin as they are in the same region as to where the dragonkin were taken. There is no evidence the Mists are related to the soul or mind, one would need to experiment directly with the Mists of Myre to determine anything more about them, as they are not a previously seen phenomenon and thus nothing will be written about them.

Topic: where did Fallon go after the priesthood? are there any mentions of the wood sword artifact? how did Fallon die?
column5: After being chased out of the monestary by bounty hunters, Dallon stopped going by his name and instead was called the Wooden Sword. He became a drunk who was out for revenge, determined to kill those responsible for his child's death, and in doing so became the world's greatest lockpick. Eventually, he went looking for a final resting place for the sword and never returned.

Topic: are there any locations on Tear known to be sacred or significant to the Mandalan dragons?
column5: There are no locations sacred or significant to the Mandalan dragons or faith on the island of Tear. The only partly-relevant location would be War's End, but there was little attachment of faith to the location.

Topic: who are the protectors of the realms against the wyrms? how is the nothingness/void different? what are the reprecussions of meluvi achieving their ultimate goal?
column5: The inhabitants of the realms are those who protect them from the wyrms (demons, celestials, etc), but not humans or effendal. Nothingness and the Void are two terms for the same thing.

Topic: how do we defend against wyrms? what are the consequences of destroying wyrms? how is a wyrm destroyed?
column5: The Great Wyrms cannot be destroyed, but their offspring can. As such, there is no documentation on this topic, beyond prophecy and the broadly accepted truth of Great Wyrms - that in the end, after they have devoured the realms, they will then devour themselves as the final action of reality.

Topic: what is the void? where is it?
column5: The Void is the space between the Spirit Realm and the Abyss. There is no magic truly related to the Void (except by naming used to envoke that sense of void).

Topic: other than funerals, what are the common customs that can be found among saek caravans when they gather, such as weddings, childbirth, anniversaries, holidays, etc. what kinds of foods can be found when the Saek gather? if the breach is virtually devoid of life, where/how do the Saek find food?
column5: As each of the Saek caravans travels independently of one another, their traditions vary wildly from caravan to caravan, as will their food options dependent on where they are in the Breach at any given time of year. Typically, however, celebrations are much less formal among the Saek than among other nations - there is no formal wedding ceremony, as committing to another or a caravan is a deeply personal act and each such event will be tailored to those involved based on the resources available to them at the time. Food again varies widely and demonstrates why their caches are so important - to leave behind dried dates or figs, small game jerky, and other non-perishables that are gathered in areas and times of "plenty" such as the rare oasis, traded for in Redemption, or stolen from the demon cities or demon bands. Military rations from the Unity army are also highly sought after for non-perishables from distant shores in recent years, as many were left behind to the Saek who were assisting with running supply lines during the Second Crusade.

Topic: can the Lazarus ritual be modified to affect a different humor? (ie fae instead of demon)
column5: the Lazarus ritual is not meant to depower, rather it is focused on the blood of immortals in the search for longevity and immortality. For general concepts of depowering individuals, your best approach would be to explore the Book of Ig.

Topic: are there applications for the pearl of a mermaid? is it valuable?
column5: A mermaid's pearl is merely a token of affection or gratitude from a mermaid. While it has no intrinsic value in itself, it can theoretically be used to sway favor with an unfamiliar mermaid or mermaid faction as proof that you are a friend to the mermaids.

Topic: can a soul in a soul gem be re-tethered? can it be moved around or refocused?
column5: A mortal soul once trapped in a soul gem cannot be released from the gem except by the most powerful of magics (often this would require divine intervention or something equally as powerful), and has been cut off from the spirit realm. While some greater creatures (like fae) can manifest a physical form around their freshly released soul, this is not generally possible for most beings. Hypothetically, with a complex summoning ritual, it might be possible to move a soul from from one soul gem to another, but  that's generally seen as unnecessary when one can just take and use the soul gem as it is, unless the soul gem has been damaged, at which point the state of the soul would be the bigger question.

Topic: what human betrayal/who was it that led to yashi's rage?
column5: With Yashi's City of Salt having been located on Tear many many years ago, it was ultimately the Coatl of Tear (Wolf and Snake bands) who destroyed the city and killed all of the people within it. This act left a lasting mark upon Yashi, who previously had given many gifts to humans, and developed into an anger towards humanity for not appreciating and even fully destroying what he had given them.

Topic: Political Insight: Drir Unity Rep
column5: The Vessel is of the Peleset and is generally more neutral in relation to the other Drir families.

Topic: Beastial Analysis: Antioc Murder Donut Monster
Underworld Insight: Bellus
column5: Bellus is most well known for his underworld organization, Brimstone's Retribution, but more than that it's an open secret that he runs the underworld within Redemption. Unlike other demons which have waged war and sought the destruction of the city itself throughout history, Bellus' focus within Redemption is corrupting the celestial and celestial-blooded who have called it home, with this subtle long-term play, he can typically go undetected for quite some time. Across the world, it's likely that he has some level of influence in nearly every gang, mercenary company, and smugglers group - whether that is actual agents within the organization, or deals with members of the organizations. There are a number of titles he is known by: The Many-Faced Father, the Maker of Monarchs, and The Corrupter. A common story that he is known to tell is that the reason he is called "The Corrupter" is that in the celestial language, this word is the same as the Demonic language word for merchant.

Topic: Inv. Criminal History: Bellus
column5: During this investigation, one of your spies was captured. Rather than killing him, however, when Bellus' followers found out what information they were seeking, they were more than happy to share with him the following information: if something is illegal somewhere, Bellus has absolutely broken that law in that place at some point in history. "All the crimes, everywhere," they declared with glee before getting your spy shit-faced drunk and sending him on his way with an advert in hand for you to receive. It's a business flyer with the slogan: "Looking for information? Come make me a deal!"

Topic: Eavesdrop: Bellus
column5: Look, Zagni. I'm just saying that this ain't normal. Things are going TOO well around here. "What do you mean? This is great! Did you see the new rooms we got upgraded to? They've got proper beds and everything!" "That's what I mean. He's been like this for months now, but I don't trust it. He's not the *worst* demon to be working for, but he's still a demon! And this is weird, even for him." "You're crazy. We've got great food, new lodgings, he even gave his circle of friends all new fancy jewelry. And our work hours are shorter! You can't be complaining about having more time off to relax and do what we want, six months ago you were complaining that you needed a vacation!" "Exactly. Six months ago we wouldn't be able to even be having this conversation without both of us getting punished for it. It's like he's trying to get everyone to like him or something. It's weird." "I *do* like him! You're just being paranoid." "We'll see, Zagni, we'll see."

Topic: Political Insight: House Ire'enil
column5: House Ire'enil makes up a significant portion of the Council of Elders within Redemption. While there is a tenuous balance - if someone is an Elder they are to be acting in the best interest of Redemption, not solely for the interests of the Ire'enil - it is known that no Ire'enil ascends to leadership in Redemption without the backing and the influence of House as a whole. Addtionally, the Ire'enil leadership will usually have the deciding say when making judgements about Effendal matters. The Ire'enil are closely intertwined to the Redemption Council, partially out of mutual benefits and partially out of fear of what might happen if the two groups - Ire'enil and Redemption - become too distinctly separated. Having a distinct subclass of citizenship with the Ire'enil has yet to cause an issue for Redemption, but it is well-known that they are their own separate entity with additional Effendal traditions that the humans and others of Redemption do not abide by or are privy to. Ultimately, House Ire'enil has the largest voice of any group within Redemption, including the non-Effendal Redeemed, due to their being the founding race of Redemption and their longevity.

Topic: Follow the Money: Bellus
column5: Bellus has a multitude of money coming in from every conceivable source - as the kingpin corrupter, he has ties to every gang and smuggler group, and every major economy in the world. In recent months, his spending has been funneled heavily through his connections in the ports of Cole and Cestral to buy dozens of ships. Notably, there is no evidence he has been purchasing anything from Dace during that time.

Topic: Inv. History: Arrow of True Form
column5: These particular arrows were crafted by the White Chalice during the War of Faces to face Meretrix, who led the shapeshifters against the human nations. Numerous arrows like it have been crafted throughout history as an arrow of true form can be used to revert a shapeshifter to their original form and stop them from shapeshifting for a duration of time.

Topic: Investigate History: King Myaeous
column5: The original King and Founder of Castle Myre, he is the father of the Smiling Prince who currently rules Myre. In the earlier era of Myre, it is said that he became obsessed with his treasures, and to protect them, he had an extensive and complex labyrinth built beneath the city, full of traps and dead ends for would-be-theives to get lost in. Rumored to have been among the first Returned in the Age of Adelrune, historical legends say he was undefeatable in combat, but it is also said that an Effendal hunter dropped him naught but with two arrows. Legends once more claim he was a extremely talented dream mage, but there is no evidence to the validity of this claim.

Topic: Investigate History: Xolo
column5: The namesake of the Ko’aat, Ko'aat Xolas was a feathered serpent like dragon who found amusement in the fledgling off-branch society of the Gael who had found wanderlust and showed a deep appreciation for the bold-spirited horses of the plains he frequently patrolled. His amusement soon grew to a fondness for their ingenuity, ferocity, loyalty, and cuisine. Ko’aat Xolas extended his protection to these peoples and facilitated their growth by teaching them how to ride and blood bond with their horses. When resources became scarce, the dragon would lead them to greener pastures using his vantage point in the sky to find ideal routes. The phrase “follow the vibrant river” became well known among the bands, as seeing the colorful plumage of the black dragon soaring in the skies above was a clear indicator that Ko’aat Xolas was showing his favor to a band by leading them to fresh water or better food sources. In time, Ko’aat Xolas would become an integral part of the Ko’aat culture and the Ko’aat culture would become integral to him, but as time passed, The Vibrant River, Ko’aat Xolas, became less and less frequent a visitor until one day, he ceased to visit all together.

Topic: Investigate History: Dragons or powerful beings who claim Port Frey so other dragons don't compete for the territory
column5: Until very recently, Port Frey and the southern portion of Tear were the terrirtory of an ancient silver (blue/white) dragon called Tyrvennical. With the approach of the Mists covering the island, Tyrvennical took a single Effendal and left Port Frey to gather draconic forces to fight and destroy the Clocktower. He has not been heard from since it's fall, and though the territory is known to be blue/white dragon territory, there is no dragon of note holding claim to the land of Port Frey at this time.

Topic: Investigate History: Order of Broken Pages
column5: There is no written history about the Storytellers because they prefer oral tradition and the freedom of creativity over being bound by corrupt leaders and the laws of kings. In some records, they are called the Order of Broken Pages who stand in opposition to the Loremaster's Guild who they believe to be corrupt and power hungry and who with their manipulation of kings, control the people of the world (they are fully in support of freewill and allowing people to make their own mistakes rather than trying to control the masses). Often in times of major revolt, there are reports of people wearing patchwork clothing supporting the people and the overthrowing of tyrany.

Topic: Investigate History: The Storytellers
column5: There is no written history about the Storytellers because they prefer oral tradition and the freedom of creativity over being bound by corrupt leaders and the laws of kings. In some records, they are called the Order of Broken Pages who stand in opposition to the Loremaster's Guild who they believe to be corrupt and power hungry and who with their manipulation of kings, control the people of the world (they are fully in support of freewill and allowing people to make their own mistakes rather than trying to control the masses). Often in times of major revolt, there are reports of people wearing patchwork clothing supporting the people and the overthrowing of tyrany.

Topic: Investigate History: Anika
column5: Anika, also called the Dancer, is the child of Lisra and Nero, and sister of Ceileen. One of the Seven Unseen Goddess of Eirie, she is also a minor goddess in the Celestine pantheon, particularly revered in both the Celestine Empire and Dace, where she is considered a patron god of The Giuseppe School.

Topic: Investigate History: Lyyt
column5: No information is to be found. As with all fae who exist in all times at once, when they die, they are dead throughout all time and their existance is erased from the world. That even their name remains is a miracle.

Topic: Second Opinion: Blue Print steps for Mage's Tower (foreman ally)
Investigate History: Stitchers of Myre
column5: When the city of Myre rose from underneath and through Ad Decimum, wreaking havoc and destruction, The Stitchers of Ad Decimum switched sides, betraying the Decimals and joining with Myre.. An agreement is struck with the Stitchers’ Guild to grant them position and home in Myre if they help destroy Ad Decimum. Between the forces of Myre, the Stitcher forces, and the soon to be Mad-Kings the place once known as Ad Decimum fell, and became the first nation conquered and assimilated into the Kingdom of Myre.  Leading the military of Myre, the Stitcher's Guild count among their number numerous mages, creative thinkers, researchers, and engineered monsters.  With the Age of Myre isolated and able to refine their techniques, their stiched creations no longer bear the roughly stitched markings of old, instead their incisions and sewn seams are nearly invisible to the eye. Their most impressive stitched creature is the three-headed dracostitch.

Topic: Military Insight: Whitehall
Investigate Loved Ones/Healthy Paranoia: Sybil Lenore
column5: Sybil Lenore's family were all executed as heretics when she was barely a child and their estates and assets were siezed by the Church of Chorus, leaving the toddler an orphan. Taken in by Lord Darius Lelaroy's family and given the protections of Whitehall, her loyalty has been to him since she was old enough to understand what loyalty is. Now in her late 40s, the effects of her childhood still weigh on her and she has struggled with having close ties and loved ones, which has only been reinforced over the years by living in a military fortress city and having anyone she was close to die at the hands of the Nadine in war. This lack of close personal relationships does not lessen her desire to help others. While not commonly known, there is an extensive collection of letters shared between herself and Sir Jarrett of the White Chalice, who has spent most of the last two decades abroad, suggesting a relationship in some form between them, though it is unclear even by reading their letters what that relationship is.

Topic: Eavesdrop: Sybil Lenore
column5: - "The Mistress just comes and goes on her own schedule, you'll think she's up in bed, but really no one even saw her leave the house!" - "There's another letter from Sir Jarrett today, what's that, the third one this month?" - "I tell ya, if ever you was wantin' a Mistress to treat ya right and not be a bother, she's the one you'd wanta be workin' for." - "One of these days, the Mistress will find someone and finally have a caller here at the house. Some day. Maybe."  - "The Mistress is upstairs in her bedchamber today, one of those dark days. Best to just leave her be, but we always make sure to leave out a food tray in the hall if she decides to eat something."  - "Yes, I know there's lots to dust, the Mistress is particularly fond of her things. Be especially careful when you're dusting the swan collection, they're all made of glass, crystal, or stones and they'll break if you bump them wrong. And she'll know too, if even one of them loses it's little head, she knows - why do you think you got hired? The last maid was fired for breaking two of their delicate little necks by accident when she sneezed too hard."

column5: REFUND

Topic: Investigate History: Blue Dragon Lena
column5: Blue and White dragons are known to have the smallest territory of the various dragon colors, keeping to the furtherst northern reaches of the Expanse. Dragons were most commonly found on Adelrune before the Age of Tane, when humanity and their writing was scarce. If there was a blue dragon called Lena, she has done nothing of note and thus gone unmentioned in the history books, as such, she isn't known to have battled the Nadine Empire, or had a territorial dispute with one of the great dragons.
