Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia/Distract the Mark: Sybil Lenore
column5: - Rumor has it, Sybil Lenore died at Whitehall.  - Rumor has it, Sybil Lenore disappeared in Whitehall.  - Rumor has it, Sybil Lenore survived Whitehall.  - Rumor has it, she's in love with Lord Darius and is the reason he can never be found.  - Rumor has it she has an obsession with swans.  - Rumor has it, she has foreseen her own death.  - Rumor has it, she's a spy for the Nadine Empire.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines/Healthy Paranoia/Distract the Mark: Sybil Lenore
column5: Sybil Lenore is normally very predictable in her life - she works, she volunteers at the local school in Whitehall, and she goes to bed every night at a reasonable time after reading novels secretly imported from abroad. Fairly reserved, when she does need to make appearances at parties/social events, she leans on her magic to show off party tricks to get through the evening. Sometimes, however, she becomes nearly invisble and impossible to track when she does not want to be found for days at a time. On an average Thursday night, she can be found at her private table at a local wine bar, reading her book while enjoying a glass of expensive wine with multiple desserts.

Topic: Research: Arrow of True Form
column5: These particular arrows were crafted by the White Chalice during the War of Faces to face Meretrix, who led the shapeshifters against the human nations. Numerous arrows like it have been crafted throughout history as an arrow of true form can be used to revert a shapeshifter to their original form and stop them from shapeshifting for a duration of time.

Topic: research: Are there other known holders of cards from the Wooden Swords deck? Known sightings or abilities of the Wooden Sword artifact?
column5: King Caius Cole (first king of Cole), Selena (Valence), wooden sword has not been seen since the early Age of Chorus, Charlie Fourfist killed in a drunken brawl

Topic: Vesh is Kuyeh connection to Snake. Letter explains the promises of greed and power leads to Vesh rising in power. Are the candles spying on the Trahazi? What are the candles doing ritually?
column5: The candles themselves are not spying, nor are they able to be seen through. They are ritually dedicated to Kuyeh, also known as Vesh, and whoever placed them upon the shrine knew exactly what they were doing - their presence on the shrine siphons many of the prayers to the pantheon as a whole or to other gods in particular, instead to Kuyeh to provide him more power.

Topic: connection between Darklings and Vesh? stories of Kuyeh spying on Trahazi through items? What conflict does Kuyeh have with the Zodiac?
column5: You do not have the appropriate lores to look for a connection between the Darklings and Vesh.  There are many stories of Kuyeh's pranks and how he would hide to watch as his pranks went off on various unsuspecting family members, delighting in their misfortune of stumbling unwittingly into his traps. While many of the more recent stories make note of his pranks being "lighthearted," older stories paint more intense and directed pranks on specific family members, exploiting their natural inclinations for his own amusement. The oldest stories you can find make reference to a particularly cruel "joke" played on Wolf, where Kuyeh destroyed his most prized possession - though the description of the "possession" can vary greatly from a physical object of great importance to a lost love or beloved sibling. There doesn't seem to be a specific single incident t that can be pointed to between Kuyeh and the rest of the Zodiac, but seems to be an inherent inclination in his behavior that causes conflict.

Topic: Elixir of Vo'ss'el: what qualities or effects does this elixir have? who was Vo'ss'el? what was was it used?
column5: While the creation of this elixir came from a time prior to recorded history, the name Vo'ss'el does still live on. They later took up residence in the city of Inexo and were heavily involved in trade with house Calae. It is common knowledge that at least one high ranking number of house Calae still shares this bloodline. They were known to be an exceptional alchemist and their elixirs are highly sought after and rare. This particular elixir's properties are only known to you because you have given the potion to those around you. First, the immediate effect is that the drinker will see spirits and have the overwhelming urge to speak with any one of them that they encounter. The long-term side effects seem to take a great deal of time to settle into the body of the drinker, as if it needs time to bind itself to the soul. At least in your friends, the observed effects include the persistant ability to Commune with Spirits and Speak with Dead, as well as always seeming to be hearing spirits whispering to them even if none are immediately present. (some status effect tags will be on the bulletin board for you, please give one to each person who you had drink the potion <3 )

Topic: what happens to a fae's realm when they die? line of succession?
column5: As an individual fae does not truly have a "realm" (ie plane), nothing ultimately happens to the Twixt and Twain when a fae dies except that small pieces of reality die and become the responsibily of the other fae to pick up. In the case of Dusk fae who have taken over portions of Paradox, those areas are left abandoned and/or left to the whims of those who live there. Additionaly, since Paradox has a government of anarchy, there is no specified line of succession followed when a governor of a particular area is no longer able to govern their territory.

Topic: how was Xolo trapped? why was Xolo trapped? by who?
column5: As Xolo predates written history and was largely connected to a culture that does not keep written records, there is no information to be found about Xolo's imprisonment.

Topic: oldest stories of Kuyeh/Kuyeh having conflicts with other gods
column5: There are many stories of Kuyeh's pranks and how he would hide to watch as his pranks went off on various unsuspecting family members, delighting in their misfortune of stumbling unwittingly into his traps. While many of the more recent stories make note of his pranks being ""lighthearted,"" older stories paint more intense and directed pranks on specific family members, exploiting their natural inclinations for his own amusement. The oldest stories you can find make reference to a particularly cruel ""joke"" played on Baki, where Kuyeh destroyed his most prized possession - though the description of the ""possession"" can vary greatly from a physical object of great importance to a lost love or beloved sibling. There doesn't seem to be a specific single incident that can be pointed to between Kuyeh and the rest of the Zodiac, but seems to be an inherent inclination in his behavior that causes conflict.

Topic: Crow vs. vampires (how to get blessing, crow's mark on a silver blade destroys vamps, etc)
column5: Vampires are simply beings that defy death by seeking one of a multitude of ways to give one extended life or even the semblance of immortality, denying the death realm their spirit as their life ought to come to a natural end. As such, there as many different ways to kill a so-called "vampire" as there are ways to achieve "vampirism". To engrave the symbol of a death god into a silver blade and have it blessed by a priest of that faith is merely one method to kill a particular line of vampires - namely those of the line of Vojin, which dominantly includes the Black Chalice and the "vampires" of the Nadine Empire.

Topic: Fox: methods/rites/holidays/etc
column5: As Fox has been dead and not worshipped in hundreds of years, this information is exceptionally difficult to find and eventually you'll discover that Caeli is the only real source of practical knowledge of Fox and the rites related to him. She would share that while the rites of Absolution and Community are associated with various gods, they are the two most often associated with Fox - cleverness and resourcefulness to ease the conscience of corruption and weight, playfulness and laughter from being together with those they are close to. She will share with you the duality of Fox - of the mischevious side, but also of the resilient survivor who is capable of disappearing to lay low when the world becomes too much, and adapt as necessary to continue on. Who knows how to bide his time, not for the right moment to spring an attack as Panther would, but for the best time to reemerge when he has taken the rest and care he needed. Often tied to the Spring, Fox is frequently envoked and tied to fertility holidays and the rebirth of the world as the winter fades and new life comes into being. Worship of Fox is often offered by acts of cleverness and play, and elaborate problem solving solutions. This is especially more prevalent now as Fox and Kitar have become one, as puzzles and problem-solving were among Kitar's favorite pasttimes.  And of course, a discussion of Kitar and Fox is never complete without sharing a rule, in this instance, Caeli quotes Rule 100: The difference between Trouble and Adventure is the spelling.

Topic: how do we go to Matsi's realm without angering her? is there a rite or ritual?
column5: The realms of death are not a place for the living to walk. Some ferrymen are willing to physically take you there, at a cost, but as the gods of death are well-aware of past actions of the Returned sneaking into their realms to collect ritual components and steal souls, many defenses have been put into place over the years. Most rites or rituals that would take one to the realms of death only take the soul itself through a version of Spirit Walk, rather than the physical body, so to do so physically requires a method to cross planes. To physically cross into Eden's Realm without angering her would often come with a heavy cost - an offering given to her that is worthy of allowing your souls and bodies to return to the mortal realm. It is often considered safest to pay the ferryman's toll, and be quick in getting in and out before you are discovered. For a necromancer, however, there is a different option - the ritual of Eden's Amnesty will protect the subject of the ritual from Eden's power and allow them to freely traverse her realm.

Topic: could a ferryman take someone from Theddespari to the death realm? a way to prolong Oleksander's sanity?
column5: Many souls from Theddespari were taken to their resting places by the ferrymen throughout history. This would include Oleksander, whose soul is intact and capable of moving on to the realms of death.

Topic: I am starting working on gathering components for theft of majesty and reincarnation. My first question is: do I need specific containers for the ritual components and if so, where would I get those containers?
column5: You do not typically need specific containers for the various components of a ritual, unless specified in the ritual itself.

Topic: if there was a ferryman who served the Trahazi Zodiac, would they serve Matsi? or the pantheon as a whole? Where would this ferryman take Trahazi souls?
column5: Ferrymen are most commonly connected to death realms. As the Trahazi Zodiac itself does not recognize a unique death god that holds its own realm on the spirit plane, it is expected that most souls will go to Eden's realm, as most souls in the world do if another god has not placed a claim or mark upon their soul. However there is no difinitive way to prove this beyond the general world knowledge that this is where souls typically go.

Topic: where do we find dream salt? can it be made?
column5: Dream Salt is a commonly required ritual component in many of the more complex rituals of the world. It can be gathered by killing a powerful (read: "normal" or greater") dream entity, and then using a unique dream spell to manifest the "form" of that dream entity into physical dream salt that can be collected. Alternatively, there are organizations on Adelrune that trade in esoteric ritual components and occult artifacts that likely are capable of getting their hands on a use or two.

Topic: Investigate History: Montauks
column5: Montauks are curious creatures with wholly unique origins in the world of Adelrune. Where most creatures are born of Adelrune or another plane that crossed over to Adelrune, Montauks are supernatural creatures that were created by mortal hands in the city of Theddespari. It has been observed by the few who have traveled deeps into the ruined city of Theddespari that there is a giant contraption in which captured Ko'aat have been placed, and rather than a body coming out, a fearsome beast called a Montauk is made from the Ko'aat. They take on a vaguely-humanoid shape with long grotesque hands, misshapen angular faces, and malformed torsos. When they are killed, rather than a corpse being left on the ground, they melt away into thousands of little black worms that crawl into the dirt to reform elsewhere. These creatures have not been studied extensively in the modern day due to this phenomenon of deconstructing to smaller pieces that are rarely able to be captured, but it's suspected if one were to be able to capture a montauk to study it, more information could be gathered.

Topic: Gather Rumors: State of House Kaelin (refugees, notable dead)
column5: REFUND

Topic: State of the Court: Unity Parliament members' current opinions about the situation (who is receptive, who is on the fence, who is against)
column5: With the current Declaration being the only documentation of "New Solace", there is no Unity member state that is in support of your proposal, as it severely lacks details, and does not take into account care for the large non-Returned population.

Topic: Investigate History: the nature and timeline of the conflict between Myre and the Nadine Empire
column5: Unknown (Age of Tane) - The Building of Myre Tower: The Nadine Empire establishes Myre tower during the Attack of Glorious Conquests as they build towers across the Expanse. 138t - The War of Poisons: Myre tower rebels against the Nadine Empire, beginning the war of poisons. 152t - Myre Independence: Myre tower wins its independence from the Nadine Empire, ending the War of Poisons. 155t - The Founding: The Kingdom of Myre is officially founded under the leadership of King Myaous. 180t - In ‘The Battle of Broken Wills’ the Nadine Empire destroys the Kingdom of Myre, though this proves to only be temporary.

Topic: Investigate History: what weaknesses of Castle Myre were exploited during their history of being raided, beseiged, and invaded?
column5: Castle Myre has no real history of being raided, beseiged, or invaded in the Age of Myre. A single knight once made it through the Walls of Distortion, but he was swiftly captured, dealt with, and the minor flaw he took advantage of in their defenses was sealed immediately.

Topic: who funds the bardic college?
column5: Looking back through the older (poorly kept) records of the Bardic Guild of Solace, it seems a woman called Lady Cressida Demere of Dace seems to be the one providing the funding and support for the Bardic College in Solace. Rather than living in Dace, she seems to be living comfortably in the city of Solace, and even owns one of the larger, private boxes to see shows at the Vision theater there.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Ko'aat Arbiters on Tear
column5: There are no Ko'aat Arbiters on Tear at this time, as all of the Ko'aat on Tear are Returned and from an era that predates the Arbiters.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: drir portals
column5: - Rumor has it, the Drir Portals have been broken for quite some time. - Rumor has it, the Effendal and White Chalice have been in conflict over the control of the Drir Portals. - Rumor has it, the Drir Portals once reached five locations on Adelrune: Fort Cestral, Castle Thorn, the Temple of Destiny, Castle Hastings, and Cole. - Rumor has it, the safety features have been removed so the Drir Portals now reach other planes. - Rumor has it, large sacrifices and heavy losses were made by the Returned the night that the Drir Portals were built.

Topic: State of the Court: Ko'aat/Alstai
column5: As the Ko'aat are not a Rules of Society nation, State of the Court is not a valid Influence Spend.

Topic: Investigate Prominent Figures: Castle Myre
column5: As Castle Myre is not a Rules of Society nation, Investigate Prominant Figures is not a valid Influence spend on this culture. However the following information can be gathered: The Ashen King is a powerful pyromancer, responsible for the Industrial District of the city. The Eroded King specializes in construction and defense of the city, responsible for the Merchant District. The Warped King: specializes in the bending of reality, responsible for the Arts District. The Twin-Stitched King’s greatest accomplishment is the three-headed stitcher dragon who protects the city, an abomination to all dragon-kind, the horrific “Draco-Stitch.” He is responsible for the Fabric District.  The Pincushion King is responsible for the defense of the city and the Military District. The Plague King is responsible for the gardens and Agricultural District within the city. The Puppet King, head of the Puppeteer's Guild and responsible for the District of Disrepair. The Smiling Prince, the leader of the nation who initially made the agreement with the fae to save the children of Myre before it's first destruction at the hands of the Nadine Empire.

Topic: more healers given to the hospitals to serve the non-Returned
column5: 10 more healers for clinic

Topic: Underworld Insight/Healthy Paranoia: Order of Broken Pages
column5: The Order of Broken Pages has it's hand in nearly every underworld organization on Adelrune, and uses this network to collect information and cause social unrest and even anarchy in every nation on Adelrune. Known for publicizing secret truths as much as they spin and weave fanciful tales and stories to discredit and undermine governements and local authorities, they claim to represent "the common people" and fight for their freedoms from tyrrany and the control of the ruling class. Rather than wear an affiliated symbol or color, the Order of Broken Pages is much more subtle while wearing patchwork clothing - it is impossible to differentiate them from a typical peasant or even a stray Trahazi. It's suspected they have been extremely active in the Amalgamation in recent years, particularly after the death of the Voice and during the Hastings Rebellion to capitalize on the unrest, though there is no proof to the truth of this rumor.

Topic: Political Insight: Mehs, opinions of Bellus
column5: As a fellow demon prince, Mehs is anti-Bellus because they are currently equals and he does not want an Abyssal Monarch that is not himself. He is quickly losing ground in the anti-Bellus sentiment, however, as Bellus' army is present in Mehs, and he has begun to find a method to seek peaceful negotiation out of necessity.

Topic: Eavesdrop: C'thon Darkweaver, opinions of Bellus
column5: - "I'm not sure I've ever heard C'thon hate anyone as much as he hates Bellus..." "What about Arasmiel?" "Those two have been feuding since the beginning of demonkind... but I'm pretty sure he hates Bellus more." - "I heard that there's talk of finding a way to kill and completely destroy Prince Bellus." "You'd better keep your mouth shut and not go spreading that. C'thon will have your head for a traitor." "Prince Bellus treats his servants better anyway." (sounds of stabbing followed by a death rattle) "Fucking idiot, I told you to keep your mouth shut. Now I have to clean this up on top of my normal chores!" - "Do you really think it's true that Prince Bellus doesn't actually have an interest in ruling all of Adelrune? I heard he's been looking for Bel'e'athru's regalia and that seems like a strange thing to do if you're not trying to take over the world." "I think that's why C'thon hates him so much - it's wasted potential. Imagine C'thon as our new Abyssal monarch ruling over demon kind and all of humanity and every other poor soul on this plane!"

Topic: Military Insight: Mehs
column5: Mehs is an island city not on the main continent of the Southern Expanse/Breach, and is much more defensible than any of the demon cities on the continent with jagged rocks surrounding it and making landing difficult. Although it's the last true demon army of Breach and managed to avoid the worst of the War of Radiance and Second Crusade widdling down their numbers, as an island city, this also means the military force is much smaller than Bellus' army. Despite the physical defenses of Mehs, with the Crown upon Bellus' head, it is expected that demons will slowly begin changing to the side of Bellus and through attrition, it is inevitable that Bellus will be victorious.

Topic: Investigate Rumors/Healthy Paranoia: C'thon Darkweaver
column5: - Rumor has it, C'thon's forces have been recruting heavily from the Company of the Ice Edge - Rumor has it, C'thon is preparing for his forces to join the Abyssal Monach in Breach - Rumor has it, there is an exceedingly large number of demons on an island among the Broken Tears, and C'thon was among them - Rumor has it, C'thon Darkweaver has captured a god - Rumor has it, C'thon Darkweaver killed his child T'tocs for his utter and complete failure against the Returned

Topic: Military Insight: Bellus' forces
column5: Bellus' forces are heavily made up of succubi and corrupters, and he has been gathering immense numbers of lesser demons and demon blooded to his army in recent months, many of whom have been scattered and displaced across the Breach since the War of Radiance and Second Crusade. He has an extensive networld of spies and courtesans, and an elite force he relies on to lead the army. His many commanders include: Meretrix, Beletrix, Danievel, Tyrous, Zartan, and Barlgura.

Topic: Investigate History: who to contact for correspondence in the Vercrux Moonmoss School of Alchemy?
column5: As Investigate History does not provide the name of someone to correspond with within an organization, I can offer you either a refund for this action, or do a general Investigate History on the Vercrux Moonmoss School of Alchemy. Please just let me know which you'd prefer <3 REFUND

Topic: Investigate History: Prime Alchemical Catalyst
column5: Prime Alchemical Catalyst is a generic term for a potent, powerful alchemical ritual component. Most older, extremely powerful alchemical rituals require it, but it is not one specific component as many different ritual components can qualify for this term. Ultimately, the component must be a true vehicle of change in the physical world - one well-known component that qualifies is a piece of a god, and other things of that sort.

Topic: Political Insight: Krisler Donnyn: International Merchant's Guild
column5: Krisler Donnyn is currently on the outs with Declan Stone and Archmagus Laken, but he's very popular with the younger generation of noble women of noble women in Cestral. He has developed a reputation that the women he dates tend to find themselves marrying someone else soon after being with him, almost always as an advantageous match for the women. He is a peasant, but due to his connections and dating life, he has a lot of power and sway in Cestral and its various guilds, even if half the Cabinet hates him. Due to some strings being pulled recently on his behalf, Krisler is positioned to stay in Cestral for the foreseeable future.

Topic: Vices and Routines/Healthy Paranoia: Declan Stone
column5: While thrifty, it is no secret that Declan Stone appreciates the finer things in life - a good home, good food, good bourbon, and good spouses. With both his partners among the wealthiest of the Great Houses - Eleanor and Willowwine, Declan has a large, stylish home in the city where they spend the majority of their time, but between cabinet sessions Declan and his partners are more commonly found at their country estate. Typically while in the city, he spends the majority of his days working and in meetings with various guilds and merchant contacts, or the other members of the King’s Cabinet. It is not uncommon for the triad to have dinner out most evenings while in town, whether at a local restaurant or at a dinner party with someone of import for either his own work or that of his spouses. The end of the week comes with a regularly scheduled date night typically involving a private dinner at a restaurant and a show or entertainment of some form, while the triad is typically joined at home by their six grown children and their families for the majority of their rest day. As a gentleman, Declan certainly drinks regularly though never to excess in public spaces as he has a reputation to maintain. He is not one to shy from conflict, particularly when he knows himself to be right, but outside of work is rarely confrontational or so argumentative as he is among colleagues. His routine is much more unclear and harder to track when the family is at their home in the country, as they rarely leave the property, but they are known to host private parties of a dozen or so friends in their home, for two to three days at a time while there

Topic: Investigate Fame: Chamberlain Caitriona
column5: Known by the pen name "Whippoorwill", Caitriona is a famous poet and opens every session of the Cestral Royal Court with a poem. She's particularly known for her beautiful and evocative prose about the nature of Cestral, but when the occasion calls for it, her poetry will call out problem nobles in subtle verse, and she has the skill to turn public opinion on a dime with a well-phrased line - famously, she brought support for the marriage alliance with the Vicaul when there had at first been loud public outcry against it.

Topic: Investigate Fame: Queen Liosa
column5: Queen Liosa Cestral has quite the reputation and fame for being one of the most impressive duelists in the country. It is said she has never lost a duel and trained her daughters to follow in her footsteps. King Dughlas first met her by watching her fight in the Tournament of Cestral when it was hosted by the Celestine Empire, where she absolutely decimated her opposition among the Cestrals, as well as every Celestine who had accompanied the envoy for the event. He was smitten immediately, and since that day she has stood as his champion and is fiercely protective of him. Queen Liosa is known for putting people in their place if they speak out of turn, at a particularly memorable Court session she challenged an impertinent Dacian diplomat to a duel and had him in the dirt within seconds. This reputation makes it even more curious that she has held her tongue when her authority was challenged by the Vicaul kings who married her daughters.

Topic: Investigate History: Threads of Fate
column5: The Threads of Fate are an extremely rare ritual component, most commonly associated with the fae who weave reality around them. The Threads can be used to mend tears in the fabric between planes, contain reality within them, and weave new creatures and beings into life. While it it theorized that it would be possible for a mortal to weave the Threads of Fate, there is scholarly debate that the cost of doing so would be too much for a mortal form to handle.

Topic: Eavesdrop: Declan Stone
column5: - “Oi, I heard Mr Stone was threatening to lob off that Donnyn bloke’s head if he raises the port taxes any more than he already has!” - “Don’t forget that Mr Stone asked you to make the arrangements for the Missus’ birthday dinner next week. He wanted the private patio at Berner’s, before they’ll be going to the theater. I’ve got to get to the tailor, the florist, the jeweler, and we’re expecting a Willowwine delivery this afternoon, so make sure that Aster clears out space in the wine cellar.” - “You think Mr Stone will quit before Prince Ahrean can remove him from the Cabinet when the Prince takes the throne? He could just retire, it’s not like he needs to be working… but I don’t want him puttering around the house all day either. I like it quiet around here.” - “Be thankful you got the anniversary gift you did, Maybelle, that was all Mr Willowwine’s doing. Mr Stone only wanted to give you the day off and a bottle of the good stuff for your wife.” - “I don’t envy Larissa being the one who always has to go to the country house with the Missus and Misters. They’ve got to run her ragged while they’re in residence, since I hear that the staff are all put on leave while they’re there.”

Topic: Eavesdrop: Krisler Donnyn: Intl Merchant's Guild
column5: While Krisler Donnyn doesn't employ servants properly, he does have underlings who work for him within the Intl Merchant's Guild, and they gossip as good as any servant. - "Did you hear Donnyn is going to raise import taxes on everything coming through Westport if Stone doesn't cough up the rebuilding funds?" - "Do you know why we've got such a big order for new ships and refurbishments out of Cestral? It's not like the nation is buying them, they already owe the guild a fortune!" - "I think I heard Donnyn is out to lunch again with the foreman from the Fishery Collective. Probably for the best, she's the only one keeping him from packing everything up and cutting all the Guild's ties with Cestral." - "Oh yeah, he's always getting mail from all over the world, but most of that has a Guild seal on it. Those ones coming out from Port Frey and Breach though? Those are new." - "You remember that one guy who came through Port last week? Captain Sadero? I overheard part of his meeting with Donnyn and it sounds like Donnyn might be up for a promotion to the head office."

Topic: Gather Rumors: Kiara Cunningham
column5: - Rumor has it, no one in Cestral writes more letters than Kiara Cunningham and half Alexander's annual paper budget is for her own supply - Rumor has it, Kiara Cunningham has favorites among those she corresponds with at length - Rumor has it, Kiara absolutely *hates* fish, and if anyone brings it within 5 corridors of her office, she will sniff it out and kick them out for the remainder of the day - Rumor has it, Kiara spent decades pining after one of her correspondence pen pals - Rumor has it, Kiara has taken a leave of absence from Alexander for the first time since taking her post as Master of Letters

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Lord Cressious
column5: Rumor has it, Lord Cressious is a member of the Princess’ personal guard. Rumor has it, he was last seen publicly at Court with the Princess (in Siegeholt) Rumor has it, his favorite color is a rich, mustard yellow. Rumor has it, Lord Cressious writes poetry and has a fondness for riddles Rumor has it, he gas a great disdain of fledglings Rumor has it, Lord Cressious experiments with the limits of hypnotism and domination.

Topic: Poltical Insight: Waykeeper Oleyna
column5: The Waykeepers are an errant order of knights attributed to the Trahazi. Oleyna is one of the oldest of the small order, with records of her name dating back to the days before they became Trahazi and were still Hazori. While Waykeepers are particularly known to stay out of the way and travel between the various Trahazi caravans, Oleyna has been seen frequently in Port Frey in recent years, and many suspect she was stranded on Tear due to the Mists cutting her off from reaching the other caravans of the Expanse. Three potential squires are attributed to her at the moment - Ryx, Galatea, and Yael - so it's also likely that they are keeping her in Port Frey as well. As with any of the Waykeepers, Oleyna's political leanings and tendencies seem to fall in line with the order - to keep the Trahazi alive, in the best position they can place themselves, and safeguarding the practices of their culture. As she has not denounced her squire Ryx after he was publicly named a traitor in the Assassin, it seems she is not opposed to working all angles of a situation for the betterment and protection of her people, though it doesn't seem she is willing to get her own hands dirty in the process.

Topic: Structure Repair: Bloodhelm Manor
Structure Repair: Grand Ballroom
Follow the Money: Four Corners
column5: Four Corners was founded on a ten gold investment from Ziix used to start the company. Of that coin, seven of it was used to purchase a hothouse during an auction hosted by the International Merchants Guild, and the other three gold was used to purchase a printing press at that same auction.   Some of the core members have frivolous spending habits, with both Alethea and Zixx having an eye for finer things. Alethea is interested in fashion, and Zixx is known to eat and drink only the highest quality food and wine.   Both Lady Lilliana and Alethea come from prominent economic based families, and are born into merchant connections outside of Tear.  Lilliana is of from House Vallaro, a Dacian House with a firm grip on the fashion and jewelry industry in Dace.  Alethea's family specializes in agricultural trade, and grows produce in the Celestine Empire on their farm,  Hortensia Orchards.  Four Corners has no outstanding debts and pays it's employees a comfortable and reliable wage. Though at the time the guild has only hired individuals with a more standard skillset and not Master or Grandmasters.  The company tends to celebrate after each gathering is sales have been successful, and are known to be generous in that time.

Topic: State of the Court: Port Frey Infrastructure for non-Returned (schools, trade schools, hospitals, farms, markets) and general economy of the citizenry
column5: The non-Returned economy and situation in Port Frey and Solace as a territory is generally quite well-off when compared to many other parts of the world, which makes sense given they would need a reason to WANT to live here rather than in the (relative) safety of their homelands. Many who came to Tear were looking for fortune or escaping war and in search of a new life, came as part of the army for the War of Radiance and stayed, are scholars and mages seeking out rare artifacts and components, are attached to their nation's representation in Unity, or came for the lucrative criminal career that has always been available on the frontier island that is Tear. As such, for a settlement that is just over a decade old and has seen multiple catestrophic wars, they're still doing fairly well. There are classes for all ages sponsored through the Guild of Academics (in the city of Solace, illiteracy rates are practically zero), an expanded orphanage with a school and art classes as well as an apprenticeship program, a variety of guilds and work for those willing to seek it, connections to every major nation with Unity and their trade agreements through the International Merchant's Guild, hospitals and weekly clinics for the sick, a wide assortment of shops and markets for nearly anything one could imagine needing, as well as religious and community centers for most major cultures and religions of the world. The harbor allows for large exports including ships from the shipyard and dry dock, and recently the Returned funded upgrades to the old earthenwork defenses by adding stone walls in strategic areas. Paved roads to Solace and well-maintained roads to Edge facilitate overland trade and movement of products to the harbor, as well as food from the farms in the interior baronies to the cities. The city of Solace has warded and protected walls and defensible structures to provide for the town at large.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Dark spot over the Nadine Empire
column5: On your trip to Cestral, you can fully confirm this is an extremely large forest fire. Clouds of smoke and ash hang heavily over the entire region, dropping ash as far east as the shores of Cestral.

Topic: Basic Scouting: Has Bellus' army reached the shores of the Breach yet?
column5: Yes

Topic: Basic Scouting: Are Meretrix and Lord Relinquo aiding Bellus?
column5: Technically this is two different questions, but I will answer both: Meretrix: Yes Relinquo: No

Topic: Golden Charm:
column5: None of the lores presented are applicable for researching this item. Though potentially with Lore: Artifacts you could make an educated guess that this is Paradoxian in origin.

Topic: Smuggling in Port Frey
column5: Unfortunately this topic is not something that can be found using research in the library as the town of Port Frey is a living and developing town. So you will need to use influence to get information about this topic.

Topic: Effendal land
column5: The effendal believe all the lands of Tear including the Northern Island and the Broken Tears to be their territory. The various tribes have territories they are responsible for on the island, but the current status of any of those territories cannot be found through research actions as they are not written about in books, but rather can be discovered through scouting or influence actions.

Topic: Topic: Who is Es'enabh?
column5: Es'enabh is said to be an extremely powerful shapeshifter demon from the line of Zyte. She is known to be gifted in the construction of portals to other realms and worked with her father to invade Adelrune during the age of Eur. While Zyte himself was killed before the portals from the Abyss to Adelrune were built on the Southern Expanse, she assisted Tane in finishing the rituals before he turned on her. As a final strike against her and Zyte, he is said to have killed her in the ensuing fight as demons poured forth from the Abyss to invade Adelrune. Presumed dead, she disappeared after that point only showing up later on the Eastern Expanse, where she ingratiated herself with the Hazori people and received a large green gemstone for her assistance in forcing those who would become Vein from their land. Within the Knights of the Braid, it's said that a shapeshifter was present in the early days of the order, looking for an in for an unknown purpose, until she was rooted out (though they were unable to kill her), and later during the War of Faces, Callum Abrill was discovered to have been dead and impersonated by a shapeshifter demon believed to be Es'enabh. It's believed she is still working on completing her goals and those of Zyte in spreading demonkind through all the planes, but no one has seen hide nor hair of her since the Age of Tane.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: Did someone make a deal with Prince Bellus to procure a vial of the Shrike's blood?
column5: Yes, it is true that someone among the Returned made a deal to get Bellus a vial of the Shrike's blood.

Topic: How did the Church of Chorus get a blessing in the graveyard?
column5: As this incident was something that happened in recent history with the help of the Church of Chorus and the White Chalice, it is not recorded in library texts. If you have learned what the effects were in game, and can elaborate on them for me, I will give you additional information from Lore: Rituals, but as the effect is no longer present, you would have no way of studying the effect as it stands currently.

Topic: Port Frey dragon territory
column5: The region of Port Frey is traditionally the territory of blue/white/silver dragons. Until just before the Mists fell on Tear, a silver dragon called Tyvennical slumbered deep in the caverns beneath the city.

Topic: Destroying Beleathru's regalia
column5: There is no recorded record of how to destroy Beleathu's regalia as they are not artifacts native to the plane of Adelrune. As such, true destruction of the pieces is unlikely, and the best that was done previously was the splitting of the throne into several pieces. Purely destructive forces like the use of Cinnorast's obliteration or dragon fire might be possible, but are untested.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Bobby D. Banker
column5: Bobby Banker is a well-known member of the Skull Children, a gang of grave robbing children based out of Port Frey. He is the face of the Bank of the Dead, which is the more legitimized organization for the Skull Children, as they had accumulated too much wealth and had been pressured by the authorities to do something productive with their silver and gold. Bobby has recently been making strong in-roads with the Rat Catchers and has been seen exploring the sewers.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Vojin's Origin Story
column5: - Rumor has it, Vojin always was. There has never been a time when he hasn't existed, he was born with the world.  - Rumor has it, he sprang forth from the night as a dark pact between the Abyss and Adelrune.  - Rumor has it, he was of the first noble family, but their name was lost to history.  - Rumor has it, no one seems to know anything in truth, but there are aways whispers and guesses.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Tears of Esparei
column5: - Rumor has it, after the Shrike was removed from the Clocktower competition, she used the Tear of Elements to destroy Palauttaa and her closest supporters. - Rumor has it, the Tear of Creation was used in Edge to cause the creation of the Hanging Tree, a seemingly sentient tree that will attempt to hang anyone who comes near to it. - Rumor has it, the Nadine Empire has 5 of the 7 Tears - Rumor has it, the Tears of Life and Dream have not been seen in centuries. - Rumor has it, with all 7 of the Tears, the Empress Nadine can ascend to godhood

Topic: Investigate History: Tears of Esparei
column5: As the story is told, the world has existed for many cycles. In the beginning there was naught but Destiny and Chaos. Destiny spun the threads, and out of Chaos with the birth of the Titans, the world was given form. Yet that was just the beginning. Over the cycles, existence ends, and so long as we are worthy, it is reborn once again. Yet this is not a passive thing, and at times we have been found wanting. Esparei, mother of all birds, and beloved of all creatures, took pity on the world. She wept at the thought of the world spiraling into entropy never to recover. Seven tears fell upon Eur's cheek. The Tears were that of Creation, Eternity, the Elements, Life, Will, Dreams, and Destruction. The Tears captured the essence of the world, and were so passionate that they convinced all of creation that life was worth renewing. Over the years there have been people claiming to possess one of these Tears, and that possessing a Tear can somehow allow the bearer to tap into that inherent aspect of creation. The legends tell, that as Esparei created the birds with her music, that each of the Tears are particularly receptive to song. The closer the song is in passion to the nature of the Tear, the more receptive the Tear will react. Though many have claimed to have seen or held the Tears over the course of history, few stories have held any semblance of truth until the final years of the Age of Chorus. It is said that when the Voice was destroyed and the Cathedral crumbled to dust, that it was because of Esparei's displeasure and the use of her Tears against those who would harm her creations. Many believe that it is because of these Tears that that set about the final years of the age of Chorus, and set about the age of Adelrune. A time of rebirth and destruction.

Topic: What magics do dream entities use? how can we combat that?
column5: Dream entities, like Demons, Celestials, and Fae are inherantly and innately magical beings and so do not cast magic or use magical schools as humans do. The best way to combat Dream threats is to minimize their reach and access into the Waking world by strengthening the veil. In a worst case scenario that they are already in Adelrune, glass weapons and Dream magic can be used to combat the entitity, but ultimately the only way to truly destroy a Dream entity is to destroy all memory of it.

Topic: Is there a connection between Theddespari and the Nameless Faith?
column5: No, there is no historical connection between Theddespari and the Nameless Faith. however that is not to say that the Nameless Faith didn't have a cell hidden within the city of Theddespari.

Topic: How are celestials vessels made?
column5: There are numerous ways - they can be summoned directly into being as a physical form, they can possess bodies or objects (tethers) to control a body, or can bond with things that already exist in this world (tree, horse, etc)

Topic: why vampires are allergic to silver
column5: Vampirism only means prolonging one's life through blood. As such, there is a wide variety of situations that are not applicable for all vampires. for those who have an allergy to silver, they likely have a link in some form to demonic power, but no one has successfully identified what that link actually is. Speculation includes demon blessings or bondings, but there is no proof to this theory.

Topic: many say that Kaelin who created thte House of Effendal seems to have similarities with the White Stag. Are they perhaps the same deity?
column5: The broadly accepted theory of the Effendal is that they were once a fae that split apart into many beings called Effendal. If they are possibly the same diety/entity, it would mean that the White Stag was destroyed in splitting apart, and as such the Old Ways worship and stories would have to pre-date the splitting of the entity into Effendal. As Stag has answered prayers in recent years, it doesn't seem this would correlate to the destruction of Kaelin. However, connections between Deneial the Effendal tribe and Deneial the Mist Goddess might be more direct.

Topic: How to activate the blood glyphs?
column5: Most major locations on Tear had the Blood Glyphs at one point, however, the glyphs in Solace, Port Frey, and the Port Frey harbor were all blown during the War of Radiance. To set off the glyphs, one must be in close proximity to the location that is going to be blown, or in the Citadel with a control artifact that was held by Phyraeon. You'd additionally need a Paragon Blood Mage who is properly trained in how to trigger them.

Topic: what enhancements or modifactions can be done to the branch of the tree of life by an artificer?
column5: It is unknown whether or not an artificer has used or altered a Tree of Life branch, but it is highly suspected to be a powerful crafting component, though it's outcome is currently unknown. One would likely need to do direct experimentation with it to determine it's viability and effect. (Note: this will likely require being pitched as a crafting recipe using the branch as a component)

Topic: is there a ritual or some way for a necromancer to become a lich?
column5: There is no information on the process of becoming a lich to be found within a normal library. However, you are able to find a brief note that mentions Deroan, the god of Undeath, and his cult which takes residence in the swamps outside of Port Frey.

Topic: who are the individuals who founded the Loremasters and Storytellers? What were the original principles of the Loremasters and Storytellers?
column5: The Loremasters Guild was founded by an ancient scholar called Chimerubim, and was intended to be an organization to collect the recorded knowledge of humanity for the betterment of future generations, so the mistakes and struggles of the past need not be repeated. The founder of the Storytellers has been long-lost to time and memory, but the beliefs of the organization were immediately antithetical to that of the Loremasters - they believed all books and recorded history should be removed from the world, for binding reality to what is written narrows one's thoughts and potential, and binds the hands of those who might create wonderous things.

Topic: what do Eden's Faith do for faithful post-burial? where do they bury them? is there a non-direct way to enter the location?
column5: Eden faithful are typically buried in graveyards on the grounds of Celestine churches, where they are watched over by the clergy and members of the rectory. Typically the most direct way to approach a graveyard is walking into it, though it's possible to tunnel beneath it if one was intent on graverobbing.

Topic: Hawk
column5: Hawk is both the Messenger of the Gods and the Guardian of the Sun, bringing sunlight to each morning in the world. Hawk is sought most often by those seeking insight, discernment and focus into their lives. Hawk and the light of the sun allows the faithful to see what is really there and take appropriate action accordingly. He teaches you to wait patiently through emotional times to prevent irrational decisions and take action when the time is right. Hawk is associated with divination and is known for their seeing opportunities, and being able to see a future that most others can’t see. Guidance, Absolution, and Nature rites are most often connected to Hawk. Holidays are both the Summer and Winter Soltice, Summer to celebrate the longest day of the year (Ko'aat, Vicaul, Vein, and Gael), and Winter to celebrate the coming of the morning light after the longest night (Cestral, celebrated by lighting every room in the house through the night)

Topic: symbol of snake
are contracts an avenue in which we can channel our mana or an outside source's mana? how do contracts interact with reality? what kind of magic do fae use for contracts?
column5: Fae contracts are powered by fae magic, and thus are not interactable with mana or general magic known to the mortal races.

Topic: if a fae's realm is changed, does this change their own nature? if an outside ritual drastically alters the realm, what effect does that have on the fae?
column5: A fae's "realm" is ultimately an area that they have claimed and shaped to their own liking, typically in Paradox, but its not intrinsically connected to their true selves. Changing the "realm" should not have any affect on the fae itself.

Topic: history of Yenneyelle
column5: (Not much is readily available outside of the Bastion libraries.)  Yenneyelle, younger sister of the twins Joriel and VitaVelNex, was a beloved healer training to be a Paladin of Radiance. She would often show up after particularly violent wars lamenting such mass tragedy and, according to legend, with a wide sweep of her opalescent wings would raise all the fallen. Joriel, following behind her, would threaten to permanently dispatch any who would dare to resume attack on their opposition for the great offense of squandering his sisters generous and benevolent gift. VitaVelNex would follow behind as well to de-escalate the conflict and to keep Joriel from needing to follow through with his very legitimate threat. Yenneyelle's sprawling compassion would often disrupt Joriel and VitaVelNex from their tasking due to their concern for her safety by taking it upon herself to visit these sites of mass casualty. After one of her trips to the battlefields of Alderune, Yenneyelle fell in love with a human and eventually had two children. Unfortunately, Those children and her connection to her human love were inevitably used against her. A warring force unspecified by any document captured her family and used them to lure in Yenneyelle. In her rush and concern for her family, she did not consult her siblings. She was then captured, used during a war to raise her captor's casualties, and after the battle was slain for parts to perform an unknown ritual. It is said that the warring force responsible disappeared afterwards in entirety, settlement and all. The only thing left behind was a smoldering land scar in the shape of sprawling wings. It is theorized that Joriel paid them a visit, as that day he was not seen next to his twin sister for the first time in many years.

Topic: Research: Building Statue Costs
column5: Statues can be made from a variety of materials: Salt, Marble, Iron, Silver, Manganese, Obsidian, and Gold. A collosus-sized statue requires ten of the same ore, and twenty craftsmen to construct it. A life-size statue requires five of the same ore, and ten craftsmen to construct it. A table-top statue/bust requires three of the same ore and five craftsmen to construct it. A hand-held statue requires one ore and a single craftsman to carve it.

Topic: where to find/how to make dream salt?
column5: Dream Salt is a commonly required ritual component in many of the more complex rituals of the world. It can be gathered by killing a powerful (read: "normal" or greater") dream entity, and then using a unique dream spell to manifest the "form" of that dream entity into physical dream salt that can be collected. Alternatively, there are organizations on Adelrune that trade in esoteric ritual components and occult artifacts that likely are capable of getting their hands on a use or two.

Topic: who, if anyone, populates Myre right now?
column5: Myre is populated by those who were brought back by the fae, as well as the descendents of Ad Decimum traitors and Stitcher's Guild, as well as refugees of Mandala and Gael who came seeking safety from their destroyed lands.

Topic: are the glyphs on the walls of Ad Decimum still working?
column5: The current status/state of things is not something that can typically be found through research in a library halfway across the world, so there is no way for us to answer this question unless through the Basic Scouting influence spend. Would you prefer to ask a different question?

Topic: IS IT POSSIBLE FOR US TO REPAIR THE RITUAL OR IS IT DOOMED? ARE THE GHOSTS THE RESULT OF THE RITUAL? WAS THE FUEL THE SOURCE OF THE MALFUNCTION?
column5: QUESTION 1: IS IT POSSIBLE FOR US TO REPAIR THE RITUAL OR IS IT DOOMED? The events that led to the deaths of everyone at Winsome Academy happened in the past, and there is no way for you to change the past to avert those deaths. But there are still things you can do to help the ghosts in a different way.  QUESTION 2: ARE THE GHOSTS THE RESULT OF THE RITUAL? These ghosts are the result of unfinished business from their lives. No one of these ghosts has the full picture of the circumstances surrounding their deaths, and none of them will be able to move on until they all understand exactly what happened.  QUESTION 3: WAS THE FUEL THE SOURCE OF THE MALFUNCTION? You have no way of knowing at present the specific cause of the malfunction. However, physical proof of what happened could be found at the site of the incident to shed further light on this.

Topic: How do we contruct the Dream Bridge?
column5: You are certainly on the right track in your thinking and approach! To construct such a bridge will ultimately require similar but mirrored steps to build the tether on Adelrune and the Anchor in the Dreaming. The Adelrune Tether:         - One of the families of Drir will need to send a trained portal ritualist as these are highly protected cultural secrets.  (Though it may take some trial and error to change the ritual to connect to a non-Adelrune based point)         - A Newborn Dream will need to be sacrificed in reality (To change the portal from something real, to something part dream)          - Something of the Amberlight will need to be sacrificed (to attach the portal to the Amberlight) The Dream Anchor:         - The assistance of one of the Cities or a significantly powerful dream creature to actually construct the portal.         - Would need to find a dream substitute for how the Drir use blood in their ritual (will likely require some experimentation to find a suitable substitute)         - A Real Person will need to be sacrificed in the Dream (To change the portal from dream to part real)         - Something of Port Frey will need to be sacrificed (To attach the portal to Port Frey) The stronger the tie the better, as weak connections in this regard cause weak/unstable portal connections and a variety of issues. See: the portal from Solace to Cestral Note:  Before any of this can be done, the Amberlight also needs to stabilize its orbit so it doesn't slide out of position once the Tether and Anchor are created

Topic: when does Teppidus leave his volcano? where does he go?
column5: Teppidus rarely leaves his volcano home. The last time he emerged, it was to lead the Drir as they marched on Mandala during the Blood Moon War, where he was seriously wounded by the emergence of a great black dragon the moment he entered the capital city. (attach 2 pics from the Assassin)

Topic: Documents that mention the Elixir of Enlightenment? Who created it? What effects does it have?
column5: The Elixir of Enlightenment is a ritual component needed for the ritual of Truest Sight in the Book of Ig. Its origin and recipe are long lost to time, though it is rumored to be an alchemical elixir that takes six full moon cycles to brew and will increase the mental capability of mortals. If any source of such an elixir remains in the world, it is highly protected, though perhaps more information could be found from the Moonmoss-Vercrux School of Alchemy. (double check this name)

Topic: how can I acquire a pet celestial cat like the ones we saw flying around in December?
column5: Typically beings such as celestials should not be imprisoned or kept as pets as they have a service to provide to the greater world and being kept from doing so will negatively impact their health, leading them to quickly deteriorate in captivity. It is recommended by the cryptozoologists of Ad Decimum to befriend and interact with such beings in a friendly manner, rather than try to capture them.

Topic: stories that involve Chorus or any of the three involving dark birds?
column5: 7 November 7r.  We are all just pawns in this greater game - unaware the game is even being played from above, from the past, from the future? Returned brought back to this specific moment in time, to play just one role - one moment - one piece of the puzzle - pawns put into all the right places to elicit a reaction, the right reaction, from us at the right moment. That is how this has all come to pass. The gods are moving again for the first time in this second lifetime of mine - I never knew what true silence was until I Returned - once again I hear the crows chatter and gossip overhead, the whispers of what is to come on the breaking waves of the ocean. It is comforting to me to have this once more, but I should have taken it for the omen it was - something big was coming. I expected it to do with the Sleeping Demon leading his army across the world - the portents I read told us that we were not ready, that time was nearly upon us - I was wrong in my interpretation. So very, very wrong.  It was not just the great demon who met his end at the hands of the Returned in recent days, but Chorus himself rained down from the heavens in a shower of red all across Tear. Were we as directly involved in his death as we were in the imprisonment of the demon? No, not knowingly, but we played a part, some of us more than others - I perhaps the largest role, though unwittingly.  Was it a bad thing that happened? Every whisper I have heard from above suggests it was inevitable, that it was coming... next week, next year, next century... it was going to happen on its own. This way... others also gained from it.  Alas, the regret and guilt are my burden to bear following this revelation. If I'd asked a simple question - why? - perhaps I would have understood enough to correct what has happened... would I have stopped the whole plot? Could I have succeeded if I even tried? I cannot answer those questions, but I certainly would have tried to correct the course so those around me did not suffer as they do now.  With Chorus' death, our greatest allies in this war suffer the most directly. Their Church was already in ruins, tatters held together with bits of twine, but their faith burned strong - emblazoned across their armor and cloaks - leading this war against the demons. With the death of their god, I share in their grief. I share in their fear. I weep for the role I played in this plot, and my heart aches for those whose faith promised them an afterlife of blessed plenty in the presence of their god. Without Chorus, his death realm is surely to collapse. Without his death realm... those marked by him - as I have been by my own gods - will find no rest when their time comes to leave Adelrune. No other realm will accept Chorus' blessed... there will be no rest, no reward for General Ootomus Rex when death finally catches up to him. He will be left to float in the endless emptiness between the realms of the dead. I spent much time there recently... it is a fate I would wish on no one.  And this fact, this is my burden to bear. Rex had every right to call me out for it - I earned every word he spoke in anger and grief. I fully deserved it.  If I do nothing else with this final chance at life, I WILL find him peace and a place he can rest when his time comes.

Topic: looking into the resurrected bloody edge and the potential link to Chorus worship, and where they are getting their power?

Additionally, looking into current situations like active new leadership of the Bloody Edge isn't something that would be found in a history book in a library, it would require influence expenditures. If there is an influence spend you would like to submit at this time I will take it and make sure you get an answer on Friday when it is paid for.

Topic: can we find a way to visit Paradox?
column5: As your previous research suggests, finding hedge gates is the most direct path to Paradox, though convincing a full fae to take you is often the next most direct route. Some gates open and close at different times of the year/day, but without studying them can be unpredictable. Possession of a Paradox Periwinkle remains to be one of the most reliable methods to cross over when a hedge gate opens. While some mortal can cross realms, rift diving is often attributed to other many other planes, not just the Twixt and Twain. Additionally, another method to cross to the Twixt and Twin is notated in a red and gold covered book in the Guild of Academics - the fae Magpie appears to have a pocket that goes directly to Paradox, though how to return to Adelrune is very unknown in this method of transportation.

Topic: can hedge-gates be manually created by mortal means? how does one acquire a paradox periwinkle?
column5: While it is *possible* to open gate ways/portals/hedge gates between various places, to create one manually in a place that is not a thinned veil between planes is exceedingly difficult and requires at the very minimum a ritual to do so, or more often a ritual with a severe cost to do so (see: the Witch of the Witherwood ripping a hole in reality between the realm of flesh and death). Rather, crossing over at places where natural hedgegates or thin veils appear semi-regularly is more common - such as it is much easier to cross between the realms of flesh and death during the fall, or realms of flesh and the twixt/twain in summer when the fae court is most often held. Paradox Periwinkle, when acquired to cross the realms, typically requires some sort of thin-point or waypoint to cross over, often these are the areas where one would "fall out of" Paradox on accident, or wander in on accident, but how and where these are located is most unpredictable unless you know what you are looking for.

Topic: seeking the name or details of the dragon that lives under the Diamond island of Cole (paired with Guidance rite)
column5: The dragon who lives beneath the Diamonds' island in Cole has a number of titles he is known by, though there is no true record of his name and he will not speak it. Most often humans call him "the One Who Waits," as he waits patiently in the darkness of his cave for any who attempt to enter his territory will be met with fierce protectiveness of the space. While others (typically dragon-blooded) simply call him the "Grieving Guardian", for the golden-hued scales around his eyes are marred with a deep bronze color patterning, giving him the appearance that is features are permanently tear-stained.

Topic: how advanced are the quality of Myre stitchers, what are some customs and practices of the puppeteer district? is dream magic involved?
column5: In general, these questions would require Native Lore: Myre to answer, as it is not information that is to be found in the Guild of Academics nor any other library on Adelrune since no one has had the opportunity to study and record their knowledge of Myre at this time (as it just emerged from the Mists recently).  As for the quality of Stitchers, it can be reasoned that because the Stitcher's Guild has had two hundred years of uninterrupted and undisturbed study within the Mists, that the skill of the Stitcher's Guild of current Myre will FAR surpass the stitcher creations the world was familiar with before the Mists. Two hundred years of focused experimentation would allow any craft to advance by untold lengths from what was known before. A reasonable guess would be that unless the creator chooses to give them a tell-tale mark of being a stitcher creation, they will likely still have marks of Myren gauze (burlap), though it's possible some particular exceptional Stitchers would choose not to have their monsters have any defining features at all.

Topic: customs/practices/rites/holidays associated with Thte?
column5: Thte is honored during most cultural holidays rather than religious, by the Old Ways cultures that are not Cestral. Performing feats of strength, drinking toasts to his strength and the strength of the competitors,  or activities such as tree cutting with axes or rock splitting with hammers depending on culture. Battle cries are always to Thte and his name is invoked in times of terrible weather or terrible disasters to stand against Balaeish with them. Rites of War and Protection are most heavily associated with Thte, and to a lesser extent Community as he is always accompanied by Wolf his most trusted and loyal companion.

Topic: how do I learn what a demon's vice is?
column5: There are a number of ways to learn a demon's vice. One might begin with studying a demon's routine and habits - what are they often drawn to most? For demons who are known to the world, it is possible that the Silver Chalice or other scholar of demonic study may have such information. Such information may also be learned from a rival or enemy demon who wouldn't mind trading that sort of information, for a price, of course.

Topic: when a celestial or demon becomes a god, what happens to their connection to their native realm? are they severed, or do they remain connected?
column5: When a celestial becomes a god, they remain connected to the Golden Realms, but they are no longer in the same hierarchy or power-structure. Taking on divinity, a celestial takes a side-step from the standard hierarchy, so while they're still accepted in celestial circles and affected by celestial things, they are not part of celestial culture itself.

Topic: how do full celestials form families? how are parents and brothers or sisters actually connected?
column5: This can vary wildly by the Celestial. In general, Celestials created in and living within the Golden Realms do not have parents, and thus would refer to all other full celestials as brother and sister. For those who choose to Descend, this is often also the case, and they would typically use the term Brother and Sister as siblings in a Choir. True family lines are only created when two full Celestials who have Descended create a Born celestial between them, and thus the Born would have true parents and true siblings as mortal races would. The Born, being created on Adelrune, are bound to the laws of the world, and so when they die they would go to the Spirit Realm as any mortal would, even as they are full celestials, unless they are able to Transcend and join their greater "family" in the Golden Realms.

Topic: is the Lady of the Mists still a demon? is she still connected to the Abyss?
column5: Yes, the Lady of Mists is still and remains a demon. Ultimately, by taking on divinity she steps out of the line of demon hierarchy in a lateral move, and thus while still accepted in demon circles and susceptible to demonic influence, she is not a threat to the abyssal monarchs or lower ranks as she is outside the structure understood by and between demons.

Topic: looking into the disappearance in a local town, where did they go? who took them? what are they doing?
column5: As this is an active, ongoing story and not something that can be found in the history books of a Library, you would need to pursue this line of questioning through influence spends or in-game actions.

Topic: what are the physical materials and techniques necessary to repair Ig?
column5: As Ig's cover has been full separated from it's physical pages and the pages in unknown places, there is nothing that can be done physically at this time.  In the Guild of Academics, you will find a notation from former Guildmistress Caeli Oakenthorn discussing the destruction of the Book of Nox at the hands of Sir Volk of the White Chalice, and how the spirit of Nox lept into the recently-deceased body of a Returned called Kuro. The notes are short and bulleted, mentioning Kuro hunting the Returned in the ensuing months, his capture of Laurel Bay and removal of her hand, the eventual death of Kuro's body, and much later in different ink and a less-hurried hand, the emergence of the Book of Nox once more as it took the time it required to reform itself on its own and come back to the Returned. It is reasonable to expect the same can be done of Ig, who took Esdaline as a human host and was able to reconnect to the spirits of its pages, and in time will reform his physical book and pages.

Topic: (2 actions) Can the Glass Sword be reforged if broken? Why can the blade only be used by newly returned and children?
column5: Should the Glass Sword ever be broken, it could theoretically be reforged in the Dreaming, but to known history that has never happened. As the sword of Mordecai, forged in the Dreaming, it is bound to to his own values and thus dictates who may wield it - the children, and those that are weakest. For those who have the ability to wield it, they often have glimpses of a backlit maestro and the sense of a stage performance of children.

Topic: Trahazi Relic that called to Val in Whitehall
column5: It has taken a few months of study and Valdoma spending time with the guitar, but eventually with time, the following latent properties have been unlocked with the guitar that was recovered from the vault in Whitehall.  Heart's Strings: guitar This guitar smells of campfire and its strings seem to carry the laughter of a whole caravan. This guitar is soulbound to its founder, cannot be replicated, stolen, or given away. It does not expire. - Living Artifact - Free-Willed  Out of game note: you do not need to carry the actual guitar with you to use this tag, merely wear a simple string bracelet to represent one of its strings.

Topic: how do true names of full fae work?
column5: True names of full fae work as true names of any extraplanar beings do, they serve as a tether, allowing the being to walk on the mortal plane of Adelrune. True names are dangerous - they can be used to summon, to banish, to harm (when etched into a sword with blood ink), to protect against (when etched into a shield), and to control extra planar beings through ritual or magic. Any being that is native to a plane other than the mortal plane have a true name. They are not names that can be spoken, nor are they of a particular language, but they are most often drawn in some format in order to attach them as a tether.

Topic: how might we find evidence of the Tear of Esparei in the Dreaming? is there a relationship between the Tears of Esparei and Theddespari? how might it be related to the Dirim (well of origins)?
column5: While there is no true evidence of the exact location of the Tear of Espari tied to the Dream, there are a number of theories that suggest it's physcial form would have taken on a different shape if it was hidden within the Dreaming. As the Tears are a liquid on the mortal plane, it is theorized that it would keep some level of that influence, if not the exact same form, within the Dreaming and of all the dream cities, the one with the most connection to potions and liquids is A'fadiin, the city of alchemists, and the most mundane of the Dream cities, further suggesting that it is more grounded in reality than the others.

column5: There are no active records of Sistinas worship to be found. However as Sistinas' fortress predates the Amalgamation and the Church of Chorus, it is likely that any evidence of such worship was erased alongside knowledge of the Hazori and other local religions of the area during the Purification.
