Topic: how can we flush Teppidus out of the volcano?
column5: Teppidus rarely ventures out of the volcano, most often being seen when something has caused him to erupt the volcano and he ventures out to destroy something more thoroughly than just through the blasts of the volcanic ash and lava. Theoretically, the arrival of an ice demon or blue/white dragon might force him to emerge, but none have been foolish enough to venture to the island in recorded history.

Topic: what are Teppidus' weaknesses? (elemental, ice, sorcery, etc)
column5: As Teppidus is a full celestial, his obvious weakness is gold, and being associated with fire and heat, he would have weakness to ice. It is also known that he is either immune to or healed by fire.

Topic: what connection/similarities do Deneial the Effendal tribe and Deneial the Mist Goddess have? is Deneial the Mist goddess a fae?
column5: Deep in the shelves of the Guild of Academics you find a well-worn and filled journal with theories and more recent history written within its pages. Unlike many of the other books, which are often on individual subjects, this one meanders and rambles, drawing connections to different cultures, across religions, planes, and races. Much of the theoretical content you find on the subject you seek are found within those pages, the factual data is notated as to its source and is able to be cross-checked in other tomes as well.   There is no historical evidence of Deneial, the Mist Goddess, being fae in origin, beyond Returned notation that fae of that name of origin once split themselves into multitudes that would become the Effendal. In fact, it is quite well-known in the world that Deneial is a demonic god, called the "Queen of Beasts" and "Enslaver of Desire", it is known that she will capture, enslave, and eventually devour both fae and mortal alike if they have enough power to tempt her. This is how she came to be known as the "Mist Goddess," after all, for the Mists were not that long ago the purview of a particular fae called Lyyt who was captured by some of the earliest Returned, led by a heretical Vicaul called Iiyanger, and fed to her. The Mist Goddess both expects and demands obedience from her followers, and will not suffer someone who disagrees with her approach to conquering the great seas atop the back of a great spiked wyrm. Theoretically, the effendal tribe Deneial was once located far to the east, near the rough seas which are rumored to be threatened by the storms of the great Destruction of Balaiesh (the closest ally of Deneial the Mist Goddess), and for the tribe of Deneial, the truth upon which their purpose in the world is built is "of blind obedience." On a page that has a map of the Eastern Expanse with the ancient Effendal homesteads marked, a line is drawn from Deneial territory (which overlaps the Ko'aat plains) to the sea between the Vicaul lands and Cestral, where a black sea monster is drawn. In the margin a theory is notated: "possible connection: Deneial is demon (maybe mixed effendal?) from Age of Tane, sought power (with effendal? stole name?), refused to follow with blind obedience. fled to the sea after an incident like the Witch of the Witherwood? need to find someone with ancient knowledge to confirm. all records destroyed during the Purification

Topic: what is the nature of Inya/Kesa's binding to the tarot? is it her divinity or her soul?
column5: Gods do not have souls in the same way humans or other metaphysical beings do, for such beings with souls have a destination the soul goes to when the body dies. Gods do not have this, and thus it is not a soul bound within the Tarot but she herself, as Haelo was once bound within an orb. The nature of the binding is impossible to discover in research, as it predates recorded history, and one would need to know who bound her to the Tarot in the first place and why in order to discern that. That she is bound within something that offers guidance and order to those who seek out the Lady Order, might suggest however, that it was not a cruel binding to lock her away from the world in malevolence, as she is still able to act in some manner.

Topic: Investigate History: did the history of War's End change?
column5: With time still shifting and settling uncertainly from the Mists, the one thing that has begun to settle is that, while War's End was abandoned much the way it had been before, many many more survivors took to the Liberator when the settlement was abandoned. While it's ultimate fate remains unknown, the stories and sightings have changed and are now much more frequent than they were before. It is said that not only does the Liberator make its ghostly apperance, there have been many more regular appearances of another mysterious ship around Tear that can seemingly traverse the Mists. And when Dacian settlers once landed upon the Isles of Ire to found a settlement in the middle of the Torrent, there was evidence of a settlement that had been established and then abandoned at some point in the previous century.

Topic: Bestiary Analysis: Abyss Spiders
column5: Abyss Spiders are as one would expect from the name - spiders who came to Adelrune from the Abyss. Whether as eggs brought to Adelrune and hatched here, or one of the ancient spiders crawling out of the Abyss to find a new source of food and nesting ground, it is difficult to know which came first. These creatures are considered ""demonic"" for the Abyssal origins, but are not classified as demons.  The life of the Abyss spider develops through five stages: egg, hatchling, juvenile (hand-sized), adult (person-sized), and ancient (approximately 10-15 feet tall). Typically Hatchlings stay close to their mother as they are in need of blood to feed on for their first few months of life. The blood they feed on at this stage will create a life-long bond that remains important to the spider even after they've grown and moved on from their mother's nest. Juvenile spiders tend to be the most troublesome as they are exploring the region to find a mate and a new home - these are typically the ones that will be found wandering into town or encountered in well-used tunnels, and will be aggressive as they do not know yet what are threats and what are not. By the time abyss spiders reach adulthood, they typically have found a mate and a seclude place to nest - ancient ruins, old abandoned tunnels, and other places away from people and cities is where nests are most likely to be found, and it is at this stage they can begin to lay eggs of their own. Ancient abyss spiders are exceptionally rare, as their size means they must move on to find a new shelter as they exit their reproductive years - it is when an an adult becomes an ancient that the blood bonds they formed with their hatchlings become important, for it is up to the offspring to care for and tend to the ancient spiders in their older years, and when they die the offspring feed on them. Because of this cycle, it is extremely rare to find a deceased specimen or even evidence of an ancient abyss spider unless you accidentally stumbled upon one.  Abyss Spiders are extremely loyal creatures to those they are blood bonded with, and have been shown to be clever and even trainable though it is excessively difficult and many people have died accidentally from attempting this. Rumors among the Returned have suggested that they can control the minds of their handlers, and past experiments by the Returned have shown that it is a very poor idea to attempt to incubate an abyssal spider egg inside a humanoid body as it will have catestrophic results for the host.

Topic: Inv. Rumors: Abyss spider nest locations or people selling abyss spider eggs
column5: - Rumor has it, Archdruidess Caeli Oakenthorn has raised multiple Abyss Spiders and allowed them to take over an entire wing of the Guild of Academics in Solace. - Rumor has it, the best source of Abyss Spider Eggs and knowledge is Bartholomew Berrington, a traveling trader who is nearly impossible to track down. - Rumor has it, there are a dozen different abyss spider nests around the territory of Port Frey, representing nearly every elemental alignment of spider. - Rumor has it, the rarest of the Abyssal spiders is the giant silver abyssal spider of Eur. - Rumor has it, you probably shouldn't go poking around abyss spider nests - the adults are extremely territorial. - "Didya hear? That druid girl been rais'in her own little mind spiders?" "Like it lays eggs in yer Mind?" "No idea, but if she starts acting funny, you'll know why." "Acting funny like rais'in spiders as pets?" "Yup." "Mind Spiders."

Topic: Military Insight: C'thon
column5: C'thon Darkweaver leads one of the largest demonic forces outside of Mehs, with nearly 10,000 demons under his command, and he is continually building his forces. Among his most trusted ranks are exceptional necromancers, who are now collecting fallen bodies from around Tear to make permanent undead with enhanced strength, stamina, and durability. Into these bodies, they are summoning weak and lesser demons because they are easier to drag out of the Abyss - a guaranteed force that cannot turn against him because the necromancers retain power over their bodies.

Topic: Eavesdrop: Bellus
column5: Following the bouts of anarchy (which are quickly seen for what they are among demons who created the concept of sowing anarchy), and many new songs hitting the streets of Mehs, Bellus calls his best bards to his chambers with delighted pleasure to rewrite one of them. Rather than a weak/soft king, he has them adjust the lyrics to that of a "gentle king" who needs little more than a soft guiding hand to inspire loyalty in his followers, rather than violence and tyrrany. With it rewritten, he pays his bards double to sing it at his court.

Topic: Veyn -  revered/worshipped, common rites/practices, recent events?
column5: Veyn is also known as the god of strategists. As Lord of Fresh Water, Veyn is revered as a god of Life along with his brother Ren. But for the Ko'aat, Gael, Veyn, and Vicaul the blessing of fresh water often comes with equally destructive spring snow melt and summer flooding. Because of this, these cultures favor holidays to Veyn in the spring and summer seeking his favor to reduce damage and loss to their home and lands during the flooding. Rites typically associated with Veyn are Absolution, Life, Guidance, and Mysticism. However, he will also be called upon in the name of War as the patron god of strategists. Which is why he is still known to and revered (though not worshipped) by the Mandalans, for their elaborate use of the waterways of their region, a hold over from an ancient era when they were still Gael.

Topic: what are the Miracles of Chorus? are they specific or just any kind of generic miracle? can they be done with normal magics or are they beyond it?
column5: Miracles of Chorus are not specific miracles that must be performed by the Voice but they are actions that reflect divine providence of blessings. Different Voices have had different miracles. These miracles are beyond normal magics such as great acts of weather, great purifications of disease or water supplies, healing crops after plague or famine, a great landslide in defense of an invading force for example. Ultimately if it is considered a Miracle of Chorus it is judged by the Council of Cardinals.

Topic: Political Insight: the New Voice
column5: The new Voice has proven to be charismatic and drawn a lot attention to themselves in recent months. Many of the disinfranchised soldiers of the Amalgamation and the War of Radiance have rallied to his side partiocularly those who have been dominated or turned to the side of Belarathru to make amends for their service on the demon side of the war. With the dissolutionsionment and the loss of De'Fey and Voi and White Hall, these soldiers are clinging to the last vestiges of the church to help navigate a new world. The new Voice preaches Absolution and Unity by calling those together who are searching for something greater than themselves. At this point, they are stationed in the ruins of Cyrus and have no establishment alliances with the Eastern Alliance or any other nation. But more information on this is unavailable due to the Mists. Note: it would be expected that their nearest neighbor Hastings is not likely allied with them.

Topic: Investigate History: Ryed
column5: Ryed, called The Weaver, was the child of Esparei and Nero, and sister of Eirie and Dietra.  One of the lesser gods of the Celestine Pantheon, she was considered the goddess of Poetry, Sculpture, and Architecture. Over the Yule holidays of 8r, the world shook with her unexpected death. While attributed initially to "the god killing force" it was later revealed to the Returned that her death had been manipulated by an being whose name has since been forgotten to memory.

Topic: Investigate History: Sistinas Fortress in the new timeline
column5: Sistinas Fortress was an Amalgamation Fortress in the Kingdom of Voi, overlooking the seat to the east in defense against Vicaul raids upon the coastal villages. It is unclear when the Fortress was founded as it predates the Amalgamation and the Kingdom of Voi, but it stood as a pillar upon the cliffs until a tidal wave in 6ar knocked out its foundation when Star dove into the sea. Over the years, the fortress hosted the Eastern Army as well as the Inquisition, both in defense of the coastline but also as a staging point for conflicts with the Ko'aat to the north. As the sea between Eur, Cestral, and the Amalgamation has a history of catastrophic storms and damaging weather patterns, it is no surprise that there were old, ancient inscriptions upon the fortress warding against storms and providing protection for those who live in the coastal villages when the winds were too strong and tides were too high.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Shadowmyst Festival
Investigate Wealth: Nadine Empire
column5: The Nadine Empire is extremely wealthy on the whole, rivaling Dace, though they don’t have the reputation for flaunting it. All the noble houses contribute to the Empire financially through taxes, and in return the Empire provides funding for war research - focused on alchemy, siege weaponry, and a variety of projects to benefit the nation. There is a large discrepancy between the wealthy nobles and the average peasant, typically because most basic laborer jobs are performed by undead that do not need to be paid for their services. While this means those with money are able to retain more of their income, it also means that the peasantry tend to take jobs that are more specialized - soldiers, scholars, inventors, and craftsmen.

Topic: Political Insight: Pontifex Lady Damek Invictus (Head of Religion)
column5: Lady Damek is particularly skilled in martial combat. The only other person in the Empire to wear 7-pins, if she desired to, she has the ability to rival the Empress by adjusting the teachings of the Blood Caudron, but she doesn’t. Middle-aged, her loyalty has been tested and found to be absolute. She has been zealous in her devotion to the Empress that borders on obsession since she was a child. From a lesser line of Invictus, the Church was her way to a stronger political position, and she has a soft spot for other strong-willed women who are seeking greater power for themselves. As long as their loyalty is in the Empress, of course.

Topic: Political Insight: Lord Miklas Trevinian (Head of Finance)
column5: Born to House Trevinian in the Kingdom of Hastings, Miklas was brought up learning to read trade arrangements and arithmetic in finances. Once in the employ of King Arthur Hastings, following his execution by the Church of Chorus, Miklas was in search of a better position. Young and single, he quickly recognized he would not reach the levels he wanted to in Hastings, so he cut his losses and fled the Amalgamation during the Rebellion, seeking out a more favorable position in the Nadine Empire. Possessing lofty ambitions, Miklas quickly proved his worth by betraying Jenavieve Swordmaker and providing information obtained from her brother for the Empress. He was used as a key player to bring credibility to the Empress’s “peace summit” with the Amalgamation kings. He is ruthless in business dealings and has a well-trained mind for making smart financial decisions, and had no qualms in selling his skills to the highest bidder - which proved to be the Empress. Those skills served him well in the Empire, where less than a decade after his defection, Miklas Trevinian was promoted to the Nadine Empire’s Head of Finance. With no strong ties or loyalties to any of the Nadine noble families, he is skilled at playing them against one another to the benefit of the Empire. While he is known to have expanded the Empire’s trade with Dace in a number of seemingly-risky financial bets, the coffers of the Nadine Empire have only grown during his tenure in the position.

Topic: Political Inisght: Lord Evzen Atius (Royal Chancellor)
column5: As the third son of lesser nobles of a minor house, Evzen Atius had no true inheritance or position promised to him upon birth, he was determined to forge his own path and focus on taking any opportunity that was available to him to work hard. As such, Evzen has worked twice as hard as most nobles to get a fraction of what they’ve been provided - this has manifested in serving nearly every noble house at some point in time and building deep, reliable connections within them. He does not have charm or even true ambition to lean on, just a strong work ethic and a desire to be known for his own reputation and not that of his house. Being compared to his family or having assumptions made about him for his family name is truly his greatest pet peeve in life. With a reputation for reliability and fairness, he was often sought to arbitrate disputes between noble houses even before reaching the position of Royal Chancellor, which he was recommended for unanimously by every noble house. As focused on his work as he is, his personal life is exceedingly dull because he does not have time for it.

Topic: Political Insight: Lord Kranus Kulgoth
column5: Not simply just a seneschal, he’s an Imperial logistics officer. Kranus is the one who issues all paperwork for the logistical requirements of the Empire, filing paperwork and issuing orders as necessary. The Empress overworks him and he’s constantly flickering through documents and notes for required tasks to accomplish her demands. However, for most, it is not easy to get a direct audience with the Empress, and Kranus revels in playing gatekeeper over what does and doesn’t pass to her ear. He works hard to remain unobtrusive in her life and get everything done as needed, which makes him particularly defensive of his position. He often finds annoyance with the Empress’s lovers and the fact they get handed the First Legion as a reward for merely holding her interest, and his position means he is one of the few who can have an attitude with them and put them in their place, because they cannot complain to her about him without sounding like petulant children. Curiously, though he has the title of Lord, there is no known noble family of House Kulgoth.

Topic: Follow the Money: Bank of the Dead
column5: The Bank of the Dead is fully funded by the Skull Children, and became a bank as a result of Unity imposing some order over a group of kids who have unspeakable amounts of wealth within Port Frey. It's well known that their intial funding from the bank came from graverobbing.

Topic: Poltiical Insight: Bank of the Dead
column5: The Bank of the Dead has a lot of clout and power in Port Frey for the wealth they have, and that power has only grown with the disintigration of Unity and having no oversight. While other merchants and organizations are struggling against one another for territory, the Bank of the Dead is already on top of controlling the merchant district of Port Frey as they have the means to just hire local guards to protect their holdings and people. It is expected that the Bank of the Dead will stay on top with whatever the new government situation is, unless something drastically changes in the near future.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Bank of the Dead
column5: The Bank of the Dead is strongly ingrained in the underworld, as it is the more legitimized front for the Skull Children. Made up of graverobbers and children, however, the Skull Children have drastically different interests and methods than most of the other local gangs and underworld organizations. While they have a lot of political and financial clout, they're generally considered small time in the underworld.

Topic: Do dream constructs (Newborn Dreams) have souls? Can they have souls? How do their souls differ from the souls of the Returned?
column5: New Born Dreams are creations of the Dreaming, and thus do not have souls in the sense that those native to Adelrune would. When their tethers to this reality all break, they fracture and return to the realm of the Dreaming, rather than a soul moving on to the realms of death as the mortal beings of Adelrune would.

column5: Without an extensive study of portals, it is difficult to get much information to do a deep comparison of the different types of porals. However, you are able to find a treatise written by Aleister Mordaine regarding Drir Portals and how they utilize blood magic/blood ritual, summoning, and many other magics. Drir portals function by cutting through different realms, and when unstable can just dump the traveler  in another random plane. As such, Drir portals are super dangerous and when not handled properly by a highly trained Drir, can unleash dangerous monsters/creatures from other planes into Adelrune. Hedge Gates, however, are highly unpredictable and the result of when a fae left a door open behind them while traveling through planes and time. They remain open for an unknown, and inconsistant length of time.

Topic: We researched the reliquary head (I have the actual tag and a phys rep) and asked 3 questions about it:  (1) What magical knowledge is stored inside this reliquary head?  (2) How can we extract it?  (3) Is there any way to put the souls to rest that were trapped inside this Reliquary?
column5: (1) What magical knowledge is stored inside this reliquary head?  -There is a massive amount of magical knowledge stored inside the reliquary head, but it seems with time and without the magic that keeps the reliquaries bound being maintained, the knowledge is beginning to dissipate. From what can be gleaned, it seems to be all manner of summoning, necromancy, and a few bits from other schools of magic as well. Most of it seems to be knowledge relevant to constructing, powering, controlling, and maintaining reliquaries.  (2) How can we extract it?  -Currently the returned dont have a way to extract the knowledge. Attempts at doing so can potentially destroy the knowledge stored inside. In particular, it is known that Xolo is able to read some if not all of what knowledge lies within.   (3) Is there any way to put the souls to rest that were trapped inside this Reliquary? -The souls were bound in such a way that releasing them would destroy the souls forever. There is currently no good way to release the souls, but they can potentially be "reassigned".

Topic: Slaughter Livestock: For the family of Terrance, the Trahazi
Investigate Loved Ones: Terrance of the Trahazi
column5: Terrance is merely a child, and while he doesn't have parents of his own, he has many members of the Melnyk caravan who look after him and take care of him. He lives with an elderly member of the caravan who he calls "grandfather" but it is clear they do not share blood, and will often walk slower than a child normally would in order to walk with the older man to make sure he does not fall. It's almost impossible to say which of the two is actually responsible for looking after the other, but they do certainly care for one another. Terrance also has a beloved rooster called Rusty that he'll often carry around, but the rooster is blind and cares not for what time of the day it is, when it chooses to crow it does - midday or midnight, it is all the same.

Topic: Investigate History: Beings known as Cyrus
column5: Cyrus was once known as the "Living City". The daughter of Cinorast and Eur, it was believed she was the soul of the city of Cyrus, a city built by the forebearers of the Trahazi people, and was once called the goddess of architecture. It is believed that Cyrus was devoured during the Feast of Souls, when Star and the celestial Aidhreal battled in the ruins of the city as the Ko'aat hordes overran the countryside during the War of Radiance.

Topic: Investigate Wealth: Unity
column5: Unity never had an excess of wealth. Taxes were levied on the member nations based on how much of the population in Solace came from their nation, and how those taxes were paid varied nation to nation from coin (Dace) to soldiers (Gael), to goods of value. Since the Mists fell over the world, many member nations were not paying in and so Unity tightened its purse strings and made due. The leaned heavily on port taxes paid from the International Merchant's Guild, and individual nations who work with the Bank of Paloma sought lending to pay their taxes with the expectation everything would balance out as the Mists continued to clear.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Unity logistics
column5: As of February and the vote to remove Unity, the dismantling and clearing out of Unity offices and storehouses was underway within the week, as politicians were not keen to hang around with no laws in effect and no protections under the Rules of Society. By April and the establishment of New Solace, they were down to the final stages of clearing out the Embassy with only a skeleton crew of seneschals and workers still in residence to pack away last paperwork and get on their way. Since this spend isn't focused on a specific person, Vices is not an applicable inquiry, and routines were that the seneschals were working diligently to get done as quickly as possible, then drinking together at a tavern down the road from the Embassy in the evenings as they commiserated over the problem of relocating to new postings and for those without homes to return to, what to do with their families.

Topic: Investigate History: Ko'aat/Trahazi relations
column5: Descended from the Hazori people, the historical enemy of the Ko'aat prior to the rise of the Amalgamation, the Trahazi are still treated coldly and as enemies to the Ko'aat. Even following the conquering of the Hazori by the Amalgamation kingdoms, the Ko'aat only watched and believe they got what they deserved, for the Hazori are those who first took much of the land of the Eastern Expanse and relegated the Ko'aat to their corner of the world. Dominant interactions between the Trahazi and the Ko'aat include underworld trade for it is uncommon for the enemy cultures to work together. However, as the Trahazi look down on money even more than the Ko’aat do, the main positive interaction is generally observed when Ko’aat trade woven grass puppets to Trahazi caravans in exchange for trinkets to be woven into horse manes or brightly-colored clothing for their grass dolls. Trading more valuable items with the Trahazi is less common and frowned upon, thus it is only seen among the underworld types. Dolls for doll-textiles, however, is a more common swap.

Topic: Investigate History: when did the blood lords show up in Nadine politics? what actions do they perform? Conflicts with other groups?

They directly serve Her Will and take their orders directly from her alone. While not in active conflict with anyone in particular, because no one would DARE to have conflict with them, no one wants them around because their presence does not bode well for anyone living.  As opposed to the Blood Lords with their direct siring, vampires within the Knights Sanguine and other organizations are often considered "lesser" for they were not sired by the Empress nor Vojin, and thus many have a low-level of hatred (and jealousy) of the Blood Lords.

Mehs' army currently includes the remants of the various destroyed demon cities, including the Twisted Legion, the Stone Legion, the Dark Legion, and fresh new demons that are crawling out of open portals to the Abyss to join in the fight.

In his eyes, the hhearts of men are already corrupt, and it is his duty to usher in the Age of Destruction by destroying enough of the world that it cannot reasonably recover before the Age of Adelrune comes to a close - beginning with what humanity relies on - food stores, crop fields, and water sources. With Bellus' army already under his leadership, there are few demons who disagree with his sentiment as it is much more fun than Bellus' sly trickery and backdoor dealings.

Topic: Investigate History: healing dragons
column5: In general, most dragon injuries can heal on their own with a long, deep nap. Typically the cultists or followers of said dragon would keep the sleeping creature safe for up to hundreds of years. For particularly damaging or poisoned woulds inflicted by other dragons or powerful entities, the wounds may take additonal healing or focused care to ease recovery, but most standard wounds will heal on their own in time.

Topic: what form does the Tear of Espari take in the Dreaming?
column5: While it is impossible to know exactly what form the Tear of Espari takes in the Dreaming, the Dreaming is known to be more fluid, and when connected to the city of alchemy it has been hypothecized that it may take on a more liquid form.

Topic: how contagious is the Myren plague? how is it transmitted? what does it do?
column5: Without Native Lore: Myre, what you can gather is that Myre seems to be a nation of bespoke, manufactured plagues rather than a single "Myren Plague". These diseases seem to spread quite easily from close contact with any Myren citizen (curiously not Returned Myren, however), though they don't seem to show any symptoms or signs of being infected themselves. As such, there is no single source or method of transmission, and you'd need to isolate a particular disease in a test subject to do any real study on it or work towards a cure.

Topic: Since the Veil separates the planes, what happens wehre there are holes( rifts, portals, etc)? Does it matter if the hole is temporary or long-lasting? stable or unstable?
column5: Holes in the Veil are points where it is easier to pass through between Planes. In some cases this happens naturally, as in the fall the Plane of Death comes closer to Adelrune to ease the passing betwene planes. Typically rifts will radiate ambiant damage in the vicinity of the rift (in the case of the Witherwood, Raven's Eye, etc), and the longer the gates are open the more damage spills out into the world as a long-open portal is more of a festering wound. As such, unstable, short-term portals are more healthy for the environment in general (like a superficial wound, different areas take a little damage) vs. stable portals are much more damaging as they are causing repetitive damage in the same area (like keeping wound open to suffer infection).

Topic: Alexander. the lineage of Vojin and the pact between the Abyss and Adelrune?

Topic: what entities can navigate through the borders of the Dreaming? what is past the borders of the dreaming?
column5: The realm of the Dreaming touches nearly every living being in their dreams, and thus anyone can cross into the Dreaming in their sleep. To move between the realm of the Dreaming and the Waking, however, is a more complex situation, typically reserved for only the strongest Dream Entities unless there is a stable bridge to pull them across (tethers, etc).

Topic: research: what are examples of magical songs in Trahazi history?
column5: Magic within songs is deeply rooted in nearly every culture upon Adelrune. While it was the Mandalan culture that developed out of the Gael due to the importance of music into their culture and the development of musical instruments, song was very important in the early development of farming among what would become the Hazori. Music was not simply a method of which to find the proper rhythm for sowing and harvest, or to pass the day under the sun, but it was worship in giving to Baba and through her blessing the rich soils of the Eastern Expanse. Music was woven with protections and wards against Vesh and Demenor. A unified voice with which they stood against the Ko'aat. Music was and is entangled in rituals, in liturgies repeated and passed down through generations, in rites and practices of faith. And as the Exile became the past and the Trahazi no longer lived upon the lands and soil they had long sung their words into, song became their protection. Both in the mundane sense of lifting spirits and setting the Amalgamation peasantry at ease, but also in the magical way of travel and protections from outside dangers. It is said that music allows those who know it to pass between planes, though there is no true written record to be found of this among the Trahazi. It is said that rituals exist that while the song plays on and the dancers remain in perfect step and rhythm, no danger from the outside can penetrate the song. The truth of these practices, however, is known only to those who actively participate in them.

Topic: is it possible to reverse the energy of the piece of the Head of Hexameran to turn it into a vector for restoration instead of disease, and if so, what would be the mechanism for this polarity shift?
column5: Typically to reverse the polarity of a demon or celestial, particularly taking a demon of disease and turning to towards restoration/healing, it must be alive because that shift is a metaphysical one tied directly to the process of severing/being redeemed, or falling. As the Head of Hexameran is still creating/spreading faint disease, leaning into that entropic matter with more negativity and power could potentially give it the ability to destroy diseases, germs, and additionally all living things within a radius of the head. As a corpse, demon bodies can be harvested to make many reagents and ritual components for a variety of effects, and in particularly the Head of Hexameran would be capable of being a Prime Alchemical Catalyst.

Topic: RESEARCH: NEEDS NEW TOPIC
stories of the Zodiac gods manifesting in the material plane and interacting with the Trahazi
column5: It is generally a known fact that the gods of Adelrune live within their own realm and are not meant to be walking upon the material plane. While there certainly are stories of those who sneak in (like Deroan) to cause mischief, or who crawl out of the depths (the demon gods), it is widely accepted that these are the very rare cases. While there are many stories of the Trahazi gods as family and the belief is strong that the family is always with them, there are no stories of the gods physically manifesting to interact with the Trahazi people on Adelrune.

Topic: city fortifications and defenses of Myre
column5: Dominantly the first (and only recorded) defense of Myre is its Walls of Distortion. The walls that surround the city cause sickening effects, disorienting those who come near. Although physically the passages through these walls are short and straight, the influence of the Warped King on these gateways dramatically alter the senses. Over a walk of maybe ten feet, you may feel like you are moving miles with each step, changing directions every second. You may lose all sense of up and down, hear the screaming of ancient wars, smell rotting flesh combined with freshly baked chocolate, or something equally disorienting. No one’s passage through the walls is the same, nor are any one person’s experiences consistent if they travel in and out of the city. There is only one person rumored to have successfully passed through the Walls of Distortion, at great risk to his companions who all perished in the process, and that route was supposedly sealed afterwards.

Topic: What did the fae get out of the deal with the Smiling Prince?
column5: In bygone days, when shadows grew, A tale of woe, in darkness true, The kingdom Myre, once strong and fair, Now faced an enemy's deadly snare.   The prince, in fear, sought fae's embrace, A pact in darkness, a path to trace. He offered the children, pure and young, To wield the fae's power, wickedly sprung.   Time flowed like rivers, Myre's light did fade, Its people scattered, in shadows played. But hundreds of years, a twist did unfurl, The fae, in magic, rewove the world.   In realms beyond, where time was spun, Myre awakened, like a rising sun. Fae's power called the kingdom back, From history's grasp, freed from the attack.   A child emerged, from distant past, Myre's redemption, a spell finally cast. The child, a beacon, fate's own decree, A chance for Myre's salvation, yet to be.

Topic: Does the death of a demon terminate an oath or contract?
column5: As the "death" of a demon is rarely a true death (destruction of the demon soul), it entirely depends on the oath/contract and whether or not the soul/bloodline is bonded in the contract, or if the contract needs to be physically enforced on the mortal plane.

Topic: Mehs and potential vices
column5: While factual evidence of the vices of a demon are rare, scholarly theory says that a fire demon - particularly one of great power - likely carries a vice in the vein of power, impatience, impulsiveness, and even destruction.

Topic: House Bendriven history (Antioc)
column5: Without Lore: Antioc, the ancient knowledge of House Bendriven before the Crusades is mostly lost to time. It is known that the last ruler of the city of Antioc was the Lord General Bryndon "The Bull" Bendriven, who left his homeland in 3ch to lead the armies of Adelrune in the Crusades, and died with his men at the Final Battle. The Bendriven family line has always been an integral part of Antioc society, back to its initial founding alongside House Tigaris and House Trevinian.  One of the most notable accomplishments of Bryndon Bendriven before the Crusades was the moment he led the armies of Antioc against the Ko'aat, dealing a particularly devestating blow that crippled their military following the fall of House Ra'e'len and Theddespari, so they could not rise against Antioc again in his lifetime.

Topic: How is the lady of the Mist tied to the fae? since demons don't have aspects of reality like fae, where does the ocean aspect come from? if the lady of mists is a demon, what would her vice be?

There is nothing that suggests the Mist Goddess controls the aspect or dominion of the ocean itself. She is known to be tied more strongly to concepts like Domination, Enslavement, and Subjugation, which scholars theorize are likely connected to or suggestive of what her Vice would be as a demon.

Topic: What rare, exotic or psychedelic ingredients would be usefull in a cocktail, alchemical brew, or recipe for bitters? Are there any notable ones that are native to/popular in/traded through Cole or Tear? How would one go about harvesting/obtaining them

As a one-off, I will be happy to provide you a tag for this on the following conditions: Collect the base ingredients by expending two levels of Mercantile check-in skills for the event (ie do not collect 2 levels of tags at check-in), as well as collecting tags for cherries (food), 2 uses of elderflower (herb), and 2 uses of honeysuckle (herb). With the tags, expend an Alchemical crafting action then come see me!

Topic: Political Insight: Hastings
column5: Princess Arielle is a highly polarizing personality on the Eastern Expanse, with few true friends and allies. While she made good in-roads with the peasantry during the Hastings' Rebellion, it is commonly viewed by both the peasantry and nobility that she brought her nation into a terrible war she didn't need to, to the detriment of the Kingdom of Hastings. In truth, she was losing and the Rebellion would have been squashed had they not gotten lucky with the outbreak of the War of Wine refocusing the attentions of the other Kings and the Church. Long, deep grudges are held against her for this, with most blaming her pride for nearly destroying the nation, causing ongoing division and disagreement within the Kingdom. Even with her so-called "allies" in the Kingdom of Vein, such sentiments still stand and relations are tense and tenuous at best, as it is unclear if they are friends or foes on any given day. While she has made big moves to bring more peasantry into her cabinet and built connections with the Churchwood Brotherhood and various other organizations, there is still a great division between the nobility and peasantry that she does not seem to have intention to change for fear of losing what control she has over the Kingdom. While her official stance is that the Kingdom of Hastings does not have a national religion, and is indeed an areligious/secular state, her personal stance is far more irreligious and even anti-faith, going so far as to alienate those who live at Castle Hastings with her unpopular laws against Church assembly within the city that forces those of faith to leave the city to attend rites and services regardless of their faith. It is well-known that she is in need of a husband and an heir to secure her throne properly and persuade the nobility to recognize her as Queen, but in recent years she has denied not only a marriage match with King Rolf Vein-Vercrux, but a number of suitable nobles put forward by the Hastings Archdukes.

Topic: Military Insight: Hastings
column5: The Hastings military is spread across a number of outposts throughout the Kingdom, with garrison troops at Hastings Castle numbering (approximately): 2900 Hastings Guards, 170 Knights (Wielding Magic Items) 6500 Poorly trained peasant Soldiers, 9000 Whitehall soldiers, 190 Spies (from Whitehall), 40 Assassins (Whitehall), 1800 Voi Guards, and 119 White Chalice Knights.  The Western Army, formerly one of the Amalgamation army forces used to defend and engage with enemy forces in times of war, consists of (approximately): 23800 Hastings Guards. 8800 Poorly trained and equiped Soldiers, and 1100 Well Eqippped Knights. The Wall separating Hastings from the Celestine Empire is garrisoned with: 2500 Hastings Guards. The Southwestern Shores are fortified with a fleet of 50 warships, as well as 3300 Hastings Guards.   The Churchwood forest is patrolled by 410 woodsman archers of the Churchwood Brotherhood. At the moment Hastings forces are not enaged in military conflict, though the forewarned attack on the Effendal homestead, as well as the situation at Castle Hastings may cause that to change any day.

column5: Topics being discussed in the Hastings Court: - the destruction of the city and rebuilding efforts - the presence of the Voice of Chorus in the city and the resurgence of Chorus faith - the Princess' prospective husbands (or lack thereof) - red eyes in the forest at night around Castle Hastings, and brutal animal maulings discovered in the morning - massive loss of life during Starfall - the belief that the refugee soldiers of Whitehall who moved to Castle Hastings are cursed

She is heavily disliked by most nobles, particularly her own due to the trouble she caused the nation with the Rebellion - a sentiment led by Archduke Gerard Trevinian. As further fall out from the Hastings' Rebellion, the clergy and military of the Church of Chorus view her with contempt. Due to her own choices and actions, the surviving De'Fey princesses hold her in a very negative light, as does the Kingdom of Vein.

Topic: Political Insight: House Calarco
column5: Formerly a powerful noble family of the Kingdom of Voi, House Calarco has suffered massive setbacks in the last decade along with the rest of their Kingdom. Where they once weilded large plots of land that held extensive vineyards the family cared for over generations, making famous wine that was shipped around the world, they are now religated to standing as Court Nobles in the Kingdom of Vein with only a minor estate and minimal lands within someone else's duchy. Known for their trade connections and negotiation skills, land is not required for House Calarco to serve their new Crown, although even these connections were weakened ten years ago when one of their (arguably) most famous and well-connected members, High Queen Caroline Calarco Cole, was killed during the last Great Game of Cole along with her younger brother Kristoph Calarco who served as her royal guard and the Ace of Hearts. The loss of Sir Tibold Calarco, the Head of the Military, in the dragon attack on Whitehall caused further damage to the House and their connections. Though Dame Morii Calarco and her brother Sir Caradoc Calarco have set themselves as the pillars keeping the House afloat and relavent, the remainder of those who relocated from Voi to Vein are struggling to find their place in the new, and vastly different, society. Their heraldry still bears the colors of their former-Kingdom with a white snake over a crimson crescent moon, on a blue field - a standing testament to what was lost.

Topic: Contact Underworld: Whispering Ghosts
Investigate History: House Vyn'e'va
column5: The histories of the Effendal Houses are particularly difficult to come by as so little of their history overlaps with human scholars who recorded it. Some records, particularly centered around the period of the Purges however, have survived. House Vyn'e'va, known as "They Who Bring the Tide", was the noble house overseeing three tribes: Ven'Lanae "The Storm Crow", Tika'ia "The Sting of the Wasp", and the Vae'Firae "The Rain of Sorrow". House Vyn'e'va was most closely aligned with the sea, and thus hunted sea monsters particularly along the northern coasts of the Expanse. Little is known of House Vyn'e'va before they enter the Purges, but when they did they came in and announced themselves boldly by having the Tika'ia launch a surprise attack on the Celestine Empire, using poisons developed by the Vae'Firae.  Proior to this moment and the assassination of the Celestine King, the Celestine Empire had not engaged in the Purges and had rebuked the Amalgamation for their actions, however the Vyn'e'va saw the Celestines gathering their forces and rather than risk their entering the conflict they struck out first. The Celestine Empire suffered great casualties, but under the leadership of their Princess, the Celestine's retaliated and dealt a devestating blow to the Tika'ia. The remnants of the Tika'ia, cut off from their parent house of Vyn'e'va, fled northwest to the Delfestrae. Years after, the Radolond plead with the Houses that they must flee west, but House Vyn'e'Va, and by House Edanaven rebuke their idea and refuse to yield in the fight.  In time, Cole joined the fight against the Effendal, attacking the Vyn'e'va from the sea, where they had had no opposition up to this point and could engage the land-bound armies with a safe distance to retreat to. Pinned down between Celestine forces and Colish ships, the Vyn'e'va are saved by the Delfestrae who send waves of tethered demons into the Celestine Empire, a distraction which allows Vyn'e'va to withdraw from one conflict.  House Kaelin rangers, making their way north to House Delfestrae, quickly engage the Colish forces, allowing House Vyn'e'va to withdraw from the Western Expanse entirely, abandoning their ancestral home, and reenforcing House Edanaven on the Eastern Expanse. The Radolond finally convince House Delfestrae and House Kaelin that their only option is to flee across the sea, though House Edanaven refuses to leave their ancestral home, and House Vyn'e'va stands with their refusal. However, Vyn'e'va does give the Radolond several boats for their escape, and vows to perform a ritual to aid in their travel, and stop others from pursuing. By 63ch, with the rest of the Houses destroyed or having fled west, the Amalgamation forces consolidate and flank House Vyn'e'Va, and House Edanaven on the Eastern Expanse. Pinned in, House Edanaven, and the Ven'Lanae are crippled in a fight against King Vercrux, forcing House Vyn'e'va to perform a powerful ritual conjuring a tsunami that hits the northern coast of the Amalgamation, engulfing a large portion of King Hastings, and King Voi's armies, but at the cost of destroying the remnants of the House in the process. Some say that the Vyn'e'va merged with the sea, but no Effendal from Vyn'e'va or Edanaven were seen on the Expanse again.
