Topic: Follow the Money: Intl Merchant's Guild
column5: Existing for nearly three centuries, the International Merchant's Guild is well-established and reliable. Much of their income comes from their various trade agreements for the movement of raw materials and the export of finished goods across the world. Expenditures dominantly are focused around the labor costs for operation of their various ports and investments, hazard pay for their crafters and laborers residing in more dangerous territories like Solace, and paying taxes to the multiple nations where they operate ports. In recent years their largest expenditures were the building and operation of the Port Frey Harbor, as well as the rebuilding of it's flagship port at Westport in Cestral, following the destruction of it during the War of Radiance.

Topic: Investigate Wealth: Intl Merchant's Guild
column5: A modern concept like GDP is not possible to be found for any groups on Adelrune, as not everything is counted or measured in a coin value equivalent.  The International Merchant's Guild has connections in every civilized nation and in nearly every port on Adelrune. With trade agreements with each nation, operating ports in various nations that don't have the ability or investment to build and manage their own, and a wide number of affiliated craftsmen and guilds across the world, the International Merchant's Guild has the ability to provide a standard of quality and quantity of product to the affiliates they work with, and taxes paid to the various nations. The Guild is among the wealthiest organizations in the world, surpassing the wealth of smaller, poorer nations.

Topic: Military Insight: Voice of Chorus
column5: The Voice of Chorus currently has: 500 Church soldiers, 1,000 religious fanatics, 2,000 poorly trained peasants, The Exiled Central Army (10,600 Poorly trained and equipped soldiers  who were formerly dominated and forced into Bel'e'athru's service, have joined with The Voice who promised them absolution through service). It is unclear however if any of those who survived Whitehall have joined these numbers.

Topic: Military Insight: Ko'aat
column5: As the Ko'aat are not a unified military force, the most reliable numbers you can gather are those who are currently in residence of Alstai: 11,000 Light Cavalry, 17,000 Light Warriors.

Topic: Military Insight: Cestral
column5: Cestral's military forces number: 57 Knights of the Braid, 1944 mounted Cavalry, 1596 Light Soldiers, and 3066 militia members. King Geir's forces on the north eastern shore of Cestral number: 400 Raiders, 240 Elite Raiders, and 5 Ships.        King Uffe's forces, under the leadership of his cousin, are on the north western shore of Cestral and number: 4,250 Raiders, 31 Ships, and 50 wolves.

Topic: Military Insight: Esturmann Clan
column5: Prior to the Mists falling, the Esturmann clan of Vicaul under King Iver numbered 2,600 Raiders, 56 Ships, and 2,150 Bowmen. The Esturmann clan are considered heretics among the other Vicaul due to their worship of the Lady of the Mists and are able to traverse the Mists as part of their raiding attacks, which they carry out undiscriminantly against Vicaul as well as Cestral.

Topic: Investigate Region: Does Balaiesh roam the seas between Vicaul and Cestral?
column5: Unclear. This area lays behind the Mists, but gods do not typically hang around on Adelrune.

Topic: Investigate Region: Has the city of Cyrus been rebuilt?
column5: Unclear. Parts of the city are occupied, but it is behind Mists and rebuilding a large city like Cyrus will take more than a handful of years.

Topic: Investigate Region: In Deneial homestead currently occupied?
column5: It is difficult to assess the status of the Deneial homestead as it lays behind the Mists and is in a particularly remote location.

Topic: Investigate Region: Is the Voice of Chorus teaching old doctrine to kill non-humans?
column5: No, that is not standard doctrine.

Topic: Investigate Region: Is the Voice of Chorus a threat to the Effendal/Citadel?
column5: No, they're on the other side of the world behind the Mists.

Topic: Military Insight: Mehs' Generals
column5: Mehs' forces are the largest demon army assembled since Bel'e'athru's demon army during the War of Radiance. However, Mehs has many more generals than Bel'e'athru had, at around a dozen. His known Generals that the Returned interacted with in July are: Sablegore, T'tocs, and Moonshroud, however it is speculated that the Returned have interacted directly with at least three more whose names were not known. Not all of the Generals can be defeated or faced through martial prowess, as Mehs knows that was Bel'e'athru's downfall and he won't make that same mistake. The most immediate threat the Returned face is Sablegore, whose armies are not only made of demons, but like Sablegore himself  were willing to seek out more power and attained it through magic. Sablegore's forces currently stand at 2500 "knight" equivalents, and an equal if not double or triple number of lesser forces

Topic: Basic Scouting: Does Thornbane appear to have any allies or forces working with or under her?
column5: There appear to be some humanoid forms in the general area of Castle Tane, but without launching a landing party to investigate further, it is unclear who or what they are or what they are doing.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: "the Church needed hope, and in their darkest moment, the light of Chorus returned to the people in the form of this new Voice, who has rallied the people together and defended them with miracles."
column5: Your investigation suggests that this rumor is true, and that miracles were performed to protect the faithful from the Ko'aat and dangers within the Mists.

Topic: Investigate Scandal/Healthy Paranoia: mistreatment of the Hazori in Nadine lands
column5: Within the Nadine Empire there are massive growing pains with the Nadine populace getting accustomed to having a substantial portion of non-Nadine within their territory, and for the Hazori who are getting accustomed to the strange functions of the Nadine culture. The Hazori are low-hanging fruit, and thus easy targets to be swindled and taken advantage of by those who are no longer on the bottom of the ladder and finally have the option to pick on someone else. Ultimately, however, the Nadine government is providing protections to the Hazori within their borders by punishing those who are caught swindling the Hazori, providing them a place to live, and the opportunity to build a new life in Concerti.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: David (the Brown Recluse) is NOT a member of the Dancing Spiders
column5: As a Returned, the Brown Recluse has been trying to get back into his former position in the Dancing Spiders network, but has had many setbacks in doing so. While he considered himself a Dancing Spider, many of the local Dancing Spiders in town say that he was not, and that his affiliation was lost when he first died.

Topic: Political Insight: Trahazi caravan at the Obsidian Tomb
column5: The Tcaci, still settled near the Obsidian Tomb, do not have much in the way of political clout at the moment. Much of the land south of them to the Nadine border was salted and razed after the fall of Whitehall, but the Tcaci were permitted by the Nadine to keep their farmland and the land north of their settlement should they expand in the future. Amongst themselves, they seem to be doing well and their elders are leading them in a new way of living where they are finally able to put down roots. Among the Trahazi caravans, there is much talk of these Hazori having gotten what they deserved for taking Lord Darius at his word, though few of the available caravans have any interest in joining them out of fear of Nadine oversight and what might come of that in the future.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Trahazi caravan at the Obsidian Tomb
column5: - Rumor has it, the Tcaci no longer consider themselves a Trahazi caravan, but have taken claim to the Hazori name. - Rumor has it, the Tcaci are expanding their village with the hope other Trahazi will come settle the land with them. - Rumor has it, the Tcaci scouts are not watching their southern borders, but their northern ones. - Rumor has it, the Tcaci have something of great importance hidden in their village. - Rumor has it, the Tcaci seem to be investing in sheep and goats almost more than farming.

Topic: Military Insight: T'tocs' forces in Cestral
column5: 100 Demon Mages, 180 Demon Mages (Burning Legion), 1,000 Lesser Demons (Burning Legion), 2,000 Demonic Butchers (Twisted Legion), 2000 Lesser Demons (Twisted Legion)

Topic: Bones of the Earth - can non-dragon learn the draconic songs of creation? how does bones of the earth function via meta science? can non-dragons form the same connection with the earth?
column5: Scholars have studied and debated for centuries about the differences between humans and the other races, particularly as bloodlines have become more and more diluted over the years. One of the lasting changes to dragonblooded that is always present is the density of bones. Dragons are creatures of Adelrune with deep, lasting ties to the earth itself, and with such intrinsic connections with magic, they can tap into different types of magic and power that are unavailable to the other races. Particularly the songs of creation and the deepest forms of magic that are known to the dragons are safely guarded and kept out of the hands of non-dragons who couldn't even begin to comprehend them.

Topic: how do we disrupt mehs' portals formation? specifically the ones in town
column5: It's well-known among researchers and mages that portals can be interferred with, but there is rarely a single solution to doing so. Ultimately, if one wanted to disrupt the formation and functions of the portals, they would need to experiment directly with the portals themselves.

Topic: can those with the mark of the astari moon/star use light & dark magic via sorcery or other schools?
column5: While those with the Mark of the Moon or Star have an affinity for light and darkness, there is no way to change existing sorcery spells to include these elements. There is, however, certainly potential for those who have such marks to research a new, unique spell allowing for a similar effect to Elemental Blast but with light and darkness damage.

Topic: the development of a ritual or artifact to change someone's house/tribe? looking for something that could help in the efforts to restore lost/endangered houses
column5: A being's family and bloodline cannot be rewritten via magic or ritual, as it is what is in their blood and their history. Ultimately, this is not a valid path forward.

Topic: how to get restricted lore: the veil
column5: So Restricted Lore: The Veil is not a valid lore to exist in the Twin Mask space, however Restricted Lore: Planes might be more in line with what you're looking for.  This can most easily be obtained through speaking with an Expert to begin the path of study down this road, or by doing extensive research into the *different* planes, and not just Dream.

Topic: Ren: revered/worshipped/common rites/practices/recent events
column5: Ren, the Root of the World, and font of life, is typically worshipped most strongly around the turning of the seasons - particularly the solstices and equinoxes - as trees follow the cycle of life through the seasons. Births and fertility are celebrated in the spring, Growth in the summer, Death in the fall, and Steadiness in the winter. As one of the oldest gods of the Old Ways, it should be no surprise that the path and tradition of the archon also follows this journey. It is believed that Ren took a direct hit when the Celestine Empire cut down the Sacred Oak, one of the great trees attributed to Ren's reach into the world, which was stewarded and protected by the Gael people for centuries, and with its fall so to did the Banner of the Old Oak. Though it was planted again not long after, it will take many centuries for it to regrow and take it's place of grandeur once more. Believed to be connected to the World Tree, the recent planting of another sapling at the fall of the Clocktower brought renewed life and power to Ren, and many of those who took part in the planted were blessed with his mark. Beyond great trees, the other touch Ren has placed on the world is the Vyren themselves, named for him, great sentient trees that can walk and serve as protectors of nature. These beings are said to have gone to sleep as the strength and influence of the Old Ways dwindled in the world centuries ago, but with a Clocktower wish from Palauttaa, they have recent reawoken.

Topic: something cool I can post in CREAD (citadel, celestine daith, dragon worship, old ways, church of chorus, lady of the mist, trahazi zodiac)
column5: Ballad of the Fallen Crown In the heart of shadowed lands, Where darkness takes its toll, The Lord of Crowns, now weak and frail, Struggles to retain control. Once a tyrant fierce and feared, With power cruel and vast, His throne now trembles, cracked and worn, His reign is fading fast. The Nameless ones with faces shrouded, With eyes of cold, dark flame, Seek to claim the empty seat, To revel in his shame. They whisper secrets through the night, In voices hushed and thin, Promising a darkened hope To draw in kith and kin. "Come to us, all that have been blessed, Who wander across the land, Who seek great power above all else, And bring your sword in hand." The Lord of Crowns, in desperate plight, Gathers those astray, With promises of ancient power, Restoring Chorus gone away. His followers, like sheep in fear, Drawn by the siren call, Believe his lies of strength restored, To lift them from the fall. He offers them a broken hope, A dream of days gone by, That with his strength and darkened might, Chorus will never die. Yet as the Nameless ones advance, Their hunger starts to show, Their plans to seize the throne of dark Grow clearer with each woe. The Lord of Crowns, though fierce and proud, Now falters in his might, With shadows creeping ever close, He struggles through the night. In grim halls and twisted lands, The echoes of his plea, Ring out with empty promises, Of a restored decree. So sing the tale of thrones undone, Of crowns that turn to dust, Of tyrants grasping at their reign, In shadows deep and unjust. And in the night, remember well, The gods of yore and old, For even in the darkest storm, A deeper darkness may unfold.

Topic: heart surgery to switch human & nbd hearts / we have seen that NBDs don't need organs/bones/a soul to function, so what DOES a dream require in order to function?
column5: There are no discenable affects that happen when switching the heart of a human and a new born dream without the proper surgical recipe and outline to study and follow. (This goes for any experimental surgeries without a proper recipe). While most extra-planar entities require a true name to take form on Adelrune, it is theorized that the Well of Heroes acts as the tethering agent to allow the dream entities physical forms. It is unclear, however, if they are able to leave the safety radius of Tear's magic and the Well itself.

Topic: how does expectation effect the bodies of NBDs? (heart transplant)
column5: While made from the Dream, the physical bodies of new born dreams are made to mimic human forms and will take on the general characteristics of human bodies, including functionality of organs.

Topic: research: differences/similarities in zodiac rite practices across caravans
column5: The practices and teachings from priests of the Trahazi Zodiac can vary wildly by caravan, many of such practices dating back to the Exile when the caravans split and many traveled without a trained priest. As the Hazori religion faded without experienced priests, the Trahazi Zodiac came into being as the people shared the stories and practices they each remembered from their younger years when they were more firmly rooted. As time passed in the Age of Chorus and caravans began to cross paths more regularly and share their practices and developments with one another, a more unified sense of the faith began to emerge - Grandfather and Uncle each went from multiple names to a single one, the way in which Nytchka and Zoka were represented became more consistent (or rather, purposely inconsistant in the case of Kuyeh and Sukkah). Prayers and offerings became more common and consistent too.  However, even as the Trahazi Zodiac itself developed into a more consistent sense of a faith, some caravans rejected the Zodiac entirely, or clung to their own practices developed in the early decades of the Exile. Caravans who still cling to the Cult of Shadow and Smoke exclusively worship Dechko. Caravans who had a priest trained in the faith of the Hazori, or who had the religious texts and holy artifacts of the Hazori still practicing earlier rites and rituals. Some even are dedicated to runaway or blacksheep "cousins" who don't appear among the main Zodiac at all. For even as the "Trahazi Zodiac" developed and took its general form during the Age of Chorus, every single caravan has some practice of its own that the other caravans do not follow as they developed in those early days of the Exile and it has been passed down through the generations as the teachings and memories of those who were once Hazori.

Topic: Historical records of Hazori magic
column5: Most records of the Hazori have been destroyed by time and the Inquisition, but it is common knowledge that while the Hazori began with the development of agriculture. Paired with this, channeling was common as their first magic long before recorded history began. It was in the later years, with the Age of Tane and the introduction of demons into their midst, that the Hazori began to take on the darker magics - blood, summoning, and necromancy were all deeply rooted aspects of their culture, alongside the worship of demons and working with demons against their greatest enemy - the Ko'aat. With the conquering of Riel and the growth of the Amalgamation, those practioners of the dark magics who were unwilling to compromise traveled west, becoming the Drir.

Topic: what is the void? is there magic or power that can be gained from studying it? are their deities that call the void home?
column5: The Void is a pocket plane wedged between Adelrune and the Abyss, sometimes referred to as the plane of shadows. There is a deep, corrupting magic that originates in this plane and can be drawn on to power spells and rituals on Adelrune. While the Etilvor locked themselves in the Void for protections, the result of spending hundreds of years there are still is still noticeable in the magic the Etilvor wield and the amount of sanity they returned to this plane with.

Topic: how does one remove a powerful possession without hurting the vessel? or evict the spirit without the soul of a person in it?
column5: The Rite of Possession is the safest and most direct method to remove a possessing spirit from a body, however keeping an empty vessel around without a spirit is a dangerous risk, for any wandering spirit may take possession of it. It is wise that if one is planning to remove a spirit from a body, that a replacement spirit be prepared to fill it shortly after.

Topic: any info on origins/workings of the whistling stones?
column5: The Whistling Stones predate Cestral, the Nadine occupation, and even the Hazori arrival on the island. They naturally resonate with a low, haunting base tone which can be disconcerting for those unaccustomed to the sound on a still night, but their natural ability to harmonize with any note whistled near them is what first drew the Hazori to them. As these stones existed before recorded history, there is little known information as to their origins, but it should be noted that there are exactly ten, have only ever been exactly ten, and even when Vicaul and Nadine forces have tried to knock them down or damage them, they've stood undamaged throughout the centuries.

Topic: who are notable arbiters in Alstai's history? what were their accomplishments?
column5: Arbiters are not remembered by their names - as part of leaving their band to assist all bands, this carries on throughout history as they act and are remembered only as The Arbiters. Their many great feats and decisions are certainly discussed in a range of manners, from awe-filled whispers to stories to teach lessons to correct behavior, and even as boasts for those rare few who truly came out ahead from the ruling of an Arbiter. Perhaps the most profound story about the actions of an Arbiter falls back to the story of two bands who now are considered to be Bands of the Lost. Once two of the most powerful bands - the Running Cheetah and the Proud Lion - the bands had a long-seated rivalry between them. Both traversing the same territories, they have centuries of close interaction, and there are are hundreds of stories as to what started their disagreement, but the truth it was something small - a slight, whether intentional or accidental, grew generation after generation. From shoving and mouthing off to one another, with each successive retaliation the actions grew more extreme until it finally reached a point that the Arbiters could not allow it to go one. Over the course of one winter in Alstai, fifty-six members of the two bands were killed by one another in retaliation for the last. It was then that an Arbiter stepped in and demanded that the two could not carry on in such a manner - they had each lost their leaders twice over in this conflict, and almost their entire next generation. The Arbiter told them thus, that they would become travel companions, not to compete for resources and power, but to provide for the other. If the Running Cheetah did not provide food, clothing, and the needs for the Proud Lion, then the Proud Lion would not be able to provide for the needs of the Running Cheetah. In combat, their healers must tend to the other's wounded. When the bands moved, they were each responsible for maintenance and construction of one another's kota. If they wished to survive, they must care for one another. Following the decision of their Arbiter, to this day the two bands travel side by side, both small but steady in their numbers while still acting independently of one another, even while required to rely on the other. Because of this Arbiter, these bands live on, rather than destroying one another in their quest for blood over a slight none remember.

Topic: what components are needed to bond to a horse with blood magic? how is the ritual performed? is there anyone on Tear who has performed this ritual?
column5: While it is not possible to learn from texts if anyone on Tear has performed this ritual, notes regarding the rituals contained within the Book of Ig seem to suggest that the Ko'aat ritual to blood bond with one's horse might be similar in design to some form of combining of the rituals of Coagulation and Entanglement. Additionally, one could theorize that such a ritual would involve both grandmaster summoning and blood magic, and that it would take some time to work out the details of such a ritual based on these things.   A blue research tracker tag will be waiting for you on the bulletin board outside of Logi at the next game if you wish to pursue creating this ritual.

Topic: if an artifact in relation to veyn, lord of fresh water, exists? or if there are any old ways artifacts related to water
column5: While it is entirely possible that an artifact of Veyn exists in the world, one would suspect it to be connected with an Old Ways culture that is connected more closely to water. (You do not have the applicable lores needed for a more in-depth answer to this question)

Topic: dragon stomach - can an alchemist use this to harvest acid? is the type of acid researchable as a new stable recipe? what are the properties of this acid?
column5: This is not a new type of acid, rather it is one of the oldest types in existance - stomach acid. As it regularly renews, it could be possible to learn how to milk it appropriately so it continues to produce over a long period of time, however you suspect that should you choose not to take this route, you might find a single, REALLY interesting use for it.  *If you want to harvest the acid, I'll give you a blue research tag to figure that out but it will only have the set mechanic for it after that. If you choose not to do that, then you may someday get creative with your stomach full of acid on a mod XD

Topic: who are the effendal who live on the northern island? why are they there? when did they go?
column5: The Effendal on the northern island of Tear are more of a scouting part/occupying force of the Svivore and Rakuten. While their purposes for being there are not written about in books that can be learned from in a library, as it's a current, developing situation, historically it is known that Tear and it's surrounding islands were the territory of the ancient House Ra'e'len. Back in the Age of Tane, many settlements were attempted by humans on Tear, only to run into the fierce members of House Ra'e'len and it's various tribes who drove away many of the inhabitants of these settlements, including War's End on the northern island.

Topic: what do Meh's generals possess that can destroy him?
column5: This information is not able to be found within written libraries and texts, however scholars have written extensively about the paranoia of demons in the greatest power and the consistent ebb and flow as others push against it. As such, most demons of substantial power possess objects or other ways to take out their most formidable foes. This was most recently noted as the case in which Prince Bellus himself kept a number of Arrows of True Form in his arsenal.

Topic: who are the stone legion? where do they come from? any connection to Palauttaa?
column5: The Stone Legion is the remnants of the demon city of Oll, who once followed the demon by that name. Oll was killed by Tane and his armies scattered under the leadership of other demons on Breach for some time. Previously part of Bel'e'athru's army in the War of Radiance, the remnants are now serving under Sablegore as part of Mehs' army. There does not seem to be any connection to Palauttaa.

Topic: can we get more information on the hierarchy of Mehs' military and any high ranking officers we should know about?
column5: The hierarchy within Mehs' army ascribes to the concept of Might Makes Right. While each of his generals bends knee to his position as Abyssal Monarch, they are permitted to retain their own autonomy and go about their tasks and goals independently. Each of the generals has armies or units of their own to command, and beneath that is the Burning Legion made up of the demon soliders of Mehs (the city).

Topic: can the salt in prometey's brain be removed and/or be used to harm yashi before it kills him?
column5: The salt is removeable, though if Prometey is conscious they will certainly berzerk when the incision is made. As such, it would be necessary for them to be dead or fully unconscious for such a surgery to be performed. While the salt cannot hurt Yashi itself, it *can* be used as a ritual component for a fae lure, which could potentially be used to attract Yashi into a trap.

Topic: Investigate History: Saerinie
column5: Saerinie, The Swift, sometimes called the Goddess of Portals. She was an Astari, a pale skinned elven figure who (when seen) was always veiled. As the Astari were immortal, somewhere along the line very much before recorded history, she obtained godhood and traveled the world, keeping to herself and out of trouble. She was hunted by the Mist Faith for the last decade in an attempt to feed her to the Mist Goddess and transfer her power over Portals to the Mist Goddess, but she very much represented her title in always managing to stay one step ahead. As a relatively small god, who traveled alone, without a church or pantheon to aide her, she relied on her own swiftness to keep out of the conflict of stronger beings.

Topic: Basic Scouting: Is the demon trying to possess House Ire'enil the same that possessed Julinar?
column5: Yes

Topic: Basic Scouting: Does Redemption have ay defenses against Portals being opened inside their walls?
column5: No

Topic: Basic Scouting: Has Mehs' army started moving on Redemption?
column5: No

Topic: Military Insight: hostile forces around the Inquisitor Tower at Edge
column5: There do not appear to be any forces at all in or around the tower. Edge, however, has it's usual makeup of gangs and unsavory types.

Topic: Basic Scouting: Do the demonic forces south of Nadine serve Mehs?
column5: There are no ships south of the Nadine Empire

Topic: Basic Scouting: Are the demonic forces south of Nadine making way for Wayrest?
column5: There are no ships making way for Wayrest

Topic: Basic Scouting: Are the demonic forces south of Nadine making way for Concerti?
column5: There are no ships making way for Concerti

Topic: Basic Scouting: are there demonic forces off the coast of the Nadine Empire?
column5: No

Topic: Investigate History: Why are some Fae referred to as "Greater Fae" or "Ancient Fae"
column5: There is no true definition for "Greater Fae" or "Ancient Fae", but typically such titles are used to refer to the fae who express more power than others - typically those that are gods in their own right, or who have gained positions of import and respect from the other fae.

Topic: State of the Court: Celestine Empire
column5: Coming out of the Mists, the Celestine Empire is in a great state of flux. The following topics were common to be overheard between nobles and national leaders as Court convened in the capital: - With the absence/abdication of Queen Diana coming out of the Mists, it is time to choose a new queen - So many years of Mists have heavily destroyed the agricultural stocks that the nation relies on, and while they had their food stores refilled midway through the Mists, the last of the stable food sources is nearly out again and there is not even enough seed to plant for a winter crop. If something is not done soon, the nation will starve - Diseases seem to be gone from the nation as a whole, and the Intl Healer's Guild is at a loss for what they should be doing regarding that - The religious are debating about the rumor of Queen Diana's supposed ascension to godhood during the Mists

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Myren of Port Frey
column5: - Rumor has it, the Myren are comprised of theives and liars. - Rumor has it, an envoy from Myre has arrived in Port Frey. - Rumor has it, there is a surgeon offering enhancements. You have but to ask. A first step towards citizenship. - Rumor has it, the child of the Puppet King is among the Returned. - Rumor has it, copper solves everything. - Rumor has it, the Professor in Pink has no interest in politics or warfare.  He just wants to make art.

Topic: Military Insight: The demons in the Nadine mountains
column5: Under a currently unidentified demon general, it appears that the group is made up of: 100 Demon Mages, 100 Corruptor Demons (Davidas' forces), 1500 Lesser Demons (Twisted Legion), 500 Lesser Corruptors (Davidas' forces), 100 Demon Knights (Stone Legion).

Topic: Military Insight: Celestine Empire
column5: While each of the different noble families have their own military forces, and the Fortresses have additional garrisonning forces, the general military makeup is as follows:       The Legion of Espari (Maiden Annabelle Grace Celestine): 10,300 Guards, 210 knights                   The Legion of Eon (Maiden Acantha): 2,160 Veteran Guards, 32 knights       The Legion of Daece (Maiden Valeria): 1,700 Elite Guards, 1,830 Veteran Guards, 194 Knights    The Legion of Mentor (Maiden Thaelia): 12,250 Guards, 202 Knights           The Legion of Aer (Maiden Synallia): 2,990 Guards (most are now afraid of water), 80 knights (most afraid of water)             The Legion of Eliena (Maiden Bale):5,850 Elite Guards, 48 Elite Knights, 238 Knights        The Legion of Destiny: Destroyed      The Legion of Lisra (Maiden Camilla): 3,690 Veteran Guards, 41 Knights

Topic: Political Insight: Celestine Empire
column5: Coming out of the Mists, the Celestine Empire finds itself in an unstable political position both within the nation and in the world. Without their Queen there is a gaping hole left in the leadership adn ability to sign off on various aspects of internal and international politics and trade, though having several exceptionally capable Handmaidens filling in the gaps has certainly eased the worst of this strain when such a situation could (and has) brought down other nations. Additionally, losing their two geographically closest allies in Ad Decimum and Mandala has put the Northern Expanse and the Celestine's western border at risk with a new, unknown enemy state on their border. The Mists still sit on their their northern border, and while it does not seem the Gael remain behind the fog, the unknown and rumors of a nation of dragons already has many in the northern territories worried. The region of West Wall doubly so, as they also have the world's largest population of Gael other than Tear residing in the territory, which continues to cause cutlural clashes as former enemy groups attempt to live alongside one another peacefully. Broken trade lines and lack of food sources is not doing well to help matters, and the news of the first envoys to Castle Thorn getting captured or destroyed is making many worry for the future security of the nation.

Topic: Investigate History: the breaking of Bel'e'athru's throne
column5: The actual breaking of Bel'e'athru's throne is unclear, as it is only vaguely referenced in stories about Star and Bel'e'athru's fight, but it is theorized that Star shattered the throne in the Abyss with a combined force of her spear and her own sacrifice in falling from the heavens, using the strength drawn from her sacrifice to complete an otherwise impossible task in shattering the throne and encaging Bel'e'athru.

Topic: Vices & Routines: Voice of Chorus
column5: building church, religious preaching

Topic: Vices & Routines: Lord Quin Solemn
column5: At the moment, with Cestral at war, Lord Solemn's routines are different than they typically would be as the Cabinet has been in War Council since T'tocs' arrival on Cestral. Solemn disappears from the Cabinet chambers a number of times during the day to take reports from his informants and scouts, but has rarely been seen entering or exiting his office in recent weeks. Widowed during the War of Radiance, Lord Solemn is often to be found in the evenings dressed down in peasant clothing for drinking and gambling in the corner of various taverns throughout the city and neighboring villages, speaking to any number of interesting characters, including some of the more disreputable of the Blackmares.

Topic: Investigate History: What was the artifact in the War of Feathers?
column5: There is no written record as to what the artifact was that was destroyed during the War of Feathers, as the Celestine Empire refuses to admit what precious artifact was taken from them, and it wasn't recorded in their financial or inventory ledgers. Additionally, so few among the Nadine Empire saw the artifact at all, and those that did were of the First Legion and refused to speak of it in the aftermath, except to say that they accomplished a great feat in killing a Handmaiden and seeing the artifact destroyed so it could not be used against the Empress by the Celestine Empire. Note: It very specifically was not one of the Tears of Espari, nor any other known divine artifact.

Topic: Follow the Money: Citizen Regent Merrick Bartholomew
column5: Merrick Bartholomew's major income seems to be through his position as a merchant, while the bulk of his spending is going to two places: helping the people (by funding the local masons who were previous contracted by Unity to upkeep the walls), and paying off the bigger gangs (including the Salamanders, the Knives, the Boiling Bones, the Bleeding Aces, and the Tuck-Ins).

Topic: Vices & Routines: Citizen Regent Merrick Bartholomew
column5: Merrick Bartholomew keeps a fairly mundane daily routine in doing business and interfacing with different facets of the local non-Returned populace, with no real noticeable vices. Most peculiarly, is that as your people follow him for an extended length of time, they'll often notice that in a few different places, the same person will always exit a building a few minutes after Merrick enters it.

Topic: Slaughter Livestock, send to Regent Merrick Bartholomew
Underworld Insight: demonic assassins in July 14AR
column5: The demonic assassinations of the previous two gatherings can be attributed to Lt. Rumphk and his subordinates, in service to Sablegore.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Praxinor and ties to Nameless Faith
column5: - Rumor has it, Praxinor worships Snake. - Rumor has it, both Praxinor and the Nameless are seeking power - Rumor has it, Praxinor likes his own name too much to be Nameless - Rumor has it, Praxinor doesn't care what your name is - Rumor has it, Praxinor worships the Nameless faith

Topic: Investigate History: House Anesen/what they guard
column5: House Anesan has done a very good job in keeping their name and their secrets hidden, which allowed them to remain undetected during the Purges and to this day they have never been discovered in Cestral. Part of this attempt to remain hidden is why they cut off all their communications methods during the Purges when it was discovered that the Inquisition had learned to tap into their communications between houses, and disappeared entirely. One of the known artifacts held by House Anesan, at least among the Effendal, was their oldest methods of communication - what Cestral call the Whistling Stones, ten great stone obelisks erected around the island nation of Cestral, each humming with a low bass tone, and were once capable of communicating with the various homesteads. Nowadays the stones are used by the Cestral military for their own communications.  Note: It is likely that there are many more tools and artifacts in House Anesan that they are looking after, but those are well-guarded Anesan secrets and not found in books, even within an Effendal library.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Endurant Tribes/Other Effendal on Tear
column5: - Rumor has it, the Etilvor are snatching anyone they can capture to experiment on - Rumor has it, the Svivore and Rakuten are being driven out of their lands in the north - Rumor has it, four diviners among the Radolond have been blinded and burned for attempting to divine on Mehs - Rumor has it, the new Etilvor are settling in at site of the Twisted Tower  - Rumor has it, the Endurant are going to take Edge

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Citadel
column5: - Rumor has it, Terra territory is being severely impacted by corruption - Rumor has it, the Glyphways have finally been completed - Rumor has it, House Kaelin is making deals with humans - Rumor has it, the Gael Banners are growing agitated and conflict has sprung up between them. - Rumor has it, the Citadel is uncertain about the release of the Etilvor

Topic: Investigate Rumors: T'tocs is looking for something specific in Cestral
column5: Yes, given that his first attack was a mildly targeted attack on the capital and then burning of countryside but moving in a particular direction and not engaging the capital or army, it certainly seems that he is looking for something in particular.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: What is T'tocs looking for in Cestral?
column5: - Rumor has it, T'tocs is looking for food stores in Cestral to burn them to the ground. - Rumor has it, T'tocs is headed for a Whistling Stone. - Rumor has it, T'tocs can't find the library because he doesn't know to ask for Alexander! - Rumor has it, T'tocs has gone right passed a Whistling Stone without paying it any mind. - Rumor has it, T'tocs might be looking for someone in particular because his troop movements and attacks have been a little erratic.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: remnant knight cells (blood chalice, dragon knights) operating in Myre a la underground resistance
column5: - Rumor has it, after the last incursion through the walls of Myre, the resistance abandoned their attempts on the city. - Rumor has it, Blood Knights can still be found in the forests outside of Myre. - Rumor has it, the Walls of Distortion have been perfected so none can enter. - Rumor has it, a resistance grows outside the walls of Myre. - Rumor has it, the eternal knight still lives within the chambers of the Puppet King.

Topic: Investigate Political Stance: Merrick Bartholomew
column5: Government: It's never been a secret that Merrick Bartholomew is anti-establishment, preferring to keep his focus and attention on the people to encourage them to rise up.

Topic: What are the arcane properties of celestial song? how can a Celestial transform songs into power?
column5: Like any ritual, words and intent have power. Anyone is capable of using song as a powering aspect in conjunction with other ritual aspects (ritual circles, burning of herbs, etc), but it was originally learned from celestials themselves. There are other beings (you lack the lore for) who are known for using song to create effects or manipulate natural magic, and there are some full celestials capable of similar feats. For celestials capable of such things, the song is a harnessing and focusing power for their words and intent, and as such, that is enough to see it through. Additionally, Artifacts would tell you there are a number of divine artifacts known as the Tears of Espari that react strongly to music and song as well.

Topic: are there any existing Churches of Chorus outside of Cestral/former Amalg?
column5: Once the most wide-spread of religions in the world, the Church of Chorus had a presence in most major cities. A grand cathedral was built in Edge at the tail end of the Age of Chorus, as well as large churches in West Riel (also just called Riel, the capital of the Celestine Empire), and Dace. Moderate to small churches were built in some cities in the Celestine countryside (Arei, Lisra), as well as Ad Decimum, Cole, and Castle Thorn. Note: As this was a Research question, the scope of the question is limited to information that can be found in books, and does not reflect current world events such as the fall of the Church.

Topic: what entity, being, or person has ever been called or given the title or moniker the Lord of Crowns?
column5: With your knowledge and resources limited to the Church of Chorus and the Amalgamation, there is no being you can find reference to that has ever been called the Lord of Crowns.

Topic: would it be possible to use a soul gem or other means to deposit a Returned's soul into a ship? specifically, what if a returned knew they had enough corruption that they will perm soon, and could deposit their soul into a ship via summoning, so that upon misting, they would immediately die permanenty but their soul would go on living/haunting the ship? based on Tropical Storm Simon's demon ship that used a soul gem & summoning to bind a demon soul to it and Esdeline putting her soul into Ig? (lore: summoning, not Dace)
column5: There are very few people in the world capable of trapping souls in soul gems, as this is an antiquated art that used to be more common among summoners but was found to be severely distasteful so instead a different tethering system was adopted. What you would know from Summoning and Souls, is that a soul can only exist in one place - so if it is in a body it will remain there. If it is bound in a soul gem, that soul is no longer in a body, or when the body dies a soul will naturally go to the spirit realm. If you sought to shove the soul of a Returned into a gem, you would need someone capable of doing so and a rare soul gem capable of containing such a strong soul (not typically one that can be gathered through the Mining Gathering tree), and their soul would immediately go into the gem upon completion of the ritual. The soul could not remain in their body until they misted and *then* go into the gem unless it was performed upon their death.

Topic: what is the viability of making ships made of other types of materials besides wood (because of the fact the shipyard can use any type of wood). can ships be made of stone, metal, or crystal (like the silver fleet). (lore: Dace)
column5: Anyone with Engineering knowledge would know that there are very few materials besides wood that ships can be made out of, even if they can get creative with the types of wood used and how they work with it because different cultures have used different types of wood from all over the world to build their ships. Stone would immediately be too heavy, iron would immediately rust, and silver and god would be far too soft to hold the shape. Additionally, for an engineer or shipwright from Dace, they would know that the Silver Fleet ships are not made purely of silver, they are merely silver plated.

Topic: Ballad of the Fallen Crown: are there other places with records of the ballad?
column5: The Ballad was seemingly squashed between some books in the library Oso originally found it in (at Effendal camp), but you cannot find copies of it in any of the libraries around town that you've searched. However, having seen the original copy from Oso, the very specific print-block letters would leave a niggling thought in the back of Kiesh's head. Though they are not an *exact* match to the ambassador letters he received for two years, the very specific block-printing and spacing suggests to Kiesh that whoever wrote it, or wrote this copy down, is likely a seneschal or secretary of some form, as all of the Unity seneschals adopted a similar handwriting for official memos and letters from Unity, even if their private or individual correspondences were written in their own hand.

Topic: what are the names of the various planes?
column5: The names, number of planes, and layout are a hotly debated topic in the academic community. There are as many different thoughts on this as there are scholars in the world, and new theories are always being presented while older theories are always adapting. The more common beliefs include (but are not limited to)  - the vertical map with the Celestial Heavens above, the Abyss below, and Adelrune in the middle, all connected by the Dreaming - the hourglass map, with the Golden Realms above, the Abyss below, with the Twist and Twain running through the middle where they cross at the Material Plane (Adelrune) - the vertical/hourglass variation, where both of the above are integrated with the Dreaming acting as the vertical connection between Adelrune and the Golden Realms, and Nightmare connecting Adelrune to the Abyss - the elemental clock - where the planes of air, fire, spirit, and water rotate clockwise above Adelrune and influence the Plane of Flesh by affecting seasons as the planes come closer or move away again There are also many other theories that name planes such as the Plane of Corruption, the Void, the Godrealms, and dozens of others in their own types of maps, but there is little evidence to fully support any true or factual map of the planes and what *actually* exists

Topic: Guidance Rite for Lore: Celestials. if there is no evidence of the Tears of Espari-Life on Adelrune, what ties might it have to other Life entities such as celestials and the Golden Realms?
column5: Celestials are broadly known to be connected to the renewal of life, but curiously not to it's creation. While a Celestial can resurrect a body and provide it another chance at life, there must have been life in the body for them to do this. Celestials cannot create life where there was no life to begin with. From your research and theorizing, it seems unlikely that the celestials as a whole would be keeping the Life Tear of Espari hidden, though it could be possible that a single Celestial might be hoarding it for it's power to create life.

Topic: who is the glass demon that was freed from Mr Lovelace's ring/artifact?
column5: (bravo having EVERY applicable lore for this in one research action!)  Glass has always had many a powerful use in Adelrune, particularly among artifacts, as both windows between across and between planes, and as prisons. Particularly in the city of Mehs, the important or cared for dead have their ashes blown into glass that is shaped into windows, walls, or looking glasses, as a means to keep them around. The glass artisans of Mehs are the best at the world in their craft, and it is clear from the remnants of the ring that it was shaped in the hands of one of these artisans, before being smuggled or traded away to Redemption. However, from what Cinder saw specifically while within the ring with the demon, it didn't seem that they had been imprisoned in the ring as one would put a demon in a soul gem or similar object, but they were using the ring as a means of transportation, and with the slowly fracturing glass around the room, it was only a matter of time before someone broke the ring (purposely or otherwise) and let them out somewhere else in the world. Little is written of demons of glass, but having assembled such a group of experts to study this one in particular, you are able to find buried notation of a being made of clear glass called Ayna living in Mehs, who excels in the creation of artifacts and spellwork, particularly glyphs and scrying, and is known for being a treasure trove of information and a master of subtlty, up until their sharp tongue deals a devestating blow. Though they may appear fragile in their delicate glass shapes, they have deadly edges that ought to be avoided as much as their words.

Topic: what are the steps to become a Golden Choir High Priestess?
column5: As there is no overarching hierarchy to the Golden Choir faith, there are no official steps or titles within the faith, thus you can call yourself whatever it is you want. Furthermore, the Golden Choir is not a national faith of a Rules of Society nation, so no pins are awarded no matter what title is claimed.

Topic: how do we pull the souls from the two ghost-like ships into the living world in our time?
column5: Without directly encountering and engaging with the two mysterious ships, it is impossible to tell what their situation is as far as if they are truly ghosts or mortal/physical beings trapped in the Mists. The Liberator and the other, unknown, ship have both been spotted within the last year, so they do already exist in some form on Adelrune in the current time. Little information beyond sightings of the ships have been recorded over the years, so to continue down this line of inquiry would need to be tracking the ships down and making contact.

Topic: what are the states of the Liberator and the other similar ship that's been spotted?  I want to know what the status of each of the ships are if possible. Do we have any stories of how they or their passengers have been doing? Do we have any stories from people who have encountered them and what did they have to say about the ships? That kind of thing
column5: There are always stories of ghost ships and other things to be seen out in the Mists around Tear and throughout the world. Some stories are of great war ships bearing down and destroying or sinking their own ships, others tell of an ominous vessel that lurks just beyond in the bank of Mists and keeps pace with other ships - never moving closer nor further - just looming there as a threat. There are tales of ships sailing in and out of the Mists helmed by a beautiful woman, as if they can be navigated easily, and still others that swear only madness and sea monsters lurk behind the wall of Mists that encircle the world. Unfortunately you do not have lores that support more knowledge on the specific state of either ship, and attempting to separate various accounts of ghostly/Mist-bound ships is difficult to identify which ships may be which.

Topic: what's the history of the ruins on the isle of Ire? was the civilization made up of War's End refugees?
column5: The Isles of Ire have a sordid history of civilizations being built on the island, only to be washed away by great waves, leaving several ruined settlements on the island. Most recently, the nation of Eirie was founded about 100 years ago, and destroyed by a great wave in 5ar, leaving the only living members of the culture those who were abroad at the time, or on ships that were away from the harbor at the time of the wave. There are older ruins that seem to date roughly to the middle-late Age of Tane, but there do not seem to be extensive records or studies of these ruins in the library, as if they only just appeared there or were otherwise undiscovered until recently. An archeological expedition may shed more light on this subject in the future.

Topic: research: common funeral practices of the Gael? do they vary by banner or position in culture?
what are the arcane properties of the regalia and how were they formed?
column5: The creation of the Regalia happened before recorded history, and like most artifacts, their origins are murky at best. The Crown – Allows control over other demons. The Scepter - Boosts the power and strength of the Crown, making it more difficult to resist. The Throne - Boosts the distance of influence of the Crown. Returned in the past have theorized that a completed Throne would not only reach all corners of Adelrune, but into the Abyss itself for control over all of demonkind.

Topic: in the context of the Myren and demons roaming the blightwood looking for something: what might they be looking for and where? (no Lore: Effendal)
column5: Typically this kind of information is something one would discover through influence spends (particularly Investigate Rumors) or other active in-game investigations like divination or Guidance rites, since it is not something that can be found in a book in a library. If you would like to re-direct the question as to be more akin to "are there ancient things (ruins/artifacts) that were located in the territory of the Blighthood" that might get you some useful information.

Topic: how would one restore or seize the Lord of Crowns' power?
column5: As with any god, expanding the Church and bringing them more power could restore the Lord of Crowns, whereas destroying/killing his followers or himself would allow a different being of significant power to seize what the Lord of Crowns still holds onto.

Topic: Potential uses for a bound fae if their true name is unknown?
column5: Ultimately, this will depend on what kind of binding is being used, what the intention is of those binding the fae, and the intent of the ritual of binding. Most binding rituals require the true name to complete the binding, but there are some exceptions to this general rule if something else of the fae is used - whether a body part of something significant to them. The fae themselves, or pieces removed from them are often used in rituals such as The Entrapment of Otherworldly Souls in the Book of Nox, which can provide full control over the bound fae through usage of the Black Sands of the Sacred Urn rather than a true name, whereas Exile of Agony can lock them away where the Twixt and Twain crosses the spirit realm, doomed to writhe in eternal agony. Alternatively in the Book of Ig, rituals exist such as the Summoning of Twilight which is used to create a fae abomination from dead fae energy, or the Invocation of the Black Gate to summon otherworldly creatures. Keeping them hostage to bleed for fae blood is not an uncommon use for a bound fae, or for powering any number of other rituals.

Topic: what are the requirements to weild the sword? are there long term effects?
column5: The only requirement to weild the blade is that it is weilded by the Chalice Knight who specifically carries the Blood Chalice itself with them. There are no long-term effects from the blade itself, but there are known side effects to high level and extended blood magic use.

Topic: what are the "Sidhe"? many writings call them the Lords of the Fae, they're thought of as mythical, but are they specifically the fae that originated the Effendal? or is it more of a title?
column5: The Sidhe ARE a fictitious race that do not exist as they are described. Anything called or considered a "Sidhe" is merely a fae with no other distinction.

Topic: the harbor authority is looking to re-establish itself under the new governement structiure. our contributions to Port Frey (including lighthouse and harbor chain project) are aimed at serving the city's trade industry and harbor security. looking to create a longshoreman's union, and collect union fees from non-returned members to pay our returned members pacts as we contribute to the harbor and the city's prosperity and safety.
column5: In general, this is not a research question, but something that should be discovered by doing. That said, however, labor unions are not something that are era or thematically appropriate for this game. Previously dockworkers were paid through the International Merchant's Guild and that path could be sought again under the new government, but workers unions are not going to happen if you press for them.

Topic: Investigate History: Demonic Hierarchy
column5: Ultimately, the "demonic hierarchy" falls to the simplest phrase: Might Makes Right. However, some demons just naturally have more power than others and are more attuned to/in-tune with the Great Conflict. As such, these demons are typically the ones who can use the Regalia and would have reason to (unlike demon gods who draw their power from their divinity rather than the Abyss). There is no set "hierarchy" of monarch-prince-etc, and it is wholly unrelated to Greater Demons/Demon Lords of Breach cities. Titles and honorifics are self-claimed denoting their importance, such as (the aspiring) Abyssal Monarch Mehs, (former) Prince Bellus, (former) Abyssal Monarch Bel'e'athru.

Topic: what are the possible ways by which souls can be transfered between realms: paradox to death realm or death realm to death realm?
column5: Like on Adelrune, the souls of those living in Paradox are living souls, but unlike Adelrune, the fae magics there stop death from "transferring" such souls to the spirit realm. Moving a soul between death realms is theoretically possible (provided it is not god marked) through a ritual found in the Book of Ig, or even by bribing a ferryman.

Topic: what history do we have of the mandalan reincarnation ritual that used to be completed? are there arny documented successes?
column5: There is no specific ritual for reincarnation, however such things feature prominantly in Mandalan death rites given to the recently deceased. Unfortunately, while people will ocassionally claim to be X hero from the past reincarnated, there is no tangible proof that this has ever been the case and reincarnation is solely a matter of faith.

Topic: Research: Are there other documented or rumored artifacts that would've been inbued with divine draconic power like the Torc of Suo or Horn of Sen?
column5: In most faiths there are always rumored to be divine artifacts or gifts given to and blessed by the gods of the pantheon, and the Mandalan Faith is no different in this regard. Like the Torc of Suo and the Horn of Sen, it is rumored that Hé granted their most devout a jar of "healing waters" Nao offered blessed spectacles, Mei gifted their favorite a fan, and Fa provided House Benlowe a chest of "lucky coins".

Topic: is there a being or diety known to have gone by the name/title "lord of crowns", and what pantheon/faith are they?
column5: From what you can find, there are very few references to the Lord of Crowns in any library. Those that do exist are vague at best, and usually buried deep in seemingly-unrelated texts. Outside of these few references that say very little, it seems that whoever this being is prefers to go Unnamed.

Topic: is luck tangible? can it be bottled?
column5: What is Luck? Relgious scholars have debated this topic for centuries - is luck merely superstition? Some herbs or objects are said to be lucky, but there is no real proof to these claims except expectation of it being true putting the holder in a more confident mindset that could account for their "lucky" moments. If, however, one was to attempt to bottle luck - one would first need something that physically embodied luck, such as using a piece of a being of luck (whether demon, celestial, fae, divine, dragon or other), and then would need to develop a process to distill this physical piece into liquid luck.

Topic: what is the sacred urn and its origin?
column5: The Sacred Urn was an artifact possessed by the Teddespari mages. When sacrifices were made to the urn, it provided the means to charge black sands from Eden's realm that were contained within, creating a potent ritual component. Some of this charged black sand was used in the creation of the Hourglass, and was needed to contain the Aspect of Time within the Hourglass. There is no notation that the urn was destroyed, but only the mages of Theddespari would know for sure.  Note: current wereabouts is information better attained through influence spends as that would not be recorded in books.

Topic: types of demonic posession? how do we safely remove possession?
column5: Demonic possession most often happens through the use of tethers, which can connect a demon in the Abyss to a physical body on Adelrune, much in the way of a summoner bringing a soul into their body for a time. When the tethered spirit is stronger than the soul of the body it is possessing, it is nearly impossible for the soul of the physical body to push out the possessing spirit on their own. This is why summoning is so dangerous when one dabbles in powers above the levels they are capable of controlling, and tethers should not just be left around for any one to pick up (as seen most recently in Dace during the War of Radiance). The most direct and safest way to remove the possession is through a Rite of Possession, though in some cases where the spirit tied to the tether aren't *particularly* powerful simply removing the tether from the physical body may suffice.

Topic: how was the healing of the blood tree achieved previously?
column5: Records about the former blood tree are few and far in between as it occurred before recorded history. As the stories go, however, two gods of immeasurable power saw the world would be destroyed by the Blood Tree. So deeply did they love one another that they could not bear to see the world destroyed and lose each other in the process, and so these two great beings fused themselves to the Blood Tree, creating something wholly new - the World Tree.

Topic: Nameless Gods hiding in other pantheons?
column5: While people have theorized for decades that the Nameless Faith hide amongst other pantheons, with everything tossed around from Old Ways to Celestine, to Chorus himself, or the Mandalan dragons, there is no evidence that this has ever been the case.

Topic: what is known about moonshard's abilities/weaknesses? any way to impede his ability to travel through shadows?
column5: Little is known of Moonshard as it is not a demon that has been walking on Adelrune or documented in recent history. From what you have observed, his connections with shadow would theoretically suggest a weakness to light or while in bright light. His ability to travel through shadows seems like it functions similarly to the socererous shadow form, rather than true teleportation, however without studying it more directly it is difficult to say. Some lively debate around town also suggests that perhaps Mehs' portal abilities are providing additional support to the impression of proper teleportation in and out of shadows, or Moonshroud might be leaning on Julinar's habit of slipping in and out of places unnoticed.

Topic: how do we get in and out of the oubliette? what are possible things within the oubliette?
column5: Any number of powerful extra-planar creature could theoretically take one to the Oubliette - usually for a price. A fae, a demon, a celestial, a ferryman, any of the above could likely do so to some extent. The Oubliette has many areas contained within it which often mean no two trips to the Oubliette are the same, for if someone finds their way to the maze within a maze within a maze and they don't find themselves lost there forever, the Returned were known to have retrieved the Guardian's Journal and the Black Sands Hourglass from its depths. The Iron Gate is said to have once trapped endless fae behind it's gate. And deeper still, its said there is a prison only accessible by a ritual requiring all six of the Chalices to open its gate, which the Returned once threw the Celestials Star and Prae through to their eternity of imprisonment.

Topic: what kind of fire was used to damage the world tree root?
column5: Mehs used demonic fire powered by his divinity to corrupt the root of the World Tree. As the power is entropic/abyssal in nature, it will slowly spread corruption in the world that will lead to the world's undoing. Scholars of demonic studies at Alexander have noted however, that the greater world of Adelrune is resiliant and can likely withstand some level of corruption settling into it (similarly as Breach has become a wasteland due to demonic entropy). It would likely take the loss of multiple roots before major effects of the corruption would be seen across the world, though localized areas to the corruptions will see such effects much earlier.

Topic: RESEARCH: NEEDS NEW TOPIC
are esparei's tears capable of being used in a ritual to hinder Bel'e'athru's regalia?
column5: Yes, it is likely that a particular one of the Tears of Espari would be capable of hindering or flat out destroying Bel'e'athru's regalia.

Topic: how do you destroy a copy of a true name? are there any special considerations or preparations for the process?
column5: destroying a copy of a true name is as simple as burning the paper that it is magically inscribed on, much as one would burn a spell scroll or ritual.

Topic: how can true names be copied or inscribed on a new object?
column5: True Names capable of being inscribed onto an object are akin to one-time use rituals, which removes the ability to inscribe them on more than one weapon/shield/etc. If a True Name ritual is appliable, it only takes a crafter of the appropriate type and a use of blood ink to inscribe it on an item.

Topic: research: glyphs on vicaul longships
column5: The glyphwrites of the Vicaul are known to be the most skilled glyph ritualists in the world. While defensive wards are seen on other cities and walls like Castle Thorn and the former-Ad Decimum, and even Cestral has adopted some of the glyphs they've found on Vicaul longships, all of that scholarship and study has been stolen from the original works of the Vicaul's glyphwrites. Glyphwrites can make longships far faster and more durable than their size would normally allow. Added glyphs can make them resistant to magical and mundane ranged attacksl, able to carry a larger capacity than they initially seem to be capable of (both warriors and cargo), divination-connected tracking and navigation glyphs, repel glyphs to keep sea beasts at a distance, glyph enchantments on anchors, and even elemental boons on the sails themselves.

Topic: is there overlapping story of the divine bow in the nameless faith?
column5: No, the story of the Divine Bow killing Eon has no analogous counterpart within Nameless Faith tradition except perhaps in the fact it is said that the "Nameless have the ability to kill *anyone* at *any moment*". Though, suggesting that the Nameless are capable of killing gods at a moment's notice in the same way they could remove sovereigns on Adelrune would likely spark religious and scholarly outcry.

Topic: who are the gods of the nameless faith? blocked divination on, consult a saek heartseeker expert
column5: The Nameless are called such for a reason - there is little documented of this pantheon or if it has any true divinity behind it at all. The ultimate goal of Nameless followers are to become gods themselves - Self Above All - and so scholars debate how many gods, if any, are truly part of the pantheon. This is made even more unclear as the "leaders" of the faith are also called Nameless. As the faith is illegal in nearly every Rules of Society nation, it keeps to the fringes of society so there are very few things known about it. The closest thing one can find to names of the Nameless are some vague monikers such as "the widow bride" or "goddess of darkness", typically extracted during questioning if a Namleless follower has been found out/discovered, though these are usually members quite low in the ranks and thus wouldn't know more as to whom these monikers might belong.

Topic: consult an expert. block divination. how is the pyr's cipher used? has it been used around town recently?
column5: Pyr's Cipher is most often used in communications between members of the Nameless in correspondences, military orders, assassin targets/hits. Additionally it may be used as an innocuous symbol or writing on posters or other alert locations around a city to quickly spread word to a local sect. However, it is not restricted to the Nameless and will occasionally be used by thieves and other underworld-sorts as a type of written thieves cant, so one cannot always assume that knowledge of the cipher immediately correlates to someone being Nameless.  Unfortunately current goings-on around town are not covered by research as it is what is found in written library texts, so you would want to explore Influence options to gather more currently-active information in town.

Topic: how do you free a soul trapped in the death realm?
column5: The most common method of moving souls within death realms is the Toll of the Ferryman, which can move a non-godmarked soul from one death realm to another. As far as stealing a soul back from the death realm, the Saek Library you used does not have this information, but perhaps Laurel Seccari would remember a past journey to rescue the soul of a friend. As Returned, however, you could quickly theorize that because the body had two souls at the time of death - one Returned and one not, the Returned soul went to the ferryman while the non-Returned soul stayed in the physical body and it did not Mist. Because the body is that of a Returned, should the body die again, it is entirely probable that the Returned soul will have the opportunity to return to their body once more.

Topic: is the blood chalice a singular item, or something that can be reproduced multiple times?
column5: The Blood Chalice is a single cup, small and made of red glass, that was used at the forming of the Chalice Orders. Each order has a specific, ceremonial cup that is a ritual component for important Chalice rituals, both secret rituals within the Blood Chalice and inter-Chalice order rituals. On its own, the Chalice appears mundane and (if you search the Guild of Academics as well) you would find records written by "Master of Coin Caeli Oakenthorn" documenting the Blood Chalice's arrival in the hands of the Returned, a record of the many years it remained under her care within the vault, and a notation in 8ar that the Blood Chalice was returned to its rightful owner following the War of Radiance.

Topic: other than bridge over the black gate, how do we open a portal to the void to bring beings through it?
column5: The simplest methods to pass between realms typically involve making deals with beings capable of crossing through different planes - ferrymen, demons, and fae are the most common. It is theoretically possible to reach the Void by way of the Oubliete or potentially something within the Book of Nox, or a far more extreme route such as when the Witch of the Witherwood destroyed a major artifact to rip a hole between Adelrune and the Spirit Realm. Regarding the Etilvor in the Void, however, is that access to reaching them is only possible for as long as the ritual to keep them there holds. If the anchor of the ritual, presumably still in the Etilvor Homestead beneath the mountain, fails it is entirely probable that those within the Void will be lost for good.

Topic: who is controlling the sea monsters sent against the vyn'e'va? how?
column5: Without being within the Mists and interacting with the situation, it is impossible to answer the question of "who controls the sea monsters against the Vyn'e'va" as off the current moment (this is better approached through influence actions, as it is a developing plot and not something recorded in a library or text from the past).   However, in recent years, it is known that the Lady of the Mists sent many sea monsters after House Vyn'e'va, and she is not one to quickly forgive and forget - nor is she one to allow just anyone to have such levels of control over sea monsthers without her blessing. From the Assassin, you would know as well that the sea monsters are working with a large fleet of over two hundred ships that set sail from Inexo harbor during the summer months.

Topic: An Ancient Spellbook from House Ra'e'len
An Ancient Spellbook from House Ra'e'len
history of Antioc and the Bendriven family. were they rulers? residents? etc?
column5: Unfortunately you do not have lores which would be particularly useful in uncovering this history. However, with your Military Experience and presence during the Crusades, you would know that General Bryndon Bendriven was the ruling head of Antioc when he departed Tear to lead the armies of the Crusades against demonkind. Whether or not King General was a hereditary title or awarded by military merit, however, is unclear. House Bendriven was indeed a founding House of Antioc, along with Trevinian and Tigaris, all noble families of the pre-Amalgamation areas that refused to bend knee to the Shrike during her conquest and thus fled across the sea to build themselves a safehaven well beyond her reach and influence.

Topic: research: time spent at Alexander - were there any wards or specific symbols on the religious scrolls? does the one not with storm/lightning motifs have any unique iconography or theme?

Sir Valdir Velyrone has one of the CT noble houses named after him. I'm looking into House Velyrone, its history, great leaders, specializations, and information to fill in the gaps. Additionally since he was alive during the founding, his relationship with the Castle.
column5: Note: Only the current timeline is relevent, and thus anything from the old timeline is not findable in research actions as it only exists in Returned memories.  Sir Valdir Velyrone is considered a war hero who fought at the Final Battle of the Crusades with the Iron Chalice. Though he served with distinction, and was provided the honor of his family name being carried on, he did not stay for the founding nor building of Castle Thorn. Before departing to places unknown with his wife Dame Ophelia, he passed his role as the Head of the Iron Chalice to Sir Mordecai. It is entirely unclear where Valdir and Ophelia settled down, but the stories say they lived out their days in a quiet and happy life following the tumultous danger and upheaval of their childhoods and young adult years.

Topic: what language is the imp speaking? can we destroy the necklace without causing collateral damage? can we take the necklace off and remove the possession without killing the imp?
column5: The language the imp is speaking is unrecognizeable and likely some particularly ancient dialect. The necklace could not be destroyed by mundane means. Any attempt to remove it caused the imp to grow agitated and bite at you, and it quickly became evident that the draw of the neckalce's power would immediately attach itself to whomever attempted to handle it regardless of wearing gloves or if it was hung on the end of a sword in preparation of cutting the chain. To remove the possession, one would need to do a religious Rite of Possession. Assuming that you've kept possession of the imp, the necklace shatters at midnight at the end of Saturday night of the November gathering, releasing the spirit that was possessing the imp.

Topic: which god/entity is controlling the imp? how do we properly restrain someone with the necklace? is it more evolved or different than the others?
column5: As the Nameless gods and followers are unknown, it is impossible to know which particular entity is controlling this necklace. Restraining someone with a Blessed necklace is as simple as imprisoning them in some way so they cannot escape - in the case of an imp, being tied up or put in a jail cell is likely to be enough. Neither the imp nor the necklace itself seems to be "more evolved" than the others, only that it has a stronger leader-type spirit possessing it that guided the other possessed imps in building the shrines and worshipping.

Topic: State of the Court: Cole
column5: The current hot topics around Court in Cole are: - The Head of the Military has recently gone missing - The upcoming launch of the largest ship ever constructed! There will be a big launch party! - early maneuvering and backdoor agreements being made for the upcoming Great Game - the large fleet of ships that previously resided in Inexo harbor and have set sail once more, this time sailing into the Mists of the Eastern Expanse - a great deal of ash and debris is in the water to the north, so everyone is giving the Drir islands an even wider berth than normal

It's best to avoid the northern coasts of the Expanse entirely.
