column5: - Rumor has it, House Ariadne has extended their spell scroll empire to Tear and the Returned to counter the loss of their trade partners in Ad Decimum. - Rumor has it, Lord Dmitri Ariadne is making a play for the throne in Dace. - Rumor has it, House Ariadne has made deep inroads in the Nadine Empire through profitable political marriages and alliances. - Rumor has it, not only will the next Queen be of House Ariadne, but the military will be led by one of her sisters following their father's retirement. - Rumor has it, with Inexo's fleet departed from the Breach city, House Ariadne is raising a force to go take the port town and found their own settlement to facilitate trade between Dace, the Nadine Empire, and the Breach cities.

Topic: State of the Court: Celestine Empire
column5: The following topics are of most note circulating the Celestine Court: - With the absence/abdication of Queen Diana coming out of the Mists, it is time to choose a new queen - So many years of Mists have heavily destroyed the agricultural stocks that the nation relies on, and while they had their food stores refilled midway through the Mists, the last of the stable food sources is nearly out again and there is not even enough seed to plant for a winter crop. If something is not done soon, the nation will starve - Diseases seem to be gone from the nation as a whole, and the Intl Healer's Guild is at a loss for what they should be doing regarding that - The religious are debating about the rumor of Queen Diana's supposed ascension to godhood during the Mists - Bards in the capital are singing a song called The Vision, spreading news about three new gods and the light they offer the world - Whispers about the stars being strange and divinations being particularly unclear in recent months

Topic: Underworld Insight: Whispering Ghosts
column5: The Whispering Ghosts are a Port Frey-based gang that specialize in smuggling and banditry along the roads, and are doing well for themselves with the instability of Port Frey's current condition. Though they claim to be looking to reinstate their official charter with the Western Mercenary Conclave, their actions in taking advantage of the situation to line their pockets and position themselves better among the other underworld organizations in relation to the new governement suggest otherwise.

Topic: Military Insight: General Melisma "The Composer"
column5: Melisma's forces are difficult to get a full read on, as they were previously spread throughout the Nadine Empire. What you can get confirmation of are the following: Lieutenant Filigree, 100 Demon Mages, 100 Corruptor Demons, 1500 Lesser Demons (Twisted Legion), 500 Lesser Corruptors, 100 Demon Knights (Stone Legion), 10 enthralled imperial scouts, 100 enthralled imperial peasants. It does seem, however, that her forces are quickly consolidating in the mountains now, and that she has a new "Lieutenant" for additional support.

Topic: Military Insight: Lt Philgrin "the Merrymaker"
column5: Lt. Filigree serves under General Melisma, and will often lead special task forces from her ranks on particularly sensitive missions.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: a Returned called Tophania Invictus holds higher loyalties towards another than the Nadine Empire
column5: It is unclear which loyalties are truly greater, but it can be confirmed that Tophania Invictus has taken up with the Children of the Witch Queen and at best their loyalties are divided.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Vyn'e'va
column5: Typically confined to their own home, the Vyn'e'va don't get out in the world much and are unlikely to connect with underworld networks. However, there are rumors that a ship of effendal were recently spotted among the islands of the Vicaul lands, but it seems to have been isolated there by the Inexo fleet and many active sea monsters in the region, leaving them with no way to retreat.

Topic: Follow the Money: Celestine Courier Service
column5: The Celestine Courier Service is a self-sustaining guild headquartered within the Celestine Empire but with outposts and couriers all over Adelrune who are known for their exceptional service and ability to deliver mail nearly anywhere in the known world, no matter the danger posed. It's largest financial partner is the International Merchants' Guild, who provide ships to move mail overseas as necessary between different outposts, where the Courier Service will once again take possession of the mail for local delivery. While the Service makes enough to pay their couriers well for their time and efforts, it doesn't make a particularly large profit, and so most citizens of Rules of Society nations are generally respectful of the couriers and let them go on their way. Though couriers are paid well, and the protection of the mail is their topmost priority, there are some individual couriers who have been known to take additional payoffs to let mail go missing or be read by someone who was not the intended recipient. However, these couriers are risking their position and will quickly be removed if they are discovered to have violated the sanctity of the mail service.

Topic: Political Insight: Celestine Courier Service
column5: The Celestine Courier Service is well-respected among all Rules of Society nations and their reputation is spotless. As such, they are on good terms and in the good graces of each of these nations, allowing them travel privledges through areas not typically permitted (such as Dace has allowed for three Celestine couriers to be stationed there).

Topic: Investigate History: Myren defenses & weaknesses
column5: Very little has been written about the defenses and weaknesses of Myre as the world has no real knowledge or interaction with Myre to have recorded such things. It is rumored that their greatest defense are the Walls of Distortion: the outer walls and entrances to the city which confound and confuse the senses of any who are not of the Kingdom of Myre if they attempt to pass through. Gael and residents of the Blightwood have made any number of attempts on the walls throughout the Age of Myre, only one successfully - and the engineers of Myre quickly corrected that singular weakness that was uncovered. For the many who were lost within the walls, it is said that the citizens of Myre watched in delight, and starvation was the kindest death they could hope for.

column5: - Rumor has it, the Banner of the Blood Crow recently lost over a thousand of their blood mages to the ranks of T'manius' vampires. - Rumor has it, the Bsnner of the Blood Crow continues to reside in Effendal lands upon Tear. - Rumor has it, the Banner of the Blood Crow intends to travel to Westwall and claim their ancient lands from the Celestine Empire now that the Gael Forests have been lost. - Rumor has it, the Blood Crow have been at odds with the Black Wolf since the Mists lifted on Tear. - Rumor has it, the Banner of the Blood Crow were blessed with magic less than a decade ago, with many of their number quite suddenly capable of great feats of blood magic seemingly without warning.

Topic: Political Insight: Moonshroud kidnapping House Ire'enil?
column5: Many of the leaders of Redemption are effendal of House Ire'enil and are longer lived, and so kidnapping these leaders would make logical sense when it comes to seeking specific old information that they would likely be the ones to still have knowledge of it. The Effendal of House Ire'enil who reside in Redemption are known to retreat to their former homestead a few times a year of special ceremonies or holidays, making this pilgrimage through deep, unmappable tunnels known only to the Effendal, and thus it is possible that what Moonshroud is seeking is located within these tunnels or near the old ruins of their homestead.

Topic: Military Insight: Moonshroud & Forces (weaknesses)
column5: Moonshroud doesn't appear to have many weaknesses except one would presume light. His forces on Breach are comprised of: 120 former Defiant Demons, 100 Demon Mages, 300 Corruptor Demons, 500 Demonic Butchers (Twisted Legion), 1500 Lesser Demons (Twisted Legion), and 500 Lesser Corruptors. They currently march unopposed on Breach with no true army in Redemption or from the Saek to stand against them.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Moonshroud's intentions with Redemption
column5: It appears that should Moonshroud and his forces continue to go unchecked, that he is determined to crack open a root of the world tree in Redemption's backyard, creating a wellspring of corruption to feed a new demon city in Breach.

column5: The Legendary Blade of the Blood Chalice is a sword is mentioned throughout history to be carried by a knight of the order in defense of the Blood Chalice. There are stories that say that the day the White Chalice executed the whole order the sword took the lives of a dozen White Chalice knights before it's weilder was taken down, but there is no factual evidence this was the case as the White Chalice reported no losses that day. If it was true, however, it is entirely likely that the sword and the blood chalice fell into the possession of the White Chalice that day.

Topic: Investigate History: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: Rumors of the Nameless Faith in Port Frey have long circulated, particularly among the Returned. Their first known, overt actions taken were not in Port Frey, but when the Returned went to Edge in July of 8ar to liberate the city from demon control - many shrines and churches had been corrupted by the demon forces, and as part of the liberation the Returned rededicated many of these shrines to their own various faiths. The Nameless Faith Returned had a particular knack for catching quite a few of the smaller shrines and converting a portion of the local populace in doing so. In Port Frey in the following years, the arrival of Belisarius from Ad Decimum came with a cadre of Nameless followers keeping an eye on him, as well as influencing those around them with the spread of the faith. Most notably, however, was the rise of the "Blessed," a subsect within the Nameless Faith who spread their "blessings" to those around them and built shrines throughout the city in secret. While a war between Yog Zura and the Nameless brewed in the background, a local Nameless sect of Returned drew the attention of the Blessed when they destroyed the Book of Ig and the Blessed finally had a dedicated force willing to push forward the goals of the so-called "Zealot". In the end, the Zealot was banished from his host body, but not before carrying out the sacrifice of Yog Zura to his beloved "Princess".

Topic: Military Insight: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: The Nameless Faith does not seem to have an active military force on Tear, nor any known military force in the world. It is rumored that most assassins, thieves, and rogues adhere at least in some manner to the Nameless doctrine, which does not seem to be a stretch as those whose skills can be bought (gang members and mercenaries) are common pawns to push their dark agendas.

Topic: Investigate Alliances: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: The Returned seem to be the greatest allies of the Nameless Faith, as they refuse to declare the faith illegal, as well as bolstering it's numbers and strengthening its influence in the region.

Topic: Political Insight: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: It is said that the Nameless have followers in positions of political leadership all oveer the world, and indeed when the creed of the faith is "self above all" politics is the perfect position for those seeking power. If they do not have members in those direct positions of power, they have members close to various leaders, for they are rumored to always be close enough to kill any significant or powerful figure at any time - though it is rarely known when an unexpected death or assassination is the work of the Nameless, as they do not advertise their actions. Within the Nameless, the most dangerous are those among the high leadership - those known as "Nameless" or "Disciples" - who oversee various sects though the sects are not usually aware of others acting in the region due to the isolation and independence of each group. These leaders are known to manipulate the nations of Adelrune in a sort of international shadow government, and are said to have shaped Adelrune's history with the removal of key figures during wars and times of peace, while replacing them with those whose ideals and goals (often unwittingly) will serve the greater benefit of the Nameless.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: - Rumor has it, there was a Nameless follower among the lower-ranked seneschals of the Unity Parliament. - Rumor has it, before the fall of Port Frey, groups of dark-clad figures met near the walls in the darkest of night to worship their gods. - Rumor has it, the most populous faith in Port Frey is the Nameless. - Rumor has it, Yog Zura sought to take what was not his - and was caged for his bad behavior. - Rumor has it, many cults unknowingly gain their boons and divine favors from the Nameless gods. - Rumor has it, even though the Zealot was banished from his host in Port Frey and the Blessed have broken, his spirit still lingers deep in the city, waiting.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Nameless Faith in Port Frey
column5: Followers of the Nameless Faith dwell in every underworld organization across Adelrune. They may not be among the leaders, but they are there all the same. For many, underworld organizations and work are taken up by either the most desperate or the most greedy of society, those who seek more for themselves whether through money or power, and so they are the easiest to manipulate because the Nameless will offer them what they want in exchange for their efforts. They do not require faith or belief in particular gods to serve the Nameless, if someone is serving themselves at the expense of others then they are acting according to Nameless doctrine.

Topic: Investigate Rumor: the wellspring of life
column5: - Rumor has it, the Wellspring of Life is the Song of Dragons - Rumor has it, the Wellspring of Life is Ren, the Root of the World - Rumor has it, the Wellspring of Life is Veyn, the Lord of Fresh Water - Rumor has it, the Wellspring of Life is the World Tree - Rumor has it, the Wellspring of Life is Destiny, who spun the world out of Chaos. - Rumor has it, the Wellspring of Life is Cri, the Torrent.

Topic: Investigate History: Zuul
column5: Zoul was called "the Imp", and was a demon god who appeared as a twisted and wrinkled black imp. He was never known to be particularly strong, but it is said he once killed a powerful fae whose name has long been forgotten to history. In recent years, he was kept imprisioned by C'thon Darkweaver and slowly drained of his power, until he was traded to Mehs as part of an arrangement to have T'tocs returned to Adelrune from the Abyss. It is said that he was killed in the ensuing ritual by The Inferno.

Topic: Investigate History: Lady Order
column5: Lady Order, also known as "the Still One" is one of the least-known among the Celestine Pantheon, as she was never worshipped by the Celestine Church. It is theorized that much of the imagery and tradition that may have once been attributed to her in the ancient past (before the rise of the Celestine Empire) is now generally associated with Destiny herself, who wove Order out of Chaos, so there is no need for her to exist within the Celestine tradition.

Investigate Alliances: the Hand
column5: It is said that The Hand have their hands in every pie and every pocket, and the saying came about for a very specific reason: the allies of The Hand are many. Every underworld organization (gang and mercenary) on Tear, in Edge, in Cole, and and most organizations on the Expanse, among others, would stand beside The Hand as ally in any type of dispute. Should the Crown of Dace declare The Hand their enemy, it will not mean well for Dace.

Topic: Follow the Money: Greater Consortium
column5: The Greater Consortium is a collective that oversees (and enforces) Dacian trade across the world. Not nobility, but certainly “upper middle class”, their work is essential, as they represent the Dacian noble houses in handling their resources as the “worker bees” of the Dacian hive. They help to mediate disputes between the houses, and are considered to be the closest thing to a neutral voice in Dacian politics. Since the Greater Consortium is made up of the heads of each of the noble houses’ trading companies, the Consortium still directly answers to the Houses, though the members are not nobles themselves. Since they control Dace’s treasury and decide where and how to invest that money, the Greater Consortium wields tremendous power and influence across the world. Many of Dace's nationally sponsored social events (such as Revel or the Fairweather Festival) are also planned by the Greater Consortium.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: small time fae activity on the island to train fae hunting on

Topic: Underworld Insight: Boiling Bones
column5: The Boiling Bones are a local Port Frey gang who are known for necromancy and kidnappings, where they will snatch someone off the street who is of import and attempt to ransom them back to their families or organizations - if they pay, the person will be returned alive and unharmed; if they refuse to pay, the person will be returned as an undead to destroy the home or organization and recover the ransom money for the Boiling Bones. Either way, they're getting paid. The Boiling Bones aren't a particularly large organization, but they are stable within the parts of Port Frey they control without overreaching, and they are clever enough to avoid directly opposing the Returned when possible.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Boiling Bones
column5: - Rumor has it, it's impossible to know how many members of the Boiling Bones there are because only a few show their faces. - Rumor has it, the Boiling Bones are taking advantage of the vampire activity in town to line their pockets while everyone is looking the other way. - Rumor has it, the Boiling Bones are pulling in big money by kidnapping and ransoming the nobles who chose to stay behind in Port Frey when Unity departed. - Rumor has it, you're next.  - Rumor has it, nobles should not travel alone in Port Frey after dark.

Topic: Investigate Fame: Noren
column5: Noren was once revered as the god of spellcraft, and was the favored god of ancient mages. Early art and stories depict him reading upon a pile of books in a comfortable library.

Topic: Investigate Loved Ones: Noren
column5: With Noren having been believed dead for centuries, there is little factual evidence of his loved ones. It is unclear among scholars if either of the following are true because accounts vary, and both theories are plausible but contradictory:  - Noren never had a mate, but did possess a horde of eggs that were smashed or consumed by a rival dragon, - Noren had a mate who was killed by a red dragon Currently, however, what is commonly accepted is that Noren appears to be a solitary dragon.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Noren
column5: Ancient stories about Noren suggest he's obsessed with literature, and was known to read extensively. While many believed he was dead for centuries, there have always scholars who liked to joke that he just "got lost in a good book" for that time.

Topic: Investigate Scandals: Noren
column5: There are no known scandals involving Noren as he has been presumed dead for centuries.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: Caeli Oakenthorn
column5: - Rumor has it, Caeli Oakenthorn cannot be killed and bears the favor of a death god - Rumor has it, Caeli Oakenthorn keeps a horde of mind-controlling demonic spiders at her disposal. - Rumor has it, Caeli Oakenthorn is not actually one person, but a team of similar-looking women - Rumor has it, Caeli Oakenthorn has blackmail she can hold against any head of state on Adelrune. - Rumor has it, Caeli Oakenthorn abandoned the druids of Cestral when they needed her most

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: Annette Bennett
column5: - Rumor has it, Annette Bennett has retired from public service - Rumor has it, Annette Bennett is a mother of four, and a grandmother of sixteen. - Rumor has it, Annette Bennett is a shapeshifter demon - Rumor has it, Annette Bennett was forced into retirement as she can no longer hold a pen or quill due to severe damage and shaking in her hands - Rumor has it, the Silver Chalice tortured Annette Bennet for information about Mehs

Topic: Investigate Alliances: Hastings
column5: Hastings has its strongest alliance within the military-minded Eastern Alliance (Kingdom of Vein, and formerly the Duchy of Whitehall), and good working relationships for trade with the Celestine Empire, Dace, Castle Thorn, Cole, and Cestral.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines + Healthy Paranoia: C'thon
column5: On an average day, C'thon spends time reading, taking reports from various scouts, and handles preparations for the inevitable reappearance of T'tocs on his doorstep. He is well aware that T'tocs is plotting against him, and countermeasures are already being put into place to stop any attack. Among is vices, C'thon appreciates the finer things in life - good alcohol, and good book, and a victory over his enemies.

Topic: Eavesdrop: C'thon
column5: - "Did you hear that some of those Returned sent the Darkweaver a package of celestial blood and a Wing Clipper sword? I guess they like him after all." - "I heard he just went to a party in Port Frey and no one even tried to stab him! They must know he's too powerful to simply be killed!" - "Have you seen that beautiful necklace he's been working on? I wonder who it's for?" - "Just found a new batch of bodies that are in good condition, we'd best get those to the camp as soon as possible to get working on them. The Darkweaver will be pleased."

Topic: Military Insight: Lightbreaker's Army
column5: Currently located in the region around Castle Thorn, Lightbreaker's army is composed of: 100 Demon Mages, 100 Corruptor Demons, 1500 Lesser Demons (Twisted Legion), 500 Lesser Corruptors, 150 Demon Knights (Stone Legion)

Topic: Military Insight: The Defiant
column5: The Defiant came together during the War of Radiance to stand against Bel'e'athu and after their defeat (and deaths in some cases), were scattered. There is no news about them coming back together since Bel'e'athru's defeat.

Topic: Basic Scouting: Are Lightbreaker's forces on the mainland of Tear?
column5: No

Topic: Basic Scouting: Are C'thon's forces on the mainland of Tear
column5: No

Topic: Underworld Insight + Healthy Paranoia: the begger king & the hand
column5: Once a fairly reliable member of the top gang in the world - The Hand - the Begger King has had a serious fall from grace and brought undue attention down on the organization. Following the assassinations of the Dacian princesses and the reveal that they were involved in making the deal for it to happen, the Begger King has gone to ground and has not been seen or heard from in a number of months. It is rumored that they are not only hiding from Viluca Ariadne, the next expected Admiral of the Dacian Fleet, but also from the Hand itself who is rumored to be cleaning house and hanging the Begging King out to dry to get Dace off their own backs.

Topic: Follow the Money: Broken Brick
column5: As the Broken Brick is an anarchist organization, keeping financial books and records is antithetical to their efforts.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: demon army searching the Blightwood for an artifact
column5: - Rumor has it, the Myrens are marching with demons in the Blightwood. - Rumor has it, something was lost in the Blightwood that the Myrens, demons, and residents of the Blightwood are all seeking to find first. - Rumor has it, the residents of the Blightwood are united in standing against the demonic forces. - Rumor has it, the demons will burn down what remains of the Blightwood to find another root of the world tree. - Rumor has it, even more trees in the Blightwood have shrivled and died in recent months, leaving a large swath of the forest with no living plant life so the demons can march through uncontested.

Topic: Looking into any potential Radolond/Ranes artifacts that may be recoverable from the Purges era
column5: There are always rumors and regrets about things left behind at home when a group is forced to move quickly, but from any texts dating to that era, the Radolond did have time to prepare to leave and seemed to have brought their artifacts, powerful objects, and necessities with them. House Ranes, however, had no such luck as they were rounded up quickly and dragged from their homes. Nor did the Ney'ewn or Deneail in the years before. It's always been theorized that their homes were left abandoned and not ransacked (or even found) by the Amalgamation forces, so it seems likely that there would be artifacts and such things left behind if one was able to find and enter their homestead and tribe territories. In particular, it has been suggested that Deneail once had an object of great power that gave the wearer the ability to dominate and control those around them. You'd also find a notation that Kitar, Adventurer Extraordinaire, once entered the Ney'ewn home and recovered a magic ring that gave him powerful fire abilities - and he escaped with only a few minor singes to his fur!

Topic: how is the Narrator tied to new born dreams and their tether loss?
column5: Narrators are not directly connected to the creation of New Born Dreams, nor their tether loss. They are merely dream entities that are drawn to new stories and wish to follow along with them to see how they turn out.

Topic: Hexamaran eating Aien
column5: It is a tale as old as time, really, in which demons and celestials will fight one another in the great Eternal Battle until Adelrune finds its end. The greatest of the celestial knights were rooted to their duty in this conflict - to contain the demonic waves in the Abyss and keep the fight out of the other planes - ever vigilant when the fight drew near, but slumbering when all grew quiet once more.  Long before recorded history began, but passed down in the stories of the Ire'enil effendal, is the tale of Aien, the Knight of Flesh. The Great Celestial bound by their oath to keep the demon incursion from reaching Adelrune, had taken residence in the lush forests of the Southern Expanse. After thwarting yet another attempt to invade Adelrune from the Abyss, Aien sought rest to recover from a grevious wound. As they slumbered, Hexamaran "The Living Disease" made his way into the Adelrune from elsewhere, spreading new and horrific diseases in his wake as he wound his way carefully toward the ever-vigilant knight. Still healing from their wounds, Aien began to rouse, sensing something off around them, but it was far too late - for the diseases that radiated out from Hexamaran had already reached and sunk into the wounds of the Great Celestial. With infection spreading rapidly through their already weakened form, Aien stood no chance - and Hexamaran set upon them with a fervor, devouring the Knight of Flesh in a horrific display to earn himself the moniker "The Flesh Eater." With Aien fallen, Adelrune's defender from the demonic invasion was gone, and it was only a matter of time before Zyte began his quest to open great portals to the Abyss.

Topic: who are the temple knights of the golden choir and what are their ideals?
column5: As the Golden Choir is not a national religion of a Rules of Society nation, there is no temple order of knights dedicated to this faith.

Topic: RESEARCH: WAITING ON NEW TOPIC
How does losing a tether effect a newborn dream and is it similar to Shakes/Ayan?
column5: Losing a tether is similar to Ayan, Shakes, and other dream entities in that a New Born Dream loses part of themself - however, unlike these mortal beings who *became* dream creatures and tether loss presents as madness, tether loss for a New Born Dream looks more like the closing of a chapter. Often this will be the loss of innocence/youth and moving into the next stage of life of their story. Additionally, all New Born Dreams are tied to the Well of Heroes, and while they would not lose their tethers if it was destroyed or damaged, it would stop NEW New Born Dreams from being created.

Topic: is there a way to reduce the madness gained though learning dream magic?
column5: There is no way to reduce the madness caused by learning dream magic. It can only be "reset" by the willfull forgetting of dream magic.

Topic: what is the history of Nadir?
column5: The name Nadir has not been heard on Adelrune for centuries, except in old bardic tales of the fearsome Lightbreakers. Nadir often fills the role of the powerful trickster or deceiver in old myths and fables, but any actual actions taken or direct references to his choices are vague at best as they occurred before recorded history. What is known, however, is that he was killed on Adelrune and returned to the Abyss to recover his strength and attempt once more to make his way back to Adelrune.

Topic: How can Sablegore's hand be used against other demonic generals of Mehs?
column5: Sablegore's hand is specifically attuned to directly counter another of Mehs' generals so they all keep one another in check with mutually-assured destruction should they attempt to turn on one another or Mehs. In this particular case, the person attuned to Sablegore's hand will be more effective in combating something that stone or dirt is capable of smothering, such as embers or smoke.

Topic: which of the other general's would have the power item to use against Sablegore?
column5: Since you don't have the full list of demon generals to study their relations to one another at this point, there is very little information you can put together clearly. However, you can surmise that whichever demon general would have the power item to use against Sablegore likely has an affinity for something that is used to cut or wear down stone.

Topic: create a ritual to destroy or at least weaken Bel'e'athru's regalia
column5: OOC note: There are no general rituals which would destroy artifacts, nor will one be approved for ritual creation, as this is liable to break the entire game.  If a ritual to destroy artifacts existed, Bel'e'athru's regalia would likely have been destroyed at some point in the past as it has shown up multiple times throughout history for great demon wars. It is theorized, however, that should an Abyssal Monarch who is in possession of all three pieces of the regalia (crown, scepter, and completed throne) *willingly* choose to abdicate the throne, that the selfless choice made by an Abyssal Monarch would destroy the regalia and the demon themself.

Topic: what rituals can be performed to embue blood runes on armor? what are some possible affects of blood runes like the ones blood chalice knights wore into battle?
column5: Any creation of blood runes for armor would need to be submitted as armorsmithing crafting research, paired with blood magic and warding, through the Rules team

Topic: what is the connection between the World Tree and the Wellspring of Life? Where and how did the wellspring of life originate?
column5: Unfortunately Lore: Old Ways is not particularly helpful for following this line of questioning, but with rituals and a World Tree priest assisting you who has visited the World Tree, you would be able to connect enough pieces to confirm that the wellspring of life is likely a reference to the spring that flows among the roots at the world tree which was not on Adelrune. In particular, the plane that contains the world tree was the closest to Adelrune it has been in centuries at the time of the crossing over during the Clocktower games, and with the culmination and destruction of the Tower, that plane has once more begun to move away from Adelrune.

Topic: what level of channeling is required to either transfer Es'enabh's curse or spread it amongst many? how many are needed to share the curse so the effects are minimal?
column5: Any level of channeler is capable to transfer the Curse of Es'enabh, providing they have access to the spell "Transfer Magic." It is impossible to judge how many participants would be needed to water down the Curse enough to have no effect on everyone sharing in the Curse without experimentation and just trying it. (If you intend to do this, please make sure that Crystal is available and present)

Topic: RESEARCH: WAITING ON NEW TOPIC
how is the Golden Realms associated with the sun? is there a way to enter/exit the Golden Realms?
column5: As the moon is considered the "gateway to the Abyss", so too is the sun considered the "gateway to the Golden Realms." There are many ways to travel between planes - if one is capable of existing in that plane in a physical form - ferrymen or powerful extraplanar entities such as fae can take someone across planes, or finding (or creating) a rift between Adelrune and the other plane is possible if extremely dangerous. It is important to keep in mind that like the Abyss, the Golden Realms is not a plane that relies on physicality and thus a physical form will not do well there.

Topic: with a certain number of channelers taking part in the ritual, how many would need to share the curse, and what might be any potential effects?
column5: In spending more time studying the Curse and it's affects on Sinead, you can determine that in addition to the weakened effect on her, she is considered "Damned" at this point in time. This means that when she permanently dies her soul will be sent to the Abyss to be devoured by demons, rather than moving on to the realms of death. As she learned in channeling the Curse through her in the tunnels, it has a profound effect on the channeler and anyone else physically in contact at the time, as the Curse spreads between each of their bodies. With too few participants to carry the Curse, it can be assumed they will all suffer from the same effects she currently is suffering from (if slightly lessened).

Topic: what celestials escaped from the Citadel when it fell and are still unaccounted for? are there different groups/factions of fallen celestials on Tear?
column5: So this information would generally be found through influence spends which are how you gain information about current going-ons in the world, while research can tell you what was written in a book in a library. I can provide some context for what celestials were previously imprisioned in the Citadel, however, and that information can be found below.  Iudicium, a fallen Celestial of Judgement, was killed as the Returned attempted to recapture him. Longanimitas, a particularly fast fallen Celestial with the virtue of Patience refused to yield to those trying to recapture him at the Citadel and subsequently died in the conflict. Navitas, a celestial ringed in fire, who carries an overly-zealous virtue of diligence, and who escaped into the depths of Tear and hasn't been recovered or killed in the months since they were released. And finally there is notation that one additional fallen celestial was recovered and returned to their containment, though their name and information is purposely being obscured.

Topic: how do House Kaelin methods for hunting celestials differ from House Ire'enil's methods?
column5: House Ire'enil and House Kaelin, while both celestial hunters, approached their task with different fervor. Ire'enil was more zealous in their hunting and execution of all celestials, and it was nearly their downfall when the fight came to combatting Vin'nial, a fallen Greater Celestial of freedom. The intervention of the Returned saved Vin'nial for a time, but only a handful of years later their homestead was still destroyed as it became the site of a conflict between Vin'nial and Zyte, the Greater Demon of Tyrrany in which both celestial and demon perished. House Kaelin, their nearest neighbors to the north, however, are usually more discerning in which celestials they choose to cull by aiming for fallen and troublemaking celestials. Prior to the separation of the Western Expanse and Southern Expanse, House Kaelin and House Ire'enil often came together for joint hunts and conflicts to support one another.

Topic: does House Kaelin have a special method for detecting fallen celestials? what is it?
column5: There is no particular method for non-celestials to be able to identify a fallen celestial - it is a sense inherent to being a celestial themselves to know this about their bretheren. The Kaelin make an effort to identify and study various celestials and watch for signs and more extreme behaviors that would suggest a celestial has (or is soon to be) fallen, and they make the conscious decision to kill that celestial by their own judgement.

Topic: stories of zodiac faithful receiving god marks for zodiac gods? can one fall out of favor and lose a god mark?
column5: For those who are exceptional in their service to the gods of the Zodiac, godmarks appear at a similar rate as they do among any other culture of faithful. It is not always clear what the mark is, as they do not match the symbols associated with the Trahazi gods, but any good priest would tell you that to be blessed with such a mark is a reason for celebration. There do not seem to be any stories of gods taking away such marks, but rare is the devout who would turn against their god after such a gift was bestowed upon them.

Topic: when Mandala fell the pilgrimage sites were lost. what is the path forward to rebuild those sites so that Mandalan faithful souls may once again return to the land of Adelrune?
column5: Tales of the pilgrimage for reincarnation are merely that - tales from a time long past before recorded history. Even while the temples still stood and Mandala flourished, the path and ritual surrounding the pilgrimage had long been forgotten (if it had ever been anything more than a story). With the loss of the temples, and the dragons having long-fled Mandala, it is unlikely the pilgramage could ever be completed again.

Topic: how can the world tree faithful return their energies to Adelrune bypassing Eden's realm, like the Mandalan faithful can with a pilgrimage?
column5: Tales of the pilgrimage for reincarnation are merely that - tales from a time long past before recorded history. Even while the temples still stood and Mandala flourished, the path and ritual surrounding the pilgrimage had long been forgotten (if it had ever been anything more than a story). As the rumored pilgrimage was powered by the dragons and was site-specific, it is unlikely that the World Tree faith could develop a similar ritual.

Topic: How do we make a glyphways at Castle Thorn?
column5: If you have all the notes, materials and recipes for building the glyphways, you would merely need to contact Castle Thorn and do so. However, the glyphways work in a particular way at the Citadel due to various details specific to the Citadel's location, and is unlikely to work in exactly the same manner if built elsewhere.

Topic: Lore: Geomancy
column5: Your research tag can be picked up on the bulletin board at logi!

Topic: Is there any way to reach out to Elliya's child to control weather to help fight/sink Inexo's fleet?
column5: There are many different ways to contact dragons and other powerful beings on Adelrune, so it is likely that the baby dragon could be reached eventually. Convincing them to destroy a fleet would require actually convincing them. What you can note from your research, however, is that if the baby takes after its mother at all, it will be notoriously difficult to catch up to because it will always be travelling and on the move as Elliya was. Elliya was most easily reachable through leaving messages with her friends who she would eventually drop back in on for her next grand adventure.

Topic: can we recover the rituals from either the War of Radiance/Ad Decimum or the Purges/Vyn'e'va that cause storms to sink ships?
column5: While any research into the Vyn'e'va's power during the Purges is far out of reach with the Vyn'e'va themselves, you have much more luck when looking into the more recent War of Radiance. There are multiple notations in the documentation of the Returned's involvement that it was Caeli Oakenthorn who began the storm to protect Cestral from the approaching ships, and it was only when she needed to turn her attention to the Strait of Roses to bottleneck the demon forces departing Breach that the Decimals took over control of her storm to continue to provide protections for Cestral. Additionally, there is a notation in Caeli's handwriting that Ad Decimum continuing this storm and running through nearly their whole supply of ritual components, along with sending twenty warders to Tear, was the initial contribition of Ad Decimum to Unity's war effort. If there was still a ritual scroll to control such a storm in Ad Decimum's archives, it is likely in the libraries of the Ashen King of Myre in the current day.

Topic: how fast does the corruption on Tear spread? can we contain or reverse it?
column5: This information is not something that be found in a library or written text, as it has never been experienced in the known world. To discover the spread rates, potential containments, or reversals, you would need to travel to the site of the corruption or find a corrupted creature to interact with it and study it directly.

Topic: why/how did the Blood Crow become blood mages overnight? were the one-winged owl or any other banners affected by a similar phenomena?
column5: Deep in the shelves of the Guild of Academics, an old, well-worn journal of brown leather sits on a shelf. It's pages are over-filled, a great oak tree etched into its stretched cover that is tied shut to keep everything contained within it. It's within this journal that extra pages have been sewn in, old Assassin articles with commentary written in the margins. Old letters sewn in to not be lost. Folded stacks of pages tucked snugly into the middle of the bundle. Sketches and doodles, pages edged in color or dried splashes of spilled tea and blood are common throughout, and all written in the now-familiar scrawl of Caeli Oakenthorn. Buried near the end of what roughly seems to be dated 9AR is an extra bundle of paper - the pages stained with tears and smudged ink. It's in these particular pages that you find what you are looking for.  [Insert the Obituary of Victoria Sela]

Topic: the process by which gods gained their new faces/aliases within the Zodiac and can one simply be added or removed?
column5: The gods of the Zodiac predate the "Zodiac" and go back to the days of the Hazori, they are not gods who have been invited in. The uniqueness of the faith of the Hazori is truly tied to their development as a culture - formerly Gael, they were once faithful to the gods of the Old Ways, but with the development of farming and the growth of a more settled, "civilized" society with the founding of the city of Riel, they began to turn away from many of the old gods and adopting new, more "civilized" gods such as those of law and architecture, or those that could help them stand against their greatest enemy - the Ko'aat. It was only when the Hazori were driven from their homes that the Trahazi created the Zodiac faces that are worshipped now as gods in their own right. Much of the Zodiac tradition exists as stories among the caravans, and these are the family members that are widely accepted as the Zodiac family. To attempt to remove any of them would be an effort that would need the whole family to choose to remove them - not only the Returned, but the greater Trahazi of the world.

Topic: what are the anti-dragon runes in whitehall and the unknown Nadine city with the cathedral and how do they work?
column5: Please note, you submitted three very different questions within your research that could not be answered as one action.  Neither Whitehall nor Siegeholt have or had anti-dragon runes. Whitehall had illusion and anti-divination runes on it that were rewritten by a dragon, while Siegeholt has large ballista around it for defense against dragons.

Topic: why is the princess of night called the widowed bride? is she related to the Lady of Darkness? (block div)
column5: You've submitted three very different research topics in your research, and unfortunately they cannot be answered by one action.  The Princess of Night/Widowed Bride is not the Lady of Darkness, these are two distinct beings. Finding a concrete story of the Nameless is as difficult as finding their names or epitaphs prove to be - for Nameless thrives on going unnamed and unrecorded throughout history. There are many events that are attributed to the Nameless, whether they were actually related to Nameless or not, and so sorting out truth from fiction and rumor is nearly impossible. In the case of the Widowed Bride, there are many stories throughout history that reference such a tragic archetype of a character - a young, new bride who is widowed on her wedding night due to unforeseen tragedy or accident - but there are just as many tales that point to a scheming young woman seeking power and her own freedom by killing her new husband and running away with his wealth (or claiming it for herself). Such stories are common among young women in Rules of Society nations, particularly those of noble blood destined for marriage whether they want them or not - and more than one woman has met her own execution under the accusation of being a Nameless Faith follower dedicated to the Widowed Bride, regardless of the truth of tragic accident or premeditated murder.

Topic: are there any signs or tells when a god has been replaced by Nameless Faith? (block div)
column5: There is no evidence to support Nameless gods have ever replaced any other god or taken over a pantheon.

Topic: Nameless Gods are known to replace or be hidden in other pantheons. Is it possible to find clues as to when?
column5: Even in the deepest annals of Alexander, there are no records which support the notion that Nameless Gods *replace* other gods to hide in other pantheons. It is highly theorized that they intercept stray prayers that were intended for dead or non-existant beings, and take advantage of the unclaimed gaps when mortals are seeking a being to provide them power (commonly cults that are not dedicated to a known divine being), but there is no proof to any of this as it is difficult to ascertain the truth of gods.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Bank of the Dead
column5: answered on site

Topic: Political Insight: House Delfestrae
column5: House Delfestrae is arguably one of the most powerful factions on the isle of Tear. Known for demon-binding, they are the counterpoint to House Kaelin's Celestial-hunting, which often puts the two Effendal Houses at odds as they generally have opposing approaches on how to handle diplomatic or other problems. House Delfestrae has a history of stronger anti-human sentiments, which came to a head in 7ar when the House refused to join Unity as they were dealing with an internal power struggle between anti-human/traditionalist Perillinen and the more progressive ones. The conflict resulted in a trial which killed nearly a dozen Delfestrae vapaaherra, including two Perillinen. Techncially considered the largest of the Effendal Houses, as they oversee four tribes - their own original three, Terra "Blade of the Forest", Oni'Ven "The Owl Who Studies Darkness", and Jokiota "The River that Takes", as well as Tika'ia "The Sting of the Wasp" whom they adopted when fleeing the expanse during the Purges. They also made an attempt to take leadership over the Svivore and Rakuten only a few years ago, but were strongly opposed by both House Kaelin and the recently-Returned Palauttaa of House Ra'e'len due to the imbalance of power and influence it would provide House Delfestrae. The death of the First (Phyraeon) in 10ar put the House once more into a time of instability and infighting as three different camps broke out each supporting a candidate to take over the House. While Ilbrim, the Sky-Piercing Lily, came out on top, this struggle resulted in the death of the Second, and mostly quieted the remainder of Phyraeon's faction though they still have multiple members among the Perillinen. The top three of Delfestrae now are all curiously bonded to other entities in some form, which is suspected to be leading the House down a different path - Ilbrim being bonded to Phyraeon's Shadow, the Second Vartija being bonded to the element of lightning, and the Third Sulatt bonded with one of his summons to share the same body. While the anti-human sentiment remains strong among many members of House Delfestrae, they have a strong historical alliance with the Gael, and so the three Banners on Tear are permitted to live in the lands allocated to House Delfestrae and its tribes.

Topic: Military Insight: Inquisition on Tear
column5: There are no notable forces associated with the Inquisition on Tear.

Topic: Military Insight: Ko'aat on Tear
column5: When the Ko'aat first Returned in 2ar, two major bands came through - the Wolf Band and the Snake Band, the bands that were on Tear in the Age of Tane and were wiped out by the Mists spreading over Tear in 16ch. Between ongoing conflicts with the Returned, their demon-blooded joining with Bel'e'athru during the War of Radiance, and Leucraven's bloodlust to murder Ko'aat, their numbers have dropped significantly. What remains of the Ko'aat on Tear is only the Snake Band, roughly divided into two separate camps along religious lines. While their numbers have been wittled down over the decade they've been Returned, they seem to currently number approximately 200-300 veteran warriors.

Topic: Contact Underworld: Bank of the Dead to buy coins of the damned
column5: Refund - The Bank of the Dead is not currently selling any Coins of the Damned

Topic: Political Insight: House Kaelin
column5: House Kaelin is arguably one of the most powerful factions on the isle of Tear. Known for celestial-hunting, they are the counterpoint to House Delfestrae's celestial hunting, which often puts the two Effendal Houses at odds as they generally have opposing approaches on how to handle diplomatic or other problems. Allied with the Gold Chalice and present at the coronation of King Syltane in 5ar, House Kaelin has always been far more progressive in human-effendal relations on Tear, and were quick to join with Unity when offered a seat on the Parliament. In 7ar, Mylleriel (the Third), was exiled for conspiring with Tyaeous and Arlenai of House Delfestrae. When Arlenai of House Delfestrae was put on trial for orchestrating the deaths of other Effendal at the hands of humans, the First (Dasyra) was killed in an explosion Arlenai caused in the middle of her trial. As a result, Astielle, the Sword of Kaelin, was promoted to First. Mestsastaja became the new Second, and Solomina jumped considerably to Third. House Kaelin has never shied away from being the first to make moves and take a strong stance, such as in declaring war against T'tocs, or supporting and celebrating the decisive actions taken by Curon Arandir to sack Edge and the Inquisition when their was talk of a second purge early in the Age of Adelrune, or Ritari Cecil in killing the fallen-celestial Teppidus.

Topic: Contact Scholars: Lore Waterways/Geography of Tear
column5: Crystal

Topic: State of the Court: Dace
column5: Current topics of note circulating at the Dacian Court: - The current color in highest fashion is red, and everyone is wearing it! - Admiral Viluca Ariadne is tasked to wipe out the Hand before she can assume her father's position as Head of the Miliary - The state of the world with this apparent demon crisis (that doesn't currently negatively impact them in any way) - Beautiful new mirrors are being displayed in all the noble houses, and House Daecen is discussing adding an entire ROOM of large mirrors to the palace - What to do about the Celestine Empire's food crisis, they were expecting to be able to import larger quantities of wheat and flour again when the Mists lifted

Investigate Rumors: House Montaige

For a fee. - Rumor has it, if Princess Lia Montaige-Daecen does not produce a male child, Lord Sebastian is next in line to take over the House. - Rumor has it, House Montaige is on top of the world. - Rumor has it, Sir Caliban “The Beast” Gaian of Castle Thorn hates House Montaige. - Rumor has it, House Montaige has blackmail material on anyone of noteworthy power and influence on Adelrune.

Topic: Slaughter Livestock x20 for Celestine Empire
Investigate History: Chaos Phoenix
column5: In the Celestine tradition, in the beginning, there was only Destiny and Chaos. And so Destiny spun the threads of creation out of Chaos, and with the birth of the Titans the world of Adelrune was given form. The Titans loved generously and bore many children and grandchildren to populate the world, but Chaos saw that Destiny's world was too perfect, and so Chaos alone birthed the seasons, and the Phoenix to protect them. The Chaos Phoenix is called the Guardian of Seasons and oversees the natural cycles of the world, from life and death, to the rising and lowering of the tides. She appears as a fire bird in religious imagery.

Topic: Investigate History: Effendal gods
column5: The Effendal have no need or use for gods, as they are considered nothing more than powerful entities like the fae who created them. Their purpose is to protect the balance of Adelrune, and the gods can do little to impact that directly.

Topic: Investigate History: The Titans
column5: In the Celestine tradition, the Titans are the children of Destiny, which she wove out of Chaos. They number four - Eur came first and is called The Mother or The World, and she is the stones and earth beneath our feet. The next is Tempest, often called by his name, but also called The Sky. He became the air we breathe and lies between the land and the heavens. Then came Nero, whose heart burns so hot that he lies beneath/is enveloped by Eur to protect the living creatures of the world. Finally, is Cri, who is called The Eyes of the Water, or more simply: Torrent. She is the sea and oceans, and the rain that falls. They loved each other freely and beget many children into the world. Though not technically considered a Titan, the Chaos Phoenix is often attributed to this category/generation of the Celestine Gods.

Topic: Investigate History: Phyraeon's Shadow
column5: The Shadow, in recent years referred to as "Phyraeon's shadow," is an entity bound to the bloodline of Rhiannon that has cropped up all throughout the history of House Delfestrae. For additional information, one would need to seek out the most heretical of texts, such as the Song of Rhiannon/the Serpent's Song, and those that are contained within the deepest vaults of the Church of Chorus.

Topic: Investigate History: Knights of Mysteries
column5: In the early days of Ad Decimum (founded 125ch on the ruins of Myre) there was no Crown Order of knights, for what need did the city of scholars have for such a role? However, only a short decade later, in 135ch, after Belisarius had long-since disappeared and the Arbiter's Tower emerged as the preeminent leadership of the city, that tune changed quite quickly. The Arcane Consortium, made up of some of the most brilliant and adventurous scholars, overstepped by bringing back to the city an artifact of immense power. They did not inform the Arbiter's Tower of this discovery, and in their subsequent studies and experimentation with the artifact, a catastrophic explosion shook the city, taking out the top twelve stories of the Red Tower and causing major damages to the streets below when the stone and rubble blasted outwards with immense force. Nearly twenty percent of the new citystate's population was killed in the blast, which eventually led to stricter rules on city planning and what could be built around the base of the various towers. The Arbiter's Tower, demanding that the Arcane Consortium take responsibility for such a disaster, set out to create a committee of oversight for the various mages of the city - not only the Consortium, but all mages in the various towers to make sure that none put the people at such risk again. This necessitated the birth of the The Arcane Order of Eldritch Understandings and Hidden Mysteries to be this oversight committee. The remaining members of the Consortium were split ideologically between those who agreed with the ruling of the Arbiter's Tower and promoted caution and study, many of whom joined the Order of the Knights of Mysteries; and those who insisted they continue to push the boundaries of magic and discovery, who remained within the Arcane Consortium. Those who went on to be Knights of Mysteries swore that they would never let a foolish use of magic bring their city to its knees again.

Topic: Background Research: Knights of Mysteries
column5: Before the dismantling of the Arbiter's Tower, Nobility, and Knights of Mysteries by Belisarius in June of 9ar, the Knights of Mysteries were highly respected as a preeminent academic institution, well-studied in all forms of magic and knowledge. They had full access to all notes, documents, books, and scrolls contained within Ad Decimum if they were requested, and were known for inquiring after vary topics of study with the scholars of the city. Exchanges of information were not uncommon between the Knights of Mysteries and the Necromancer's Tower in the Nadine Empire, particularly when it came to keeping some of their necromancers in check, though they only requested the assistance of the Black Chalice on one memorable occassion. Unfortunately, however, there was a falling out between the Order and Alexander in Cestral, when the librarians accused the knights of unreasonably protecting knowledge and information on various topics and artifacts rather than supporting the sharing of information and academic inquiry. Following the dissolution of the Order and nobility, all access, assets, and records the Knights of Mysteries once had were revoked and turned over solely to Belisarius.

Topic: Follow the Money: Knights of Mysteries
column5: Before the dismantling of the Arbiter's Tower, Nobility, and Knights of Mysteries by Belisarius in June of 9ar, the funding for the Knights of Mysteries was administered and overseen by the Arbiter's Tower. Expenditures beyond stipends for those in active service were more sporadic and unique - a unique artifact acquired from a merchant, a paid guard to travel with a caravan, healers kept on hand during study, etc. Following the dissolution of the Order and the siezing of the assets and funds of both the Order and the noble families, the Knights of Mysteries were left with no particular income and either retired or sought out work elsewhere. For those who were once associated with the Order, the largest benefactor and income source in recent years seems to be the Dancing Spiders.

Topic: Military Insight: Knights of Mysteries
column5: The Knights of Mysteries were disbanded and thus do not have active military forces.

Topic: Political Insight: Knights of Mysteries
column5: Following the lead from Unity, after the dissolution of the Order, many Rules of Society nations recognize the Knights of Mysteries as a former knightly order and thus still afford them their titles and pin recognition in the same manner the world honors the titles of nobility once held by Returned in their first lives. Following the dissolution, those Knights who remained were largely outspoken against Belisarius and his support for experimentation without oversight or guidance, and so they were either imprisoned for speaking out, fled the city for better opportunities, or were driven out when threatened by the Founder's Eye. In the wake of the emergence of Myre from the Black Mists, the Council of Honor has been in debates over the fate, recognition, and classification of the former knightly orders from Ad Decimum and Mandala, but thus far no agreement has been reached.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Knights of Mysteries
column5: - Rumor has it, the House of Clubs hired a former Knight of Mysteries to guard something important. - Rumor has it, the Knights of Mysteries are going to be reclassified and no longer be considered a Crown Order. - Rumor has it, if you're willing to pay, a former Knight of Mysteries is willing to sell you what you need. - Rumor has it, the Mad Kings of Myre kept the former Knights of Mysteries to draw their modern knowledge out, only disposing of them when they were no longer useful. - Rumor has it, if you can find the Owls, you'll find what remains of the Knights of Mysteries.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Knights of Mysteries
column5: For the Knights of Mysteries who left Ad Decimum in the wake of the dissolution of the Order, some of the more desperate members took all they could carry with them and have been slowly selling these artifacts and items off to the Dancing Spiders, who are well-known collectors of occult and magical artifacts. Some of the more knowledge-inclined members took refuge with and became members of the Parliament of Owls, a secretive academic society originally from the Nadine Empire, though with the Black Mists the status of their former-Ad Decimum chapter is unknown.

Topic: Military Insight: Inexo's Fleet
column5: Inexo's Fleet is moving as a generally organized fleet, moving in a searching grid type pattern in the northern sea. The numbers (as observed from a distance) seem to be approximately: 100 Demon Mages, 23 Sea Monsters, 270 damaged ships, 50 Corruptor Demons, 1000 Lesser Demons, 50 Demon Knights. The sea monsters specifically seem to be acting as scouts that move out further than the fleet before returning to the lead ship with reports.

Topic: Bestiary Analysis: Corrupted Abyss Spider Corpse
column5: Visibly affected by the Blood Root Corruption, corrupted Abyss Spiders are significantly more powerful than their normal counterparts due to their abyssal nature being boosted by the additional abyssal corruption - their bites are twice as strong, and fire spiders are even more damaging. Their health is greatly increased, particularly when they reach adulthood, as well as having resistances to fire damage (even if they are not fire-oriented naturally) and other physical resistances.

Topic: Second Opinion: Mage opinion about the Woe Stalkers
column5: The Woestalkers have nearly all died out, and good riddance to that! They mostly died off when the Mists covered Tear early in the Age of Chorus, but like an insidious bit of mold you can never get rid of, one of them has always seemed to hang around Castle Thorn as a direct threat to any mages who live and work here. Sure, they won't make a major public play at having killed anyone, but you can bet your ass that whenever a powerful sorcerer or other mage dies unexpectedly, they're the first ones to be looked at. And as a mage, you know they're always watching, waiting for you to slip up. It's no way to live, having to watch over your shoulder all the time.

Topic: Military Insight: the demon army in Blightwood + who leads them
column5: Without boots on the ground, it's difficult to discern proper numbers or even the leader of the demonic military force in the Blightwood as it is deeply mixed with a number of Myren disease specialists and explorers. The forces seem to number upwards of 2000 demons, dominated by butchers and fire-oriented demons, while many of the stronger leaders seem to be blood-oriented. They have carved a path through the Blightwood, leaving no tree or rock unturned in their search for a root of the world tree.

Topic: Military Insight: Qu'rux and his forces
column5: Qu'rux has mostly been operating independently, but in the greater context of Mehs' demon forces, he does fall into the hierarchy as a Lieutenant under Nadir. Qu'rux primarily seems to be split between reconnaissance and artillery. The lieutenant commands a small squad of about fifteen to twenty shapeshifter demons, along with a sizable amount of imps that are treated less as soldiers and more as living bombardment ammunition. The demons under his command seem to attack with more ferocity than that of a typical shapeshifter demon, and in major engagements their primary objective is often to disrupt the back lines of the enemy, either by sowing chaos with enraged combatants or by raining fire from on high.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Coins of the Damned and it's relation to Theddespari/Ferrymen
column5: - Rumor has it, the Theddespari Mages made the Coins of the Damned as a fuck you to Eden. - Rumor has it, Eden threw a pile of Coins of the Damned at Theddespari to curse them to oblivion and never to find rest. - Rumor has it, Leucraven was a merchant in Theddespari who made the Coins of the Damned and sold them from his shop front. - Rumor has it, Coins of the Damned used to be painted in bright colors and were called Coins of the Blessed by the residents of Theddespari. - Rumor has it, Coins of the Damned are the currency most sought after by the ferryman to pay their own debts in death.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Father Sebastian & Brachen
column5: - Rumor has it, Father Sebastian and Brachen have been feeding those who need it in the slums. - Rumor has it, Father Sebastian and Brachen appear to be true believers of the Old Faith. - Rumor has it, Sebastian chooses to use the title “Father” as an affectation caused by time exposed to the terminology of other faiths. - Rumor has it, Father Sebastian is called "Father" because he is Brachen's father. - Rumor has it, Father Sebastian and Brachen have been sacrificing people at the docks in the name of Balaiesh.

Routine and Vices: Arkon Fenwhistle
column5: In your investigation, you discover that while a postbox is held for Arkon Fenwhistle at the Pen & Rose, there is no such person. Postmaster Penrose Selwyn is responsible for taking names to open postboxes and delivering mail to them, so it is likely he might have more information in your search.

Topic: Military Insight: Mehs' generals and their relationships
column5: As a number of Mehs' generals have not made themselves known yet, it is not possible to confirm all of them. Sablegore was operating on Tear before his presumed death, T'tocs who was defeated in Cestral (and subsequently moved to support the Nadine front), Moonshroud who is operating in the Breach, Nadir who is operating near Castle Thorn, and Melisma who is operating in the north of the Nadine Empire. Mwg'rios and My'nok are beginning to make moves, but it is unclear where their attention is focused due to the Mists. It is theorized that there is a General leading a fleet of ships in the northern waters, but nothing is known of them.What IS clear is that the generals, while all working towards separate goals their unique skill sets are best suited for, are working well in tandem with one another and have been seen to appear quickly in other regions to back up Generals who need more assistance or someone's particular specialty at the time.

Topic: Political Insight: Mehs' generals and their relationships
column5: As Abyssal Monarch, Mehs' generals are each loyal to him for one reason or another. For some, it is because he pulled them out of the Abyss to lead his armies, for others their loyalty has been long-standing, still others positioned themselves to their most advantageous following the death of Bellus and getting in with the new Abyssal Monarch who was much more predictable and clear in his desires. Mehs may have a temper, but it is a known element, and thus his generals, lieutenants, and soldiers have a clear understanding of their orders to fulfill his plans for Adelrune. Ultimately, each of the Generals is held in check amongst one another because a different General has a weapon or tool that is particularly deadly to them being held as collateral for mutually-assured destruction.

Topic: Investigate Enemies: Mehs
column5: Among Mehs' enemies are anyone ice related (Arasimiel, etc), celestials (particularly the Harbingers of the Crimson Sun who are rumored to have bound him to the island his city is on), and the Glassbreakers of the Saek. In one particularly historic moment, in a conflict with the Harbingers, Mehs declared "You'll never be able to defeat me!" before the Harbinger disagreed: "You don't have to be defeated to be bound here forever," before stabbing Mehs through his firey cloak, pinning him to the island. The sword is rumored to still be among the Harbingers.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Mehs
column5: Mehs has a particular fondness for literature, in which he burns the books to absorbs the knowledge. On an average day, much of his time is spent in his throne room with his looking glasses of souls, keeping himself available to those who request his aid/support. Though, most notably, anyone petitioning him needs to be deferential, or his quick temper will snap and engulf them in flame - there is a *very* small window between these two states. While Mehs is always down for a bbq and well-cooked meats, he doesn't walk the city very often or interact with the greater public, as he prefers his more isolated time.

Topic: Investigate Loved Ones: Mehs
column5: Mehs has no known family or close relationships

Topic: Investigate Rumors + Healthy Paranoia: Delfestrae Nameless Faith
column5: - Rumor has it, Vhynn is a Nameless Faith follower. - Rumor has it, Ritari Aelynn is a Nameless Faith follower. - Rumor has it, Audax is a Nameless Faith follower. - Rumor has it, Ke'wyn is a Nameless Faith follower. - Rumor has it, Ritari Thel'orom is a Nameless Faith follower.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Tableau Syndicate
column5: The Imperial Tableau Syndicate is an officially government-sanctioned organization which technically does not have underworld ties, but when poking around there are some members of the organization who individually have connections to various underworld organizations such as the Hand and the Dancing Spiders, as well as the Guild of Doves. It's even said, if you slip some gold into the right hands, you can make nearly anyone in the Empire disappear into the mines, never to be seen or heard from again. Particularly as the month comes to a close, they are known to be even more strict in making sure there are enough fighters in the pits or workers in the mines when numbers start to dwindle. The bulk of their income comes in from gambling for the gladiator pits, and members of the Syndicate are sometimes known to fix a fight beforehand to benefit from their own betting.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Tableau Syndicate
column5: - Rumor has it, diviners are always watching and will the tell the Syndicate where to be to catch you committing crimes. - Rumor has it, the Syndicate is preparing to round up a number of the Hazori in Concerti to keep the rest in line. - Rumor has it, not even nobles are safe from the Syndicate. - Rumor has it, the Empress disguises herself as a member of the Syndicate to blow off some steam. - Rumor has it, after Ackeroniell's Forest burned, the Syndicate went through the wreckage and rounded up bodies and anyone left living.

Topic: Investigate History: Inexo's Fleet
column5: Formerly one of the greatest port cities in the world, Inexo's fleet was well-maintained and traveled extensively to various ports of trade, particularly in the Nadine Empire, Dace, and Cole. Following the disappearance of Inexo, a great many of the ships were detroyed during the War of Radiance while transporting demons to Cestral, the Nadine Empire, and Tear. However, during the Unity-led occupation of the city during the Second Crusade, it was believed that the fleet was destroyed along with the city under Nadine direction. Recent actions taken by someone sailing nearly two hundred ships through the Eastern Waters has shown this to have been a falsehood to the rest of Adelrune.

Topic: Political Insight: Zatan relationship with Mehs
column5: Zatan formerly served under Bellus, and thus is not trusted by Mehs or serving among his hierarchy.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Zatan
column5: Following the death of Bellus, Zatan fled the city of Mehs and has taken refuge among his underworld allies in Redemption.

Topic: Political Insight: Mwg'Rios relationship with Mehs
column5: Mwg'Rios is serving as a General under the Abyssal Monarch Mehs. As with the other generals, his loyalty appears absolute.

Topic: Military Insight: Mwg'Rios
column5: The Stifling Smoke is known to be subtle until he isn't. Mwg'rios prefers demonic forces largely made up of irregulars, soldiers who don't fall into standard structures and flow across the battlefield or fill multiple roles. The force Mehs has provided him he utilizes differently then their normal roles. Raiders, sappers, skirmishers, and assassins make up his ranks- flowing between different roles and operations. Since his emergence from the Abyss he has delighted in choking the fighting strength of his foes, cutting off their supply lines and burning their fields so the smoke & ash darken the skies. Taking the strengths of his foes and showing them that smoke will always find a way inside.  Mwg'rios' favorites are his assassins that act independently from him to gather information, kidnap key individuals, and brutally assault his enemies before fading away in the wind. His current force is made up of approximately 2500 demons, though there are significantly less demon knights among his forces than serve under some of the other generals.

Topic: Military Insight: demons north of Celestine blocking trade routes
column5: There are no demons north of the Celestine Empire, that region is currently the Mist-covered Dragon Archipelago or open ocean. Inexo's Fleet is blocking the seas to the northeast of the Celestine Empire on the Veinish coastline, which is impacting trade by sea but those trade routes have been used minimally in the last 5 years since that whole region is still blanketed by Mists.

Topic: Poltitical Insight: Nadir's relationship with Mehs
column5: As Nadir was in the Abyss until just recently, his loyalty to Mehs is absolute as it was willingly given in exchange for being raised out of the Abyss and returned to Adelrune.

Topic: Military Insight: Nadir
column5: 100 Demon Mages, 100 Corruptor Demons, 1500 Lesser Demons, 500 Lesser Corruptors, 150 Demon Knights.  The army is encamped a half day’s march from Castle Thorn.  There countryside is swarming with scouting parties and a number of smaller entrenchments have been constructed, some in locations of dubious strategic value.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Nadir
column5: Lightbreaker and his army do not seem to have a significant underworld presence.

Topic: Investigate Enemies: Nadir
column5: Nadir seems to have a particular grievance with Kenrin Arakai.  The Kaelin have whispered about attacking him and he seems eager and amused by the idea.  His forces have been harrying Castle Thorn supply lines, but he has not made an assault on the walls.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Nadir
column5: Nadir seems focused on searching the countryside near Castle Thorn until recently, but that search seems to have stopped.  He regularly sends out dispatches seeking Hope.  He is very focused on his mission, but has seemingly gone out of his way when a rare or unique volume is reported to him.

Topic: Military Insight: Mehs' known generals
column5: As a number of Mehs' generals have not made themselves known yet, it is not possible to confirm all of them. Sablegore who was operating on Tear before his presumed death, T'tocs who was defeated in Cestral, Moonshroud who is operating in the Breach, Nadir who is operating near Castle Thorn, and Melisma who is operating in the north of the Nadine Empire. Mwg'rios and My'nok are beginning to make moves, but it is unclear where their attention is focused due to the Mists.

Topic: Political Insight: Myre
column5: As Myre spent an entire age under the Dark Mists of Myre, having long-ago defeated their nearest neighbors, it has existed almost entirely in isolation for over a century. Ruled by the Smiling Prince and the Seven Mad-Kings of Myre. As the city seems impenetrable, getting more specific information at this time is not possible. What is known, however, is that they are the active enemy of the Blightwood residents and those who live in the Dragon Archipelago, as all shared in the same Age of Myre. Particularly, residents of the Blightwood are considered their greater enemy as they have mounted a number of attempts and attacks on Myre over the years. Due to this, it is rumored that some Myrens have been working with Mehs' demon forces to mutual benefit in the Blightwood.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Myre working with Mehs
column5: There seems to be some truth to this rumor, as mages and alchemists from Myre were traveling with a large contingent of demons to burn down huge swaths of the Blightwood.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Cole working with Mehs
column5: There seems to be some truth to this rumor, as an excess of ships from Cole was sold to Bellus, and then went into the possession of Mehs.

Topic: Military Insight: Myren encampments on Adelrune
column5: While there are small groups of Myren who have traveled to Tear, there are no real military encampments of Myren to be found on Tear.

Topic: Military Insight: Colish encampments on Adelrune
column5: Cole has a markedly large presence on Tear, as the nation of Cole rules the city of Edge which lies on the northernmost tip of Tear. This settlement was formerly an unmanaged and wild outpost of explorers, gangs, and fortune-seekers, but eventually fell under the leadership of the exiled-Amalgamation Princess Katria De'Fey. She married King Connor of Cole during the War of Radiance, rolling the city of Edge under Colish control at that time. Though it changed hands during the War of Radiance, first to demon occupation, then reclaimed by Unity before being returned to the Colish, it currently is a city dominated by gangs, mercenaries, and merchants.

Topic: Political Insight: Non-Returned Council
column5: Political Insight is not a spend that can provide you all the information you requested, particularly as a single spend, but this information should be helpful to you:  Non-Returned Regent: Merrick Bartholomew Academic: Archmage Theodore Glass Economic: Martha Aranell Underworld: Archibald Scond Military: Cartha Krisantha

Topic: Underworld Insight: How & why was Ayna smuggled out of Mehs
column5: The "underworld" of most Breach cities is much less insidious than in foreign nations, for few things are truly outlawed or illegal. In the case of the glass ring containing the demon Ayna, it was merely traded to a Dacian merchant in exchange for a stack of paper as it left Mehs, but later it was the arrival of the merchant in Redemption where it was smuggled into the anti-demon city about 20 years ago. Given as a "gift" to one of the Ire'enil leaders of the time, the ring acted as a tether, allowing Ayna control over the unsuspecting Effendal. What was done while the ring was in the possession of the Effendal is unknown to your spies, but a scattering occurred only a few weeks afterwards.

Topic: Underworld Insight: District 2 major gang & leader
column5: answered on site

Topic: Underworld Insight: District 3 major gang & leader
