Topic: Political Insight: Smiling Prince
column5: Having spent 200 years well-protected and comfortable within the walls of Myre under the Black Mists, the clearing of those protections and rejoining with the rest of Adelrune has changed the landscape. The walls are starting to close around him, and the playing field has opened up with the receeding of the Mists, putting his throne at greater risk. As his power wavers, he has allied with My'nok, but it is rumored he is looking at which nation could potentially bolster his power. It is said he is growing more paranoid with the introduction of more outside threats, and he does not know who he can trust now. With the failure of the Eroded King to discover the Nadine's infiltration of the city recently, his ire is directed at the Eroded King and the Merchant District as a whole. Unable to hurt the Eroded King (or any of the Mad Kings) directly due to their fae pacts, the Smiling Prince is instead focusing on those of the Merchant District, with the Triumphant Collosus spending more time than normal patrolling the streets and punishing subjects and citizens alike for even the most minor of infractions. Once a year, tradition dictates that the Smiling Prince can make a new deal with the fae, and there is debate as to whether he will use this year's wish to solidify his wavering power or bolster the defenses of Myre.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Smiling Prince
column5: As the ruler of Myre who sets the laws and sits above them in the Spire of Myre, the Smiling Prince has no direct ties to underworld organizations as there are few things that classify as "underworld" activities within Myre. He would never associate with necromancers, after all. His Princely Host, however, exist to serve his every whim and do his bidding, and are known to have ties to every organization in the kingdom to see to it that he has anything he might want.

Topic: Military Insight: Ko'aat on Tear
column5: When the Ko'aat first Returned in 2ar, two major bands came through - the Wolf Band and the Snake Band, the bands that were on Tear in the Age of Tane and were wiped out by the Mists spreading over Tear in 16ch. Between ongoing conflicts with the Returned, their demon-blooded joining with Bel'e'athru during the War of Radiance, and Leucraven's bloodlust to murder Ko'aat, their numbers have dropped significantly. What remains of the Ko'aat on Tear is only the Snake Band, roughly divided into two separate camps along religious lines. While their numbers have been wittled down over the decade they've been Returned, they seem to currently number approximately 200-300 veteran warriors.

Topic: Investigate History: Ko'aat on Tear, when they arrived
column5: Found in the oldest archives left behind by House Ra'e'len, there is notation that sometime around 20t that longboats arrived on the eastern shores of Tear, pulled by sea serpents and bringing the first humans to Tear - the Ko'aat bands of the Wolf and Snake. Led by those called "The Chosen", the leader of the Snake Band was said to have the power to "sing to the serpents" and have them as allies.  The Vicaul longships of the Esturmann clan went on their way not long after, leaving the Ko'aat behind and in conflict with the Effendal of House Ra'e'len. No other humans would come to Tear for another 50 years.

Topic: Military Insight: Gael on Tear
column5: Three banners of the Gael are living in Effendal lands of southern Tear, as provided territory by the Citadel. They are no longer allied under one banner, but each inhabit different territories as they have had conflicts between one another and don't want to share similar areas or hunting grounds. The Banner of the Black Wolf, led by Commander Klev Vol'ghos numbers nearly 10000, veteran warriors.  The Banner of the One-Winged Owl, formerly led by Erontu, numbers approximately 9000 warriors. The Banner of the Blood Crow, under the leadership of Scadox, recently took some major losses to their numbers and in particular their blood mages. As such, updated numbers are unavailable, but formerly were approximately 11000 Elite Warriors and 5000 Blood Mages.

Topic: Investigate History: Gael on Tear and their history with the Effendal
column5: The history, and long-standing alliance, between the Gael and Delfestrae goes back centuries. Even before the Purges, the two cultures shared a geographical space with the Delfestrae living beneath the ground and whispering through the trees of the forests, and the Gael gave them their space and respected the boundaries of their territory. During the Purges, it was the Gael who provided support to the Delfestrae against the Celestine Empire, and provided cover for both Kaelin and Delfestrae while the Effendal retreated to the coastline to set sail for Tear. They did not attempt to enter the abandoned Delfestrae homestead in the intervening centuries, and when Perilinen Siili Sininen and her entourage returned to the Homestead in 5AR, they provided safe passage through the forest even as the Northern War raged. Following the War of Radiance, the broken portals stranded large numbers of Gael on Tear and the Citadel Effendal offered them land to inhabit and hunt when food sources ran scarce in Unity lands.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: fallen Celestials on Tear
column5: - Rumor has it, a celestial is building a cult around themself. - Rumor has it, a celestial was seen bathing in the blood of the World Tree root. - Rumor has it, a celestial has been seen joined the Mist Faith in worship. - Rumor has it, a celestial has been hiding deep in the forests of Tear. - Rumor has it, a celestial is murdering bandits outside of Edge.

Topic: Military Insight: largest faction of fallen celestials on Tear
column5: Celestials, fallen or not, are generally solitary beings who travel and act as to their virtue. They will come together in their Choirs to fight demons or in wars, but too many of them coming together on Adelrune has been a great risk since Anziel's Wedding led to the formation of the Gold Chalice, and so many avoid coming together in large groups if it is not necessary. That is not to say that celestials (fallen or not) are always alone, for Celestials are known to inspire those around them, and bring many into the fold to follow their teachings and virtues. It is likely that any current Celestials located on Tear have some form of small cult-like following surrounding them.

Topic: Military Insight: Inexo's Fleet
column5: Inexo's Fleet is moving as a generally organized fleet, moving in a searching grid type pattern in the northern sea. The numbers (as observed from a distance) seem to be approximately: 100 Demon Mages, 23 Sea Monsters, 270 damaged ships, 50 Corruptor Demons, 1000 Lesser Demons, 50 Demon Knights. The sea monsters specifically seem to be acting as scouts that move out further than the fleet before returning to the lead ship with reports.

They are said to be the patron god of children who protects and watches over them beneath the light of the moon. Particularly fond of games and enjoyment, it is rumored that Yhaslev did not go to sleep as many of the great dragons did, but instead took on a childish humanoid form to play with children of all types.

Topic: Follow the Money: Nadir
column5: Much of the funding for Nadir and his forces can be traced back to two sources: the port city of Mehs, or the overtaken trade caravans coming south from the Celestine Empire. His only major expenses seem to be large quantities paid for books bought from abroad through specialty merchants who are willing to deal with him.  Lightbreaker and his forces seized the contents and wealth of several supply trains heading to Castle Thorn.  He seems more than happy to part with any of the seized coin to simplify supply if anyone will trade instead of requiring raids.  He was briefly fascinated by printed money as he is with all printed documents of significance, but he has since accepted their function.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Inexo's Fleet
column5: When the city of Inexo still stood, it was a thriving merchant town that benefited from both above board trade and the seedy blackmarket trade with their nearer neighbors of Dace and the Nadine Empire. In fact, many of the ships built in Inexo were outfitted with smugglers holds for moving more illicit goods overseas. While the majority of the fleet was lost during the War of Radiance, a substantial force was damaged in the Second Crusade but still sailable, though this marks the first time the fleet has left Breach as a large force. No additional stops were made as the fleet traveled north up the coast of the Eastern Expanse on their mission, so any current underworld connections are untraceable as of now.

Underworld Insight: Thaddeus Greave (Dan)
column5: Thaddeus Greaves is a known member of the Bleeding Aces, a gang known to be affiliated with the House of Diamonds in Cole. Previously in Port Frey, he was known for hosting fighting rings, gambling rackets, and giving out loans to collect on at exhorbitant rates. After Harvester made him disappear into a pile of pumpkin leaves two summers past, it seems he escaped whatever corner of the Ever Autumn Forest he was banished to, as he was recently spotted in Cole making moves and positioning himself for the upcoming Great Game.

Topic: Political Insight: Inspector Bartholomew who working for the citizen's council is investigating non-returned murderers (potentially stitchers?)
column5: Special Inspector Bartolomeo does not sit on the Citizens' Council and does not appear to have publicly espoused any political positions. However, what your sources are able to deduce is that he earned a reputation as a capable detective in Port Frey and built his career primarily investigating interpersonal matters among local merchant and the nobility. During the meeting of the Citizens' Council where he was appointed to his position some concerns were raised that he had comparatively little experience investigating murders and other violent crimes. However, these voices proved to be in the minority and the temporary position of "Special Inspector on Behalf of the Citizens' Council" was created to afford him some legitimacy as he investigated a string of mysterious murders and mutilations possibly linked to Stitchers. Further, despite the similarity in names, he appears to have no familial ties to Citizen Regent Merrick Bartholomew.

Topic: Inv. History: Hyperia
column5: Hyperia, considered the ice-aligned primogenetor dragon, is never mentioned in history books without her twin brother, the water-oriented dragon Vylarion. They are spoken of as two of the six dragons who sang Adelrune into creation, however there is no written history on what became of them afterwards. Snippets of letters and foreign newspapers suggest small groups dedicated to the worship of Hyperia alongside the other primodial dragons scattered about Adelrune, but such groups are very small and secretive, and are not numerous enough to be considered a separate religion outside of 'Bractos', a general, sometimes derogatory, term used for anyone who worships dragons of any kind.

Topic: Inv. History: Avirtia
column5: Avirtia, also known as "She Who Cuts the Storms," is known as the patron god of sailors, and considered the goddess of calm seas. Many (religious or not) pay her a prayer or offering before setting off on a ship voyage. In the Celestine Faith, she is the child of Mentor and Cri. The largest harbor in Dace is named for her, and nearly every Dacian ship has some representation of her aboard. It is said that she can reveal the secrets of the seas to those who she shows favor,  and ships blessed by her hand can sail smoothly through even the worst of storms as if it were a calm, sunny day.

Topic: Political Insight: House Calae (specifically Javier)
column5: While not of the main family line, Javier now sits in a very crucial position in providing immediate political and financial security to House Calae. Due to the family's recent string of bad luck, both with losing very important business relationships in the nation of Ad Decimum as well as the tragedy of the Royal marriage, House Calae is on the brink of losing their status as a major house of Dace. The house is currently placing significant pressure on a marriage contract between Javier Calae and Lilliana Vallaro, as the business arrangement that the contract provides would be enough to see House Calae back on the financial rise.   Lord Javier himself has been nothing if not a professional, idyllic gentleman of the house. He has taken his duties seriously, and has devoted himself to the study of botany, and as an expert he runs the botanical enterprises needed for the family's alchemical business.  In social situations, Javier is often a wallflower amongst the elite nobles that frequent the ballrooms of Dace because he is not of the immediate line and carries few hobbies or an eccentric flair that others find charming. He has never been the center of the social circle that he now finds himself in with his upcoming wedding being a focal point of conversation in Dace.  Unlike many in the political world, the lord has a gentle heart and tends to forgive with grace rather than hold a grudge. However he always seems to have an opinion on anything presented to him, even if he does not have all the information needed to give an educated answer.

Topic: Eavesdrop: House Calae/Javier
column5: - “It’ll have to wait, I’m off to send out the second round of invitations, then to the seamstress, the tailor, the florist–the good one mind you. On the way back, I’ll have to pick up samples from the confectioner, and over to the docks to sign for the delivery of Willowwine. And don’t even get me started on tomorrow’s arrangements! And they say everyone loves a wedding…” - “Don’t bother bringing the master his food just yet  Fiammetta, he won’t be bothered to eat it, and the chef will just have to make it again. The lord has been up there all week working on those expanded greenhouse plans. Poor dear hardly sleeps, but you think he would at least make sure to eat, it’s starting to show on his complexion.” - “Lavinia– you might want to stay out of the master's way as much as you can today. It seems like mister Nicolo and Lady Lilliana didn’t show up as expected on the ship docking in from Tear, and I swear I thought the Lord was going to rip the paintings off the walls.” - “Oh, Fiammetta, you’ll never believe who was having tea with Jaiver today– Ambersherry! Oh, and he was just as dreamy as he looks in the drawings. But Fiammetta, you couldn’t believe the shock I had to hide on my face as I was setting the table, and saw the Lord taking detailed notes of all things! Imagine having to pay someone to tell you the latest court gossip.”   - “Yes, cancel the order. I know that Lady Cliantha insisted on traditional peony’s for her daughter's wedding, but Lord Javier will not budge on the datura. He’s been growing them for months and if he says they're his fiancee's favorite flower, who am I to object? Plus Cliantha is such prude, I cannot wait to see the smug smile fade when she sees they swapped the floral arrangements.”

Topic: Follow the Money: looking into the trade movements of House Calae with a focus on Mangostene dye
column5: House Calae has a wide variety of economic interests and trade arrangements, with specialties in: Alchemy, Silks, Spell Scrolls, Statues, and Mercenaries. Recently they took a major financial hit with the loss of their trade partners in Ad Decimum, as well as losing their marriage arrangement with House Daecen. The gardens of House Calae, which were mapped out back at the founding of Dace, have the only known mangosteen trees in the world, making their fruit rare and difficult to come by. Currently they are sold to a number of different clients in Dace for various products - the ripe fruit is good fresh, as well as baked into pastries or canned, while the rinds from ripe fruits can be used in tanning leather and dye creation (reds and purples). The fruits are also used as alchemical components for treating a number of sicknesses, particularly those commonly contracted aboard ships. In the current market, the mangosteen has a known price fluctuation throughout the year - the cost is much lower during the short summer harvest season, while it can spike to ten times the cost in the deep winter. House Vallaro, however, has discovered that by using the *unripe* mangosteen pods, a rich and vibrant green dye can be extracted - but the fruit is unuseable in doing so. The contract between House Calae and House Vallaro, would stop all production of ripe mangosteen fruit for a period of five years, driving the price exponentially higher due to its scarcity. In preparation to reep the rewards of this cost increase, it is believed that House Calae has canned and stored a portion of their harvests from the last three years, which they can carefully distributed during the scarcity to keep the prices up.

Topic: Inv. History: "Godfall"
column5: December 9r Godfall. That is the only thing we can call this.   What else do you call it when rather than one singular god dying and having his power scattered across the island in the form of red candles only we can touch, instead it is dozens upon dozens of gods shedding their power for an unknown reason?  For every candle divined on, a different gods name or visage came to us. Across every faith, it seemed nearly every god we know the names of and many we do not shed this power. They are not dead, nor does it seem they were attacked. This is not like Chorus last year. This is something else, something different entirely.  In a vision I received, it seemed as if the gods were fish in a pond - each attempting to maneuver into the optimal position for whatever is coming. Making themselves smaller, of less consequence. Grabbing for power wherever they could. To be the biggest fish in the pond. To be small enough to hide where they cannot be found or reached among the rocks. To not be the smaller of the medium fish, but not to be too big among the medium fish and threaten the big fish. To find a school to swim with to appear bigger.   Is this a continuation of the game of gods that resulted in Chorus' death? Was that only the first round and there is more to come? What does this mean? All we know, is that as the Celestine peasantry sang their Yule songs, and upon the lyrics “our gods aren't dead, nor do they sleep,” three hundred red candles appeared across the island, all at once. No one of faith feels comfortable tonight. We are all waiting for the other shoe to drop.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Tuck-Ins
column5: A particularly small gang in town, the Tuck-Ins are at their core simply assassins for hire. Work has been limited lately due to the Returned interfering in the other districts of Port Frey, so they have been supplementing their normal work by retaliating against the Returned. Unfortunately, all of this has led to them getting into it with the Vulchurs who seem to be moving up in the Underworld ranks and extending their reach. After a particularly rough run in between the two organizations during the winter, it seems that the Tuck-Ins have entirely abandoned Port Frey and were seen fleeing towards Edge over a month ago. No reports from Edge suggest they ever reached the city, however, and rumor has it, they found a much more interesting opportunity along the road in the corrupted lands.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Regent Merrick Bartholomew
column5: While acting as the Non-Returned Regent, Merrick Bartholomew's main dayjob is as a merchant. Merrick Bartholomew keeps a fairly mundane daily routine in doing business and interfacing with different facets of the local non-Returned populace, particularly in handling and paying off a number of the bigger gangs (including the Salamanders, the Knives, the Boiling Bones, the Bleeding Aces, and the Tuck-Ins) for their assistance in keeping the peace in Port Frey's outer districts. He has a particularly strong reputation among the gangs for being a man of his word and looking after all of the non-Returned, not just the "palatable" sort the Returned show preference and favor for.

Topic: Inv. Rumors: wealthy nobles who favor music/dance/bardic things
column5: - Rumor has it, Bobby D. Banker LOVES a good performance! - Rumor has it, Ursala E. Brightwood is an extremely generous tipper to a good musician that catches her fancy! - Rumor has it, Lady Cressida Demere was heartbroken to return to Dace and leave behind her box at The Vision in Solace. - Rumor has it, House Cestral patrons a Court Poet who performs at many royal functions and diplomatic events.  - Rumor has it, Lord Remnus Pandrego patrons the Bardic College in Solace and even built them a grand theater!

Topic: Inv. Rumors: guilds/schools/noble houses that specialize in bardic things
column5: - Rumor has it, House Vallaro has provided more music and performance artists to Adelrune in the last fifty years than any of the other Dacian houses. - Rumor has it, the Flower of the Thorn hosts new traveling bards and musicians nearly every week! - Rumor has it, House Demere is particularly known for hosting and performing elaborate musical performances.  - Rumor has it, House Calis is particularly well-known for their members' love of verse, and they are often referred to as "warrior poets".   - Rumor has it, the bards of House Gaheris, while extrodinarily talented, are also cursed.

Topic: Bardic Tidings: research and understand Dacian customs around seeking musical patrons
column5: Benefactors and patrons of the arts are common in most civilized nations, though some specific practices do vary between them. Particularly in Dace, a wealthy patron will often discover or be recommended an upcoming bard or artist who they appreciate the work of, and will then privately commission them to create various pieces as requested. Many artists, actors, and bards who come up through The Giuseppe School have no trouble finding patrons, as the conservatory has an impeccable reputation for connecting artists with those seeking to offer patronage - this often begins with putting on a showcase where artists perform or show off their best works for potential patrons.

Topic: Spread Through Song: the success of the Shanty Six and others performing at Yule
Political Insight: current gang activity around the docks
column5: With the Salamanders going legitimate and keeping others in line, crime and gang activity have dropped dramatically around the docks. The Dancing Spiders are still moving goods through the area between ships and their various warehouses, but they're particularly good at keeping their heads down and not causing problems for anyone, allowing everyone to go about their days.

Topic: Political Insight: current gang activity around the slums
column5: Following the Minister of Defense and the Black Chalice taking down the Boiling Bones in what appeared to be a Nadine-on-Nadine brawl in the streets while dragging necromancers from their homes, the Slums still haven't settled. The Vultures are based out of the slums, bullying and pressuring other gangs into their service, and robbing the peasantry who are coming home with their paychecks or goods from the market. While not directly bothering the peasantry, the Torn Hides have a strong presence in the slums and will occassionally drag back their hunts and spoils giving the locals quite a shock. Whereas the Dancing Spiders are a large employer of locals in need of simple guard work or moving crates around town, as well as renting or paying handsomely to those who have large storage spaces and warehouses in the district.

Topic: Inv. Rumors: sea monster hunting
column5: - Rumor has it, something big is lurking in the marshes south of Port Frey. - Rumor has it, the local fishermen have been bringing their ships in earlier and earlier in the day, as something has grown hungry and is hunting in local waters after nightfall. - Rumor has it, sailors have been telling tall tales about mysterious singing heard off the coast in the dead of night. Some say that if you sing back to them, you'll get a red-gold coin. Others say that you'll be dragged to a watery grave. - Rumor has it, a large, serpantine-like sea beast comes around to Port Frey every few months when someone throws a bunch of cows off a cliff and into the sea. - Rumor has it, sailors up north have heard strange music coming from below the water, and a crew of Colish are interested in hearing the song and bringing a crew of capable guards to find out just what might this song be.

Topic: Political Insight: Puppeteers Guild
column5: Without their King, the Puppeteer's Guild has generally been left to their own devices with a severe lack of funding and support, along with much of their ilk in the District of Disrepair. When they have projects and interests that can gain funding from shared efforts with the Lithomancer's Guild or Stitcher's Guild they have been known to create great works and wonders, but for many in the District of Disrepair there is a struggle to find the materials necessary to create the wonderful puppets concocted in their minds. Many necromancers are known to frequent this district, having collected dead bodies for puppet materials and practice, before moving up to bigger and grander puppet designs. They are frequently in a shifting relationship with their neighboring districts, as typically when they are in an era when they are better neighbors with the Fabric District and Stitcher's Guilds, they will be in stronger conflict with the Industrial District. This positioning can flip quite regularly as when the Stitcher's no longer have need of the for a current experiment, they will rely on the Pyromancers of the Industrial District to allow them to snatch up the dead. Though other guilds will deny their assistance and input in order to claim their own glory, much of what the Lithomancer's Guild was able to create with the Prince’s Triumphant Colossus was built on foundational work of the Puppeteer's Guild. Their use of bonding magic along with fae magic keeps them on good terms with the Arts District, who they often work with on a variety of projects for the Smiling Prince's Birthday Puppet Parties.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Bartholomew Berrington the abyss spider egg merchant
column5: Bartholomew Berrington is what one might call a trader of exotic creatures. He is known to travel the world collecting rare species and breeds of creatures, favoring the extraplanar and magical varieties, and has strong ties with both civilized and wild nations. House Atius, House Tairgrid, House Roccio are among his many trade partners, but he is not unknown to the Ko'aat, Saek, or even the former-Green Tower of Ad Decimum. It's rumored he can travel freely to and from Paradox, and is said to already be working to build connections within Myre for some of their more exotic animal creations.

Topic: Inv. History: Mehs (City)
column5: One of the few demon cities still ruled by its namesake, the “City of Glass” remains under the fiery heel of the demon god Mehs, “The Candle”—also known as “The Inferno,” “The God of a Thousand Bodies,” and “The Fire That Walks.” A demon who becomes wreathed in flame when angered, Mehs is short-tempered and feared by even his highest-ranking subjects.  Mehs virtually never leaves his island city, instead using his forces to defend against outside invasion. The city is littered with protective glyphs, and it is said that he would rather destroy all that he has created than ever accept defeat.  Lighthouses are positioned both along the coast and far inland, and the outer towers are darkened during times of war, tricking unwary ships into striking the sharp rocks, cracking open and spilling their valuable contents like eggs for the demon’s breakfast.  However, the city has one coveted gift, a unique product of unparalleled beauty that traders are willing to risk any dangers to acquire: colored glass. Mehs, the City of Glass, utilizes the minerals of the island and the skill of its tradesfolk to make incredible, translucent works of art in every imaginable color, selling figurines, chandeliers, looking-glasses, and even stained-glass windows to the (mostly Dacian) mortals who arrive at their docks. Glass buildings tower over the horizon in the day, shining in rainbow splendor (though the smell of the glass blowing workshops can detract from enjoying the view).  While the locals barely notice it after a lifetime in Mehs, the whole city hums around them; the glass resonates in eerie, dissonant chords.  Occasionally, if one looks into a window, mirror, or colored glass artwork, as it is humming particularly loudly… one might see eyes watching from the darkness at the heart of the glass, or beyond.

Inv. History: the world tree and its roots
column5: The World Tree is known to be intrinsically linked to the existance of Adelrune and the turning of the planes and ages. While the stories say it was once corrupted in a time before time began, it was cleansed for the world to begin anew, and its roots spread throughout Adelrune holding together the fabric of reality. It is said that there are ten known roots throughout the world, each placed under ancient protects and lost to history and human record as to where the roots could be located.

They have settled in and bathed in the blood as guided by Zarerith, and are listening to the lessons of the celestial.

Topic: Military Insight: Zarerith
column5: While specific numbers are unclear, Zarerith like many other celestials, are known to inspire those around them to follow their teachings and it seems to be influencing passerby and local animals to join them and "seek refuge" with them in the blood. These followers include a small gang from Port Frey, a merchant caravan with their guards, some other stray travelers, and many local animals and beasts.

Topic: Political Insight: Zarerith
column5: Rather than being released when Harvester loosened the shackles of those bound in the Citadel, Zarerith seems to have gotten out before the all of that and gone into hiding deep in the forests of Tear. While hidden away, there is no knowledge of what they were up to until they stumbled upon the font of power that is the corrupted blood root of the World Tree. However, as many celestials do, Zarerith has inspired followers to join them in their activities and gaining power from the blood.

Topic: Inv. History: Fallen Celestials on Tear
column5: Tear does not have a history of fallen Celestials being common except in those brought to Tear by House Kaelin. Among the bound, fallen celestials brought to Tear was: Iudicium, a fallen Celestial of Judgement, who was killed as the Returned attempted to recapture him. Longanimitas, a particularly fast fallen Celestial with the virtue of Patience who refused to yield to those trying to recapture him at the Citadel and subsequently died in the conflict. Navitas, a celestial ringed in fire, who carries an overly-zealous virtue of diligence, and who escaped into the depths of Tear and hasn't been recovered or killed in the months since they were released. An additional fallen celestial was recovered and returned to their containment, though their name and information is purposely being obscured from the records by House Kaelin. Fallen Celestials who escaped prior to the release of everything bound within the Citadel include Zarenith, Jarien, and Austiuious, all of which seemingly went to ground and have not been encountered or recovered.

Topic: Inv. History: what are some prominent liches in history? what IS a lich? has there ever been an example of a lich who wasn't evil?
column5: While the term "Lich" has appeared in numerous places and texts throughout history, it has been used in varying capacities. Most often you can find it mentioned in old Decimal texts in reference to a long-lived/eternal undead, but even among Decimal scholars this definition is not standardized and can be attributed to many different forms of undead. While undead, as a whole, are prone towards violence and heinous acts, there are necromantic spells that can instill morality within an undead, which would suggest that they are not bound/required to do evil acts.

column5: The military protections of Edge have long depended on their fortifications and were heavily reliant on the use of mercenaries and gangs to stand against the Effendal when they attempted to sack the city.  At this point in time, with the Blood Rose nearly wiped out, there is no central military force to defend the city, but rather smaller individual mercenary groups as hired by the Colish governor. As it is near the spreading corruption, it is possible there will not be a city to defend much longer if the corruption is not stopped.

Topic: Inv. Loved Ones + Healthy Paranoia: Avery Dunnelon
column5: Avery Dunnelon, while young and unmarried, has strong and deep family connections both in the Celestine Empire and in Cestral. Their older brother, Oliver Dunnelon, serves as the Royal Seneschal for the Celestine Empire, and the pair have always been particularly close even when their duties keep them apart. Their father died at the Temple of Destiny during the Northern War, while their mother resides in West Riel and is a school teacher for prospective Handmaidens. They also seem to have two cousins in Cestral they correspond with regularly, both of whom were married through peace treaty to the Vicaul that call Cestral home.

column5: - Rumor has it, theoretically one could drop into the city off the back of a dragon - so long as they are careful to avoid landing on the jagged glass spires that top many of the buildings. - Rumor has it, Bellus and a force of Returned once teleported directly into Mehs' private study and stole some of his belongings. - Rumor has it, a favored merchant can simply sail their ship to Mehs and walk into the city to trade. - Rumor has it, a demon was recently discovered having been smuggled out of the city while contained within a ring. - Rumor has it, those who anger or threaten Mehs will be incinerated long before they are able to even consider escaping the city.

Topic: Military Insight: how to get a strike force into Mehs (City)
column5: Mehs virtually never leaves his island city, instead using the geography of his island and his forces to defend against outside invasion. There are few docks for ships to land on, and the city is littered with protective glyphs. It is said that he would rather destroy all that he has created than ever accept defeat.  Lighthouses are positioned both along the coast and far inland, and the outer towers are darkened during times of war, tricking unwary ships into striking the sharp rocks, cracking open and spilling their valuable contents like eggs for the demon’s breakfast in shallow waters. Whereas in the past, particularly motivated demons might have teleported onto the island to prevent detection, new measures were put in place in the last year to make certain such things never happened again, and if they did Mehs' power over portals could easily stop the intruder before they appeared, or relocated the intended location of teleport to anywhere of his own choosing without their foreknowledge.

Topic: Follow the Money: groups trading with Mehs (city)
column5: There are a number of groups who trade with the city of Mehs, as it creates the best and most beautiful glassworks in the world - many artisans and merchants in Dace trade heavily for stained and blown glass crafts - particularly House Vallaro uses Mehs glass in their chandelier work, but House Raguel has a corner on the market of moving large, beautiful stained glass windows from Mehs to Dace, and such windows are found in most every noble house of note. Even House Daecen has recently placed a large order for exceptionally large mirrors out of Mehs to create an entire mirrored wing in the palace.

Topic: Inv. Vices & Rotuines: Mehs + Healthy Paranoia
column5: Mehs has a particular fondness for literature, in which he burns the books to absorbs the knowledge. On an average day, much of his time is spent in his throne room with his looking glasses of souls, keeping himself available to those who request his aid/support. Though, most notably, anyone petitioning him needs to be deferential, or his quick temper will snap and engulf them in flame - there is a *very* small window between these two states. While Mehs is always down for a bbq and well-cooked meats, he doesn't walk the city very often or interact with the greater public, as he prefers his more isolated time.

Topic: How would yo enter/exit the City of Glass? Are there ways to do so undetected?
column5: The City of Glass is located on an island off the coast of Breach, encircled by rocks and steep cliffs. It has limited docks that act as entrance points. Some form of arcane teleportation would be the most likely way to enter the city undetected, though it is likely Mehs will sense any attempt to open a portal within the city. An aerial drop in is possible, but as the towers and much of the island are constructed from glass, that would be highly dangerous to those attempting to enter.

Topic: Item Tag: Arcane Glass: inert now, but in Mehs it pulsed with arcane energy. what can we make/craft/do with this? Sense the Arcane, Sense Function, Sense Aura, Guidance
column5: Sense the Arcane: Inert Sense Function: It is glass, which can be used as a craftable component in anything that requires glass. If you choose to use it for crafting, do not take this crafting action to Logisitics, but rather contact Crystal. Sense Aura: Resolve. Guidance: You see a island city, where buildings of pure glass tower over the horizon in the day, shining in rainbow splendor. You see a craftsman working, mixing components with sand to change the color of the glass as it blown and worked into larger panes. Small pieces of glass being cut and shaped to make elaborate panels of stained glass; oval panes being laid in frames; windows on buildings; being shaped into jars and bottles; delicate baubles being strung together for a chandelier. You look through a window of vibrant green glass and notice a smudge, squinting you look harder and quickly fall backwards as eyes appear to be looking back at you but are gone just as quickly in barely a blink.  Glass has many uses in the world - in crafting, in art, and also in divination as many focus items use glass (or water) as a main component to them.

Topic: Item Tag: Sunfire Band: a ring, golden with a radiant gem set in the middle (looted from Mehs). what is the ring's function and what is it used for?
column5: The ring seems to be simply that - a ring. On further and closer inspection, your summoning knowledge makes you suspect that the gem set in the middle may be a soul gem, but without mining knowledge or practical application of summoning, you do not know for certain.

Topic: what relationship/connections exist between realms and the world tree roots (ie. are portals manifestations of world tree roots?)
column5: Though the "how" is heavily debated among scholars, what is known is that the World Tree is integral to the continued reality of Adelrune and the turning of the Ages and cycle of ages, as the Clocktower used to fill a similar role. When looking specifically at something like portals, however, no, there does not seem to be any correlation or supportive academic debate or theory that these are manifestations in anyway connected to the World Tree.

Topic: Researchable Weapon: Sword

Topic: House Valence (Celestine Empire)
column5: House Valance of the Celestine Empire is a major house who bears the heraldic colors of blue, white, and silver, and their crest is a falcon holding a key. One of the most wealthy of the Celestine Noble Houses, they have been around since the earliest days of the Empire and are known for War and Fencing. In particular, House Valance is known for producing heroes of legend, but also those of notoriety - the most well known among the people in Port Frey would be Leucraven Valance the founder of the Brotherhood, and his sister Antares Valance, a Theddespari Mage. The presence of Valances in Theddespari's history suggests a splitting of the house midway through the Age of Tane, when those who did not agree with the Celestine goal of expansion left the country in search of a home that would not draw the attention or ire of the Nadine Empire. Among the Valance who remained in the Celestine kingdom as it became an Empire are those such as: the last king of the Celestine Empire, King Tobias Valance Celestine before his assassination; Sir Davion Valance who took the final blow to kill the Demon Lord Tane (which the Age of Tane is named for); and Handmaiden Valeria Valance Celestine who was nearly queen only a few years ago if not for Diana and who still leads the Legion of Daece.

Topic: what nutrients are being drained from the world tree? can alchemy used to fertilize the world tree to promote healthy regrowth and healing moving forward?
column5: There are no nutrients being drained from the World Tree, rather the demonic divine fire burns through the saps and liquids of the roots, turning them to blood with its corruptive forces. To experiment with alchemy and fertilization to promote healthy growth, you would need to arrange for a trip to the root in which to attempt to do so.

Topic: can I use my god mark for the ritual that pertains to Mehs' portals?
column5: It's *theoretically* possible to create some form of ritual that would act similarly to Theft of Majesty, which would drain the divine power from your godmark and put it into a physical object that can be used as an object of power to make use of the Mehs portals, yes.  If you would like to pursue this path, I can provide you a blue research tracker to create such a ritual that can be picked up from the bulletin board at check-in.

Topic: Item Tag: golden charm: makes you feel lucky. what makes it give the feeling of luck and what is this item's purpose?

When thinking about connections between the Dawn and Luck, you get the sense there is something missing there, something forgotten or erased, a name that will forever sit on the tip of the tongue but never be spoken.

Topic: is there a way to purify corrupted materials and food tags? what adverse affects can we expect if this progresses? can they be used productively utilizing its adverse effects?
column5: Demonic Corruption of this scale has never been seen on Adelrune, and thus there is no notation about how to purify it in any library or resource available to you. It can reasonably be inferred from this level of corruption, with food tasting off and minor issues appearing in crafting materials, that those problems will only grow worse as the corruption spreads, eventually making them inedible or unusable as one might find in the Breach.

Topic: div block. descriptions of the lord of crowns, princess of night, lady of darkness
column5: There are no recorded descriptions of the Nameless gods. In fact, even their names are not written down in libraries or sources accessible.

Topic: div. block. in the ballad of the fallen crown, it mentions a "deeper darkness may unfold" - what could this mean?
column5: Given the context of the Ballad of the Fallen Crown, it could reasonably be interpreted that this is less about a specific "deeper darkness" and more of a cautionary note not to grow too focused on any one darkness and allow others to dwell in their shadows.

Topic: how does the locking mechanism/Aegilis' last stand work? how would one bypass it?
column5: Aegilis' Last Stand was not something written, that has occured before or since, but a desperate act taken by those who remained behind at the House Kaelin homestead. What is written is that it has fully locked the homestead, and it was intended that there was no way to bypass it. As for how it works, while you can find many theories about what it could possibly be, perhaps some form of soul-binding, or last final grand creation, no one truly knows.

Topic: are ther trahazi stories related to removing, preventing, or healing demonic corruption? world tree? root locations? SHE'S EFFENDAL
column5: While there aren't stories about affecting demonic corruption among the Trahazi, Vasya's particular caravan had a large effendal population within it, and though her searching for information about the world tree and it's roots is less than fruitful, there are just enough pieces which call back to a long forgotten memory. A story she was told as a child, of how the soldiers of the Inquisition and the Amalgamation drove the effendal from their homesteads and their duties to the Balance - not only in keeping demons, celestials, sea beasts, and land beasts in check - but also in protecting an even greater secret, in that their homesteads were built over great roots that reached near the surface of the land but did not emerge. The Effendal of Vasya's caravan were not of the Houses and Tribes that fled to Tear, but those few remnants of the Eastern Expanse who slipped away during the conflict or who weren't at home when the worst occurred - those who suffered the greatest at the hands of the Inquisition - who spoke of their homes in soft, grief-stricken whispers, and who kept half-mad family members tucked quietly away in caravans when they were too near the villages and towns. They spoke of the diplomats and advisors of House Ranes, who lived in the highest reaches of the Veinish mountains - "Eternity's Will", and her tribes, Ney'ewn "The Nine Tailed Fox", Deneail "Of Blind Obedience", and their only tribe who survived to flee west - the Radolond "Pathfinders of the Way". They mourned their loss from the Churchwood Forest when the demon hunters of Durhen Megil "The Blinding Sword" were lied to and destroyed, along with their tribes of Deranar "The Vigil of the Moon", Daene "Radiance of Duty", and Fyneven "The Scent of Ashes". They whispered words of hope for Edanaven, "The Winter's Breath", near to the capital of the Kingdom of Vercrux who hunted the great beasts of the land. Rumors persisted that the House survived and fled - not west, but north, with the Vayhalil "Who walk in Shadows" after the sacrifice paid by their lost tribes the Lory'Falyne "The Hunter's Companion", and the Vilraene "Of Raven's Blood". They murmured that these are the Effendal of the Trahazi, and their homes should never be forgotten for the secrets they hide.

Topic: div block. how can someone reach and enter the Clocktower?
column5: The Clocktower was destroyed and is no longer accessible.

Topic: research: consulted an expert on the Abyss. what does abyss coloration signify beyond an elemental affinity? why do silver abyss spider webs work as a ritual component rather than other types of abyss spiders? what makes silver spiders so special?
column5: In general, coloration of Abyssal spiders is simply a signifier of elemental affinity and bloodline, rather than anything deeper, and though the behaviors and temperments will often align with their coloration there is no guarantee this is always the case - example, a red fire abyss spider will be more tempermental and cranky, while a white ice abyss spider might be more cuddly and affectionate in an effort to get warm. Silver Abyss Spiders are much older and well-established beings, which means they have begun to manifest more of the nature of the Abyss through themselves. On Adelrune, this typically manifests as magical properties within their webs, while in the Abyss it is simply that the silver webbing can be used to capture demons upon which they can feed, because silver is a truer form that will harm and touch demons directly.
