Topic: could the Empress gain god power from the sacrifice of a god marked individual wanting her to gain that power? or is there some other way to use this mark for the Empress? (Prometey's Ren mark)
column5: It is possible that one could research a ritual that would sacrifice a willing individual with godmark to transfer said power to another diety.  If you would like to take this path, let me know and I will get you a blue research tracker.

Topic: what is the knowledge that the blood cauldron has about the blood tree, and what the connection might be with the corruption of the world tree?
column5: The Blood Cauldron does not have any additional information about the Blood Tree or it's connection with corruption, as anything regarding the Blood Tree is from previous cycles that predate recorded history.

Topic: how to locate, hunt, kill (and harvest) krakens + Consult an Expert (legendary beasts)
column5: Most sea beasts live in the deeper oceans, particularly beyond the Mists that ring the world, and live solitary lives. Within the Mists, they are safe and protected, but they will venture out into open water to hunt for food, or seek out ships that come too close to threatening their nesting grounds. Locating sea beasts is often a situation of chance more than anything when on the Torrent, but for those who can call to the creatures of the sea, such as priests of the Mist Faith, they are more capable of finding them with ease and frequency. Hunting and killing such beasts is always dangerous as many of them are as large (if not larger!) than ships, with strong bodies and potentially multiple limbs to grab onto a potential threat. Ballista, harpoons, and other such sharpened range weapons are most effective in combat with such beasts, and the goal is always to keep them at a distance as much as possible. Sorcerers and Blood Mages are also effective for ranged combat. If the creature is close enough to slam into a ship or take hold of a ship, it is often too late because the last thing a sailor wants is to have their hull cracked, their mast broken, or be knocked into the open sea with such a creature. Many of the stories of those who have ventured into and returned from the Mists are filled with the most horrific sea monsters and beasts that were stumbled across, driving the sailors mad who encountered them, including a particularly gruesome tale of one poor soul who had been locked in the brig when the ship encountered one such creature. When he was finally rescued by a passing ship, he told his saviors about how those who were free to roam the ship were driven by the call of the beast to kill one another with their bare hands, ripping flesh from bones with no reluctance or hesitation, only saved himself by the fact he had been locked away for the day for swiping wine from the captain's quarters, and thus could not be reached by the crew members when he coward in the furthest corner of the cell.

Topic: what is the relationship between Lady of the Mists and Lady Luck/Miss Fortune? are there any rituals that can be performed using the golden charm?
column5: Truly there do not seem to be any connections between the Lady of the Mists and Lady Luck/Miss Fortune beyond that they are both cults heavily influenced by the Returned, and favored by those who reject the Patron God of Sailors, Avirtia. Particularly, Lady Luck/Miss Fortune is not even affiliated with the sea itself besides being favored by Colish. Regarding the Golden Charm, while there is a remnant of magic on it, the tag is expired and thus the magic has faded beyond usability.

Topic: is the well of heroes a gateway to the Dreaming? could it be empowered by worship? does it have restoritive properties for dream creatures?
column5: The Well of Heroes is, in effect, a one-way gateway out of the Dreaming. It itself cannot take worship as it is not a *being* capable of possessing power. If there was a "restoritive property" it would be "brings them back to Adelrune again" after they have broken a tether and been returned to the Dreaming.

Topic: what are the names and locations of the Drir Elders laid to rest in the Witherwood
column5: Alright, so circling back to this. Modern burial grounds and body locations are still something that is not typically covered in research unless someone is actively adding such information to a library or research object. However, I can give you this: The Mortichyte Drir refugees who lived in the Witherwood swamps between Solace and Port Frey practice necromancy as part of their ancestor faith, and in doing so they do not bury the bodies of their dead. Rather, they raise them and keep them close as family members who continue to take part in daily tasks - whether that is labor, caring for children, or simple hunting or gathering of food. As the Witherwood was formerly the site of a tear between the realms of Adelrune and the spirit realm, it has always been much easier to commune with spirits in that region, particularly when not directly interacting with a body or tethering object.

Topic: is there a way to combine Drir dead spirits so they can accept god candles, and how can we do that?
column5: No, as mortal spirits move on to the death realms naturally, trying to combine such spirits would require a truly evil act to forceably stitch souls together into an abomination. Furthermore, mortal souls are not capable of accepting divine power.

Topic: (Illiana and Priscilla are Churchwood Effendal) - are ther any notable traditions of the Churchwood Effendal? ie flight of sparrows, etc. are there any significant similarities/differences between the traditions of the Churchwood and Citadel effendal? in what ways has interaction and integration of humans in Churchwood impacted or modified Churchwood effendal traditions?
column5: Churchwood Effendal is somewhat of a misnomer in that there are many Effendal who went into hiding among humans who lived in the Churchwood, but also there was a hidden Effendal stronghold in the Churchwood called Trier. Around 97ch, this stronghold tower was built and many Effendal survivors of the Purges took refuge there from the many different Houses and tribes of the Eastern Expanse. These "Churchwood Effendal" held truer to their roots, but not in the same way as the Citadel did because they did not have a single house with tribes to divide themselves amongst. Rather, they have their histories and some varying practices and traditions, but over the Age of Chorus they have all allied and relied on one another. Some traditions live on like the Flight of Sparrows, though it is not referred to as such nor is it such a big deal as it is among the effendal of the Citadel, if only because they do not segregate themselves by their Callings. Rather, they favor their children to be well-rounded and capable of defending themselves and others, as well as providing for one another in what manner they are capable of, so their "Flight" is more designed to make sure they are capable of surviving regardless of their situation. While they still receive "Flight Names", these are not something that are used to call someone by except during moments of recognition or ceremonies, much in the way human titles and honorifics are. In 182ch, the tower Trier was discovered and besieged by the Inqusition, but did not fall and it and its inhabitants live on now, though are harried on occassion by the Church. (Please Note: this particular fact may shift with the new lore document)   Among the most commonly represented Houses and tribes in the effendal of Trier are those of Durhen Megil who were not at home when the house was wiped out through human treachery: Durhen Megil "The Blinding Sword" (demon hunters whose homestead is in the Churchwood Forest near Cyrus), Deranar "The Vigil of the Moon" (scholar), Daene "Radiance of Duty" (soldier), Fyneven "The Scent of Ashes" (hunter). In 38ch: their leaders were captured after false diplomacy and a surprise raid by King Hastings. Edanaven "The Winter's Breath" (hunters of beasts, whose homestead is in the lands of Vercrux), Lory'Falyne "The Hunter's Companion" (hunters and soldiers), Vilraene "Of Raven's Blood" (soldiers, who were lost in 51ch when they were defeated by King Vercrux while buying time for the Radolond to escape to House Edanaven), and Vayhalil "Who walk in Shadows" (scholars). Edanaven refused to leave their homestead when the Kaelin and Delfestrae fled across the sea, and in 63ch, House Edanaven, and the Ven'Lanae were crippled in a fight against King Vercrux. House Vyn'e'Va performed a powerful ritual conjuring a huge wave that engulfed a large portion of King Hastings, and King Voi's armies, but destroyed the remnants of House Vyn'e'va in the process. (A HIGHLY KEPT SECRET, BARELY WHISPERED ABOUT EVEN AMONG THEIR OWN) It is believed by the Churchwood Effendal that the Edanaven and Vayhalil were not defeated in the Purges and possibly went into hiding elsewhere sometime after 65ch when all contact was lost without confirmation of a conflict or battle. The last common House represented are those who were not at the Homestead or were separated durng the conflicts from: House Ranes "Eternity's Will" (advisors and diplomats, homestead in the Veinish mountains) who were captured by King De’Fey in 53ch and brought to Cyrus in chains, Radolond "Pathfinders of the Way" (diviners/scholars, escaped to Tear), Ney'ewn "The Nine Tailed Fox" (hunters) who were defeated by King Vercrux in 51ch as they attempted to flee towards House Ranes, Deneail "Of Blind Obedience" (soldiers) who were destroyed by King Voi of the Amalgamation in 46ch, as they defended House Ranes’ retreat.     For those "Churchwood Effendal" who are not of Trier and rather live more loosely among the various Trahazi caravans that pass through, or in remote human towns, many of these traditions and memories of their homes are long-lost as they have assimilated into Hastings society.

Topic: research: book from House Ra'e'len in an ancient language. what is the historical significance of this book? what language does it appear to be written in? are there illustrations of note in this book?

what components for the glyphways atttune to the Castle better? are there any specifialists within the Castle who would be able to support it's construction? are there any objects or components that would allow us to modify the blueprint to fit within the castle?
column5: Ultimately, what is missing from Castle Thorn that will cause it to function different than at the Citadel is the inclusion/support of divine power. Additionally there are many more leylines which converge at Castle Thorn, and the old magic used to create the strait at the end of the Crusades has left them unstable.

Topic: Is there documentation in Castle Thorn history that would fit the description of an Abyssal Princely Artifact, or the Castle Thorn equivalent of an Aegilis Rune Hammer
column5: Without access to Castle Thorn's libraries and records, you are working from limited resources and knowledge that are available in Port Frey. From Vhynn's notes, you can reasonably discover that an Abyssal Princely Artifact is not likely to be something acquired or held within the Castle itself, but likely would need to be obtained from a demon of equivalent rank or power that Bellus was. Regarding the Aegilis Rune Hammer, you would know that while Castle Thorn has competent smiths within its walls, there are other human nations who are far more exceptional in matters of smithing and craft, which Castle Thorn may be able to trace its roots to.

Topic: what would be the most ideal location within the castle for the placement of a glyphways gate?
column5: As the Castle is designed as set of concentric rings with the keep in the center, the most ideal location to place a Gylphways is at the outermost gate.

Topic: what methods can be used to harvest and use a large quantity of obsidian for a city? (Trahazi want to harvest the Obsidian Tomb)
column5: As obsidian is a specialty mining component and particularly brittle, only the most skilled miners are capable of collecting it successfully in portions large enough to make use of. As a specialty item, it is generally used for objects that are exceptionally sharp but do not need to be particularly durable (arrowheads, cutting implements, etc). It is also highly favored in the Nadine Empire along with black marble for the shiny black finish it has when carved and shaped to bring out that quality without breaking it. It's brittleness makes it a poor choice for general building materials, but exceptional for decoration and facade work on a more durable structure.   Note: It is highly likely that any large scale mining of the Obsidian Tomb will result in trouble with the former inhabitants and allies of Whitehall.

Topic: what is Oro's story/where does he sleep?
column5: The Star Seer is said to be able to read the stars in the night sky and foresee what is to come, a lesson which he once taught humanity. Though information about Oro is rare, and it has long-since been forgotten that he originated this particular practice, this style of divination is most common in the Celestine Empire. Though no longer attributed to him, Oro's lessons in divination and astrology carry on to this day, and every diviner in the world knows the most important divination lesson of all, because Oro was insistant that humanity understand the greatest risks of divination. For those who spend their days looking ahead, not only can they become lost in it, but when seeking the future it is not uncommon to glimpse one's own death. Doing so, for many, will drive them mad as they jump at shadows and are always waiting for that day to come, so they forget to live their lives in the present. It is said the Star Seer taught humanity this lesson because hundreds of years ago he saw what awaited on the prescribed day of his own death, and he accepted it.

Topic: what does Eden want souls for? how do the death realms function?
column5: As Eden is the Goddess of Death, taking mortal souls after they depart Adelrune is her right. She does not use them in any particular way, merely judges them and places them in the afterlife she deems they have earned. For necromancers and those who defy death and the natural order, this is the Pits. For those devoted to her service or she favored, the Estates are waiting. There are many other areas of her Realm that one could be placed as well, all on a varying scale - the Forges for those who will serve her for eternity, the Library for those that disgust her but are not outright breaking the rules of death, etc. This is how all death realms function, as the universe intends - mortal souls are given life on Adelrune, and when they die, the souls move on the Spirit Plane.

Topic: comparing notes on the interplanar travel that has happened when the veil is at its thinnest, the research team attempted to apply the ease of movement to the Amberlight Gateway project. if the dream bridge is timed to only open on the full moon, when the veil is thinnest, will this reduce the strength or size of the "true pieces" of Adelrune or the Dreaming needed to tether the ends? will this pattern/schedule reduce damage/harm to the veil?
column5: The more you compare notes on this project and experiment with timing under the full moon, you find that the size or strength requirement of the true pieces does not seem to make a difference as only a nominal (hand-sized amount) of such pieces is required to act as the tethering agents. Simply, a handful of dirt from Adelrune, and a handful of Dream sand. However, you do find that until this point, the veil between Adelrune and the Dream has not yet thinned enough to allow easier cross-over, as often happens in the fall when Adelrune and the Spirit Plane come close to one another, but as December fades into January, you do notice the thinning is much more pronounced.

Topic: Antioc research mod: what is the nature of the unknown school of magic? channeling and  and other magics were repelled & ineffective
column5: The unknown school of magic seems to be less something unique and new, and more a horrifically twisted and malformed conglomeration of Dream Magic, Warding Magic, and something older still woven into the mountains. These three things already had been twisted in fascinating and unique ways into the very foundations of the city and were torn asunder by whatever incident ocurred at Antioc that flattened the fortress to rubble. This additional power twisted in with what was already tangled magics to rip apart the Iron Chalice vault, letting loose countless unknown defensive and protective magics, destroyed dozens of dangerous artifacts of unknown variety and power, and are now all reactions to one another in catastrophic ways.

Topic: Antioc research mod: can the corruption found at the epicenter be healed/altered/sealed/replicated/diminish/controlled?
column5: As the twisted and conflicting magics that were used throughout Antioc are reacting dangerously to one another and corrupting anything that wanders near into new, twisted creatures, it seems unlikely there is a single method that could be used to treat the corruption. In all likelihood, from the time you spent there, you suspect it would take dozens of scholars years of studying at the site of what was once Antioc to begin to understand what magics were spun there by some of the greatest mages who ever lived on Adelrune, such as Chimerubim. Spending such time there would also likely lead to repeated attacks from creatures against any scholars, and likely twist and corrupt the scholars themselves who spend too long in that place.

Topic: antioc research mod: can the unknown magic be replicated? what were the conditions that created this anomoly? is it unique to high levels of dream magic?
column5: The history of Antioc is long and storied, built from the mountains to withstand Nadine forces, it instead held off Ko'aat of incredible power for centuries. The same Ko'aat who took down House Ra'e'len and their Tribe Leneth, who took down the thriving city of Theddespari, and still Antioc stood. Some of the greatest mages of history contributed to the defenses and stability of Antioc - Chimerubim of the Brotherwood, still believed to be the most powerful warden in history, among others of his friends who greatly excelled in their own magics and creativity - and to its infamous Iron Chalice vault, said to be the most secure location on Adelrune with unknowable spells and defenses protecting it. Later inhabited by the Gold Chalice with their own protections, laid seige to by demonic forces under the leadership of C'thon Darkweaver for over a year who caused dozens of issues to the fortress itself, as well as forceably blowing a hole into the Vault by way of incredible powers to steal a single book. Taken over by Nadine and their own fortifications. Troubled by Dream Magic, and Channelers drawing too much from the earth for years while they were trapped within the Mists, and finally with whatever cataclysmic event led to it's ultimate destruction that leveled the whole fortress and destroyed the vault and all its contents in one powerful moment - it is likely there is no other place on Adelrune that has ever experienced magic so twisted and forced together in impossible ways to compare to this magnitude.

Topic: Political Insight: Melsire
column5: Your political advisors struggle to find surface level information regarding Melsire, as bigger names, such as Mehs and Moonshroud, take center stage in the world of demons. A close confidant of yours remarks that some have been referring to Melsire as “The Whisper in your Ear”, which paves way for new information. Under the name of “The Whisper in your Ear”, Melsire presumably wields a subtle yet potent influence in the political realm of demons and in the current conflict, but it’s hard to know how tangible it is. Unlike others who rely on brute force or direct manipulation, Melsire thrives by sowing seeds of doubt and paranoia in the minds of prominent figures. Her motivations are elusive and sporadic, while her maneuvering often takes place in the form of whispers, rumors, cryptic messages, and disinformation campaigns. Despite her behind-the-scenes tactics, her presence is felt throughout the Breach. Her ability to exploit peoples’ fear means that, in every city or group she touches, there are those who forever are looking over their shoulder.

Topic: Military Insight: Melsire
column5: Military reports regarding Melsire’s history and activities are scattered and filled with uncertainty, as her methods are filled with secrecy and lies. Watchtowers have picked up signs of her presence in the area, though few can confirm the full extent of her forces. Her tactics seem to defy traditional military strategies, as she doesn’t rely on sheer numbers or overwhelming strength– instead opting to influence battlefields with creeping fear and manifested lies. Some rumor that her soldiers blend seamlessly with shadows, whispers, and among the Returned. Military consultants rumor that Melsire herself will be in town Friday night*, though no one can agree on what forces will be with her. Some say she will be leading a small but highly trained group of agents, while others claim she’ll appeal alone with legions of demons at her fingertips. Her unpredictable movements and cryptic strikes keep even the most seasoned commanders on edge, and those who fear being the target of her often find themselves fighting invisible foes– paranoia becoming as much a weapon as any sword or spell.   *OOC Note: be around in town at 11pm <3

Topic: Underworld Insight: Melsire
column5: While Melsire’s negotiations with the Keepers of Sanctuary lead many to believe that finding the remaining caravans returning from the current scattering are a primary goal, whispers from the underworld suggest that she is also in search for a powerful relic– an artifact that was last seen with the Elders currently in one of these caravans. This relic, it’s said, wields immense powers of vitality, strength, and recovery. This has your underworld connections baffled, with some even positing that the caravans are purposefully not returning from the Scattering out of fear of interception. As time passes, you too begin to wonder why these remaining groups with Elders of Redemption in them wouldn’t have been notified of the Scattering’s conclusion. Some are saying that this effort to stay hidden and cloaked seems organized and well thought of by the Elders, but they may not know that the demonic forces are in the process of tracking them, nor that their position has been compromised.  Your underworld confidants heard a very recent rumor that the caravans have stopped communicating with the city of Redemption, as tensions rise and fear fills the air.

Topic: Underworld Insight: The Widowers
column5: The Widowers are always on the move, tracking various nobles and their behaviors, and holding those accountable who overstep. With some of the more recent chaos in Dace,

Topic: Investigate History: Origins of House Montaige
column5: House Montaige can trace its origins back to the Celestine Empire, as can many of the founding Houses of Dace. In the Celestine Empire, however, Montaige was merely a peasant, merchant family who grew to become a great power and driving force of knowledge and information. In fact, in the Celestine Empire, they were once scholars and bookmakers, and only became information brokers later after they settled in Dace when they found that the information they had gathered before their departure could be used to build their wealth. There was plenty of wealth to be made in the realm of secrets brokering.  Among the founding families with the most money, House Montaige helped to bolster the government in the early days of Dace, and gained possession of the Mask of Prophecy which helped to greatly expand and grow their house over the decades as they were rumored to take advantage of the foresight the mask provided them.

Topic: Investigate History: Nadine Necromancers during the War of Radiance
column5: It is well-documented that the Necromancer's Tower of the Nadine Empire contributed 100 Necromancers under the leadership of Prefectus Victor Corpsenari to the Unity Army for the Battle of Port Frey at the end of the War of Radiance. while nearly fifty percent of their necromancers were lost to the efforts, they handled no less than 10,000 undead over the course of the Battle, on more than one occassion turning the tide in the favor of the Unity forces when living forces were cornered or in a bad position, or when an action was needed that would have led to certain death. Many of the undead they raised were fallen Unity soldiers, particularly those from the Amalgamation and Gael as their soldier numbers were much higher than other nations contributions.

Topic: Underworld Insight + HP: Gang Activity in the Embassy District
column5: With the clearing out of the Tuck-Ins from the Embassy District, it only appears that the Happy Gentlemen remain to cause trouble. As they are represented by finely dressed Dacian gentlemen, it is quite difficult to pinpoint any particular perpetraitors of robberies and muggings because they do not stand out among the others who live in the district.

Topic: Eavesdrop: C'thon Darkweaver's necklace
column5: - "I heard the Darkweaver has a secret new lover he is wooing with that necklace." - "I'm sure that necklace will only be for the most powerful and loyal of the Darkweaver's Court!" - "Have you seen that necklace? It's fit for a Queen!"  "Yeah, an Abyssal Queen!" - "I heard the necklace is being sent to Dace in exchange for all the Delfestrae demon tethers from the War of Radiance." - "Yeah, well I heard its made of moonstone and was blessed by the Monarch before he returned to the Abyss. It's meant to be a gateway that can be activated at any time and bring forth a legion of demons even greater than Anziel's Wedding!"

Topic: Contact Scholars: Mist Faith priest to discuss Lady of the Mists demonic background
column5: MG, Smokehouse in Murder Alley

Topic: Investigate Wealth: Gold Dragon in the Dreaming with a vast horde of wealth they hold no great value, is it truly so much wealth?
column5: There does not seem to be any evidence of a lightning dragon in the Dreaming, with or without a horde of wealth.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: gold dragon in the Dreaming
column5: There does not seem to be any evidence of a lightning dragon in the Dreaming, however there are rumors of a powerful consciousness of grief that permeates a particular corner.

Topic: Contact Scholar: Forbidden Faith (not demon faith)
column5: Crystal: Loremaster Lena

Topic: Investigate History: The World Eater
column5: Any truth of the World Eater predates written history, but prophecy tells of a Great Wyrm who is locked away, whose freedom marks the end of all time - for if the World Eater is freed the cycle will fall into the Age of the Great Death. This will then trigger the other Great Wyrms to devour their associated planes, as the World Eater devours first all of Adelrune, and then finally itself. Leaving only a vast nothingness.

Topic: Investigate History: Valdir
column5: Sir Valdir Velyrone was an exceptional Liege Knight of the Iron Chalice, who is revered for having fought at the Final Battle of the Crusades. Born as a child of Theddespari, he fled to Antioc during the fall of the city, and grew up among others to become the Brotherhood. Both as members of the Brotherhood and Iron Chalice, he was known for being a fierce warrior and taking down both fae and demonic threats, alongside his wife, Ophelia. While his many allies and Brotherhood members were called to different roles during the Crusades, Valdir and Ophelia fought gallantly throughout the war, before retiring elsewhere to a quiet, unremarkable life, where they faded into obscurity.

Topic: Background Research: Bastion
column5: There is not an adequate source of literature to provide the information you are searching for, as Bastion does not have libraries or keep any written texts about its history, and it is so isolated that it does not exist in foreign archives either. Celestials are immortal beings that have no regard for history, as it is not their purpose. As such, they do not record it, and with the current means of access, you are unable to locate any that may exist.

Topic: Military Insight: Mehs' forces on Tear
column5: Mehs has no known forces on Tear at this time. Following the destruction of House Ra'e'len's homestead and the corruption of the World Tree root, he and his forces escaped the island through a series of portals.

Topic: Political Insight: Cressida Demere
column5: Lady Cressida Demere (in her youth) was a prodigy and one of the finest students and performers to come out of the Guissepe School. For decades she was a preeminent musician and singer who performed throughout Dace and the Courts abroad, and had the favor of nobles the world over, until a drastic turn in her health during the War of Radiance eventually impeded her ability to perform. Since then, she has settled into her retirement while seeking out talented young bards as a well-known patron of the arts. As she recused herself from the responsibilities to her House many years ago and has her own independent fortune amassed from her years as a performer, she has lived a happy and independent life in Solace for the better part of the last decade. Funding both the Solace Bardic College and owning one of the largest private boxes at The Vision Theater in Solace, she was instrumental in bringing in the most incredible performers from all over the world. Unfortunately as all of her funds were in the care of House Paloma, however, her fortune quickly dried up when banking issues arose last spring and she was forced to end her local patronage and return to Dace. Even retired, however, she is still quite popular among the nobles who enjoy inviting her to take in performances with them and hear her critiques of the shows. She is also known to write a very popular article in the Dacian papers which can make or break a performance, and recently she shut down what was intended to be a six month run of King Atenza by publishing an absolutely scathing review after only the first week.

Topic: Contact the Influential: Cressida Demere
column5: MG

Topic: WPE & Formal Invitation + Ply with Gifts: Cressida Demere
column5: MG

Topic: Follow the Money: Sebastian Montaige
column5: Much of Sebastian Montaige's income comes from his work: selling secrets, and performing assassinations as a Court Assassin. Among his most common expenditures are his habit of throwing money around with the Returned (often to disguise the fact he is buying intel from them), and supporting his posh lifestyle and love of fine wines and rich foods. His travel expenses are particularly high to upkeep his luxury ship that he only stocks with the  highest quality supplies, and the upkeep of his manor in Dace even when he is away.

Topic: Investigate History: Ckoredeijia
column5: Little of Ckoredeijia's history is recorded in historical texts. Founded in 162t, the site of the settlement has evidence of decades-long conflict with the local Effendal population and dramatic logging efforts and fires being spread to minimize the tree cover in the surrounding area and remove cover for the Effendal forces. From a cursory study before being driven away from the settlement, the one explorer who visted it noted that there were skeletons and remnants of the people scattered throughout the city with food still on the table and many still sitting in their chairs or at their posts. They have noted that this evidence likely suggests the settlement was killed off in 16ch when the Mists spread across Tear and took out the remainder of humanity that lived there.

Topic: Investigate Alliances: Ardent Choir
column5: The Ardent Choir are peasant zealots of the Church of Chorus, but their zealotry generally keeps them from strong alliances even within the varying Church factions.

Topic: Investigate Rivals: Ardent Choir
column5: As peasant zealots of the Church of Chorus, the Ardent Choir has enemies in every corner - the Nadine Empire, less-zealous Church factions, the Trahazi, any non-human blooded, Ser Aislynn of the White Chalice, the Celestine Empire, and the Kingdom of Hastings.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Ardent Choir
column5: There do not seem to be any significant underworld ties or connections to the Ardent Choir, as they are simply peasants doing their best to live.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Ardent Choir
column5: - Rumor has it, the celestialblooded are going to die. - Rumor has it, Chorus is coming back! - Rumor has it, the demonkin are going to die. - Rumor has it, the effendal are going to die. - Rumor has it, the faeblooded are going to die.

Topic: Follow the Money: Ardent Choir
column5: The Ardent Choir are made up of peasants, so there is very little money moving through their hands at any point. Incomes are mostly from day work and selling farmed goods, while expenses are simply the basics which they need to live.

Topic: Background Research: Ardent Choir
column5: As peasant zealots of the Church of Chorus, the bulk of the Ardent Choir are illiterate, and are far more likely to burn any books they come across than they are to read them.

Topic: Investigate Rumors + HP: Location of Beleathru's Crown
column5: - Rumor has it, Bel'e'athru's crown is at the bottom of the sea. - Rumor has it, Bel'e'athru's crown is in the Oubliette. - Rumor has it, Bel'e'athru's crown is with the Returned. - Rumor has it, Bel'e'athru's crown is in a Dacian vault. - Rumor has it, Bel'e'athru's crown is atop King Syltane's head.

Topic: Investigate History: artifacts being unable to stay in the Oubliette (ie. Beleathru's crown falling out)
column5: While the true functioning of the Oubliette has been impossible to determine throughout history, the Brotherhood used part of it as storage for exceptionally powerful artifacts that they did not trust to remain on Adelrune or within the reach of humans or demons. In the direct case of the prison into which Bel'e'athru and Prae, it is commonly theorized that the portal into this space expells powerful artifacts such as the Crown because it could help the prisoner to reach out across the planes and gain assistance in breaking out.

Topic: Contact Scholars: Divinity and Demon Gods
column5: Crystal: Loremaster Lena

Topic: Speaking Fees: Member of the Nameless Faith

Topic: Etiquette: Citadel Effendal, preffered way to receive aid/charity

Topic: Political Insight: The Inquisition
column5: The Inqusition, elite knights of the Church of Chorus, had a great deal of power and influence in the Age of Chorus when both the Amalgamation and the Church of Chorus were intact. Under the theocracy and the Voice of Chorus leading the nation, Inquisitors stood above the Crown knights of the various kingdoms of the Amalgamation to lead the five armies in war. For a nation as rife with internal struggle as the Amalgamation, the Inquisitors served several roles.  The first was to spread the mandates of the Church.  The second was to seek out heresy and threats to the nation and destroy them.  The third was to maintain order.  All Inquisitors are skilled in the arts of torture, and were chosen and indoctrinated since their youth. Since the death of the Voice in 200ch, and the ensuing destruction (and dissolution) of the Amalgamation, and the destruction of the Cathedral and deaths of the College of Cardinals, the Inquisition has lost the oversight of a governing body, and so the strongest Inquisitors have rallied their own forces to them, splitting the Church into various factions. In the last year, since the destruction of Whitehall, many of these factions are rallying in Cyrus under the new Voice.

Topic: Investigate Rivals: The Inquisition
column5: The Inquisiton did an exceedingly good job at maintaining order through fear in the Amalgamation, however there was always a tension between the ruling nobility and the theocracy, particularly following the Echoing of Voices and the declaration of the Amalgamatio to be a theocracy.  Under the Voice of Chorus, no one was above the Church, and therefore the Inquisition had no hesitation at executing heretical nobles. This ongoing conflict caused many enemies of the Inquisition, particularly in the Kingdom of Hastings where Princess Arielle Hastings led a rebellion (called the Hastings Rebellion) against the Church after the execution of her father and brother for being heretics. Dozens of other nobles likely harbor negative feelings towards the Inquisition though they are not as outspoken about it. Historical enemies of the Church and the Inquisition include, but are not limited to: effendal, the Nadine Empire, the Celestine Empire, the Trahazi, the Vein (prior to the War of Giants), the Ko'aat, Castle Syltane, demons, and celestials.

Topic: How do you deactivate/neutralize blood magic portals in a localized area
column5: There are many types of blood magic portals, and each would need to be studied differently to detrmine how best to cut them off - simple blood magic, demonic blood, contract magic through blood, and Drir blood portals. Contract portals through blood are tied to the spirit, so the easiest method to avoid them is not to make the deal that would allow them to function. In general, simple blood magic portals needs porous ground for the liquid to come up from or sink back down into. A documented instance of this documented by the Returned was a time when Tyaous was seen dragging Myllerial out of the Bronzeleaf Tavern to be able to blood teleport them both away as he could not do so inside the building.

Topic: Is it the effects of the blood root fungus, other manifestations of the world tree corruption, or something else that have been causing severe earth tremors on Tear? How might we be able to ideally pacify or less ideally contain the seismological effects that have been taking place on Tear?
column5: This is not really information you can gather through studying in library as seismological activity is not well-studied in Adelrune. However, between recent copies of the Assassin and other news coming to Port Frey, you're able to piece together that the entire mountain range itself in northern Tear is splitting apart from its base as the root of the World Tree twists and emerges from its usual location deep beneath the soil and stone. The further the demonic corruption spreads, the earthquakes will continue to happen and move further south, as more of the root is affected and begins to warp and distort in the process of taking on aspects of the Blood Tree. It would likely take a massive effort to stop the earthquakes as the corruption would first need to be contained, and as discovered by Returned while studying the Clocktower, the only way the Blood Tree was stopped and reverted back to the World Tree the last time it became corrupted was due to the sacrifice of two gods to bind themselves to it, creating a new world tree.

Topic: what are the occurances of the name Vesh in religious and historical texts that could provide a clear pircture of all the dieties or entities Vesh has posed as?
column5: The name Vesh is most common among Trahazi texts, though worship of this rumored-demon god is often considered to be one of the Forbidden Faiths. Amongst the Trahazi, many of the references to his name are that "Vesh always follows" as an encouragement for the Trahazi to keep moving to stay ahead of him or they will face another betrayal and terrible fortune will befall a caravan who lingers too long in one place. It is said that he was given the epitaph "The Betrayer" for his betrayal of Morcin, though what that betrayal was predates written history. The name Vesh does not appear among any other faiths, however betrayals among the gods are not uncommon so it suggests that whatever act gave him this particular epitaph was a betrayal of a particularly great magnitude if this is how history remembers his name.

Topic: what is a source of divine power? do the spirits at the castle act as a source of divine power? would they affect the glyphways?
column5: A source of divine power can be a great many things, though in the case of the Glyphways at the Citadel it was red candles that were given to the First of Delfestrae to have their power shaped for the structure. Other common sources of divine power could be (but are not limited to): a physical piece of a god, a weapon that has wounded a god, sacrificing a powerful demon, an unpaid fae boon, or a god boon (not blessing). If the souls at Castle Thorn were to be used as divine power for the Glyphways, it would likely consume tens of thousands of souls to power the ritual.

Topic: why can't gods directly influence/impact adelrune? are they limited to rules they need to abide by?
column5: There are always stories of times before, times before written history, times before time itself, times of the world when gods walked upon it's surface with their creations, and led their creations in battle, warring amongst themselves and using their faithful to do so. Times when the world was rendered asunder from the power of gods fighting in physical spaces, times when tens of thousands of faithful died following the demands of their gods. And yet, it is said that the gods said "never again" and placed a wall between themselves and Adelrune to protect their faithful from themselves. To let time turn as it was meant to, and lives of mortals be lived to their fullest.

Topic: how does a god mark bypass Eden's realm?
column5: God Marks do not *bypass* Eden's Realm, but they are a metaphysical mark upon a soul that can potentially lead a soul to a different death realm, or provide them better favor or punishment by the god which has marked them. As most souls go to Eden's Realm when they pass on to the spirit plane, a god mark from a different faith that has possession of a death realm simply means that such god has exerted power on that soul and will bring them to a different area of the realms of death, which is colloquially called "Bypassing Eden's Realm" but it isn't really bypassing in any real sense except landing elsewhere. Particularly, what a god mark means is that a soul cannot forcibly be moved to a different death realm, even through powerful rituals in the Book of Ig.

Topic: how does a god create a god mark? what does it cost?
column5: There is no way to truly know how god marks work unless one has divinity themselves and the ability to bestow such marks. What IS agreed upon by religious scholars, however, is that there is a not-insubstantial cost associated with exorcising such an act, which is why gods do not give them out so freely. Rather, they provide them to those who have served them in a profound method or provided them some great service or power. God marks are also said to allow the bearer to weild specially-blessed weapons or artifacts in a much more powerful way than if an un-marked were to use it.

Topic: what do we know about Valdir? any connections between Valdir and the Returned Ritual?
column5: Sir Valdir Velyrone was an exceptional Liege Knight of the Iron Chalice, who is revered for having fought at the Final Battle of the Crusades. Born as a child of Theddespari, he fled to Antioc during the fall of the city, and grew up among others to become the Brotherhood. Both as members of the Brotherhood and Iron Chalice, he was known for being a fierce warrior and taking down both fae and demonic threats, alongside his wife, Ophelia. While his many allies and Brotherhood members were called to different roles during the Crusades, Valdir and Ophelia fought gallantly throughout the war, before retiring elsewhere to a quiet, unremarkable life, where they faded into obscurity. It is believed that he died more than a century before the Returned came into being, elsewhere on the Expanse, and thus would have no connections to the Ritual that created the Returned.

Topic: can the Dreaming receive worship and its worshippers receive the benefits of rites? can the Dreaming be an aspect of divinity similar to nightmare?
column5: It is impossible to know what non-godly entity is capable of receiving worship, as many cults around Adelrune worship any number of things that provide them power in return. There are scholars of Ad Decimum who have claimed to worship simply "magic" while others have devoted themselves to any number of virtues, causes, or charismatic beings, and any of these have dozens of examples of their prayers being answered. Additionally, while beings such as Demenor are reffered to as a "demon god of nightmares" there is no way to definitively say what Demenor's aspect of divinity is, whether or not it is nightmare or dream, or if nightmare or dream are even aspects of divinity that can be held, or are currently held.

Topic: leading scholarly theories about what happens when demon gods die, and historical examples if there have been any
column5: The general prevailing theory is that because demon gods ARE demons, when they are killed in their mortal forms, they just return to the Abyss in a weakened state as any other demon would. It is likely possible that they could still be killed in the Abyss, as any other demon can be, and thus be truly considered "dead" if such an incident was to occur, but this is merely a hypothetical theory. Historically demon "gods" that have been killed include: Zyte, Haen, Oll, and Xiewe. In the case of Oll he was killed twice and ultimately the second time was destroyed by Tane, while Xiewe only had the one death before being consumed by his murderer.

Topic: PICK NEW TOPIC
is there a way to help the new Dream entity organize their memories?
column5: Without Lore: Dream Magic it is hard to say if dream magic can be used to help an entity organize their memories and thoughts. However it is generally known that dream magic causes insanity and distance from reality, thus suggesting it might not be effective for the entity themselves to use dream magic in an attempt to do so. Your knowledge of divination, however, almost suggests to you that this entity is far more aligned with Divination than dream - for you would know that as spellcasters progress in the art of Divination, they generally find that their minds come a bit unmoored from the flow of time. Because their art lets them peer into timelines, histories, and possibilities, it becomes difficult to connect events, people, and memories in any sort of linear fashion.

Topic: Political Insight: Trahazi of Tear
column5: The majority of the Trahazi of Tear are comprised of the Returned Trahazi in Port Frey, who by and far have the most influence and power of the Trahazi in the region. There are, however, a handful of stray Trahazi with origins in various smaller or destroyed caravans who came together looking for something better on Tear. Many of them came during the War of Wine to seek land to farm in the Antioc region under King Syltane, along with hundreds of displaced Amalgamation peasantry. After the fall of Antioc, however, they began their lives again as travelers, though they are limited by human-effendal treaties to only travel the roads between Port Frey and Solace, and north to Edge. With additional allowances for one half-mile on either side of the roads to make camp. As they have stayed within their travel allowances, and trade with the Effendal who have approached them, they have good standing relations with many of the Effendal tribes (though not all - they are still severely distrusted by the Rakuten and Svivore). They are welcome in Solace, though prefer to stay in the rolling hills and farming regions immediately east of the city for portions of the year, assisting the farmers of the area as needed in both the planting and harvesting seasons.

Topic: Follow the Money: Trahazi of Tear
column5: The majority of the Trahazi of Tear are comprised of the Returned Trahazi in Port Frey, who by and far have the most influence and wealth of the Trahazi in the region. With the few non-Returned Trahazi who have come to Tear, they dominantly work with the farmers near Solace during the planting and harvesting seasons, as well as the Intl Merchants Guild throughout the rest of the year, moving materials from Solace to Edge or Port Frey where they are delivered to various artisans, or shipped back to the Expanse. They have little in the way of expenses beyond simple comforts for living and boots and clothing, and have built small trade arragements with some of the local Effendal tribes to facilitate trade between the humans of Solace and the Effendal houses.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Trahazi of Tear
column5: The majority of the Trahazi of Tear are comprised of the Returned Trahazi in Port Frey, who by and far have their fingers in many pots and underworld dealings. There are very few non-Returned Trahazi on Tear, and among those the majority of them work for the Intl Merchants Guild for part of the year giving them legitimate work and keeping them out of underworld dealings. As very little was actually illegal under Unity, there was not much they would engage in that qualified as "underworld". While it is known a member of the Trahazi-mercenary group the Obsidian Daggers resides in Edge, she is living there as a member of the House of Clubs of Cole rather than a Trahazi and has little interaction with the Trahazi of Tear.

Topic: Underworld Insight: She Who Flies With the Wind

It is said that nothing happens in the Churchwood that she is unaware of for she can be anywhere - hidden in the depths of the shadows, on the thinnest of tree branches overhead, or within the trees themselves. Topic: Investigate History: Snake Queen (Ko'aat)
column5: The Snake Queen is considered the greatest of the Ko'aat leaders of old, as she rose to power during the Age of Tane and only expanded the strength and might of the Snake Ko'aat with her. Even during the height of power of the Ko'aat on Tear, the Snake Band were considered to be infinitely more evil than the other local warbands and were willing to take any power they could get regardless of its source. Conquering first House Ra'e'len in 185t, she led her Band against Theddespari and Tribe Leneth in 191t which ended with both in utter ruin. It wasn't until her attack and siege of Antioc in 192t that the Snake Band faced a setback, as the Snake Queen was killed against the walls of Antioc and her forces faltered in their assault. Since Returning in the Age of Chorus, the Snake Queen and her warband have generally laid low as their power had been greatly diminished in death. It has been speculated that as Returned, the lives of Syltane's forces were all bound to his life as an anchor, because it was observed that the Gold Chalice members who Returned with him never Returned again from their deaths that predated his, and upon his death the remaining few collapsed in their own immediate deaths. Given this precedence, it is speculated that the Ko'aat of the Snake Band are all tied to her in a similar way, and killing her is likely to kill them all.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Snake Queen (Ko'aat)
column5: The Snake Queen is believed to be hiding in the tunnels of Tear and out of the light, so even the best spies struggle to track her routines, and those who may get close are only rumored as they do not seem to come back from her den. It is said that the Snake Queen likes to toy with her prey for hours or days before finally ending their miserable lives. She has a fondness for torture and blood, and employs the uses of various venoms to keep things interesting as she is rumored to inform the person she will be killing of exactly how their body will react to the venom before she administers it. The Snake Queen, true to her moniker, surrounds herself with the most deadily and dangerous snakes, keeping them close to her so she can milk their venom for use not only in torture but also in her weaponry.

Topic: Investigate History: Map of Leylines in Nadine Empire & their cartographers
column5: Maps of leylines within the Nadine Empire are highly prized and tightly regulated, and such a map cannot simply be obtained within text records or libraries outside of the Empire itself. Through study, however, it is easily uncovered that most texts written about leylines from recent history are penned by Professor Antony Anhavia, who specializes in this area of study and who published several essays on the topic within the Nadine Empire. It is also likely that such a map would be held by the Head of the Arcane of the Nadine Empire, as well as potentially with the Necromancer's Guild.

column5: Over the last few decades, Vesper and Valens have made multiple appearances in academic circles across adelrune. While certainly not a scholarly duo, their shadows have darked the doors of forges in Vein. They have also visited multiple academic institutes and salons to sell rare, first edition tomes and books to shrewd collectors and scholars alike. More recently, their last foray into academia was a visit to the Red Tower of Ad Decimum before it's fall.

Topic: Criminal History: Valens & Vesper
column5: While one would never see Vesper and Valens colluding and rubbing elbows with gangs, it is not unlikely to see them visit their old haunts in Cole. From there and in the city of Edge, they made a name for themselves in selling questionably attained Eerie artifacts. While preferring nobility for their clients, they do not withhold their services for those with enough coin regardless of background or intent. They are left relatively unbothered and unthreatened by the criminal underworld due to rumors that any who cross them fall under bad luck.
