Investigate Fame: Sir Bash
column5: ⦁    Squired under Rex, during the War of Radiance. ⦁    Was the sword bearer for the swearing in of Ser Aisylnn the first Celestial to be a White Chalice Knight ⦁    Surving member of the forces that defended house Kaelin escape during the War of Radiance. ⦁    Member of group that killed the Nadine Head of Magic at battle of White Hall

Topic: Investigate Rivals & Healthy Paranoia: Sir Bash
column5: Rival is a White Chalice member that is angling for promotions Bash could receive if he returned to Hastings.

Topic: Investigate Wealth: House Roccio
column5: House Roccio falls just below the median as far as wealth goes among the noble families of Dace, but still is considered extremely wealthy when compared to the rest of Adelrune. As breeders and trainers of exotic animals and plants, much of their wealth is generated by the sale of such creatures, but since this a luxury good their wealth is at the whim of the economy and it can plummet with scarcely a warning at any time.

Topic: Political Insight: Beth
Political Insight: Maren Abernathy
column5: seneschal to the Lord Commander of Celestine Army

Topic: Investigate History: Fae Gods (other than Rown and Shenanigans)
column5: Multiple fae gods have existed throughout history, from Rown the god of animals, to Ecqe the god of luck. Styx, called the Blood Fae or the Lady of Whispers, and Inexo (who paraded as a demon) called "The Blue Fire".  Furthermore, unsuccessful attempts were made over centuries to raise Olya, Yog Zura, Briar, and Burrow to godhood as well, and the Malcontent wielded divine power (though not divinity) for quite some time.

Topic: Investigate Enemies: Snake Queen (Ko'aat)
column5: The list of the enemies of the Snake Queen are long and varied. In recent history, the Returned are among her greatest enemies, along with Leucraven. Historically, in her first life, the child members of the Brotherhood were her favorite prey to stalk through the forests of Tear, while her armies were led against Antioc and Theddespari. House Ra'e'len and Tribe Leneth suffered at her hand most directly though, as she is said to have personally killed a number of the ruling members of both Ra'e'len and Leneth. In recent years, it is said that she has sworn a blood oath to kill Palauttaa and take the rest of Svivore and Rakuten down with her.

Topic: Political Insight: Inquisition Factions
column5: You'll get a broad overview with this spend as there are multiple factions, so for more in-depth information you will need to do additional Political Insight on each faction.  After the collapse of the Church of Chorus and the dissolusion of the Amalgamation, the Inquisiton was spread across Adelrune with little contact between higher ranking knights and no overarching organization for the Church to give direction. Six leading factions emerged centered around the highest ranking member of the Inquisition in different regions at the end of the War of Radiance - Pious Justinious (currently on religious retreat) Lord General Ootomus Rex (now presumed dead) Inquisitor Claudia Hospitalus (currently in Cyrus) Inquisitor Letholdus (currently in Cyrus) Inquisitor Jarrett (currently in Cyrus) Inquisitor Grenfell (currently in Cyrus)

Topic: Investigate History: what happens to kids who are born to the Citadel but are later found to have non-Effendal blood? are they safe and provided for? do parents abandon their children?
column5: Effendal children born with non-Effendal blood are rarely held accountable for the circumstances of their birth. Rather, it is the parent who was involved in the situation who is to remain responsible - while many such cases will result in the parent taking the child and leaving the Citadel to raise them elsewere, it is the rare occassion that one might dumped their child in the woods before anyone knows of their fault. Since Ilbrim's appointment to the First of Delfestrae, however, he had shown sympathy to such children and thus they have been more accepted to remain in Citadel society, while House Kaelin continues on with their tradition of sending theirs away to be Volf'aer in the woods - tasked from their birth to the protection of the House and other Effendal.

Topic: Contact Scholars: Lena
column5: AUGUST

Topic: Investigate History: Xiewe
column5: Xiewe, called "the Scarred One" is classified as a "forbidden god" across much of the world. Those attracted to torture and harming others are most commonly found among his followers. It is said he was killed and devoured by the Celestial called Aidhreal during the War of Radiance.

Topic: Investigate History: Nykolas
column5: Nicholas, also called "The Wanderer", exists dominantly in whispers and ancient tales from long before recorded history. He is said to be a quiet, unassuming man who travels across Adelrune with a shadow-formed raven called Thomas, a herald of terrible things to come. The Wanderer is said to carry a beautiful sword, seeking out the injustices of the world to right wrongs and protect those who are incapable of protecting themselves.

Topic: Investigate History: House Etilvor and the Nadine Empire
column5: There is very little shared history between the Nadine Empire and House Etilvor. In fact, even during the Purification it appeared that the Nadine Empire was unaware of the presence of Effendal in their territory at all, until an entire mountain collapsed suddenly and without warning one afternoon in the Decimus region. It was only in the exploration of the collapsed mountain that they discovered any evidence of Effendal at all. Most of the Effendal-blooded living with the modern Nadine Empire can trace their lineage back to Etilvor, but as their culture was abandoned as they assimilated into Nadine culture, there is little any of them could say about the House at all.

Topic: Investigate Vices & Routines: Tyaeous
column5: Tyaous is particularly difficult for your spies to track down, as he has a terrible habit of disappearing into puddles of blood that sink into the earth. Paired with the frequency of his travels to obtain and collect reagents and rare critters for his blood alchemy experimentation, Tyaous is known to keep to himself and generally avoids effendal society. While particularly proud and unwilling to admit his own faults, he nonetheless will do experimentation on himself to advance his power and knowledge of blood alchemy. It is not uncommon to discover him reading, as he is a collector of ritual tomes and magical theory.

Topic: Background Research: Vicaul Warders
column5: The Vicaul, even their warders and those considered "wise", are not known to engage with the academic societies of the world. They are, however, widely believed to be the inventors and pioneers of the school of Warding, and warders in Cestral have spent centuries studying Vicaul glyphs on destroyed ships or corpses to learn their practices and record them in the annals of Alexander.

Topic: Political Insight: Headhunters
column5: Headhunters is more of a coloquial term and generally not one used by the Effendal people themselves - rather, this is a term coined by the humans who came to Tear in recent decades and found themselves at the wrong end of a pointed weapon wielded by Effendal. As such, this is not any sort of organized group or calling, as typically they are simply Effendal soldiers and scouts tasked with keeping the humans on Tear within the allowance they have been given - their settlements of Edge, Antioc (until this land was revoked in 13ar), Port Frey, and Solace, the former lands of Unity (until this land was revoked in 14ar), and within 1/2 mile of the roads between the human settlements. Any humans who venture beyond these allowances and into Effendal territory without an Effendal escort or special permission are subject to being redirected, attacked, or killed by the so-called "headhunters".

Topic: Political Insight: Vicaul
column5: The Vicaul are not a unified political faction and thus it is not possible to get a full sense of the range of their political situation or leadership with a single Political Influence spend. The Vicaul rally themselves around their various "kings" which can range from around a dozen to more than two dozen at different points in history. New kings might emerge when a military force of sufficient size and capability is able to claim a particular territory, or a king splits his territory and forces between two of his children, and the number of kings can drop off suddenly when one wars with others and conquers neighboring kings and their factions and territories to bring them under his own name. The Vicaul are widely divided in lifestyle, but ultimately are a warrior nation who will go to war with neighboring kings over scarce resources or prime territories leading to an ever-changing political climate.

Topic: Investigate History: The Oubliette
column5: The Oubliette is a strange pocket location, commonly believed to be part of the Twixt and Twain, but also an intersection point with Adelrune. It has dozens of locations within it, giving credit to the belief it is a "mini-plane" in some form, but other scholars suspect it to be more of a singular, elaborate dungeon of sorts. The release of the Malcontent revealed its deepest recesses of a prison sealed by an iron gate, while it is known that the Chalice Orders once used a different part of the Oubliette as a cell block for beings they could not kill or defeat. This portion of the Oubliette is reached through a ritual that requires all six original chalices to be used to open a portal to it, which the Returned performed during the Battle of Port Frey to seal away Star/Bel'e'athru and Prae within the depths of the cells. Prior to that, such a ritual had not been employed since the early days of the Chalice Orders. The cells are not the only part of the Oubliette which was used by the Iron Chalice, however, as fae hunters, the Brotherhood had discovered early on how to access the upper halls of the Oubliette, which are often referred to as "The Labyrinth" - an area written about as "a maze within a maze within a maze" in which one could spend their whole life looking for the exit and never find their way out. The Brotherhood and Iron Chalice frequently used the outer edges of this space as a dumping ground for powerful artifacts that could not be left in the hands of humanity or other entity on Adelrune. Objects such as the Black Sands Hourglass, the Black Orb, and the Knife called Murder, were entombed there in the late Age of Tane, until being recovered by Returned in the Age of Adelrune. It is said that one can wander into the Oubliette on accident, similarly to the way one finds their way to Paradox, and might encounter an oracle who will answer their questions about Adelrune and its secrets, but the truth of this matter has not been verified beyond rumors.

Topic: is it possible to create new/additional tethers for newborn dreams?
column5: It is not possible to create new or additional tethers for Newborn Dreams, as all stories have a beginning, middle, and end.

Topic: would breaking the leylines with a ritual akin to the ritual to create the Breach stop the spread of the corruption? are the leylines part of how the corruption is spreading?
column5: Leylines do not seem to play any part in the spreading corruption of the Blood Tree as there are no known leylines in northern Tear.

Topic: has there ever been a mention of "the daughter" in the Church of Chorus, or who her mother might be?
column5: Any and all mentions of "the daughter" are considered herasy and were removed from written records.

Topic: NEW RESEARCH TOPIC
NEW RESEARCH TOPIC
is it possible to grow crops without them becoming corrupted in Port Frey? what alternate methods are available?
column5: Port Frey currently has not been touched directly by the demonic corruption of the World Tree, and thus can be farmed by traditional methods in areas that are less swampy. Areas that are highly influenced by magic and left barren, like the Breach or near Castle Thorn, have developed farming methods that work in those territories and may be compatible in demonic-corrupted territories, but there is no record of any attempts at this being made.

Topic: if the knights of the Braid are so important and first learned from fae knights, how did the general view on the fae become what it is now? did the fae inspire/teach our knights and if not what or who did?
column5: It is said that the first knights of Adelrune are those of the Braid, taught to stand to their ideals and take part in an ancient, binding ritual by a fae creature only known to history as the Hawthorn Knight. This being did not stay in Cestral to teach or lead, but as the legends go, it simply pointed humanity on an honorable and binding path forward. The history of fae visiting the island since then has had for more drastic of an impact on modern sensibilities regarding the fae - as their children have been whisked away in the dark of the night to places unknown, or their own human children swapped for a fae-touched child of moss and sticks, many families in the countryside are particularly attentive over their young, and when able to, send them to school in the city where they are much less likely to encounter a fae that might be waiting to snatch them away just out of sight over the hill or behind a tree.

Topic: Stories of those who've been god marked by Old Ways' Wolf. Circumstances leading up to their god mark and their lives after the mark.
NEW RESEARCH TOPIC
Research: if the Life Tear of Espari is at the wellspring of life, how would one approach removing it?
column5: Generally one would need to determine what form the Tear is in and collect it as such. As the known Tears are in elixir (liquid) form, it is possible the Tear is simply contained in a bottle hidden at the Wellspring, but it could be in a variety of other liquid forms that can only be theorized - it might appear as rain at the Wellspring, contained within a tree or gourd, or buried deep as the ground water. It could take the form of the morning dew, a river, spring, or pond. It might be in local fruits, or even need to be sweated and collected from a tree. Without approaching the Wellspring itself and attempting to make the Tear react to discover it, there is little one can say of its location with any guaranteed truth.

Topic: how can I ritually bind two willing souls together in one body?
column5: There are two rituals in the Book of Ig capable of putting two souls together in a single physical body, the Ritual of Coagulation, and the ritual of Entanglement.  Coagulation: This ritual allows a Summoner the ability to merge two Spirits into the same body. This is similar to the ritual called “Entanglement”, except for a spiritual bond is not shared, Instead the bond is purely physical. As with the ritual of entanglement, this ritual poses benefits and also problems, as all ritual magic. Sharing a body with another spirit can be extremely taxing-- for many reasons. While it could be beneficial to hold the spirit of a beloved scholar, or lover, feuds can always arise. It is possible for the two spirits to fight over the body in a war of supremacy if the spirits are not at peace with each other. It is also possible that one Spirit May consume or dissolve the other’s connection to the physical body, effectively banishing it to wander the afterlife. As with the ritual of entanglement, the Summoner must be prepared for these eventualities and should meditate on what this ritual would set in motion. Beware this ritual has also been used poorly by Summoners in the past who were tricked by entities into sharing their bodies, only to be possessed fully. Beware.  Entanglement: This ritual allows a Summoner to create a permanent bond between two living spirits. The effects of this ritual manifest in many different ways some positive and some profoundly negative--depending on your viewpoint. Two entangled Spirits gain the benefit of having a sense of each other's presence on the mortal world. They have a bond which allows them to vaguely understand where they may be physically as well as whether or not they have passed from the world of Flesh. Depending on the strength of the spirits, other effects of the ritual have manifested--communication across great distances via their bond, shared magic, etc. Some negative drawbacks however is that when two Spirits are entangled, the Destiny or more precisely the afterlife of one spirit can directly impact the other. Furthermore, In many cases this ritual is used not to connect two Mortal spirits but instead the summoner's spirit with a familiar or other such outworld spirit. This poses its own complications depending on the creature or the condition of the creatures spirit. In all cases caution should be used in deciding to entangle two spirits. This process cannot be undone by any magic known to us.

Topic: how can we use the black sand urn to conduct a ritual to somehow "undo" the ritual that turned the Theddespari Mages into their current state? we want to SOLVE THIS (2 research actions)
column5: As there is no written record of what ritual the Theddespari mages performed, or even the exact specifics of the outcome of it, there is no way to reverse or solve the ritual unless you were to somehow go back in time.

Topic: how can Minos be drawn to a location? can the Returned aid Minos in fighting the Theddespari?
column5: Minos answers the call of Eden, no other. It might be possible to find more information through the Church of Eden, but there is no guarantee that would help.

Topic: what methods/magic can be used to protect ourselves (ritualists) from Theddespari while we perform the ritual to reverse the effects of the original ritual
column5: There is no known way to reverse the effects of the Theddespari ritual, and thus cannot build a plan to protect against it.

Topic: how can we use the Urn and Minos together? what does Minos do in the presence of the Theddespari?
column5: There doesn't seem to be any reason to use the Sacred Urn and Minos together. Minos simply follows the will of Eden and was hunting the Theddespari mages for a time.

Topic: why hasn't Minos been following the Thedds? would additional black sands lure Minos?
column5: It is most likely that Eden has recalled Minos to a different task than hunting the Theddespari Mages.

Topic: Research: can demons be impacted by blood curses? can we learn about the creation of blood curses from Vanya Cuil?
column5: Demons who are on Adelrune in physical bodies with blood can certainly be targeted by blood curses, though doing so often requires access to that particular demon's blood to do so. Simply having "demon blood" won't be provide the specificity that a blood curse typically requires. In the case of Vanya Cuil, this cure was created to stop the ever-mutating Blood Rage Disease that was caused by Shezrak, a demon of pestilence who was chopped into tiny pieces at the end of the Age of Tane and infected each person individually and was spread through blood, rather than a true blood curse.  For more information regard Shezrak, Vanya Cuil, and the Blood Rage Disease, see: A Brief History of the Second Occurance of the Disease by Vapaaherra Siili Sininen that is held in the Guild of Academics archives.

Topic: is blood ink compatible with a human body? ie if we inject blood ink into the body?
column5: Blood Ink is a concoction in which the recipe is highly coveted and thus protected tightly by those capable of creating it. It is not a substitute for blood, and would likely have negative effects on the human body, but you would need to actually do this experiment to discover what those effects would be.

Topic: Research: Blade of the Meek: spent 30 min "talking" to sword, being cut by it, whetstoning it, heating it, tapping it, newly returned wield it. can it be broken by normal means? can it be repaired? anything about it's crafting/history?
NEW RESEARCH TOPIC
by what method can god marks be made without a god?
column5: God Marks are something solely created by those who have the power of divinity, and thus cannot be made by beings that do not possess the powers of a god.

Topic: Research: how can we get to the Princess's (Nameless) realm?
column5: In general, it is not possible to just visit a god's realm, as they aren't a place normally accessible to humans. Occasionally, when gods spend time in the death realms, it possible they are more reachable, but typically (like all extraplanar beings) unless they descend to Adelrune there is not a lot of research to be found about visiting the realms of gods. It is more difficult particularly in regards to the Nameless gods, as they do not have a "known death realm" or connection to any one place or church that could provide a point to begin from in such a search. Additionally, even in the theoretical commentary you're able to find regarding this topic in texts, there is a note at the end of the passage that the author was killed under mysterious circumstances, presumed to be related to his studies.

Topic: Research: Deneial's origin story in each of the different cults to her?
what is the nature of the stars relationship to the proto-god? is it linked to Morphina?
column5: There does not seem to be a direct correlation between the stars and the Dream City of Morphina, more that symbolically the stars are somehow connected to the so-called "proto-god" in some manner that is unclear in texts because there are a great many beings associated with stars throughout history. The more you have investigated this topic, the clearer it becomes that you have exhausted all researchable sources regarding this topic.

Topic: Research: how did House Calae and Ariadne's rivalry begin? ie why do they have the same house colors?
column5: Though the truth of the rivalry is long-lost to history, those within the House are likely to quote a similar (though opposing) story passed down through the generations of House Calae and House Ariadne. Back in the days of the Celestine kingdom, a merchant built his trade in the creation and sale of spell scrolls. With the assistance of his twin sons, their business thrived and the availability of their scrolls helped to spread trained mages across not only the kingdom, but the whole Expanse. Each of the twins married, but when their father unexpectedly passed away before naming one of them to be the heir, the business suffered because the twins could not agree on how to move forward. Refusing to compromise (each family blames the other for being unreasonable), and unable to even share their family name due to the severe concern about sabotage between the brothers, they chose instead to split the assets between them and rather than either one using their father's name for their business they each took their wife's name - Calae and Ariadne - as their new merchant house name to differentiate between them. The House colors, however, they refused to yield on and so both Houses kept the same colors and silver/gold gyronny field - differentiated only by the red tree of Calae or the red feather of Ariadne overlaid on it.

Topic: what is known about the Returned Ritual in the new timeline? who cast it? where was it recorded?
column5: The Returned Ritual was cast by Archmagus Gomadawn of the Celestine Empire in a cave near the Celestine military encampment that would later become Solace. The ritual itself had been discovered carved into the wall of the cave, though it does not seem to be there now.

Topic: Research: are there records of a soul being anchored to Adelrune? or being in a sentient, mobile form? what happens if their tether is broken?
column5: There are not records of souls being bound to Adelrune itself, however such an investigation would lead you down the road of summoning and the binding a mortal soul to a soul gem or other object capable of containing a soul (often times masks are inlaid with small soul gems for such purposes). To bind a soul in such a way as to provide them a sentient, mobile form however is rarer still, and something generally believed to be done through necromantic ritual. For a bound mortal soul's containing vessel to be broken will shatter the soul itself, while immortal souls bound in such a way will typically find a new host body or return to their own realms as they are no longer tethered to Adelrune.

Topic: Research: how do imps travel between the void and the abyss?
column5: Imps are among the lowest of the low powered among demonic beings, and as such they do not have the power to climb out of the Abyss on their own. For those that were born in the Abyss and were not consumed, they can move to other planes such as Adelrune or the Void through portals opened by stronger beings, but this is often no more than a few dozen at a time. Rather, once they find themselves in a space that doesn't have the immediate threat of being consumed by more powerful demons, they will reproduce and spread rapidly as an invasive species taking advantage of a new region.

Topic: Research: Who/What is in the Blightwood?
column5: The "Blightwood" refers to a region in the north-west of the former Gael lands, which after the Dark Mists is the northern peninsula of the Western Expanse near the Dragon Archipelago of Dhakaar. This region was given this particular moniker by the Myrens after they sent a horrific blight tearing through the Gael forests and spread disease and pain to any living thing it came into contact with - many died outright from this blight, though others who cast powerful protective magics upon themselves and the forest became twisted and mutated versions of their former humanity. Even now as the Blightwood has begun to heal, the trees and plants remain twisted and changed to appear unlike any natural growth found across Adelrune (including the Corrupted territories).

Topic: Research: what types of demons would be most weak to darkness?
column5: As Darkness is often an element connected to the Abyss, it is the rare demon that would take particular harm from the element. One notable exception being Kor'a'Fell, "The Sun Setter". For this particular demon, it is well-documented that using Light magic against him heals him rather than harms him. He can devour celestials and their might to fuel his own power and strength, something most demons cannot do as they are harmed by the light that infuses Celestial bodies. Ultimately, however, these adaptations mean he is harmed by Darkness magics as much as he is silver.

Topic: what have been some of Mehs' most notable victories or defeated foes that might be bound in his mirrors?
column5: The most well-known souls trapped in Mehs' mirrors are those of three celestial lieutenants of Clarius (Harbingers of the Crescent Sun). Their names are difficult to find, but with some effort you can discover them: Aquila, Sothis, and and Delphine.

Topic: how do the Drir build their catacombs?
column5: The building of Drir Catacombs is something that you would need an Engineer with the Inventor skill to discover. As this would be a story-approved recipe rather than one needed to be pitched to the Rules team, it can be submitted as an open-ended research action to obtain the blue recipe tag. To account for this action you have done, I will bank this particular action to count towards the tag, even though you don't have the necessary skills to do so.

what do regular Cestral fishermen think about mermaids?

Topic: are there other ways to create black sands than the sacred urn?
column5: While black sand can be gathered from the shores of Eden's Realm, this sand is not empowered as the ritual component Black Sands until it has been placed within the Sacred Urn for an extended period of time.

column5: fae and dreaming are opposed, with dreaming being the mind and memory, so fae are innately more fleeting. in the way dreamers use tethers, fae often use tethers and tokens of remembrance as a way to remember things of note. bowl was used to "forget" things/cleansing the bad memories. Grim is the keeper of the bowl

Topic: is there a correlation between the 12 participats of the clocktower games and the zodiac, or is it mere coincidence that Vesh and Morcin participated and now share the the zodiac pantheon? ALEXANDER BONUS TAG
column5: There does not seem to be any evidence correlating the majority of the Trahazi Zodiac with participants of the Clocktower as many of the Zodiac have long since been gods and thus could not have participated. Deep, deep in the recesses of Alexander and notes about the Clocktower and godhood, in a handwriting you recognize, exists a list with the names Morcin and Vesh, who are joined by Euryale, Belisarius, Kitar, Saerinie, and Styx.

Topic: Political Insight: Radolond
column5: Unlike the Citadel Houses, Radolond does not ascribe to a hierarchy system of numbering their leadership. Rather, they are governed by a Council of Elders who are chosen by the tribe as positions are vacated. While generally having positive relations with the Citadel Houses, as their main task is as advisors and mediators between the two Houses with conflicting approaches to matters, such relations were put into jeopardy in 9ar when Phyraeon, the First of Delfestrae, sought information the Council of Radolond was unwilling to give. Knowing he would kill any or all of them to get this information, the Council sent the rest of the tribe away while he demanded they tell him what they had foreseen. Given their ongoing refusal, he killed not only the members of the Council but a number of their other prominent leaders. In the following years, with a full new Council and swath of leaders forced to step up to fill the great void left in their core, the Radolond have confirmed time and time again their neutrality and separation from the others, which has only distanced relations with the Citadel even further. Though they allowed refugees from the Citadel into their lands after Harvester's incident at the Citadel, they have generally drawn back from mediating conflicts between the two Houses, leading to strained relations within the Citadel itself between Kaelin and Delfestrae. While Radolond has regualarly asserted they will not bend knee to any House, as it is not their place to do so as a neutral tribe, they have however, strengthened relations with the Rakuten and Svivore by sharing information and resources with the other Endurant Tribes, and Palauttaa has promised the Radolond military aid should they ever be put in a position to need so.

Topic: Political Insight: Drir of Tear
column5: The Drir located on the isle of Tear are dominantly those of the Mortichyte who fled to Tear through Solace's portal system during the Blood Moon War. Made up almost entirely of their elderly, injured, and children too young to fight the war, the Council of Solace welcomed the refugees into their lands and provided them land to live by their own traditions in Solace's deepest swamps. With a strong alliance with the Returned and providing the construction of portals for use in the War of Radiance, along with membership on the Unity Parliament, the Drir had little want as they had easy trade access with the Returned and merchants traveling along the road between Solace and Port Frey. Ten years on, most of the children are nearly grown and some of the Mortichyte have chosen to return to their home islands, but with extensive deaths in their home islands during the Blood Moon War that cost many their families, some have chosen to remain on Tear as they have built new lives and have little to return to in the Drir islands.

Topic: Military Insight: Ardent Choir
column5: As the Ardent Choir is not a military force, simply a cult made of up peasantry, it is not possible to get a sense of their military strength as they do not appear to have any.

Topic: Investigate History: Coins of the Damned
column5: Coins of the Damned are a colloquial term used to refer to a currency that can be used to barter with the Ferrymen upon death. They were never created intentionally, but rather came about purely by accidental happenstance, and yet still carry a great weight of power and influence in the circles of death. At the fall of Theddespari, as coins and supplies were being moved with the Queen to evacuate the city, crates were open and being packed hastily. A number of rituals were happening that night, and in particular a ritual occurred which imbued one of her strongest soldiers, Leucraven, with the power to overcome death. As part of the ritual, he began to bleed heavily and his blood splattered on some of the coins. Because of the ritual, Leucraven's blood was divinely-infused with Eden's power and influence over death and so with it upon the coins, they became a new form a currency - the Coins of the Damned. They are a finite resource which were created in that singular moment, but there is no denying the power and influence they still have, particularly among the Returned.

Military Insight: Celestine Empire
column5: The military of the Celestine Empire is divided into 8 legions, each of which began historically with 5 divisions. - Legion of Esparei: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Annabelle Grace Celestine. Approximate 10,000 soldiers and 200 knights, stationed in West Riel. - The Legion of Eon: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Acantha. The 2nd and 4th Divisions were destroyed during the Northern War. Approximately 2100 soldiers and 25 knights. Stationed at Eon Fortress. - The Legion of Daece: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Valeria. Approximate 3500 soldiers and 200 knights. Stationed at Dae Fortress. - The Legion of Mentor: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Thaelia. Approximately 12,000 soldiers and 200 knights. Stationed at Mentor Fortress and currently fighting demons near Castle Thorn.   - The Legion of Aer: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Synallia. Approximately 3000 soldiers and 80 knights. Stationed at Aer Fortress.  - The Legion of Eliena: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Bale. The 5th Division was lost alongside the Legion of Destiny during the Northern War. Approximately 6,000 soldiers and 175 knights. Stationed in Eliena and Arei.  - The Legion of Destiny: Formerly 1st-5th Divisions. The entire Legion was lost during the Northern War. - The Legion of Lisra: 1st-5th Divisions under the command of Maiden Camilla. The 4th Division was lost during the Battle of Port Frey at the end of the War of Radiance. Approximately 7,700 soliders and 225 knights. Formerly stationed in Lisra until the Northern War Treaty required their relocation. Currently garrisoned in West Riel and on Tear. Only Maiden Thaelia and the Legion of Mentor are actively engaged in conflict at this time.

The Effendal of Tear are no strangers to wanting him out of the way for good with the hope it would take out the last of the Church with him, but the Empress Nadine is close on their heels with wishing him ill and preferring him dead so he cannot raise another army on her northern border. She killed the last Voice, surely a literal child with a minimal military force and no fortress to cower wouldn't be too difficult to overcome!

Topic: Investigate History: the Broken Tower in Paradox (has lore Thedd history)
column5: Tumbling end over end, the remains of the upper half of a mage’s tower forever drifts through the skies of Paradox, moving fast enough to leave a scattered trail of masonry and grass-covered “mini-islands” in its wake. Some of those secondary islands seem large enough to have the ruins of stone structures on them, but it is hard to get close enough to tell for sure… for the Tower itself occasionally shoots meteors of wild magic through the surrounding countryside of Dawn, causing all sorts of curiosities to manifest.  (Sometimes those effects are not so much “curiosities” as “explosions.”) While the protective magic of the realm prevents outright death as a result of this chaotic legacy, serious accidents can still occur. Unlike the other sky islands, the Broken Tower flies on an extremely erratic orbit, often crossing over the line of the Suncrest Mountains or unexpectedly showing itself in silhouette against the moon as it crosses over the Boundary from Night. It is said that tomes containing the most powerful and truly dark magics can be found in The Broken Tower, and that the mages that reside within are often hostile, and they robe themselves in twisted and torn black vestments that help mask their shrieking, horrifying faces.  It is said that the broken tower found its way to Paradox during the fall of Theddespari, when fae magic was being thrown around it to a dangerous degree and entangled with the great powers of the Ko'att. Accounts from that day are rare, only whispered about by the children who survived the night, but it is rumored that a rogue blast of magic struck the tower, and when one of the fae fled the battle into Paradox, the tower attached itself to the fae and hitched a ride across the planes. Rumor has it, the mages within the tower were once members of the Theddespari mages, but while lost in their own right after living in Paradox for countless hundreds of years, they may not be quite as lost as the mages who stayed behind.

Topic: Investigate Rumors: Broken Tower in Paradox
column5: - Rumor has it, the fae who brought the Broken Tower to Paradox did so to save their human lover from certain death at the fall of Theddespari. - Rumor has it, anyone who approaches the Broken Tower will be blasted from the sky. - Rumor has it, the mages of the Broken Tower are trying to find a way to return their tower to its base and rebuild Theddespari. - Rumor has it, the lost tomes of Verody have been collected in the Broken Tower. (Crystal note: this is ONLY a rumor, do not pursue it please) - Rumor has it, the mages of Theddespari can cross from Adelrune to Paradox and back simply by climbing or descending the stairs of the Broken Tower.

Topic: Military Insight: Southern Vicaul
column5: The Southern Vicaul kings - Vidar, Egil Scylvol, and Iver Esturman - took a range of losses from the star falling upon their coastlines, not only to their military but to their territories as a whole as great floods swept up the canals and channels, overwhelming the waterways.  - King Vidar, after losing half his forces and ships, stands with approximately 6100 warriors and 27 functional ships, with 20 additional ships that are damaged but salvageable. - King Egil Scylvol was killed in the flooding, along with half his warriors and nearly 2/3 of their fleet were lost. His son, now King Godrodar Scylvol, has taken command of the region and their approximately 2700 remaining warriors and 12 ships, with the hope of recovering 10 more heavily damaged ships. - Not in the direct path of the torrential waves or star itself, King Iver Esturman was relatively lucky, losing only 500 warriors and half his ships in the flooding. He is left with approximately 2600 warriors and 26 ships, as well as 15 damaged ships. (Note: as this was Military Insight it will not provide any indication of civilian, infrastructure, or economic losses)

There are seemingly no records at all of celestials coming back to Adelrune after falling and being sent back to the Golden Realms.

Topic: Investigate History: can fallen celestials be rehabilitated?

From the records you can find, a celestial can potentially be pulled back when such effects like ataxia are starting to show, or they are beginning the first steps towards falling (usually by redirecting their attention against a demon squad and reminding them of their purpose to Adelrune), but once a celestial is fallen they cannot be treated for such a condition on Adelrune.

Topic: Research: What would happen if a ferryman is unbound from service to a god? Do they die? Do they persist as a freelance ferryman?
column5: A ferryman's attachment to a particular god is typically part of the creation or pact that creates a ferryman in the first place. As such, the breaking of such bonds can vary in response dependent on their particular pact. In the recent case of the Infernal, his pact is not necessarily broken and thus he should not suffer lasting effects - rather, he gained divine power, which serves as a promotion of sorts, and while it places him above needing to do the tasks of a ferrying souls, it does not implicitly break his connection to Eden. With this knowledge, one could expect that the Infernal will continue to work with Eden in some regard, though now it will be of his own free will because he is no longer bound to do her bidding. Ultimately, however, it depends on the nature of the ferryman and the pact that created them, as some ferrymen are created without a binding to a god, while others become ferrymen by making agreements, pacts, or oaths to serve a particular god in perpetuity and thus it would be up to the god to choose the punishment or effect of the breaking of such a pact.

Topic: Research: can we store the urn in the Death Realm until we have the proper ritual? Can we make a deal with the Death Knights to protect the Returned?
column5: Artifacts cannot simply be sent off to the death realms, as they are rooted on Adelrune and will remain here unless carried directly through a rift to the Spirit Plane, or carried by a being of death such as a ferryman. Furthermore, since the Urn was created by the Theddespari Mages to defy death, it is unlikely that Eden would allow such a blasphemous creation to exist at all within the Spirit Plane, and theoretically would send her servants to destroy it.  Any dealings with Death Knights would need to be asked of them, not found in a book in a library.

Topic: Research: how does a Celestial could possibly escape the citadel, besides how Harvester released everyone
column5: As a general rule, anything bound can fight against its bindings. Very few prisons are fool-proof, and the Citadel certainly is not. There are records over the years of a celestial breaking in to the Citadel to break one of their companions out of its prison; of Nadine infiltrating the Citadel painted with effendal blood to open numerous cells and let out not only celestials but demons to cause chaos during Towerfall; even Effendal themselves setting free those who they grew to build a rapport with over the years while on patrol in the cells.

Topic: Research: legends of Hawk
Research: regarding the Sacred Urn - magically, what has changed about the urn now that it is full of charged black sand? how long does it stay charged? can more be added now that it is full?
column5: Nothing magically has changed in regards to the Sacred Urn itself, simply the state of the Black Sand within it going from uncharged to charged over time. You can remove the sand and it will not lose it potency, but more cannot be added to an already full Urn.

Topic: Research: What is the cultural, historical, and religious significance of Klisknin Fortress?
column5: Klisknin Fortress, in the most remote part of the Veinish mountains, is an ancient settlement overseeing the mining operation of the only true silver mine in Vein. Klisknin Fortress has been operational for over 300 years, with the many gateways to the mineshafts beginning within the protections of its walls so those who live and work there are able to stay within the safety and shelter of the fortress against the inclimate weather beyond. At Klisknin, silver is processed from raw ore into consistantly-sized ingots that are exported in shipments across not only the Kingdom of Vein but throughout the former-Amalgamation, as well as historically to the Ko'att and Vicaul.

Topic: Research: when a fae dies, where do their aspects go? are they absorbed by other fae? is a new fae made? how does it affect those who are tied to him via oath?
column5: Deep truths regarding the metaphysics of extraplanar beings who predate time are always a difficult thing to know with any degree of certainty. What is commonly believed and theorized is that when a fae dies, that aspect of reality dies with it. There is not a single fae who controls all of an aspect, so for example if a fae of trees dies, that does not mean all trees disappear - it is believed that a similar fae or one with complementary aspect can pick it up to fill in the gaps. Such as when the tree fae dies, it is probable that a bush fae may pick up trees, because what is a tree if not a bush on a trunk? Others suggest that the death of a fae is more like a single stitch breaking in a great quilt, where the tension holding the quilt together would shift to its neighboring stitches, like similar fae within the same sphere of influence. This also holds true for many oaths and bindings made to fae - it is entirely probable that even if a fae ceases to exist, the oath simply transfers to a neighboring fae with a similar interest or intention.

Topic: Research: NEEDS NEW TOPIC
Research: any history of a fallen celestial being redeemed or fixed without returning them to the Golden Realms?
column5: While there are records of many celestials who were on the verge of falling being redeemed and brought back from the brink of disaster by their dedicated celestial siblings, there is no record of a fully fallen celestial being redeemed or having their course corrected on Adelrune. This practice always seems to require being sent back to the Golden Realms for judgment.

Topic: Research: Historically, has Castle Thorn or other places fought light demons before and did they have any successful tactics to defeat them? If the Castle has fought light demons before, what defenses do they have now against them?
column5: Light demons are believed to be exceedingly rare, as light is not an element typically associated with creatures from the Abyss. Typically, great rituals have to be performed to create or adapt a demon to be able to absorb light. Most often, however, creatures called "light demons" are not actually demons of light - they are mostly fire demons with some particular aspects or abilities that can be misrepresented as "light". In searching written archives, nearly all references to "light demons" or "sun demons" are actually actually about celestials rather than these particularly created creatures. As with all demons, however, silver will always be effective against them because they are still in their origin and nature, demons.

Topic: Research: what all can Soul Claimer's Veil actually do? who can wear it?
column5: Soul Claimer's Veil blocks all divination and scrying used against the wearer. Additionally, it is Soulbound to the wearer to who follows her faith and provides the wearer with 2 additional uses of Prayer each event. However, it also requires the wearer to make three oaths about the things they are most dedicated to. These oaths must be sent to and approved by Crystal, and if they are broken, the wearer immediately enters death counts and cannot be resurrected. When the wearer Returns, their soulbond to the veil is broken and cannot be renewed.

Topic: Research: Who are the founding members of House Calae? Initial rise as a major house?
column5: The names of the founding members of House Calae are long lost to history because they originated as simple merchants in the Celestine Kingdom and went unrecorded as most peasants of Adelrune. Unhappy with their situation, the Calae merchants banded together with the other wealthy merchant families of the kingdom who were unable to gain nobility in the kingdom and instead sought out land to found their own nation where they could call themselves nobles and have more privledge than the merchant class did in the Celestine kingdom. As one of the founding families of Dace, House Calae asserted itself as a major House and has held to that position consistantly over the last 250 years with little chance of being unseated due to their strong control over the alchemy and herb trade of Adelrune.

Topic: Research: magical properties of blood ink? does it have other applications than scroll scribing? how is it made?
column5: Blood Ink is simply a necessary component for creating magical scrolls to weave the magic within it and allow a mage to become attuned to it and allow them to cast the spell in question. It is sometimes used as a ritual component in various rituals that require writing, though often Aether Ink serves a similar purpose for more potent rituals connected to divinity. The recipe for Blood Ink is a highly protected secret held by academic institutions or governments, though few are capable of producing enough to act as a trade good or export. Much of the Blood Ink in current circulation comes from the Effendal House Delfestrae, though prior to the Dark Mists of Myre, Mandala was the most common source of it in the world.

Topic: Research: neither demons nor celestials go to the spirit realm upon death, yet they refer to their war as a spiritual one. in what way does their conflict involve the spirit realm?
column5: Spiritual in this sense is less in regards to a literal spirit, and more a poetic use of the term when referencing their eternal, vast separation of purposes since such things as virtues and vices are not tangible as a body would be, they are "spiritual".

Topic: Research: Black Sands (Thedd vs. Eden vs. Nightmare) and their metaphysical importance
column5: Black sand from Nightmares are simply a different variation of Dream Sand and would be used in the way other dream sands might be. The black sand from the shores of the river in Eden's realm are simply that - black sand with no real property to it. It isn't until this sand from Eden's Realm is placed within the Sacred Urn for a time that it becomes "charged" and is called Black Sands, a potent ritual component. Beyond the use of them for rituals and such things, there is no *metaphysical* importance to them.

Topic: Research: did Myre acquire the recipe for Blood Ink from Mandala and/or were they able to manufacture it themselves?
column5: It is unclear if Myre has the recipe for Blood Ink, however, as with many governements across the world and the former government of Ad Decimum, having the recipe does not mean one has the materials required to create Blood Ink on any amount of mass scale. While Mandala was the main source of Blood Ink for Adelrune before the Dark Mists of Myre spread, there has never seemed to be any abundence of it within Myre.

Topic: Research: Aquila, the celestial lieutenant of Clarius that is trapped in one of Mehs' mirrors
column5: harbingers bound spirits to Mehs, many escaped but for those trapped by the old magic. good guy players will need to strengthen the bindings to keep them trapped. Celestials need willing possession.TALK TO HOWARD OR CLARKE

Topic: Research: given we would need to look back in time to learn the Theddespari ritual, and we have previously seen the past of Theddespari in the Dreaming, what actions would be advisable to view this specific past moment from inside the city?
column5: As the fall of Theddespari in the dreaming was shared with the Returned by Ophidaeus, the Dream Naga, as the story of their own creation, it was not necessarily full truth of what happened that night and had limited scope in what could be shared in that Dream. If you wanted to know exactly what happened in that room with the Theddespari and the ritual, the only place that knowledge was ever held is in the long-broken minds of those who were in the room when it happened. Given the state of the Theddespari Mages, it is extremely unlikely that you will ever be able to discover the truth of the ritual that was performed that night.  ((Please Note: the story team has chosen not to allow pursuance of this topic at this time. If that ever changes, we will let you know, but any additional actions taken towards this subject will be refunded for the foreseeable future))

Topic: Research: is there a lore specific to doorways/gates/portals/rifts? who are non-drir folks we should contact for more information?
column5: Who to contact is more of an influence spend rather than a research question, as it has to do with living people rather than what is written about in texts.  There is not a specific lore or topic to study things such as portals and rifts, but rather a variety of different topics crossover to do so. This could be specialty such as Planes, Metaphysics, Summoning, Ferrymen, any extraplanar beings, Hedge Gates, or Mists, among a variety of others.

Topic: Research: is there a safe procedure to attach a dead celestial body part to a living person?
column5: While there has been extensive experimentation with transplants of extraplanar body parts into mortal bodies, a curious situation continues to happen time and time again when attempting to do such practices with body parts of celestials. There is trouble in the transplantation process in which adding the celestial body part to another body corrupts the celestial body in the process, rendering it "inert" in the sense that it no longer carries the touch of the Golden Realms as it did before being added to the new body. The Stitcher's Guild has done the most extensive research into such practices, but to date there has never been a successful transplanted celestial body part into a mortal race.

Topic: Research: tell me something I don't know
column5: The Churchwood forest is quite large, spanning the majority of the Kingdom of Hastings and an extensive portion of the Kingdom of Vein (particularly in the Vercruxian lowlands but also up into the mountains), as well as extending into the former Kingdoms of De'Fey and Voi.

Topic: Research: how to better use divination or summoning Detect Possession to detect trace demon possession? what are the signs of possession?
column5: There is no publicly known way to detect trace demonic possession, nor residual possession after the fact. The scope of Detect Possession is restricted to possession that is currently active, or identifying tethers themselves through other divination and summoning means. In general, there are no overt outward signs of possession, as it is typically the behavior of the possessed that will cause those around the possessed to note something is amiss. This simple fact is why the War of Faces was so catastrophically debilitating for Adelrune, and why seeking out shapeshifters and deep-rooted possessions is the specialty and purview of the White Chalice.

Topic: Research: The Marrer Sword - how to we strengthen the blade to remove it's fragile quality? can a glyph or ritual be used to protect and strengthen it?
column5: When the Marrer Sword was first forged, as with most artifacts, it was particularly durable and resistant to breaking under most mundane methods. However, in creating a weapon of such power, that strength can still dwindle over time as it is used for particularly strong maneuvers such as binding a greater demon to a physical location. At this point, while the Marrer Sword is still capable of being used, it's strength has dwindled as it has taken ongoing damage throughout the centuries. As with most artifacts, due to the specificity of it's origin and forging, it cannot be reforged or strengthened at a later time. Additionally, even reinforcement glyphs like the Glyph of Permanence cannot be added to magical weapons, as they only increase the durability of mundane items and adding additional magics will interfere with the original magic and function of the sword.

Topic: Research: in Silvernose's model of the planes, can a fae die of old age? demons? dreams?
column5: Hershel and Silvernose are two different people.  In the journal of Archmagus Hersel, there is no clear answer to these questions, they are simply questions he has posited as part of his understanding of seemingly-immortal beings who do not live in linear time as those on the mortal plane do.

Topic: Research: Hershel (aka Silvernose) has posited his theory of the planes. how was he able to prove/support this theory? how might we?
column5: Please Note: Hershel and Silvernose are two different people.  The documentation that you have found from Archmagus is simply a new theory and map of the planes. As with most divine or metaphysical aspects of the world of Adelrune, there is no true way to prove this theory.

Topic: Research: According to Archmagus Hershel (Silvernose)'s research, he posits the existence of a new elemental plane - is there any further evidence of this undiscovered plane? how might we find such evidence?
column5: Please Note: Hershel and Silvernose are two different people.  Archmagus Hersel posits the existance of a potential new elemental plane, but beyond his own notes, there is little to support the existance of such a plane as it was only so recently "discovered" by him and thus has not been studied or discussed in any depth by planar scholars to be proven or disproven beyond the theoretical existance of a previously unknown elemental force.

Topic: Research on the Heretical Journal: is there a way to identify the bloodline of the child of Chorus
column5: Outside of geneological records, there is no known or reliable method to identify or trace blood lines. Powerful blood mages do claim, however, that such identification could be done through exceptionally powerful ritual blood magic, in which a curse or mark can be placed on an entire blood line, but this of course would require blood of the one whose line is to be identified. Unfortunately, due to the need of blood for a ritual, it is unlikely this approach would be usable to identify the bloodline of Chorus.

Topic: Research: How did the Celestine Faith originate and develop from before receiving Aer's Cyphers to what we've come to know and understand it today/post receiving Aer Cyphers?
Research: what is known about the original creation of different ferryman, specifically His Eminence. Can ferrymen be destroyed or otherwise deposed of their duties
column5: You don't really have any lores that would aide you in this research, but I will give some general scholarly theories.  There are multiple different methods of creating a ferryman, the most well-known being a ritual in the Book of Ig, but it is theorized that something like making a deal with a death god (or perhaps any god of sufficient power) could also make a ferryman as they are indebted to work for said death god for some unknown purpose. It is generally known that this is somewhat of a trap, as part of the ferryman process involves losing the memories of who they were before becoming a ferryman, so they would not know how or why they became a ferryman. As for being destroyed - even gods can be killed and destroyed, so it stands to reason that a ferryman can be too.

Research: what is the general history of the Moonmoss-Vercrux academy of alchemy and its mission, tenants, and specializations?
column5: The Moonmoss-Vercrux Academy of Alchemy is highly secretive and not generally written about in texts beyond the occasional war report or journal that acknowledges the appearance of a neutral party on the field of battle - that of a healer or two who is there simply to treat the wounded regardless of what side of the conflict they fall on.

Topic: Research: Will the Princess weaken if her followers are killed?
column5: Yes, it is likely that the Princess of the Night will weaken dramatically if her followers on Adelrune are killed in significant numbers. As gods are intended to remain in their own realm and off of Adelrune, most are reliant on their followers to take actions on their behalf on the mortal plane. Without her leading priests and most devout followers serving her will, it is unlikely that she will continue to expand her power as she has in the last decade among the Returned and non-Returned of Port Frey. It is entirely possible, however, that even though the Blessed were "defeated" by exorcising the Zealot from his Returned Host and cracking the physical tether ritual, that he simply found another host body that was soul-marked by the Blessed and continues to act on her behalf in or around Port Frey.

Topic: Research: what is the history of the Broken Tears?
column5: While it is not documented extensively, the Broken Tears have long served as a haven for those with something to hide. The channels between the various islands (some of which are little more than large glorified rocks) vary in depths and widths, but are generally quite shallow and extremely dangerous to maneuver full sized cargo ships through. While there are a number of hidden or protected bays in the area, they are difficult to find without knowing the exact passage to get there at the right tide, or else you run the strong risk of running aground. In the late Age of Tane and early Age of Chorus they were most often used by smugglers and pirates looking to comandeer trade ships headed to Kotu. Of particular note in this era, Captain Odessa Atherton was known to use the Broken Tears to both lure ships into a trap where they can be captured, or otherwise sieze another vessel and direct it purposely into the rocks. With hundreds of sea caves in the area, there have always been rumors about sea monster breeding grounds and growing among the islands until they are large enough to make their way out into the wider ocean. The history of the Broken Tears, like Tear itself, is dominantly unknown after the Mists rolled over the islands in 16ch, at least until they opened up once more at the very end of the Age of Chorus. Bandits and smugglers once more made the Broken Tears their home, sneaking onto Tear to gather important components and magical artifacts before fleeing the Effendal who were land-bound to Tear itself. One even went so far as to venture out of the Broken Tears to build Port Frey as his own personal smuggler's trading post before the Effendal and the Returned got involved. In more recent years, old wrecked ships were found among the Broken Tears, as well as a fairly large settlement was founded - first inhabited by the Sorrowful, a group of cultists from the Celestine Empire who blamed all of the troubles of the Age of Adelrune on the actions of the Returned; and later the abandoned settlement was taken over by the demon C'thon Darkweaver where he has built himself quite an expanded base for his demonic forces to live and prepare for their next attack on the world. While less documented, it is likely there are still bandits and pirates lurking among the islands and hidden bays, however rumor has it a large sea monster was recently freed from a shallow lagoon and continues to linger in the area as someone keeps feeding it.

Topic: Research: has there ever been a celestial or demon that has ascended to godhood?
column5: There are many celestials and demons among the gods. In truth, scholars expect there are far more demons than celestials, simply due to the desire of demons to gain more power, while celestials tend to be content with where they are without seeking the need for more power. Many of the demon lords who once led cities of their own are considered among the demon gods - Tane, Haen, Mehs, Oll, Inexo, Zyte, and even Necayre - but even those who did not claim cities claim godhood - like Xiewe, Arasimiel, and Bellus (however briefly). The celestials are fewer but not insignificant - those among the Golden Choir, though their names are long lost to history, or the more well-known La’bord’iel or Halo'vale.

Topic: Research: how do Eden/Edenites feel about Nameless Faith taking souls and trapping them in their own death realms? Are they allies?
Research: what is the historical significance of crows/ravens to House Delfestrae?
column5: There is little significance of crows/ravens to House Delfestrae as far as general imagery or symbolism goes. That said, there is a particular black bird called Void that has been part of House Delfestrae for living memory, if not longer. More recently attached to Arlenai "the Rose of Delfestrae" before her death, he has continued to hang around and do his own thing in the aftermath. Sometimes known for delivering messages or drawing attention, there is also rumor that when the Returned phenomenon first occurred, he particularly enjoyed pecking the eyes out of Inquisitor Griswold of the Returned. He has long been believed to be a demon bound within the body of a black bird - whether raven or crow, however, the identification varies - and explains the unnaturally long life he has displayed.

Topic: Research: is there any power to the musical element of Vojin's sword? are there any other powers associated with it?
column5: The most well-known power of Vojin's sword is in the flute attachement, which was known to allow him to cast a magical musical message spell to communicate with Aeris during the Crusades. While it is known in legend and stories that the sword had many other powers, without the sword in hand to study it is difficult to determine any abilities with certainty - rumors about it include the ability to summon a giant shadow lizard, stealing the souls of those it kills to trap in the blade and use their powers, or even teleportation.

Topic: Research: historical records from Castle Thorn of demonic fleets and how the were previously defeated.
column5: Demonic fleets are not a common occurance as the Beach has few ports along it's rocky coasts - truly the only one is the many merchant ships of Inexo, which until recently in the War of Ten Candles, did not act as a unified fleet. In truth, the largest exodus of demons via ship happened during the War of Radiance when a great number of demons took to ships to go to Tear to support Bel'e'athru in his great battle. While they took their own merchant ships, as well as as the ships of Dace, many also took smaller vessels that were not particularly well-made for trans-ocean voyages. As it was not known that Dace had sided with Bel'e'athru at the time, and many of the demon ships were flying Dacian flags to pass by unaccosted, Castle Thorn did not engage with them as they passed through the strait. While Castle Thorn could have certainly used their siege weaponry that is typically directed to Castle Tane to take out various ships, an unknown mage instead drummed up a massive hurricane in the Strait of Roses and around Demenos Island which sunk the smaller and less robust ships to prevent them from crossing the Torrent for the Battle of Port Frey. Castle Thorn is not particularly equipped to handle fleets of ships unless they are known demon ships that are passing directly through the Strait beneath Castle Thorn.

Topic: Research: history and importance of the Invictus sword?
column5: [All of the information found in Port Frey related to the subject of the Invictus sword appear to be papers left behind by Juniox Invictus, along with small scraps of ancient mining records. Below is the longest of the reports that Lord Juniox has written in relation to the weapon.]  “The history of the Invictus sword is tied to a woman whose past has been greatly overshadowed. Its origins trace back to the age of Tane, when the notorious Lady Civar Invictus, also known for the title “ The Master of a Thousand Swords”, forged what would unknowingly become the legendary weapon of House Invictus.   Reclusive in her life, Civar was known for taking a radical approach and turned the whole mining operation at Dead Man's Claim in a direction that has not been seen since. Within her territory, she forged many weapons in her obsession to create the perfect blade.  Unfortunately, little information can be found detailing her personal life, as she never left the mountain in which she was stationed since she took over the mining operation at the age of 18.   What information could be collected however,  is documentation on the weapons she made.  Her moniker, “Master of A Thousand Swords” is not only true, but there is evidence so suggest that the sheer amount of weapons created surpassed a thousand. The records show that her forge mastery abruptly ended with a weapon she considered so perfect, that nothing she could create going forward could compare.”  Other papers written from the miners of Dead Man’s Claim referenced her as the Mistress of the Mine, obsessed with collecting the blood of the unforgivable so that she may sate the hunger of a blade that longs to bleed the living.   There is no record of her death, only an ambiguous disappearance, in which to this day there seems to be no indication on Lady Civars death or possible desertion. Notably, Civar operated the mine for a very long period of time. The date of her disappearance may noy be exact but my calculation lead me to believe that the period in which she controlled the operation spanned nearly a century. An impressive timeframe given the blacked snow and billowing smoke of the mines often leads many to a shorter lifespan. In either case the mines were transferred to another noble to manage, and the weapons were collected to either be distributed amongst soldiers of the 3rd legion, or cataloged in the catacombs of Castle Invictus.   For there Civar’s sword stayed in collection. It wasn’t until Lord Eink ‘The Bloody Menace’  Invictus, a skilled Tribune in his time, was seen using a blade sharing a similar description to Lady Civar’s final weapon.   Tribune Eink had a long violent career, and eventually worked his way to head of house Invictus. From there the sword was passed down through the generations to the sons that would inherit the title of Praetor or Head of House.   The Sword of Invictus has long since been wielded in many battles by the men who champion the third legion. However the impressive craftsmanship that it took to forge such a fine blade would not exist if not for Lady Civar Invictus. A woman whose story has been untold for centuries.”

Topic: Research: what happened to General Bryndon Bendriven's hammer?
column5: As the Crusades were over 200 years ago, there is no record of actual witnesses to the final ritual that went off that day. It is well-known that the hammer was with General Bendriven on the isthmus when the explosive ritual went off, and so all speculation since that day has been that it fell into the strait along with General Bendriven and the last of the forces of Antioc. Although there have been many attempts to search the strait and the sea over the years to recover the hammer or other famous artifacts from that battle, none have been successful in finding it.

Topic: Research: how did they (the Myrens) make these cultures (mandala/ad dec) vanish from the timeline? can we prevent this from happening again?
column5: The Myrens did not make any cultures vanish from the timeline. At some point, before the original fall of Myre, the prince of Myre made an agreement with the fae Ruin to bring Myre back in the future. Mandala still existed, the Gael still existed, Ad Decimum still existed and built its city on the foundations of the destroyed city of Myre. In the late summer of 13ar, the fae Ruin made a great wish powered by divine magic to allow Myre to rise from its ancient foundations - which Ad Decimum had built on top of. Under the Dark Mists which overtook the region, Myre rose from its ancient foundations and incited the Age of Myre - a period of 200 years in which the Smiling Prince and the Mad Kings of Myre convinced Decimals to come to their side and join the recently returned Myrens who had been safely sequestered in the Twixt & Twain since the initial deal with Ruin was struck. Over that period, those of Myre conquered Ad Decimum, then destroyed Mandala, and finally destroyed the Gael, so when the region emerged from the Dark Mists early in 14ar, these cultures had been conquered and/or destroyed, though they existed in all current memories until the Mists fell across Adelrune in 11ar. In a theoretical sense, scholars do not believe such a moment would ever happen again, but it is difficult to say if such an extraordinary turn of events could potentially find a way to occur once more.

Topic: Research: what is a desert and where does the term desert come from or how can I find where the term originates?
column5: Although you do not have lores that would help you with this question, I'm going to go ahead and answer it.  The term 'desert' when referring to a geographic region such as the Southern Expanse only developed in the last century or so. Formerly, when the Southern Expanse was lush and green before it was invaded by demons, it was what one would consider a rainforest. There was no where else on Adelrune that was so devoid of life that could even be compared to the modern day Breach, and so there was no need for a word to describe the vast wasteland it would become. Many refer to the landscape of the Southern Expanse as a wasteland now, but in some circles a different word developed - desert, which was formerly used by the Nadine Empire to describe someone who abandoned their assigned post and left their spot vacant, empty, and unattended. As the Nadine Empire was one of the few nations that traded regularly with the city of Inexo, their soldiers and merchants were not uncommon visitors, and in time the term desert began to be used to refer to the vast emptiness of the interior valley of the Breach where in addition to the vast wastelands, deep sand has gathered and pooled in some parts. That region is particularly known to be empty and unattended, with the demon armies mostly circled around their cities and water sources, it is only the brave or desperate few who venture through such desolate areas as the desert.

Topic: Research: where is the staff of Belisarius and how did it get there? who holds it?
column5: This is not information that is written about in texts within a library, as Belisarius was seen wielding his staff only a few years ago before his disappearance and presumed death in the Dark Mists of Myre.

Research: how can one repair the book of Ig?
column5: The Book of Ig was unique in that it was not simply a book of rituals copied down by hand, but each ritual was a spirit and soul bound to the book. Following the destruction of the Book of Ig, Captain Esdeline sacrificed her body to act as a casing to collect and retain all the personalities and memories of Ig. In doing so, she was no longer capable of walking on Adelrune among the Returned, but it is theoretically possible that her spirit can still be reached out to and perhaps provide knowledge or information that is sought from within the pages that were once Ig.
