Research: after the Nadine descecrated the Chorus shrine a ritual was put on the graveyard preventing the raising of undead/kill/repel undead. I wish to research a way to recreate or find a similar ritual
column5: Firstly, broad assumptions are made here that are incorrect.  The White Chalice, as requested and assisted by Sir Zacharius Volk, cast a ritual upon the graveyard, providing it a spirit guardian - that of Thorin of the Black Chalice who willingly bound his soul to service - to protect the graveyard. While he was bound to the graveyard, the dead could not be raised, and any undead who crossed onto the consecrated land were destroyed. During Towerfall, the Nadine desecrated the graveyard and broke this ritual, releasing Thorin's spirit from his task, and allowing them to raise all that were buried there as undead. In the aftermath of Towerfall and the Siege of Port Frey, the faithful of Chorus reconcecrated the land to Chorus, but could not put a new spirit guardian in place without the assistance of the White Chalice meaning there was nothing protecting the graveyard from raising the bodies buried there as undead.

Topic: Research: the former kingdom of De'Fey was known as a kingdom of subterfuge and spycraft. when did that come to be? Is there any form of intelligence or diplomacy that may add or subtract from that reputation?
Research: with Mehs in the Abyss, what is his influence on Adelrune? is he still bound to Mehs' City? can he still control portals?
column5: Please note: A research action only covers one question or topic, and you've submitted three different ones.  This information is impossible to find within written texts in a library, as it is something that only just happened and would be theoretical work at best. Many of the great demons hold influence and dominion over Adelrune from the Abyss - particularly if they possess the regalia with them. During the Age of Tane, Bel'e'athru sat upon his throne and wielded power over the demon hordes of Adelrune from the safety of the Abyss. With the fact Mehs was often seen away from his island city in recent years, one has to ask if he was truly bound to the island even when he remained on Adelrune, or did his abilities with portals provide loopholes?

Topic: Research: What are the properties of Mangosteen Fruit in alchemy? it was known to be used in medicine
column5: In studying (and expending) a use of Mangosteen Fruit, you can discover that it has properties related to Healing, Rejuvenation, Purification, Spiritual, and Enhancement. With additional study (expending a second use of the tag), you are able to discover that it is useful in treating skin infections and rashes, keeping wounds clear of infection, treating dysentery, and soothing abdominal pain or distress.

Topic: Research: what is the nature of the corruption in the world tree on Tear?
column5: The Corruption of Adelrune at large is currently caused by the corruption of the Ra'e'len Blood Root, spreading through roots of trees on Tear like a fungus, herein known as the Blood Root Fungus. This corruption was spreading out from the Ra'e'len homestead slowly until it encountered a firebreak. This corruption is slowed by areas without roots (rivers, high mountain ranges) but can jump them eventually (roots reach under rivers, or through mountain passes). The corruption's spread is stopped by the ocean. This Corruption is caused by the Blood Root corruption, "infecting" these living things. An uncorrupted living thing does not quickly become a fully corrupted creature whilst in the area of corruption.  Creatures seem to have to eat/sleep/etc. in the area for weeks before becoming a fully “corrupted version” of the creature. This Corruption results from these beings being in the area of Blood Root corruption, but corrupted creatures can leave that area, and they remain corrupted. The fungus seems to spread microscopic spores this way, leaving a trace in those who consume it. This Corruption is not contagious from proximity alone -- a corrupted being doesn't infect or corrupt the beings around it simply from its presence. This full Corruption might occur if one consumed enough corrupted creatures. For now, eating only a little bit of corrupted food seems to only leave a trace of the corruption with no very noticeable impact at the current corruption level of the lands. Corrupted food and materials seem to be sourced directly from the area of corruption or from sources that were corrupted inside the area and moved out, such as a soft pelt from a deer that got corrupted inside the area of corruption, then wandered out. The corruption originating from the Blood Root seems to be demonic and entropic in nature, and theoretically if enough roots are infected, it seems logical that the whole of the World Tree will suffer from this spreading disease in its roots

Topic: Research: given the power of souls expands a god's realm, what is the nature of the soul that makes it desireable to gods?
column5: The power of souls does not necessarily *expand* a god's realm, simply that souls generally provide the god who possesses them more power. Whether that is a god now has a spirit army to fight a war between gods in the spirit realm, or a food source to feed to the great Spirit Wyrm to make it stronger, ultimately however, souls do not provide a god with an exceptional amount of power. Not anything more than prayers or similar actions do. Scholars suspect that its actually the non-death gods who benefit more from souls they get to control or possess or devour - such as demons who get to devour a human soul that has been damned or made an arrangement that their soul will go to the Abyss, which provides a direct meal of power to the demon in question.

Topic: Research: Does the Princess of Nameless Faith have any other weaknesses besides killing her followers? Do Gods have Souls, and if so, are they harder to contain/destroy?
column5: Most notations about Nameless Faith that can be found written in text are heavily redacted, torn out entirely, or are soaked in old dried blood as if the writer (or reader) had their throat slit over the book in question.  Your line of questioning, while about Nameless, is also generally god theory and so you are able to find more information on that topic than specifics about the Princess of Night. While theoretically many gods do not have particular weaknesses, especially those who are born to other gods, often specific ambitions or goals they possess can be used against them. Regarding Nameless in particular, however, the nature of the faith says that mortals themselves can obtain godhood through the practices of this faith and so it can be surmised that these "gods" might have once been mortal. And for a god that was previously mortal, they typically still suffer some of the weaknesses of their mortality - most often in how they approach their challenges or goals. While it is nearly impossible to just do away with a god who is where they are intended to be (ie. not on Adelrune where they can be attacked as any other mortal or extraplanar being in a mortal form can be), sparking an inter-god conflict or somehow influencing the other plane can certainly reach out to them. In recent years, this approach is noted to have been attempted by Yog Zura against the goddess of possession in an attempt to claim her power for his own and achieve godhood directly, except in that he failed to reach her current form before her agents were able to snatch him in retaliation for his actions taken against her.

Topic: Research: History and potential weaknesses of metal demons?
column5: Metal demons are not a common subsect of demons, and thus there are few records about them to be found. Far more common are the Stone Demons (mostly from the demon city of Oll), which metal demons are generally included under. As with all demons, however, silver is likely to be the most exploitable weakness of all demons.

Topic: Cultural Understanding: who has the Arbor of Ra'e'len?
column5: The Arbor of Ra'e'len is with the First of the House Palauttaa, the Stillness of the Dawn.

Topic: Investigate History: the Arbor of Ra'e'len?
column5: The Arbor of Ra'e'len is a mysterious artifact that few have had the opportunity to study or know the truth of, for its magics were a well-guarded secret until it disappeared with the fall of House Ra'e'len and its Perilinen. Said to be a stone-petrified branch of the World Tree, it has always been a symbol around which Ra'e'len and its tribes have rallied. When the Arbor was discovered in 8ar, both House Delfestrae and House Kaelin sought to obtain it from the Returned - for whomever bore the Arbor would have the loyalty and fealty of Svivore and Rakuten, and cement their position as the stronger of the two Effendal Houses. It found its way to the hands of a fresh, newly Returned Effendal - Palauttaa, the Stillness of the Dawn - once born (and died) unranked in House Ra'e'len in the Age of Tane, and suddenly thrust her into the role of First of an otherwise dead house.

Topic: Political Insight: Palauttaa, the First of Ra'e'len
column5: The First (and only) of House Ra'e'len, Palauttaa's political position among both the Endurant and the Citadel is tenuous at best and has been a string of bad luck since her Returning in 8ar. Foretold in prophecy to be Returning by the diviners of Radolond, she was already targeted by the three conflicting factions of House Delfestrae before she even stepped foot on Tear as a Returned - one wanted her dead, one wanted her to step in as a neutral voice between Kaelin and Delfestrae, and the third wished to push her to ascend to godhood. Thrown into the Clocktower game within a week of her Returning, it was almost sheer happenstance she was recovered by Perilinen Siili Sininen in the Terra forests and squirreled away to Port Frey to intervene in a conflict between House Kaelin and House Delfestrae over which House would lay claim to the Arbor of Ra'e'len, and her Endurant Tribes. Nearly killed by Phyraon that day, she was gifted the Arbor by the Returned and rushed out of town using both blood teleports and the ancient portals her House had once placed in Kotu while the Returned interfered with Phyraon's pursuit of her. This would prove to be a reoccuring trouble as the Clocktower games grew progressively more dangerous, leaving her little time to take leadership and establish herself with the Svivore and Rakuten as a whole. The Radolond took her in for a time and kept her hidden from scrying, a dissenting sect of Delfestrae waylaid Phyraon at every opportunity, the Returned saved her time and time again - from C'thon Darkweaver, from Phyraon, from Nicodemus, from Hakkon. Aided her in using the Tears of Espari to cage the Shrike and eliminate the Nadine Empress from the Clocktower entirely. The Returned were in Port Frey to welcome her back when the Shrike retaliated with a Tear of her own and executed Palauttaa and the Council of Ra'e'len she had built from her noble class of Svivore and Rakuten, and her other allies. They pushed her ahead of Belisarius. Convinced her not to step out of the Clocktower games when the Tower itself offered to return both House Ra'e'len and the Tribe Leneth to life as if they had never been killed. And fought for her in an honor combat against the best of the Celestine Empire. With the Clocktower done, she finally found the opportunity to bring her tribes together and be the noble responsible for them both, but even then a political struggle ensued as the tribes were not used to having a House to aide them. She built relations between the new Council of Radolond. She rebuilt the Council of Ra'e'len. And then she was hunted in and forced to flee, using the last of the divinity granted to her by the Clocktower to cloak the Ra'e'len Homestead from being discovered, even by those who knew where it was like her ancient enemies in the Ko'aat. She could not power the spell indefinitely, however, and when it ran out, the Ko'aat led the demons to her doorstep. She and a small band of Returned saved what they could that night. Important pieces and artifacts of House Ra'e'len that have given her people hope as they've been forced from their lands and south into the ancient territories of Leneth. And Palautta, who had once been a naive young channeler, has lost her patience and gentleness. She is not the pushover and vulnerable girl of the Clocktower games. She has learned from those who rallied to her and stood with her through it all. Tear is the land of House Ra'e'len.

Topic: Second Opinion: Professor - would the "tiny book of names" contain names of all demon generals?
column5: It is unlikely, as the book has been heavily used over the years and is far from complete as true names have been used and expended from within it. Theoretically, you would just need to go look at the book to check this fact.

Topic: Political Insight: Sir Rudolph Kaya
column5: Sir Rudolph Kaya has always been a popular knight amongst the Nadine Empire. Whether attending court, or amongst the battlefield, this charismatic knight stands apart from the many cold, intimidating leaders he is often in rank alongside. Sir Rudolph Kaya currently serves in the 4th legion-  First Division, alongside his squire, Feylance Decimus. One of the three knights within the division, and fighting in the frontlines in the battle against T’tocs and Melisma, Rudolph Kaya’s already well established reputation is now soaring. His popularity rises amongst the court of nobles, as well as becoming a beacon of inspiration within the legion. With the Empire's attention directly upon the battlefield, incredible pressure is put on the 4th legion to succeed after the recent devastation of Angistine Fortress.  The Empire is looking for a hero, and Rudolph Kaya answers with unwavering will and a barbed silver sword that rips apart enemy hoards, saving the division from breaking formation on several occasions. His dedication is said to be so true, that as more enemies spilled from the Etilvor ruins, Rudolph sacrificed his own land and estate in order to maintain the foothold needed to protect the enemy from spilling into the Empire.   However, even great heroes cannot stand forever, and with the complications of fighting in the Etilvor mountains begin to multiply, the first division is forced with the decision of retreating or making one final attempt to vanquish their enemy. It is rumored that the Tribune of the first division has fallen, and until a proper replacement can be chosen off of the battlefield, Sir Rudolph Kaya has taken leadership of the division. In these last moments, the responsibility of the entire division falls upon his shoulders.

Topic: Military Insight: House Kaelin
column5: With half as many tribes as House Delfestrae, and having taken significantly heavier loses in the Purges than their counterpart did, House Kaelin's whole population is barely a fraction of that which House Delfestrae can boast, and their military is similar is ratio. With approximately 2000 warriors across both House and tribes, they do have a number of specialty units that can and do step up when possible, however they have often played a more defensive and diplomatic approach in avoiding conflict with the humans of Tear. Among their specialty units are approximately 500 sorcerers and blade casters, 200 wardens, 200 demon and celestial hunters, and 200 Ritari who are capable of filling in most roles as necessary. For nearly a decade, their numbers were belayed by their alliance with neighboring King Alric Syltane and the 2000 knights of the Gold Chalice who were garrisoned at Antioc.

Topic: Military Insight: House Delfestrae
column5: House Delfestrae provides the largest Effendal military force on Tear, with specialties in surface combat and magics. With nearly 5500 warriors across the House and tribes, they specialize in sorcery, blood magic, warding, bladecasting, and assassins which gives them a broad range of maneuverability and flexibility in combat situations. The Blood Glyphs buried deep in the caverns of Tear provide further protections against large populations of invaders in a particular region - though it is said they are written to target the human population, they have never been set off to target humans specifically. In Solace, they were re-written to target demon blood when the city was overran and was likely to fall under the onslaught, while in Port Frey they were re-written to act as a general blast of concussive force which was targeted towards the invading demon armies but also caught a large swath of Amalgamation soldiers in the crossfire. The military forces of Delfestrae, often led by Ilves "the Descending Talons of Terra" have a reputation for never failing - both in successfully taking Port Frey as retaliation for the Returned killing Phyraeon, and showing up at the Citadel to bring an end to Towerfall when the Nadine sieged the city, Terra's forces have not met a challenge they could not conquer on Tear.

Topic: Spread through Song: Starfall Song (Crystal's inbox)
Investigate History: which side did the other major houses support during the Year of a Thousand Duels
column5: When most House rivalries get going the other Dacian Houses tend to stay out of it to the best of their ability. Joining a particular side is only liable to lose them business and cause dramas with the Houses who choose the other side, so in most cases, especially a longer-running situation like the Year of a Thousand Duels, the Houses tend to support both sides so they can ultimately benefit from the conflict. In the case of the Year of a Thousand Duels, House Montaige was selling information and gossip to both sides, while House Calae was raking in the financial benefit of their mercenaries being hired as impartial bodyguards and peacekeepers, and their alchemy being needed to heal up injuries in the aftermath.

Topic: Political Insight: Court Assassin Amara Lavenssa
column5: Amara Lavenzza, an impressive artist in her own right, was recruited to the position of Court Assassin only a decade ago where she has made a strong name for herself. Though she is not in the leagues of Lord Sebastian Montaige or Lady Mancini, she is still seen as both reliable as well as a sculptural genius as she favors the use of archery to place her target in a perfectly sculptural death pose, where the placement of the body and even how they will bleed is taken into account before the assassination takes place so she never needs to approach after the arrow has been released. She has long been a favorite of Queen Chiara Daecen, who collected her private sculptures before she became a Court Assassin, and who has been a dedicated patron that had a strong hand in the development and success of Amara's career. It's said that Amara is the most successful of the Court Assassins tasked with ridding the population of the King's illegitimate heirs, having taken out seven to date. Amara's success and connections allow her beloved bride to live a well-kept life as one of the Queen's ladies-in-waiting, always known for having the most beautiful gowns and new jewels while she attends parties nearly every night either in the company of or on behalf of the Queen. Though Amara is not in any line of succession, she seems to be quite content in her position and having her path paved for her so she can simply focus on her arts and doting on her bride.

Topic: Poltical Insight: C'thon Darkweaver
column5: C'thon Darkweaver is known to be extremely influential in demonic circles, in fact, he is among the most influential demons on Adelrune. With a quick tongue and dripping in charisma, he can talk nearly anyone around to his side or making moves that benefit his goals, not that they would necessarily know who they are making deals and arrangements with as he is known to take on various forms in different nations and does not always use his most public name. He is famously known for being responsible for more celestial deaths than anyone else in history, including influencing (read: causing) the creation of the Gold Chalice following his interference at Anziel's Wedding. Less in the political sphere, but scholars and those in academic circles are very well aware of him and his academic pursuits.

Topic: Investigate Loved Ones + Healthy Paranoia: The Begger King
Political Insight: Northern Vicaul post-Starfall
column5: As the Vicaul are not a unified culture with a singular military force, this spend cannot be answered accurately for a broad topic such as "Northern Vicaul". In the future, please note that actions such as Political Insight are intended for a single, specific faction.  The three northern kings - Halvar, Arvid, and Tore - have complicated relationships between them, and even more complicated feelings about the Southern Kings. Following Starfall, they have adopted a more superior attitude over the lifestyle of the Southern Vicaul - claiming that the gods punished the southerners for their practices, while favoring them and protecting their people from the disaster. Even with this in common, however, none of the three get along between one another, and tensions have only grown in recent months as an influx of southern refugees have moved north seeking new homes and shelter for the coming winter.

Topic: Investigate History: what happens to souls after death?
column5: In speaking of "souls", most scholars are speaking directly of those belonging to mortal beings such as humans and the lesser-blooded descendents of magical beings that mixed with mortal blood. It is well-known and widely accepted on Adelrune that when a mortal soul dies, the next step in its progression is to pass over to the Spirit Plane where it will be ferried to the appropriate death realm. Most commonly this is Eden's realm for she has the most power over death and the souls of the dead, unless an outside force changes their course. Such changes in course might be another god or godmark pulls a soul into a specific part of the death realms, or even another mortal being binding the soul into a soul gem before it can cross over to the Spirit Plane. In particularly traumatic deaths, a soul might remain bound to the place in which they died - these beings might manifest as ghosts or banshees. In other cases typically through dark ritual, a soul might be torn out of the Spirit Plane entirely and dragged back to Adelrune, bound to a specific purpose - these are more commonly manifesting as wraiths or other vengeful spirits. In any case, the natural progression of the mortal soul is to live a live on Adelrune and then pass over to the Spirit Plane for the remainder of its eternity. There are, as always, rumors of other paths - souls that are dedicated to great powers such as demons might get dragged into the Abyss and consumed, powerful dragons might allow a soul to be "reincarnated", or the particularly good and benevolent might be raised to celestial-hood, a fae might whisk a soul away to live in the Twixt and Twain where death cannot reach them - but there is no proof of truth to any of these claims.

Topic: Cultural Understanding: What does it mean for House Kaelin to "keep the balance" of celestials? Balance compared to what, demons? How is that linked to the nature of Adelrune in general?
column5: Keeping the balance is a phrase intended for all Effendal - not just House Kaelin - to keep Adelrune in balance in all things so that when the cycle of Ages comes to an end, the world will begin again and not fall into the Age of the Great Death where the world will ultimately come to its end and be devoured by the Great Wyrms. For House Kaelin, that means keeping the Celestials balanced against demons, but also against humanity and their own nature. Celestials that fall tip the balance towards entropy and vice, so by House Kaelin returning them to the Golden Realms, that power is reset and reinvested to the side of balance.

Topic: Investigate History: Children of Tane
column5: The "Children of Tane" are not actually the direct and immediate *children* of Tane, but simply more of a bloodline and lineage that can be traced back to Tane - often they are grandchildren, great-grandchilden, or even further removed from their primogenitor. Typically, the "Children of Tane" are extremely large demons, who are especially strong in combat or leadership roles. Such a lineage is more akin to dragon lineages rather than being a specifc subsect of demons, like fire demons or others. The "Children of" are not an organized group, and as such they do not necessarily like each other, making them more akin to legendary monsters. Tane is not the only demon with such bloodlines, many of the most powerful demons have those who claim their lineage, such as Lightbreaker and Hope Lightbringer are among the "Children of Meretrix."

Inv. History: Davidas
column5: Davidas has an illustrious history on Adelrune, while never seemingly having stepped onto Adelrune itself. Since arriving on the scene in the Age of Chorus, it is generally known among demonologists and historians that he has remained in the Abyss, but will interact with Adelrune through corrupted or possessing objects, especially masks. He has safely and comfortably pulled strings from within the Abyss, shaping the development of Adelrune through the last age with simply a well-placed murmur and possession. Particularly he led a secondary wave of trouble during the War of Faces, where he was not actively shapeshifting on Adelrune but could still influence or attempt to orchestrate the downfall of the various human nations. While it was generally believed that he had been on Adelrune for his famed fight with the celestial Aidhreal in 5ar, it is more likely he was simply possessing someone who wore a tethered mask for the entire situation, and when the body was killed and mask destroyed, that particular connection to Adelrune was severed.

Topic: Inv. History: Twisted Legion
column5: The Twisted Legion is simply the miliary forces of Inexo city, with a long-standing history but few documented engagements as most of their conflicts throughout history were between the various demon cities. Inexo's port position on the northern coast put it most closely in proximity to Castle Tane, which was the main military force the Twisted Legion engaged with over the years in various skirmishes. In more recent years, after the death of Inexo, the Twisted Legion engaged not only in the War of Radiance and sailing demon forces to Tear, but also with the Second Crusade when Unity attempted to take over the city of Inexo and destroy it, and finally in the War of Ten Candles where remnants of the Twisted Legion and Inexo's fleet sailed north to seek out one of the roots of the world tree. The remaining land combantants and soldiers of the Twisted Legion have been dispersed throughout all of the armies of the various generals engaged in the War of Ten Candles under the oversight of Mehs.

Topic: Inv. History: Stone Legion
column5: The Stone Legion are the military forces of the city of Oll. It’s generally believed that the demons of Oll were the strongest in the Breach, and his “Stone Legion,” while technically infantry troops, functioned more like walking siege weapons. The Stone Legion was most often involved with altercations with the other demon cities that were not documented, but being the demon city nearest Redemption, they were often the force that would lay siege to the city and cause a scattering. In more recent history, the Stone Legion joined with Tane to fight against Castle Thorn in the War of Radiance, and their forces have been scattered amongst many of Mehs' generals in the War of Ten Candles.

Topic: Inv. History: Davidas' Forces
column5: Davidas's forces only began to develop and expand during the Age of Chorus. Dominantly made up of Corrupter demons, Davidas' forces are spread all over the world and on nearly ever battle field representing a variety of sides. These Corrupter demons specialize in whispering temptations into the ears of the desperate, afraid, or greedy, and make deals to change the course of the future. Often this happens as they convince soldiers to switch sides, trick people into betraying their friends and allies, or promising great fortune and future for taking part in their machinations. While not the forefront demons in the War of Faces, the corrupter demons of Davidas' forces were deeply involved in a number of incidents and situations to deceive humanity and support and provide cover to their shapeshifter counterparts.

Topic: Political Insight: Maiden Nephele
column5: Age 32, Maiden Nephele is on the cusp age for allowance to become the next Queen, likely only able to do so if the next is chosen quickly. Maiden Nephele serves the nation in Arei where she dominantly oversees trade agreements and the protection and defense of overland trade routes and supply lines between the Empire and its allies of the Western Expanse Alliance. Consulting regularly with Head of Finance, Lord Rémy of Flurdaine, they work together to keep the nation financially stable and sustainable. Some years ago now, she gained herself the dubious recognition of having signed Prince Harvester's petition for Rules of Society recognition, but in doing so had all of the Celestine Empire's foodstores replenished to capacity and is recognized for saving the nation from widespread starvation under the Mists. She often works closely with Maiden Annabelle Grace, who oversees the Legion of Esparei in Arei, but does not lead a legion or a large military force herself.

Topic: Inv. History: Qualities of moonstone, why might it be used for a moonstone pendant?
column5: Moonstone is not actually stone of the moon, but simply a unique gem stone found in deep mines on Adelrune. Because the stone is a pale white or grey in color and seems to glow with an inner light, it has been given the name "moonstone," and is popular among underworld groups and those who prefer to travel and trade under the cover of night. There is no exceptional value in moonstone beyond its ability to take enchantments very well, and personal preference for the pretty stone in jewelry as it can be cut and shaped in a variety of ways.

Topic: Investigate Enemies: C'thon Darkweaver
column5: With a long-standing presence on Adelrune, C'thon Darkweaver has developed a strong reputation and extremely bitter enemies. Among his most intensely hated enemies are Arasimiel, the greater ice demon, and his son, T'tocs. On a more broad spectrum, ice demons of all strengths are opposed to him, as well as celestials in general due to his actions that led to Anziel's wedding and the fact that he has killed more celestials than anyone else in Adelrune's history.

Topic: Political Insight: The Guild of Heralds
column5: The Guild of Heralds is an international organization based in the Kingdom of Hastings, which records and tracks nobility in all Rules of Society nations. This Guild closely tracks geneology and all legitimate births within noble family lines, prepares and processes writs of nobility (and keeps identical copies on hand for comparison later), as well as tracks all heraldry and title rewards granted by the various Crowns they work with. They are on good terms with all Rules of Society nations as they are a neutral party that exists simply to assist with documentation, and became a necessary aide in dealing with the Returned phenomenon as suddenly hundreds of dead nobles were returning to Adelrune and demanding their lines of succession be reinstated and recognition of allowances and titles be renewed after long-passing on to later inheritors. It was through their tireless work that they have mostly subsided wild claims and confidence men who attempted to take advantage of various noble houses across Adelrune, and they were the first to put forth the laws of recognition and succession that govern the Returned - namely, that as Returned they have been removed from their respective lines of succession and cannot inherit any title or land they held prior to death - they would need to earn back titles and land in their new lives.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Ingrid Nairn
column5: Ingrid Nairn is most known as the successful owner of The Underroot Tribute, a pastry shop in Siegeholt that exclusively sells both sweet and savory pies and tarts. The business appears to be an orderly establishment free of conflict. Those who are new to the underworld of the Nadine Empire have the idea that she is a back alley fence for stolen goods, yet when they appear at her bakery, they are met with confusion, seeing only a well maintained storefront serving fresh pies. These individuals are given a complex order form, and gently shooed away.   Nairn herself does have several strange habits, in that she splits her time between the Nadine Empire and visiting offshore expenditures, often being away for an entire month. The self made baker also only hires the young orphans of Siegeholt, claiming she has an open heart and never the time to have children of her own. She calls the orphans her little crumpets and trains them to carry out the large number of deliveries they get throughout the city.

Topic: Follow the Money: Moldy Whale Tavern map of Myre sales ledgers
column5: The Moldy Whale Tavern, having recently changed hands to new ownership in the last year, does not keep particularly extensive records. Much of their income is noted for the sale of alcohol and food, or the rent of rooms, while their expenditures are generally noted for salaries and supplies. Of outstanding note, there is a particularly large, repeating expenditure noted for Nora Marrow of the Ash Hats for "goods supplied" since the new owners took over the Moldy Whale. There does not, however, seem to be any notation in the ledgers of the sale of any maps - Myre or otherwise.

Topic: Investigate History: what and where is the Amulet of Salamaa?
column5: Among the magical community, there are always whispers about the Amulet of Salamaa - named for the greater elemental being bound to it. While it is most often claimed to be an artifact of immense sorcerous power which can only be weilded by a Paragon Socerer, it is also theorized to be a summoning tether which can be used by a particularly skilled summoner to weild intense elemental powers. The Amulet is rumored to grant the wearer the ability to turn into a great elemental power with the capability of mass destruction, however, it has not had a confirmed sighting or use of its power in over a century. It is believed that the Amulet of Salamaa was involved in an early catastrophy in Ad Decimum when misused by the Arcane Consortium, and was rumored to have been locked away in the labyrinths beneath the city for not only its own protection, but that of everyone who lived there, only to be brought out in the most dire of circumstances. With the loss of Ad Decimum and the rising of Myre, however, the truth of this seems even more unlikely to be discovered.

Topic: Abduction: member of a dragon cult in Concerti

Topic: State of the Court: Northern Vicaul lands
column5: As the Vicaul are not a Rules of Society nation, nor a unified culture, this spend is treated more as an Investigate Rumors than a State of the Court.  - A great star hit the southern coast of the Wilds, leaving great swaths of destruction in the wake of great waves that reshaped the landscape. - The Northern Vicaul are blessed by the Gods to have been spared - The Gods have given their judgement about the lifestyle of the Southern Vicaul - Being overrun by Southerners escaping north and seeking new homes - War is coming. It cannot be avoided.

Topic: Miltiary Insight: Northern Vicaul
column5: As the Vicaul are not a unified culture with a singular military force, this spend cannot be answered accurately for a broad topic such as "Northern Vicaul".  The armies of the Northern Kings - Halvar, Arvid, and Tore - are generally smaller than those of the Southern Kings for they do not need such vast armies for raiding. Rather, their armies of warriors are typically less than 5,000 because the majority of the conflict and fighting they face is amongst the other Vicaul. These kings moreso have hunters who can double in to support the fighting forces as needed, but their focus is on feeding and supporting their smaller communities.

Topic: Investigate History: logistics of the gardening caravans of Trahazi
column5: Before they were forced onto the road, the Trahazi were known as the Hazori - those that are credited with the development of farming and domestication of crops on Adelrune. For some, they abandoned these farming practices when they took to the roads and learned new skill sets, but for some, like the Halipova and Melnyk they simply found new methods to continue growing crops while on the move. In fact, the basics of their planted beds on carts that can be pulled along is what eventually led to the development of Castle Thorn's garden boxes and rooftop farming. In fact, nearly every single one of the various wagons and carts within these two caravans are lined with potted plants and hanging baskets of rare herbs and spices collected all across Adelrune, and are grown, dried, and traded as necessary; for the people of these caravans, they are particularly busy during the planting and harvesting seasons where they take on day work for a number of farmers in whatever region they happen to find themselves in at that time.

Topic: Investigate History: logistics of the gardening ships of Dace
column5: In your investigation into the rumors of gardening ships in Dace, you discover that there is no such thing. Ornamental plant boxes beneath windows are popular though, especially when filled with rare and beautiful flowers, a farming/gardening technique that was borrowed from Castle Thorn where the ground is so devoid of life that nothing will grow. Instead, Castle Thorn employs use of its many windows in both homes and on the fortress towers, as well as the roofs of shorter structures to plant extensive gardening beds with unique growing practices that were developed by the Cornerstone Farm Collective.

column5: With Port Frey forced back into its infancy and being rebuilt up from an entirely new foundation, there will be many individuals, groups, and organizations seeking to claim land or territory in the city for their own gain and future positioning. For the three mentioned, they are not anyone of particular note or connection, which can be ascertained simply by the fact that they were looking to *purchase* the land rather than just making a grab for it or occupying it with a force. At this time, there is a great opportunity to shape the future of Port Frey and how it begins - when it was first built in the modern age, it was built by smugglers and inhabited by gangs as an underworld sanctuary and enclave - it is up to the Returned to decide who gets to have early access to the city and put down the foundations to shape its future.

Topic: Follow the Money: C'thon and Dace
column5: C'thon Darkweaver is known for preferring the luxuries and comforts of fine things, so it should not be surprising that a large portion of his expenses involve buying expensive fabrics, luxurious foods, and the finest of wines and art from Dace. He enjoys a mask and a venture into the streets of Dace in a guise, though it is unknown what he gets up to while he is there. He does not seem to draw any incomes from his rumored involvements in Dace, but he doesn't seem to need it to spend there freely.

Topic: Second Opinion: Mage - what are the effects of Moonstone jewelry?
column5: Moonstone jewelry does not have any known effects of its own, simply it is exceptional for enchanting work. It is a gemstone long connected to deities of the moon, such as Wolf and various other night-oriented dieties, and is favored in particular by the Moon Faith. Moonstone is generally considered a talisman for growth and new beginnings, and like moonlight provides in the darkest of nights, it is said to bring hidden truths to light.

Topic: Follow the Money: Atius & Gowler
column5: There is only one transaction on the books between Gowler Pert and House Atius, and that is a gift paid in kindness for two purebred canines. The ledgers note the dogs themselves are noted as highly valuable, and would normally sell at a price relative to the age and capabilities of the animal. However the two dogs, Titus and Nero, are an inseparable pair of brothers and are thus a more valuable asset, expected to sell at around 30  gold. Along with the dogs comes the confidence that they are well trained, following exact commands at the sound of a whistle specifically used by house Atius. Because House Atius ensures quality and peace of mind in their animal husbandry, Gowler was safely assured that without the exact whistle he has to control Titus and Nero, no one else will be able to command the pair.  This transaction happened nearly 2 years ago, and there haven’t been any other dealings between house Atius and Gowler Pert.

Topic: Follow the Money: Ardent Choir in Port Frey (past and present)

Abandoning their homes with little to their name, for most they only had enough money to pay for the crossing to Tear. As the Ardent Choir is not an organized group with collective funding, most of their members are working in menial labor roles in the town and spending what little money they can scrape together to pay for food and other basic necessities. The largest collective purchase made in Port Frey's history by the members of the Ardent Choir was following the death of Chorus when the demoness Mieletassa told them all that if they collected red candles and brought them to the Church that Chorus would come back to them. They used every last copper they had to buy out all of the red candles and wax in Port Frey to fill the Church of Chorus with red candles.

Investigate History: what historical examples are there of the Golden Realms being corrupted?
column5: There does not seem to be any evidence of the Golden Realms being corrupted in recorded history. However, you are able to find various theoretical works, particularly from the early Returned, who theorized that if the world fell further out of balance and the Dreaming continued to diminish that the Golden Realms and the Abyss might both collapse into the mortal plane of Adelrune. There is further notation about this theory, however, that much of the information used to build this concept or theory came from a severely biased and unreliable source as they were noted as being "a dream mage driven to madness". Other theories suggest that the corruption of the Golden Realms would push Adelrune and the planes so far out of balance that it will being about the final prophecy - in which the wyrms devour all living things and the planes in their entirety, before finally devouring themselves at the end of all things.

Topic: Political Insight: King Godrodor Scylvol
column5: King Godrodar Scylvol, having recent taken over the Scylvol from his father King Egil who was killed during the flooding caused by the Siege of Seven Stars, has been biding his time and growing his power base for well-over a decade. He is exceptionally ambitious, with grand plans for the Vicaul to establish themselves across the Adelrune, and he has long-sought to build a Vicaul outpost on Tear so the Vicaul can be within reach of all of the known world. While he is a mediocre fighter himself, he knows how to play the game of inter-kingdom politics and has built himself connections to begin to rally some of the other Kings to work with him towards this effort. His reputation, however, is strongly rooted in fear, for he is known to be cruel to prisoners, and he takes great delight in killing them in creative ways.

Topic: Political Insight: King Halvar
column5: One of the Northern Kings, King Halvar is particularly outspoken against the Southern Kings and blames their lifestyle and failure of faith for their recent suffering during the Siege of Seven Stars. He leads one of the larger fighting forces in the Wilds and as such he is no stranger to conflicts with his neighboring kings. Halvar's strong dislike of King Tore comes simply because he considers Tore a weak and ineffective leader, while his hatred of the Scylvol runs much deeper because he blames Godrodar for taking his daughters Aud and Drifa away from him. While leading only half the forces King Tore has at his call, Halvar has been chipping away at Tore's numbers for over a decade, and has called those devastated by the star's destruction to join him to gain new lands and a new life for themselves rather than rely on weak Kings who have led them astray. His rhetoric has grown more zealous in recent months, claiming to speak with the insight of the gods and their judgment of the other Kings, and he has a new advisor who stands at his side while they draw more warriors to their cause - which is believed to be a religious crusade to hold the other Kings accountable for their failings in faith.

Topic: Military Insight: Dace
column5: Dace doesn't have a true military force since they are an island nation and have no need for a proper standing army. They do, however, have a Royal Guard staffed at 5000 guards, with 1000 Noble Assassins to keep the peasantry in check, and 100 Royal Assassins to keep the nobility in check as Dace does not have specified laws beyond the ever-vague Laws of Beauty. When foot soldiers are needed, Dace has the money to hire as many mercenaries as they could possibly need. What they do have as far as a military goes, of course, is their navy - the Dacian Armada which is comprised of some 699 Ships (300 Damaged)  of varying types because War Ships need Cargo Ships to support them while at sea, and led by there most elite 41 ships of the Silver Fleet. While the Head of the Military is still Admiral Marco Ariadne, his daughter Vice-Admiral Valuca has been preparing to take over the role when she complete an assignment from the Crown - an assignment she has shirked in favor of focusing on the War of Ten Candles and bringing Dace into the conflict.

Topic: Military Insight: King Halvar
column5: A fierce enemy of King Tore and the Scylvol, King Halvar's men regularly clash with the warriors of their neighboring kings. Having escaped the devastation wrought upon the Southern Vicaul kings, Halvar's men stand strong at 4300 Well Equiped Warriors, 1070 Shieldmen, 500 Archers, and 25 ships. It seems they are gearing up for something big - he has put out invitation to the warriors of the Southern kings to turn and come to his side in exchange for lands and a more favorable and profitable position to weaken Tore and the Scylvol further than they already have been by Starfall.

Topic: Military Insight: King Arvid
column5: King Arvid "the Dragon Slayer" is a Northern King with a small territory but they hold their ground well due to the fact their Northern neighbors typically war more with one another than they give any attention to Arvid. After battling an ice dragon fiercely when the dragons woke in 10ar, King Arvid came out victorius, but only at great cost - to his warriors, to his own body and health, and to his townships. With only 2300 warriors and 13 Ships remaining, they have yet to recover from the damage done by the dragon.

Topic: Military Insight: King Tore
column5: Among the Northern Kings, King Tore's men regularly clash with those of King Halvar. Although his numbers are higher than Halvar's with 7000 Well Equiped Warriors, 80 Elite Spearmen, and 20 ships, Tore has taken more hits from Halvar than they have dealt back in recent history. King Tore has been loudly outspoken that the gods of the Old Ways allowed such devestation to hit the Southern Kings and their people because the lifestyles chosen by the Southerners has displeased the gods, and he has invited those who are truly faithful to come join him in the north.

Topic: Miltiary Insight: King Godrodor Scylvol
column5: Having recently taken over as King following his father's death during Starfall, King Godrodor Scylvol is no stranger to conflict and he has big dreams and even bigger aspirations to conquer all of Adelrune. First though, he will need to rebuild his forces and his wealth as both took heavy hits from the star colliding with the island and great waves. Down to 2700 Well Equiped Raiders, 12 intact Ships, and 10 badly damaged ships, he has a lot of work ahead of him to rebuild his forces to a level he's willing to split his forces for a full raiding season to recover their financial losses.

Topic: Political Insight: Lakeland Willowwine
column5: Lakeland Willowwine is one of the younger nephews of Lord Carwyn Willowwine, and of Lord Declan Stone by marriage. He is exceptionally skilled with contract law, and took his apprenticeship abroad with the International Merchants Guild after completing his school years at Alexander.  With the IMG, Lakeland was stationed in Concerti (Nadine Empire) for nearly a decade, arranging for trade contracts between Concerti (particularly House Umbrosus) and various Cestral noble families. A few years ago he became romantically involved with Lord Corbin Terminatus and once engaged the pair were able to travel easily between the Nadine Empire and Cestral as their work and families demanded. With their wedding date finally set for Yule 15ar, Lakeland willingly threw away a promising financial career as the expected successor to Cestral's Head of Finance in favor of marrying a fairly powerful Nadine noble. Abandoning his expected political carrer, Lakeland was more than willing to stay on as an independent merchant and contract negotiator with the IMG until his unexpected separation from Lord Terminatus in the spring of 15ar. As of the fall of this year his future is uncertain, and with the approaching date of his now-cancelled wedding, he has retreated from Cestral and returned to his work in Concerti where he has been invited as a personal guest into the home of Lady Poppaea Umbrosus.

It is far easier to navigate there during the right season, such as spring when the plane is closest to Adelrune, and/or when the mundane weather is most appropriate to traverse the Mists. Divination can certainly help time this, correctly, but any attempt to cross planes will always be extremely dangerous and highly lethal.

Topic: Research: what are the 12 arcane magics? what is the arcane clock?
column5: Rumors of the "12 arcane magics" are derived from an ancient text written by Lillian, and her model of how the magics worked together were laid out in a model often referred to as "Lillian's Star" by arcane scholars. In the last 200 years since her theory was developed, it has largely been disproven - particularly the notation and existance of 12 different magics - but it is still often studied by arcane scholars as early exploration into the subject. More commonly accepted in modern study is the model known as the Arcane Wheel, which focuses on the 9 proven magics.

Topic: Research: what makes the materials of stable alchemy able to be stable? why are potions unstable when created with mana but stable when brewed with herbs & other achemical materials?
column5: Stable Alchemy and Unstable Alchemy are inherently different, particularly in their creation. Many stable alchemists believe that "Magical Alchemy" isn't really alchemy at all because it doesn't rely on components or their properties, while unstable alchemists often argue the opposite saying stable alchemy is just trial-and-error so they don't really need to know what they're doing. Stable alchemical concoctions are considered "shelf stable" because they are simply created from the natural qualities found in herbs and other components, and thus can be stored for any length of time because they are not created from something intangible and tempermental like mana. Unstable, or magical, alchemy only lasts for 30 minutes because mana is not something that can just be left sitting around indefinitely - it will always flow somewhere. 30 minutes is approximately how long mana lasts before it dissipates into the air or sinks back into the earth, or simply becomes inert if it is contained in a sealed vessel leaving behind only the carrier components that were used to imbue mana into the potion.

Topic: Research: Before the Mists of Myre, where was the "territory" of House Kibou? The coastal port city of Nichiren? the north eastern part of Mandala?
Research: Who is the Lady of Pain? How is she connected to Styx water?
column5: While you would expect Lore: Demons to provide information about Styx, it is in fact, Lore: Fae that actually provides knowledge about this subject. Often referred to as "The Blood Fae," "The Lady of Pain," or "The Lady of Whispers," Styx has been described as a fae-like female with red eyes and long nails. Worshipped as a goddess of pain and retribution, some sects of her followers revel in the point where pain meets ecstasy, taking the hedonism of practices like the Eirie Faith to a whole other level. Lake Styx in the Southern Expanse is named for her, for drinking the waters of the lake brings extreme pain and torture to the living being who dared to drink of its waters.

Topic: Research: ways beyond standard religious rites that have successfully yielded direct help or interaction from gods when help is requested?
column5: Typically, the gods exist separately from the plane of Adelrune and cannot reach across to affect the world in some great manner. Religious rites and traditions are a small way in which gods can send a tiny bit of their power to benefit their followers, but in general they are going to be more focused on their own matters. However, for some beings, like demonic gods that aren't among the other gods and can walk upon Adelrune, can provide significant power to their followers in a much more direct manner as they are accessible to speak to directly rather than through prayers and potential visions. Different faiths have certainly made claims that rituals and practices within their faith can have much larger effects upon the world, but these situations typically come from bold claims and incidents of great magic that cannot be confirmed as divine in origin.

Topic: Research: Trying to uncover the origins and past masks of Father Neruk if any predate Morcin?
column5: The name and tradition of Father Neruk were created by the Trahazi during the early years of the Exile as a means of covering Morcin's dark past and protecting the Trahazi and their religious practices from Inquisitorial accusations of demon worship. Father Neruk has never been used as a name to refer to another being.

Topic: Research: How do ships navigate the ocean?
column5: Most sea-travel on Adelrune keeps generally within sight of coastlines, with the exceptions being crossing to the outer island nations like Eur, Cestral, Dace, Eirie, and Tear. In earlier eras of exploration, overshooting the goal location will just bring one closer to the wall of Mists that surround the known landmasses, and it is fairly easy to navigate back from there because the Mists press close to the outer islands and in a worst-case scenario a ship can simply sail in parallel to them and stumble upon sight of land eventually. The rise of international mercantile trade necessitated a more refined method of navigation, however, and this brought about the standardization of hiring diviners to serve as navigators, particularly with the creation of the spell Eyes of the Sea - not only could they navigate to more precise locations and landing areas, but they were more aware of other vessels in the area. For early adopters of the inclusion of diviners, this led to a rise in piracy of vessels otherwise unaware they were being approached if they did not have their own diviner aboard. Since the joining of Vein in the Rules of Society and the expansion of trade of their specialty ores known as lodestone, compasses have become a much more common way to navigate and allow for less reliance on diviners and their fees. Major ports of Adelrune have a massive lodestone pillar located somewhere within their habor which acts as a beacon for all compasses on Adelrune that are attuned to that particular stone and will always point towards it, regardless of distance. Each port has its own different lodestone, so a widely traveled ship known to frequent ports of call with vast open sea between them might have a panel of up to a dozen lodestone compasses all pointing to different ports on the quarterdeck or in the wheelhouse.

Topic: Research: The best landing location for a ship on the west coast of the Expanse? Is there a safe road to the Celestine Empre or Hastings area from this port?
column5: The most common port of call along the Red Coast is Cole, which has overland trade routes and established (well-maintained) roads to the Celestine Empire, Castle Thorn, and the former citystates of Mandala and Ad Decimum. Through the Celestine Empire, the roads extend across the isthmus into the Kingdom of Hastings and the Eastern Expanse.

Topic: Research: Can fae stones be created/destroyed?
column5: Fae Stones is somewhat of a misnomer, as they are in fact stones that were engraved by dragons with draconic runes and had an original, unknown origin before the fae got a hold of them to use them for their own purposes. As such, they can *theoretically* be created by dragons of immense power, but it seems unlikely to be the case since their original purpose was likely to already have been used and passed if the fae are using the now. There are two such notations in Returned history about stones being destroyed: the first occurred some years ago, after a particularly powerful vampire had been killed a stone was destroyed/consumed in the process of bringing him back, and the second was in recent months when a ritual was performed and the stone was fractured and theoretically destroyed. There is notation, however, that some Returned are attempting to fix this one, though they have not yet been successful.

Topic: Research: how do sea monsters fit into the "ecosphere"? apex predators? do they live in nutrient dense water with tons of food? do ocean currents affect them, their migrations, or their food sources?
column5: Sea Monsters is somewhat of a broad classification as great sea beasts fall all along the spectrum from filter feeders at the lower end to apex predators at the top, each with different and wildly varying diets and requirements. The term "sea monsters" gets even more unclear when regading the cold waters around northern Vein and Eur, where megafauna are common and so even normal fish might be considered a sea monster by an unsuspecting sailor unaccustomed to the area. Furthermore, sea beasts and other such creatures have not been able to be studied when they venture into the Mists as it is too dangerous for the common scholar or sailor to pursue so our information is limited by what has been observed. Many sea beasts also dwell in deep waters or are protected/hidden by various cultures and faiths which dampens information further. Migration is seen in some species throughout the year, particularly for those found in the Torrent, where many species will summer nearer to Tear and hover near the warmer waters of the Drir Isles during the winter. Throughout the rest of the world, however, their territories seem to be a little less expansive as they have adapted to the various ship routes - more predator-oriented sea beasts (like the Bakunnanu) will actively follow ships to hunt them with cleverness and patience for the right moment to strike, while others might seek out remote coastlines and shallow waters to sunbathe - one of these such creatures, the Pleco which can grow up to 40 feet long, is a passive sea beast and only known to eat algae by licking the rocks clean and then laying in the sun to aid in digestion. They are seemingly incapable of subsisting on just algae without the sunlight.

Research: experimenting with creating a heal 5 or a mana 2 potion with mangosteen fruit as a component. any special properties noticed? coming from the angle with specialized herbs have more specific effects
column5: Please mark off two uses of Mangosteen if you have not yet.  There do not seem to be any particularly special properties in a Healing Draught or Mana Draught potion when using mangosteen fruit to create them. You expect this is because such simple potions only draw on the immediate/obvious properties of an alchemical component and are not building from the more nuanced wholeness of the fruit itself and its more complex inherent properties.

Topic: Research: folklore about Fox, what stories Vicaul parents would have told their children
column5: As Fox was considered dead for hundreds of years, he was not worshipped nor do many tales of his exploits carry on even in the oral tradition. Most of what is said about him in the way of stories are tales of caution more related to Snake and the dangers of the influence of "Civilized Ideals" - for it was only after Snake observed the new gods warring among each other for power that Snake learned that to kill one of his bretheren could provide him more power than he currently possessed, and in taking such actions, he was banished from the pantheon to survive alone among the other (more powerful) new gods without the support of those he once stood beside.  In the course of your study, you find that any references to Fox are all written in the same cursive hand, but are frustratingly non-specific in their references to Old Ways, as if specifically following to the tradition of not putting the stories of the Old Ways into the written word. Crossed out among a list of other seemingly unrelated topics such as "birthday gift: yellow umbrella from Aislynn" and "tail eaten by a dragon" is a note of "is Kitar just Fox descended and weakened?"

Topic: Research: do Ferrymen use coins of the Damned to pay off their debts to the Bank of the Dead? how does a ferryman pay off their contract? how many coins of the damned does a ferryman's bell cost?
column5: These are all vastly different topics, and some of which are not covered by research as such answers cannot be found in a book and would be better investigated through influence actions or simply asking a ferryman in the game space.  For a ferryman to pay off their contract and fulfill the cost of their service varies greatly as each ferryman is created in a different method and under a different contract so there is no simple answer. It is known, however, that part of the process of becoming a ferryman strips the being of their former memories and life - and in doing so, it is strongly theorized by scholars that they lose the memory of the terms of their service. If the memory of their contract is lost, it is theorized that a ferryman cannot ever pay off their debt of service, and thus are doomed to act in their role as ferryman until the world finds oblivion and the Great Wyrms consume their final meal.

Research: what forms are celestials known to embody when they are on Adelrune?
column5: Celestials can take a great many forms on Adelrune, though they can vary greatly based on how much strength the Celestial retains after descending. Forms that require significantly less power to keep on Adelrune are possessing forms - when a mortal being of Adelrune consents to allowing a celestial to share their physical form. These host beings will often manifest some form of celestial features such as wings or thickened skins that cannot be cut with mundane blade, and are the most common way a celestial blooded is created. Such beings do not need to be human or even humanoid, however, as some celestials favor the form of a horse. When a celestial shares the body of a horse, it often results in the horse growing a horn or great wings, and such beings are referred to as unicorns or pegasus. For stronger celestials that have the power reserves to manifest their own physical bodies when descending to Adelrune, they will generally take on more humanoid forms so as not to overwhelm local populations with monsterous forms, however they too will still often have obviously celestial manifestations that reflect their powers and abilities, and give them away as being non-human.

Topic: Research:  what distinguishes a spell being stable or unstable alchemy? why can you drink lots of unstable mana or health potions, but limited stable?
column5: Stable alchemy and unstable alchemy inherently draw their creation and capabilities from the component parts that are used to create them, and as such have drastically different effects in the long-term. Unstable Alchemy, also called Magical Alchemy, is in many ways, not true alchemy - rather, an unstable alchemist is using their components as carriers to feed their own mana and intent into the potion as it is being mixed. Anything imaginable can be used as a component in a magical alchemical concoction because of this, even in the most dire circumstances there will always be something terribly mundane available to the alchemist, even if no herbs or standard components are within reach. It is said among the Saek that magical alchemy is more common than channeling simply because all an alchemist needs is a bit of spit and a few grains of sand to create a healing draught, while there is little life in the earth for a channeler to draw from. Because the flow of recouperation is coming directly from the mana capabilities of another, there is no restriction on how much one can imbibe, the limiting factor is rather in what the alchemist is capable of giving. Whereas with Stable Alchemical concoctions, the imbiber is limited by other effects that will cause negative effects to the body - namely, toxicity and poisoning from taking in too much of any particular herb that will cause them to get quite ill or even die. In particular, with both Healing Draughts and Mana Draughts requiring both purification and rejuvenation aspects, these can quickly upset the mortal body - with an overabundance of purification herbs causing severe gastrointestinal distress as it works against stomach acid, or reguvenation causing parts of the body to grow unexpectedly with cysts or great pustules.

Topic: Research: what is the Drir history of summoning and binding of demons, and how can we do it?
column5: The Drir are not a culture of writing down their practices in books that are kept in civilized libraries, so the information you can find is scarce. The Peleset are the Drir that are most well-versed in the summoning and binding of beings, not just demons but those are among the most common due simply to accessibility. These practices long pre-date the Drir as a people, and can be traced back to their historic roots as Hazori before the exile that drove them west during the Purification. These practices are just one of many reasons the Amalgamation and Inquisition took offense to their presence, but rather than give them up, the Peleset took to the road with their neighbors and simply traveled to a land beyond where anyone would come looking for them - to the very edge of the known world. The traditions of summoning and binding are closely-guarded secrets kept within the Peleset, and are only taught from master to student.

Topic: Research: NEEDS NEW TOPIC
Research: how is Phoenix connected to Drir's past? how can we solidify the relationship between Drir and Phoenix?
column5: Much of this information cannot be found in books within a library as it is an actively developing situation and the building of a relationship with Phoenix. However, you are able to pick up a few tidbits related to the territory of dragons, and the Drir history before the Purification. Among the first written records, when the Drir were still Hazori and an age before the exile that drove them west to escape the Inquisition, the Hazori lived across the Eastern Expanse, including in the territory that was once the home of a pair of red dragons. This territory is where Phoenix was awakened from before being offered the volcano of Kin'Mae as her new roost in the Drir Isles.

Topic: Research: why are specific herbs needed for potitions? specificially, grandmaster potions needing naming specific components?
column5: Many herbs and alchemical components have various properties than can be pulled on or enhanced to create simpler potions, such as using any particular type of sedative herb to soothe pain or help someone recover from injury through rest. With more complex potions, however, it is not simply these individual properties that are needed. In many cases, these complex potions rely on the more nuanced specifics that are contained within a single base component (like Eucalyptus leaves) to build the rest of the potion from. Using the Guild of Academics text "A Brief History of the Second Occurance of the Disease" allows us to continue the study of Eucalyptus leaves in this example, as this herb is particularly necessary in cleansing a body after it has come into direct contact with evil or entropic influences. Thus, Eucalyptus was the base component in creating the disease cure Vanya Cuil, a disease that spread through direct contact with a greater demon of plague or another being infected by it. As for other complex spells that require specific alchemical components, it is expected that each of the named component has some sort of additional property in this nuanced category that cannot simply be classified by calling something a "sedative herb".

Topic: Research: how do we find a ritual that separates bound souls, like one opposite of the binding ritual in Ig? Can Ig (or it's remnants) be found?
column5: The core goal of such a ritual would be to remove a single soul from a larger vessel containing multiple souls. In the situation of the Shadow, however, it might be more complicated or nuanced because the Shadow is not trapped within Ilbrim - it has a degree of free will and the ability to come and go as it pleases so building a directly opposed ritual would require having the original ritual done to bind the Shadow to Delfestrae. You suspect, however, that a combination of Coagulation and Entanglement from the Book of Ig were likely used as the basis for this ritual. For nearly all rituals across Adelrune there is an opposing ritual that has been researched, and in the case of most Ig rituals such as Coagulation and Entanglement, such counter-rituals were researched by the Black Tower of Ad Decimum. As the Returned have enough notes copied from the Book of Ig when it was still intact, it would also be possible to research your own version of the ritual at the cost of 30 research actions.  (please let me know if you would like to pursue the research track and I will provide a blue research tracker)

Topic: Research: What is the first recorded instance of the entity known as "Phyraon's shadow" being bound to a Delfestrae?
column5: Most records of the Shadow have been lost to history in the abandonment of the Delfestrae homestead, however it seemingly has always safeguarded the Effendal people not solely Delfestrae. The methods of its protections have varied over the years, but it has not always been bound. In notes attributed to a Radolond scholar identified as "Her Eyes Turn East", it is theorized that the Delfestrae were likely trying to control it and strengthen their connection with the Shadow to make it more reliable in its aid, and less prone to wandering. More recent documentation notes it was was briefly bound to the Returned called Belladonna, but wasn't always with her. With the appointment of Ilbrim to being the First, the shadow seemingly chose Ilbrim, and in the aftermath he got sick due to its influence, as one might from an organ transplant.

Topic: Research: is there a way to restore Phyraon's Shadow to her original state before he was burned?
column5: The Shadow is simply a shadow, it is not the being known as Rhiannon.

Topic: Research: how could an entity become similar to the Shadow?
column5: There are no other records of beings like the Shadow. It is likely you would need to be up close and personal to study the shadow to learn its true nature and what touched it to create it.

Topic: Research: what is the bound spirit of the Zealout capable of? does the Zealot's power have limitations now that it is bound? are there effects on the person who summons it?
column5: The binding of the Zealot to the skull of Yog Zura simply limits his reach into the world of Adelrune, but he is by and large still extremely dangerous. Particularly if someone unaware, untrained, or incapable of fighting his domineering personality were to put on the skull. It is immediately clear that it takes an exceptionally strong-willed person to resist his strength, with a minimum skillset of a Grandmaster Summoner. Furthermore, the longer the skull is worn (does not need to be on the head, but simply worn on the body in some manner) or the spirit invoked, the more difficult it is to push the Zealot back into the skull when you wish to retake your own body. When wearing the skull or invoking the spirit, the summoner must expend a use of willpower for each three minute duration that it is worn in order to retain control over their body and push the Zealot out. Should the body be killed while the Zealot is either in control or present on a body, you can theorize that he will likely attempt to snatch full control of the form, though this will require more research to understand the full scope of what that would mean. The binding of Lucien in the skull with the Zealot may be somewhat of a wildcard in dealing with this particular situation, but you cannot begin to sort out what that particular situation might be.  Next gathering, you will have a blue research card on the bulletin board at check-in that will allow you to figure out the extent of the Zealot's power you have available to you. Feel free to catch me at game with any lore bids or other chaos y'all wanna do with it!

Topic: Research: how does the death of Mehs affect the world tree root corruption, and will it make it easier to stop or slow the existing corruption? will Mehs' generals be able to corrupt a healthy world tree root without Mehs or his domain of Hatred?
column5: As this is not something that has occurred before and has not been written about in books to research, nor as it had an observable affect, so the best you can do is theorize possibilities for the future.  With the Ra'e'len root of the World Tree defiled, Mehs' immediate presence in the area didn't seem to have any effect on the rate of spread of the defilement, so you would presume it will continue to spread regardless of what plane of existence Mehs is currently residing in. As the roots were hidden away and protected for centuries, it stands to reason that the various roots across Adelrune will continue to be vulnerable to defilement by any demon of sufficient power who has the backing of Mehs.

Topic: Research: What historic incidents or rumors exist regarding people or divine powers interacting directly with the golden realms?
column5: Within the native lores you and your assistants have, there are few historical records regarding mortal beings interacting directly with the Golden Realms, and none of outside divine powers since divine beings do not typically reveal their actions to mortals on Adelrune. Most interactions seem to be one-way with beings descending from the Golden Realms to Adelrune, while most records of mortals reaching out to the Golden Realms is due to summoning magics and rituals. Cultural Understanding: How does Castle Thorn grow food/feed itself?
column5: Castle Thorn is surrounded by a wasteland that was created from countless battles (and possibly the Abyssal miasma that has ruined the Breach, creeping into the Expanse). Since crops are difficult to grow, Castle Thorn relies heavily on the city states and Empires to its north for food, supplies, and armaments.  That said, Thorns are some of the best farmers and gardeners in the world, utilizing the little they have to grow what they can in windowboxes and extensive rooftop garden beds. Spices and teas from Thorn are well regarded, and they add flavor that hides the questionable meat and dried beans imported from other nations.

Topic: Research: what are the Dread Knight's current status, history, and tenants?
column5: Unfortunately "current status" updates are not covered by Research actions, as it is not something written about in texts in libraries.  The Dread Knights of Breach are not a singular, organized group - rather, like the different militaries of the independent demon cities, they are unique to the demon lords they serve and the cities they serve within. Similarly to other errant knight orders of the various wild nations, the Dread Knights do not have set tenants to their order. Honor is rare amongst those who dwell within the Demon Cities of Breach. Their Dread Knights are infamous, though exceedingly rare.

Research: what are the different ways in which the world tree roots are getting defiled, so that we can know what to look for when trying to stop the defilement?
column5: Defilement is, at its core, a corruption that is spread from demonic sources. Seen on a previously unmatched level in recent years, Defilement spreads quickly as it feeds on the roots of the World Tree and transitions the formerly healthy root to that of the Blood Tree. It differs greatly from the simple entropy of the Abyss which leaks out into Adelrune from the demon portals opened in the Breach at the end of the Age of Eur, though no solution has been found to counteract even the slow-spreading entropic corruption after more than 400 years. The only known stopgap for the spread of the Breach's entropic corruption, a slower form of Defilement, was the ritual that ended the Crusades at the Final Battle by severing the continent in two - isolating the Southern Expanse (colloquially called the Breach) from where it had once touched the Western Expanse. A similar (though drastically different) concept was used to drain the land of life across a wide swath of Tear, creating a so-called "firebreak" to stop the spread of the Defilement before it took over the whole island in a matter of months. As observed twice now, water seems to be the most reliable barrier to stop such corruptions from spreading, or some other drastic maneuver to cut off anything that it could touch.

Research: Through what process does the act of channeling restore the body to a more hale state? does it accelerate the normal processes and if so, how does it reset limbs to their proper state? why would the act of healing through channeling not also de-age the recipient?
column5: The act of Channeling does not accelerate natural healing rates, moreso it is the act of moving new life force into something that isn't currently holding it. So channeling to heal or to mend broken limbs is done by putting life into something that is actively in the process of dying or in a state of death to return it to a state of living. As this exchange of life and death energies passes through the channeler, it places longterm wear and tear upon the channeler themself leading to premature aging and fragility.
