Topic: Research: how is a knightly order funded? what kind of financial burden should be expected?
column5: A knight order requires the funding to provide for itself and support its various members.  An order is not simply comprised of knights, but of scholars, seneschals, workers, smiths and crafters, and others who require payment and materials to do their jobs to support the knights of the Order. They require funds to be sent abroad as liasons to other Courts or to handle situations elsewhere in the world, as well as simple things like paper and ink, stablehands and care for the horses, training gear and armor, or even foodstuffs are the responsibility of the Order to provide. For an Order to be effective in Adelrune, it needs a basis of financial support or else the individual knights will need to find a way to support themselves and be paid in some form of day job, drawing their attention away from their duties as knights. Every individual knight requires funding both to serve their Order effectively, but also to be able to afford to live as people in the world, as do auxiliary members of the order. A small Order of ten knights might not require more than a handful of gold each month, but the larger the order the numbers grow exponentially.

Research: what does refounding a house entail? is there a metaphysical/magical/spiritual sense?
column5: In general, "refounding" a House is not something simply done. In the situation of the reforming of House Ra'e'len, the Svivore and Rakuten rallied to Palauttaa after her Returning and she raised a number of ritari and others to join her as nobility within the House because she had the trust of her tribes that were still living, and she knew the secrets and practices of the House so there was no loss in the knowledge. For the other fallen Houses, unless they have a tribe to rally behind them, it is unlikely that any of the other Houses will be refounded with any amount of strength or support as most they lost their tribes with them, and a great deal of loss of information occurred as part of the Purges.

Topic: Research: is Ney'wen "the Nine-Tailed Fox" related to the Gods?
column5: Ney'ewn, "The Nine-Tailed Fox" does not seem to have a direct correlation with the Old Ways gods, at least until very recently. The most support for this theory you can find is a page of theory crafting in an old, worn leather journal hidden in the back of the Guild of Academics:  What is Kitar? - "kitsune," not a fae (confirmed) - once had nine tails, but has lost them over the centuries so he only has one left (one eaten by a dragon, one snatched by the Iron Chalice, others unaccounted for) - hasn't had a home in over 700 years, we'll fix that - celebrating his birthday next week because Aislynn and I decided he deserves a party - she's making him a cute yellow umbrella, I'm going to give him a home next to mine - pyromancer, has a ring that gives him extra power to his fire wards - clever warden, he added a fear glyph to my doorframe the little shit - his tails have power and can be expended to do important things - confided that he would give up his last tail for me if he had to... I do not know what to do with that information except protect him so he will never have to. I think... I think I might be falling for him, and this makes me think he might feel the same - he's added new tiles to the hallway outside our bedrooms for "protection" (he just wanted to see me trigger the glyphs) - Kitar is missing... I cannot find him - he needed to hide from the demons so he locked himself away in his umbrella - I found it in the forest and had to beg the gods to give him back to me... I don't know that I can do this without him - he's safe now, but I cannot stop myself from checking on him again and again to make sure he hasn't disappeared again - Kitar is not Fox (confirmed) - I think he knew Ney'ewn once upon a time, but he does not remember - his memories from the early ages are messy at best and many were lost  - from all I can find, Kitar has only ever been the one kitsune

Topic: Research: mangosteen interaction with the disease plaguing Concerti, moss skeleton fungus, & blood root fungus

Research: for the Myren disease effecting the Nadine - if we have a temporary cure, can we concentrate it? can it be combined with Holly's Kiss to improve results? can magic be used to increase effectiveness?
column5: The temporary treatment that was researched only treats the first symptons of the Suchron disease, it does not provide a cure for the advancing disease. As the Suchron Disease does not affect the direct life force of the infected, Holly's Kiss does not seem to provide any additional benefit. Creating the treatment with magical alchemy does nothing to treat the disease or symptoms at all, as it does not have the base components needed to aid in recovery.  In your experimentation, you begin to observe the second stage of the disease developing in your patients: severe lethargy begins to set in, causing the infected to sleep for great periods of the day. With a massively reduced appetite, they will begin to starve and deteriorate in this stage as all the proteins in their body begin to twist and develop into spun sugars within their body. Keeping them awake and active seems to be the most important part of slowing this development, which you are able to aid in treatment by creating a brew of 3 Stimulant and 1 Spiritual herb per dose.

Topic: Research: what are the fae? what are aspects?
column5: While childish song and rhyme certainly provides a whimsical glimpse of the fair folk, it does little to illustrate just how archaic, powerful, and oftentimes duplicitous the Fae can truly be. They hail from a timeless age, with some records of their activity on Tear predating even the ancient city-state of Theddespari. Little is known about their motivations (if any), and those that spend too much time in pursuit of them routinely go missing. They can take a myriad of forms, and posses a mastery over the magical arts that puts to shame even the most accomplished of magi. Thankfully, Iron is remarkably effective against the Fae and their kin, but heed this: if you are foolish enough to strike a deal with the Fair Folk, even iron will not save you, should you break your word. From the Notes of Ambassador Archmagus Gomadawn of the Celestine Empire 170ch - 5ar  The Fae by their nature are paradoxical. At all times they are both warriors and the furthest thing from it.  By Nature, they are the guardians of the boundaries between worlds. For this reason they are blessed with great arcane power, but they must always be aloof, for while they are quite strong, they are also incredibly weak. The Fae are considered the masters of realities, in that for the strongest of Fae, their power is only limited by their imagination. In the age of Eur, when the Dragons ruled the sky and all things were in balance, Fae power was at its height and natural magic was supreme. The Fae are divided into various courts that befitted their mercurial natures and alliances - these are often referred to as Dawn-Dusk or Twixt-Twain, but are not so simply defined. They are comprised entirely of magic; as such they do not cast through spellwork, but instead weave it directly into the fabric of reality as an expression of their will.

Topic: Research: why do fae fixate on Manners? how do oaths and deals work? curses?
column5: “Thank you”, and “I’m sorry” These are utterly VILE swear words among the Fair Folk. It’s no strange thing for the Fae to take a great measure of offense hearing them. They are meaningless words mortals let tumble out of their mouths without any second thought, dismissing the true sincerity they are meant to express. The Fae don’t want to HEAR you say say you are sorry, they want to KNOW why you are sorry. All Fae innately know how to weave a curse, dripping misfortune into the lives of mortals who invoke their ire. The wrath of a Fae curse tends to fall in proportion to the offense;

Topic: Research: are there any wood types outside of the known ones, and if yes, where are they found?
column5: There are no other wood types outside of the documented ones, however all woods have additional or more specific properties to them than simply their attributes such as dense and durable. For example, pear wood is used in musical instruments and is said to make the most beautiful music favored by beings such as dragons, trolls, and even divine entities.

What is known from general history, however, is that as the Nadine marched on the city of Myre in the War of Poisons, the young child of King Myaous made a pact with the fae.  His pact ensured that one day Myre would be restored, and all that was lost would be returned.

Ruin's wish was that Myre would rise from its foundations and rejoin the current world of Adelrune.

Research: what instances are there of combining alchemy with other magics? why is it more versitile/efficient than other magics?
column5: The combination of magics strictly limits what is being done to the realm of rituals rather than normal magic. Alchemy is an important aspect to nearly all rituals - from the creation of bone chalk to the mixing of herbs and other components in the right combination, to portions of rituals that are intended to be consumed or injected - alchemy is often an integral part to the ritual process. Alchemy itself is not more versitile or efficient than the other magics - it has many extra steps requiring additional components and has a more difficult delivery method through elixir or injection. Though some aspects like a healers draught may seemingly offer more healing in a pinch than channeling, the need for preparing the potion and having the components necessary take time and more effort, whereas channeling life in a desperate situation can simply be drawn straight from the channeler if open earth is not available.

Research: physical sites of Myzka worship near Riel
column5: Myzka, said to have once been the patron goddess of Riel, has long-been erased from the city. Once the most beautiful city in the world with a grand cathedral in the center of the city built in her honor, the conquering of Riel by the Amalgamation brought an end to Myzka's place there. Instead, the cathedral was re-dedicated to the Church of Chorus and had its statuary and stained glass replaced to reflect its new faith. Further south, near the mountains, great stone quarries have long been excavated for their beautiful white stone - that which was used to build not only Riel, but also the fortress city of Whitehall - in Trahazi tradition, it is said those quarries were blessed by Myzka herself as her preferred stone for carving and creating from, and that the touch of her divinity (and concecration) still runs through the stone.

Topic: Political Insight: Old Gaius
column5: Lord Gaius Calae is the uncle of Duke Piero Calae, an elderly alchemist who has quietly tended to the greenhouses and House Calae's herb supplies for decades. Perferring a life out of the spotlight and attention, Lord Gaius took on dozens of apprentices and students over the years, with many of House Calae's alchemists trained under his direct supervision. Generally free of scandal and intrigue due to his reclusive and solitary anture, he was unfortunately shunted directly in the line of fire following the War of Radiance when it was revealed that he had been the teacher of Mieletassa, a full demon Returned who had orchestrated the trade of Calae herbs for effendal jewelry through her connections to House Calae and House Delfestrae. Though Gaius had not been involved in the exchange in any way, he still was painted as the scapegoat for the arrangement even being approached - with multiple Court Assassins seeking his head, it was only due to his reclusive nature that he was not killed before Lord Phineas Calae could interceed on his behalf. Instead of being painted the scapegoat, he is now treated as a fool, one who was susceptible to the wiles of a demon and let himself be played - that is, he is now considered a liability to the House. While he is no longer the target of assassination, he has lost all good-will with the House and polite society, so he spends most of his days sitting the Lord Phineas as they both advance in age, or in his private gardens. Due to the scandal and being viewed as a liability, he is no longer permitted to work in the Calae greenhouses or take on students.

Political Insight: Clove Tackett
column5: Althought he is a Returned, Viscount Clove Tackett has built both a name and reputation for himself in Cestral. Serving two years as the Cestral Seneschal to the Returned, he often filled in for the duties of the Ambassador, and made it a personal quest to track down Cestral's missing Unity Representative following the receeding of the Mists in 11ar. Though the missing representative was not recovered, Clove did see Lord Liam Montgomery's mission on Tear to its completion, and in doing so saw to the death of a shapeshifter demon who has hovered on the fringes of Cestral society for decades. The completion of this mission not only brought home a historical relic of great importance, but stopped an assassination attempt on the King, and even more catastrophic trouble from befalling Cestral. He was awarded the title of Viscount for his service to the Crown and has developed a strong rapport with both the Queen and Crown Prince. When the War of Ten Candles threatened Cestral's capital, it was Clove Tackett who alerted the Crown of the immanent threat and gave them enough warning to rally the military against the sudden appearance of the demon horde through portals just outside the city gates, preventing the city from being overrrun by T'tocs and his forces. Clove returned to Cestral in the immediate aftermath, taking on an emergency position as liaison to the Effendal Returned and coordinating the retaliatory attack against the demon forces that had retreated to the countryside, as well as serving as an advisor to the War of Ten Candles war council. Though he has a very publicly antagonistic relationship with the Cestral Head of Finance, he has been a strong asset to the Crown and has mediated a number of inter-House issues and international trade arrangements. He has remained in Cestral since the War of Ten Candles began, still serving as an advisor to the Crown.

Topic: Investigate History: Wellspring (Rory's letter from the Nightingale)
column5: The Wellspring is not quite specific enough a phrase to gain reliable information, but your researchers are able to narrow down some options or theories as to what it may be referencing. The location of The Wellspring seems rather unclear, with dozens of options and theories written about it, particularly when trying to determine which "Wellspring" is being referenced - context clues about demons suggest it could be the Wellspring of the Abyss, the portals in which the demons pour into Adelrune from the Abyss. However, the general consesus your researchers come back to again and again is that The Wellspring is most likely referring to the Wellspring of Life, a sacred and divine location from which all life has sprung. Different faiths refer to this place as sacred ground of different gods - Veyn or Ren for the Old Ways, Cri or Destiny for the Celestine - others suggest that rather than a consecrated land, The Wellspring may be a poetic way to reference Dragon Song, whereas local cultists in Port Frey suggest it may be the site of the World Tree.

Topic: Political Insight: Phineas Calae
column5: Lord Phineas Calae is among the lesser nobles of House Calae, though he once wielded quite a bit of influence in his younger years.  With the recent founding of Ad Decimum, Lord Phineas ventured to the rumored magical city with his sights set on establishing a thriving trade empire to exchange House Calae's finest spell scrolls for works of magic. He found a great deal of success in a particularly lucrative trade arrangement, and was once known as the man who brought a limited supply of Ad Decimum's newest invention - the clock! - to Dace for many years before Dacian artistans learned to craft their own. The loss of his daughter and a series of bad business ventures plagued his middle age, losing him position and influence in his later life, and he had been assumed to fall into a life of obscurity in his old age... until the War of Radiance. Following the reveal that it was Lord Gaius Calae's student who had brokered the trade agreement for effendal jewelry, House Calae itself had turned on the old man and put the full blame on him rather than their own negotiations and lack of checking their imported effendal jewelry. Seeing the move for what it was, however, Lord Phinease used the last bit of his influence and sway to threaten Duke Piero Calae, though no one knows what happened behind closed doors or what exactly the threat was. In the aftermath, the story changed - it was the sole fault of Gaius' student, a full demon who had taken advantage of the old man's kind nature and favor of alchemical study over people, and Gaius could not be held accountable for being susceptible to such a being's sway. In the end, however, it had cost Lord Phineas everything. Nearing ninety years of age, it was a distant relation who took pity on the old man, taking him in to see that he would have a comfortable retirement out of the public eye and away from scrutiny or talk. Lord Phineas and old Gaius still seem to be close, still spending many of their days together, though Lord Phineas' mind fades more and more by the day.

Topic: Underworld Insight: Phineas Calae
column5: Running a lucrative international trade operation of spell scrolls often means having dealings with those of lesser morales, particularly in the decades following House Tremaine's fall from grace. Blood Ink was fairly difficult to come by except through Mandala and formerly-House Tremaine, so to make sure he had enough Blood Ink to satisfy his deliveries of spell scrolls to Ad Decimum, Lord Phineas Calae built a strong trade arrangement with blood ink suppliers from the city of Inexo. Though Inexo was a bustling trade city, the Breach was always lacking in its necessities, and so Lord Phineas Calae made himself a prominent provider of the nicer things that the merchants and wealthy of the city could not otherwise get their hands on except through trade. This arrangement worked well for him for decades, rebuilding and restablishing House Calae as a reliable source of spell scrolls, and even allowed him to bring new scrolls to the Dacian market and Decimal towers that had been created in Inexo.

Inv. Vices & Routines + HP: King Halvar
column5: King Halvar has always been a generally unpleasant man, prone to violent outbursts, and favoring an excess of alcohol. In recent months, however, these habits have only grown worse. Where he previously paid heed to the old gods, toasting Thte with every round of drink, and competing in feats of strength and sparring with his strongest soldiers midday, his actions have taken on a much darker tone. He is no longer content with defeating those around him in competition, now he goes for blood or even death, his rage heightened and his toasts to Thte have become calls to war. His warriors and clan have answered this call, their own rages growing more intense as they spar, preparing for war. A new ally stands among them, egging on their rage and strength, taking the place of the former druid who once advised Halvar seemingly overnight. A beared man short in stature but large in presence, seemingly pleased with the preparations for war.
